All vectors must have the same length, and all elements must lie in a common field. The distances distribution is a list d of length Length(vec)+1, such that the value d[i] is the number of vectors in vecs that have distance i+1 to vec.
DistancesDistributionVecFFEsVecFFE.title: DistancesDistributionVecFFEsVecFFE

DistancesDistributionMatFFEVecFFE.name: DistancesDistributionMatFFEVecFFE
DistancesDistributionMatFFEVecFFE.description: returns the distances distribution of the vector vec to the vectors in the vector space generated by the rows of the matrix mat over the finite field F. The length of the rows of mat and the length of vec must be equal, and all entries must lie in F. The rows of mat must be linearly independent. The distances distribution is a list d of length Length(vec)+1, such that the value d[i] is the number of vectors in the vector space generated by the rows of mat that have distance i+1 to vec.
DistancesDistributionMatFFEVecFFE.title: DistancesDistributionMatFFEVecFFE

AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFE.name: AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFE
AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFE.description:
These functions run through the f-linear combinations of the vectors in the rows of the matrix mat that can be written as linear combinations of exactly cnt rows (that is without using zero as a coefficient). The length of the rows of mat and the length of vec must be equal, and all elements must lie in the field f. The rows of mat must be linearly independent. AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFE returns a vector from these that is closest to the vector vec. If it finds a vector of distance at most stop, which must be a nonnegative integer, then it stops immediately and returns this vector.

AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFECoords returns a length 2 list containing the same closest vector and also a vector v with exactly cnt non-zero entries, such that v times mat is the closest vector.
AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFE.title: AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFE

AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFECo.name: AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFECoords
AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFECo.description:
These functions run through the f-linear combinations of the vectors in the rows of the matrix mat that can be written as linear combinations of exactly cnt rows (that is without using zero as a coefficient). The length of the rows of mat and the length of vec must be equal, and all elements must lie in the field f. The rows of mat must be linearly independent. AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFE returns a vector from these that is closest to the vector vec. If it finds a vector of distance at most stop, which must be a nonnegative integer, then it stops immediately and returns this vector.

AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFECoords returns a length 2 list containing the same closest vector and also a vector v with exactly cnt non-zero entries, such that v times mat is the closest vector.
AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFECo.title: AClosestVectorCombinationsMatFFEVecFFE

CosetLeadersMatFFE.name: CosetLeadersMatFFE
CosetLeadersMatFFE.description: returns a list of representatives of minimal weight for the cosets of a code. mat must be a check matrix for the code, the code is defined over the finite field f. All rows of mat must have the same length, and all elements must lie in the field f. The rows of mat must be linearly independent.
CosetLeadersMatFFE.title: CosetLeadersMatFFE

ValuePol.name: ValuePol
ValuePol.description:
Let coeff be the coefficients list of a univariate polynomial f, and x a ring element. Then ValuePol returns the value f(x).

The coefficient of x^i is assumed to be stored at position i+1 in the coefficients list.
ValuePol.title: ValuePol

ProductCoeffs.name: ProductCoeffs
ProductCoeffs.description: Let p1 (and p2) be polynomials given by the first len1 (len2) entries of the coefficient list list2 (list2). If len1 and len2 are omitted, they default to the lengths of list1 and list2. This operation returns the coefficient list of the product of p1 and p2.
ProductCoeffs.title: ProductCoeffs

ReduceCoeffs.name: ReduceCoeffs
ReduceCoeffs.description: Let p1 (and p2) be polynomials given by the first len1 (len2) entries of the coefficient list list1 (list2). If len1 and len2 are omitted, they default to the lengths of list1 and list2. ReduceCoeffs changes list1 to the coefficient list of the remainder when dividing p1 by p2. This operation changes list1 which therefore must be a mutable list. The operation returns the position of the last non-zero entry of the result but is not guaranteed to remove trailing zeroes.
ReduceCoeffs.title: ReduceCoeffs

ReduceCoeffsMod.name: ReduceCoeffsMod
ReduceCoeffsMod.description: Let p1 (and p2) be polynomials given by the first len1 (len2) entries of the coefficient list list1 (list2). If len1 and len2 are omitted, they default to the lengths of list1 and list2. ReduceCoeffsMod changes list1 to the coefficient list of the remainder when dividing p1 by p2 modulo modulus, which must be a positive integer. This operation changes list1 which therefore must be a mutable list. The operation returns the position of the last non-zero entry of the result but is not guaranteed to remove trailing zeroes.
ReduceCoeffsMod.title: ReduceCoeffsMod

PowerModCoeffs.name: PowerModCoeffs
PowerModCoeffs.description: Let p1 and p2 be polynomials whose coefficients are given by the first len1 resp. len2 entries of the lists list1 and list2, respectively. If len1 and len2 are omitted, they default to the lengths of list1 and list2. Let exp be a positive integer. PowerModCoeffs returns the coefficient list of the remainder when dividing the exp-th power of p1 by p2. The coefficients are reduced already while powers are computed, therefore avoiding an explosion in list length.
PowerModCoeffs.title: PowerModCoeffs

ShiftedCoeffs.name: ShiftedCoeffs
ShiftedCoeffs.description: produces a new coefficient list new obtained by the rule new[i+shift]:= list[i] and filling initial holes by the appropriate zero.
ShiftedCoeffs.title: ShiftedCoeffs

InfoMatrix.name: InfoMatrix
InfoMatrix.description: The info class for matrix operations is InfoMatrix.
InfoMatrix.title: InfoMatrix

IsMatrix.name: IsMatrix
IsMatrix.description:
By convention matrix is a list of lists of equal length whose entries lie in a common ring.

For technical reasons laid out at the top of Chapter 24, the filter IsMatrix is a synonym for a table of ring elements, (see IsTable (21.1-4) and IsRingElement (31.14-16)). This means that IsMatrix returns true for tables such as [[1,2],[3]]. If necessary, IsRectangularTable (21.1-5) can be used to test whether an object is a list of homogeneous lists of equal lengths manually.

Note that matrices may have different multiplications, besides the usual matrix product there is for example the Lie product. So there are categories such as IsOrdinaryMatrix (24.2-2) and IsLieMatrix (24.2-3) that describe the matrix multiplication. One can form the product of two matrices only if they support the same multiplication.
IsMatrix.title: IsMatrix

IsOrdinaryMatrix.name: IsOrdinaryMatrix
IsOrdinaryMatrix.description:
An ordinary matrix is a matrix whose multiplication is the ordinary matrix multiplication.

Each matrix in internal representation is in the category IsOrdinaryMatrix, and arithmetic operations with objects in IsOrdinaryMatrix produce again matrices in IsOrdinaryMatrix.

Note that we want that Lie matrices shall be matrices that behave in the same way as ordinary matrices, except that they have a different multiplication. So we must distinguish the different matrix multiplications, in order to be able to describe the applicability of multiplication, and also in order to form a matrix of the appropriate type as the sum, difference etc. of two matrices which have the same multiplication.
IsOrdinaryMatrix.title: IsOrdinaryMatrix

IsLieMatrix.name: IsLieMatrix
IsLieMatrix.description:
A Lie matrix is a matrix whose multiplication is given by the Lie bracket. (Note that a matrix with ordinary matrix multiplication is in the category IsOrdinaryMatrix (24.2-2).)

Each matrix created by LieObject (64.1-1) is in the category IsLieMatrix, and arithmetic operations with objects in IsLieMatrix produce again matrices in IsLieMatrix.
IsLieMatrix.title: IsLieMatrix

DimensionsMat.name: DimensionsMat
DimensionsMat.description: is a list of length 2, the first being the number of rows, the second being the number of columns of the matrix mat. If mat is malformed, that is, it is not a IsRectangularTable (21.1-5), returns fail.
DimensionsMat.title: DimensionsMat

DefaultFieldOfMatrix.name: DefaultFieldOfMatrix
DefaultFieldOfMatrix.description:
For a matrix mat, DefaultFieldOfMatrix returns either a field (not necessarily the smallest one) containing all entries of mat, or fail.

If mat is a matrix of finite field elements or a matrix of cyclotomics, DefaultFieldOfMatrix returns the default field generated by the matrix entries (see 59.3 and 18.1).
DefaultFieldOfMatrix.title: DefaultFieldOfMatrix

TraceMatrix.name: TraceMatrix
TraceMatrix.description: The trace of a square matrix is the sum of its diagonal entries.
TraceMatrix.title: TraceMatrix

TraceMat.name: TraceMat
TraceMat.description: The trace of a square matrix is the sum of its diagonal entries.
TraceMat.title: TraceMatrix

Trace.name: Trace
Trace.description: The trace of a square matrix is the sum of its diagonal entries.
Trace.title: TraceMatrix

Trace.name: Trace
Trace.description:
Trace returns the trace of the field element z. If two fields L and K are given then the trace is computed w.r.t. the field extension L > K, if only one field L is given then LeftActingDomain( L ) is taken as default for the subfield K, and if no field is given then DefaultField( z ) is taken as default for L.

The trace of a matrix is the sum of its diagonal entries. Note that this is not compatible with the definition of Trace for field elements, so the one-argument version is not suitable when matrices shall be regarded as field elements.
Trace.title: Traces of field elements and matrices

Trace.name: Trace
Trace.description:
Trace returns the trace of the field element z. If two fields L and K are given then the trace is computed w.r.t. the field extension L > K, if only one field L is given then LeftActingDomain( L ) is taken as default for the subfield K, and if no field is given then DefaultField( z ) is taken as default for L.

The trace of a matrix is the sum of its diagonal entries. Note that this is not compatible with the definition of Trace for field elements, so the one-argument version is not suitable when matrices shall be regarded as field elements.
Trace.title: Traces of field elements and matrices

DeterminantMatrix.name: DeterminantMatrix
DeterminantMatrix.description:
returns the determinant of the square matrix mat.

These methods assume implicitly that mat is defined over an integral domain whose quotient field is implemented in GAP. For matrices defined over an arbitrary commutative ring with one see DeterminantMatDivFree (24.4-6).
DeterminantMatrix.title: DeterminantMatrix

DeterminantMat.name: DeterminantMat
DeterminantMat.description:
returns the determinant of the square matrix mat.

These methods assume implicitly that mat is defined over an integral domain whose quotient field is implemented in GAP. For matrices defined over an arbitrary commutative ring with one see DeterminantMatDivFree (24.4-6).
DeterminantMat.title: DeterminantMatrix

Determinant.name: Determinant
Determinant.description:
returns the determinant of the square matrix mat.

These methods assume implicitly that mat is defined over an integral domain whose quotient field is implemented in GAP. For matrices defined over an arbitrary commutative ring with one see DeterminantMatDivFree (24.4-6).
Determinant.title: DeterminantMatrix

DeterminantMatrixDestructive.name: DeterminantMatrixDestructive
DeterminantMatrixDestructive.description: Does the same as DeterminantMatrix (24.4-4), with the difference that it may destroy its argument. The matrix mat must be mutable.
DeterminantMatrixDestructive.title: DeterminantMatrixDestructive

DeterminantMatDestructive.name: DeterminantMatDestructive
DeterminantMatDestructive.description: Does the same as DeterminantMatrix (24.4-4), with the difference that it may destroy its argument. The matrix mat must be mutable.
DeterminantMatDestructive.title: DeterminantMatrixDestructive

DeterminantMatrixDivFree.name: DeterminantMatrixDivFree
DeterminantMatrixDivFree.description: return the determinant of a square matrix mat over an arbitrary commutative ring with one using the division free method of Mahajan and Vinay [MV97].
DeterminantMatrixDivFree.title: DeterminantMatrixDivFree

DeterminantMatDivFree.name: DeterminantMatDivFree
DeterminantMatDivFree.description: return the determinant of a square matrix mat over an arbitrary commutative ring with one using the division free method of Mahajan and Vinay [MV97].
DeterminantMatDivFree.title: DeterminantMatrixDivFree

IsEmptyMatrix.name: IsEmptyMatrix
IsEmptyMatrix.description:
Returns: A boolean

Is true if the matrix object M either has zero columns or zero rows, and false otherwise. In other words, a matrix object is empty if it has no entries.
IsEmptyMatrix.title: IsEmptyMatrix

IsMonomialMatrix.name: IsMonomialMatrix
IsMonomialMatrix.description: A matrix is monomial if and only if it has exactly one nonzero entry in every row and every column.
IsMonomialMatrix.title: IsMonomialMatrix

IsDiagonalMatrix.name: IsDiagonalMatrix
IsDiagonalMatrix.description: return true if the matrix mat has only zero entries off the main diagonal, and false otherwise.
IsDiagonalMatrix.title: IsDiagonalMatrix

IsDiagonalMat.name: IsDiagonalMat
IsDiagonalMat.description: return true if the matrix mat has only zero entries off the main diagonal, and false otherwise.
IsDiagonalMat.title: IsDiagonalMatrix

IsUpperTriangularMatrix.name: IsUpperTriangularMatrix
IsUpperTriangularMatrix.description: return true if the matrix mat has only zero entries below the main diagonal, and false otherwise.
IsUpperTriangularMatrix.title: IsUpperTriangularMatrix

IsUpperTriangularMat.name: IsUpperTriangularMat
IsUpperTriangularMat.description: return true if the matrix mat has only zero entries below the main diagonal, and false otherwise.
IsUpperTriangularMat.title: IsUpperTriangularMatrix

IsLowerTriangularMatrix.name: IsLowerTriangularMatrix
IsLowerTriangularMatrix.description: return true if the matrix mat has only zero entries above the main diagonal, and false otherwise.
IsLowerTriangularMatrix.title: IsLowerTriangularMatrix

IsLowerTriangularMat.name: IsLowerTriangularMat
IsLowerTriangularMat.description: return true if the matrix mat has only zero entries above the main diagonal, and false otherwise.
IsLowerTriangularMat.title: IsLowerTriangularMatrix

IdentityMat.name: IdentityMat
IdentityMat.description: returns a (mutable) m×m identity matrix over the ring given by R. Here, R can be either a ring, or an element of a ring. By default, an integer matrix is created.
IdentityMat.title: IdentityMat

NullMat.name: NullMat
NullMat.description: returns a (mutable) m×n null matrix over the ring given by by R. Here, R can be either a ring, or an element of a ring. By default, an integer matrix is created.
NullMat.title: NullMat

EmptyMatrix.name: EmptyMatrix
EmptyMatrix.description: is an empty (ordinary) matrix in characteristic char that can be added to or multiplied with empty lists (representing zero-dimensional row vectors). It also acts (via the operation \^ (31.12-1)) on empty lists.
EmptyMatrix.title: EmptyMatrix

DiagonalMat.name: DiagonalMat
DiagonalMat.description: returns a diagonal matrix mat with the diagonal entries given by vector.
DiagonalMat.title: DiagonalMat

DiagonalMatrix.name: DiagonalMatrix
DiagonalMatrix.description:
Returns: a square matrix or matrix object with column number equal to the length of the dense list vector, whose diagonal entries are given by the entries of vector, and whose off-diagonal entries are zero.

If a semiring R is given then it will be the base domain (see BaseDomain (26.3-1)) of the returned matrix. In this case, a filter filt can be specified that defines the internal representation of the result (see ConstructingFilter (26.3-2)). The default value for filt is determined from R.

If a matrix object M is given then the returned matrix will have the same internal representation and the same base domain as M.
DiagonalMatrix.title: DiagonalMatrix

DiagonalMatrix.name: DiagonalMatrix
DiagonalMatrix.description:
Returns: a square matrix or matrix object with column number equal to the length of the dense list vector, whose diagonal entries are given by the entries of vector, and whose off-diagonal entries are zero.

If a semiring R is given then it will be the base domain (see BaseDomain (26.3-1)) of the returned matrix. In this case, a filter filt can be specified that defines the internal representation of the result (see ConstructingFilter (26.3-2)). The default value for filt is determined from R.

If a matrix object M is given then the returned matrix will have the same internal representation and the same base domain as M.
DiagonalMatrix.title: DiagonalMatrix

PermutationMat.name: PermutationMat
PermutationMat.description: returns a matrix in dimension dim over the field given by F (i.e. the smallest field containing the element F or F itself if it is a field) that represents the permutation perm acting by permuting the basis vectors as it permutes points.
PermutationMat.title: PermutationMat

TransposedMatImmutable.name: TransposedMatImmutable
TransposedMatImmutable.description:
These functions all return the transposed of the matrix object mat, i.e., a matrix object trans such that trans[i,k] = mat[k,i] holds.

They differ only w.r.t. the mutability of the result.

TransposedMat is an attribute and hence returns an immutable result. TransposedMatMutable is guaranteed to return a new mutable matrix.
TransposedMatImmutable.title: TransposedMatImmutable

TransposedMat.name: TransposedMat
TransposedMat.description:
These functions all return the transposed of the matrix object mat, i.e., a matrix object trans such that trans[i,k] = mat[k,i] holds.

They differ only w.r.t. the mutability of the result.

TransposedMat is an attribute and hence returns an immutable result. TransposedMatMutable is guaranteed to return a new mutable matrix.
TransposedMat.title: TransposedMatImmutable

TransposedMatMutable.name: TransposedMatMutable
TransposedMatMutable.description:
These functions all return the transposed of the matrix object mat, i.e., a matrix object trans such that trans[i,k] = mat[k,i] holds.

They differ only w.r.t. the mutability of the result.

TransposedMat is an attribute and hence returns an immutable result. TransposedMatMutable is guaranteed to return a new mutable matrix.
TransposedMatMutable.title: TransposedMatImmutable

TransposedMatOp.name: TransposedMatOp
TransposedMatOp.description:
These functions all return the transposed of the matrix object mat, i.e., a matrix object trans such that trans[i,k] = mat[k,i] holds.

They differ only w.r.t. the mutability of the result.

TransposedMat is an attribute and hence returns an immutable result. TransposedMatMutable is guaranteed to return a new mutable matrix.
TransposedMatOp.title: TransposedMatImmutable

TransposedMatDestructive.name: TransposedMatDestructive
TransposedMatDestructive.description: If mat is a mutable matrix, then the transposed is computed by swapping the entries in mat. In this way mat gets changed. In all other cases the transposed is computed by TransposedMat (24.5-7).
TransposedMatDestructive.title: TransposedMatDestructive

KroneckerProduct.name: KroneckerProduct
KroneckerProduct.description: The Kronecker product of two matrices is the matrix obtained when replacing each entry a of mat1 by the product a*mat2 in one matrix.
KroneckerProduct.title: KroneckerProduct

ReflectionMat.name: ReflectionMat
ReflectionMat.description:
Let coeffs be a row vector. ReflectionMat returns the matrix of the reflection in this vector.

More precisely, if coeffs is the coefficients list of a vector v w.r.t. a basis B (see Basis (61.5-2)) then the returned matrix describes the reflection in v w.r.t. B as a map on a row space, with action from the right.

The optional argument root is a root of unity that determines the order of the reflection. The default is a reflection of order 2. For triflections one should choose a third root of unity etc. (see E (18.1-1)).
ReflectionMat.title: ReflectionMat

RandomMat.name: RandomMat
RandomMat.description: RandomMat returns a new mutable random matrix with m rows and n columns with elements taken from the ring R, which defaults to Integers (14). Optionally, a random source rs can be supplied.
RandomMat.title: RandomMat

RandomInvertibleMat.name: RandomInvertibleMat
RandomInvertibleMat.description: RandomInvertibleMat returns a new mutable invertible random matrix with m rows and columns with elements taken from the ring R, which defaults to Integers (14). Optionally, a random source rs can be supplied.
RandomInvertibleMat.title: RandomInvertibleMat

RandomUnimodularMat.name: RandomUnimodularMat
RandomUnimodularMat.description: returns a new random mutable m×m matrix with integer entries that is invertible over the integers. Optionally, a random source rs can be supplied. If the option domain is given, random selection is made from domain, otherwise from Integers
RandomUnimodularMat.title: RandomUnimodularMat

RankMatrix.name: RankMatrix
RankMatrix.description:
If mat is a matrix object representing a matrix whose rows span a free module over the ring generated by the matrix entries and their inverses then RankMatrix returns the dimension of this free module. Otherwise fail is returned.

Note that RankMatrix may perform a Gaussian elimination. For large rational matrices this may take very long, because the entries may become very large.
RankMatrix.title: RankMatrix

RankMat.name: RankMat
RankMat.description:
If mat is a matrix object representing a matrix whose rows span a free module over the ring generated by the matrix entries and their inverses then RankMatrix returns the dimension of this free module. Otherwise fail is returned.

Note that RankMatrix may perform a Gaussian elimination. For large rational matrices this may take very long, because the entries may become very large.
RankMat.title: RankMatrix

TriangulizedMat.name: TriangulizedMat
TriangulizedMat.description: Computes an upper triangular form of the matrix mat via the Gaussian Algorithm. It returns a mutable matrix in upper triangular form. This is sometimes also called Hermite normal form or Reduced Row Echelon Form. RREF is a synonym for TriangulizedMat.
TriangulizedMat.title: TriangulizedMat

RREF.name: RREF
RREF.description: Computes an upper triangular form of the matrix mat via the Gaussian Algorithm. It returns a mutable matrix in upper triangular form. This is sometimes also called Hermite normal form or Reduced Row Echelon Form. RREF is a synonym for TriangulizedMat.
RREF.title: TriangulizedMat

TriangulizeMat.name: TriangulizeMat
TriangulizeMat.description: Applies the Gaussian Algorithm to the mutable matrix mat and changes mat such that it is in upper triangular normal form (sometimes called Hermite normal form or Reduced Row Echelon Form).
TriangulizeMat.title: TriangulizeMat

NullspaceMat.name: NullspaceMat
NullspaceMat.description:
returns a list of row vectors that form a basis of the vector space of solutions to the equation vec*mat=0. The result is an immutable matrix. This basis is not guaranteed to be in any specific form.

The variant TriangulizedNullspaceMat returns a basis of the nullspace in triangulized form as is often needed for algorithms.
NullspaceMat.title: NullspaceMat

TriangulizedNullspaceMat.name: TriangulizedNullspaceMat
TriangulizedNullspaceMat.description:
returns a list of row vectors that form a basis of the vector space of solutions to the equation vec*mat=0. The result is an immutable matrix. This basis is not guaranteed to be in any specific form.

The variant TriangulizedNullspaceMat returns a basis of the nullspace in triangulized form as is often needed for algorithms.
TriangulizedNullspaceMat.title: NullspaceMat

NullspaceMatDestructive.name: NullspaceMatDestructive
NullspaceMatDestructive.description:
This function does the same as NullspaceMat (24.7-4). However, the latter function makes a copy of mat to avoid having to change it. This function does not do that; it returns the nullspace and may destroy mat; this saves a lot of memory in case mat is big. The matrix mat must be mutable.

The variant TriangulizedNullspaceMatDestructive returns a basis of the nullspace in triangulized form. It may destroy the matrix mat.
NullspaceMatDestructive.title: NullspaceMatDestructive

TriangulizedNullspaceMatDestructive.name: TriangulizedNullspaceMatDestructive
TriangulizedNullspaceMatDestructive.description:
This function does the same as NullspaceMat (24.7-4). However, the latter function makes a copy of mat to avoid having to change it. This function does not do that; it returns the nullspace and may destroy mat; this saves a lot of memory in case mat is big. The matrix mat must be mutable.

The variant TriangulizedNullspaceMatDestructive returns a basis of the nullspace in triangulized form. It may destroy the matrix mat.
TriangulizedNullspaceMatDestructive.title: NullspaceMatDestructive

SolutionMat.name: SolutionMat
SolutionMat.description: returns a row vector x that is a solution of the equation x * mat = vec. It returns fail if no such vector exists.
SolutionMat.title: SolutionMat

SolutionMatDestructive.name: SolutionMatDestructive
SolutionMatDestructive.description: Does the same as SolutionMat( mat, vec ) except that it may destroy the matrix mat and the vector vec. The matrix mat must be mutable.
SolutionMatDestructive.title: SolutionMatDestructive

BaseFixedSpace.name: BaseFixedSpace
BaseFixedSpace.description: BaseFixedSpace returns a list of row vectors that form a base of the vector space V such that v M = v for all v in V and all matrices M in the list mats. (This is the common eigenspace of all matrices in mats for the eigenvalue 1.)
BaseFixedSpace.title: BaseFixedSpace

GeneralisedEigenvalues.name: GeneralisedEigenvalues
GeneralisedEigenvalues.description: The generalised eigenvalues of the matrix A over the field F.
GeneralisedEigenvalues.title: GeneralisedEigenvalues

GeneralizedEigenvalues.name: GeneralizedEigenvalues
GeneralizedEigenvalues.description: The generalised eigenvalues of the matrix A over the field F.
GeneralizedEigenvalues.title: GeneralisedEigenvalues

GeneralisedEigenspaces.name: GeneralisedEigenspaces
GeneralisedEigenspaces.description: The generalised eigenspaces of the matrix A over the field F.
GeneralisedEigenspaces.title: GeneralisedEigenspaces

GeneralizedEigenspaces.name: GeneralizedEigenspaces
GeneralizedEigenspaces.description: The generalised eigenspaces of the matrix A over the field F.
GeneralizedEigenspaces.title: GeneralisedEigenspaces

Eigenvalues.name: Eigenvalues
Eigenvalues.description: The eigenvalues of the matrix A over the field F.
Eigenvalues.title: Eigenvalues

Eigenspaces.name: Eigenspaces
Eigenspaces.description: The eigenspaces of the matrix A over the field F.
Eigenspaces.title: Eigenspaces

Eigenvectors.name: Eigenvectors
Eigenvectors.description: The eigenvectors of the matrix A over the field F.
Eigenvectors.title: Eigenvectors

ElementaryDivisorsMat.name: ElementaryDivisorsMat
ElementaryDivisorsMat.description:
returns a list of the elementary divisors, i.e., the unique d with d[i] divides d[i+1] and mat is equivalent to a diagonal matrix with the elements d[i] on the diagonal. The operations are performed over the euclidean ring ring, which must contain all matrix entries. For compatibility reasons it can be omitted and defaults to the DefaultRing (56.1-3) of the matrix entries.

The function ElementaryDivisorsMatDestructive produces the same result but in the process may destroy the contents of mat.
ElementaryDivisorsMat.title: ElementaryDivisorsMat

ElementaryDivisorsMatDestructive.name: ElementaryDivisorsMatDestructive
ElementaryDivisorsMatDestructive.description:
returns a list of the elementary divisors, i.e., the unique d with d[i] divides d[i+1] and mat is equivalent to a diagonal matrix with the elements d[i] on the diagonal. The operations are performed over the euclidean ring ring, which must contain all matrix entries. For compatibility reasons it can be omitted and defaults to the DefaultRing (56.1-3) of the matrix entries.

The function ElementaryDivisorsMatDestructive produces the same result but in the process may destroy the contents of mat.
ElementaryDivisorsMatDestructive.title: ElementaryDivisorsMat

ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMat.name: ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMat
ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMat.description:
ElementaryDivisorsTransformations, in addition to the tasks done by ElementaryDivisorsMat, also calculates transforming matrices. It returns a record with components normal (a matrix S), rowtrans (a matrix P), and coltrans (a matrix Q) such that P A Q = S. The operations are performed over the euclidean ring ring, which must contain all matrix entries. For compatibility reasons it can be omitted and defaults to the DefaultRing (56.1-3) of the matrix entries.

The function ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMatDestructive produces the same result but in the process destroys the contents of mat.
ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMat.title: ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMat

ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMatDest.name: ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMatDestructive
ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMatDest.description:
ElementaryDivisorsTransformations, in addition to the tasks done by ElementaryDivisorsMat, also calculates transforming matrices. It returns a record with components normal (a matrix S), rowtrans (a matrix P), and coltrans (a matrix Q) such that P A Q = S. The operations are performed over the euclidean ring ring, which must contain all matrix entries. For compatibility reasons it can be omitted and defaults to the DefaultRing (56.1-3) of the matrix entries.

The function ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMatDestructive produces the same result but in the process destroys the contents of mat.
ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMatDest.title: ElementaryDivisorsTransformationsMat

DiagonalizeMat.name: DiagonalizeMat
DiagonalizeMat.description: brings the mutable matrix mat, considered as a matrix over ring, into diagonal form by elementary row and column operations.
DiagonalizeMat.title: DiagonalizeMat

SemiEchelonMat.name: SemiEchelonMat
SemiEchelonMat.description:
A matrix over a field F is in semi-echelon form if the first nonzero element in each row is the identity of F, and all values exactly below these pivots are the zero of F.

SemiEchelonMat returns a record that contains information about a semi-echelonized form of the matrix mat.

The components of this record are
SemiEchelonMat.title: SemiEchelonMat

SemiEchelonMatDestructive.name: SemiEchelonMatDestructive
SemiEchelonMatDestructive.description: This does the same as SemiEchelonMat( mat ), except that it may (and probably will) destroy the matrix mat.
SemiEchelonMatDestructive.title: SemiEchelonMatDestructive

SemiEchelonMatTransformation.name: SemiEchelonMatTransformation
SemiEchelonMatTransformation.description:
does the same as SemiEchelonMat (24.10-1) but additionally stores the linear transformation T performed on the matrix. The additional components of the result are

a list of coefficients vectors of the vectors component, with respect to the rows of mat, that is, coeffs * mat is the vectors component.

a list of basis vectors for the (left) null space of mat.
SemiEchelonMatTransformation.title: SemiEchelonMatTransformation

SemiEchelonMats.name: SemiEchelonMats
SemiEchelonMats.description:
A list of matrices over a field F is in semi-echelon form if the list of row vectors obtained on concatenating the rows of each matrix is a semi-echelonized matrix (see SemiEchelonMat (24.10-1)).

SemiEchelonMats returns a record that contains information about a semi-echelonized form of the list mats of matrices.

The components of this record are
SemiEchelonMats.title: SemiEchelonMats

SemiEchelonMatsDestructive.name: SemiEchelonMatsDestructive
SemiEchelonMatsDestructive.description: Does the same as SemiEchelonMats (24.10-4), except that it may destroy its argument. Therefore the argument must be a list of matrices that are mutable.
SemiEchelonMatsDestructive.title: SemiEchelonMatsDestructive

BaseMat.name: BaseMat
BaseMat.description: returns a basis for the row space generated by the rows of mat in the form of an immutable matrix.
BaseMat.title: BaseMat

BaseMatDestructive.name: BaseMatDestructive
BaseMatDestructive.description: Does the same as BaseMat (24.11-1), with the difference that it may destroy the matrix mat. The matrix mat must be mutable.
BaseMatDestructive.title: BaseMatDestructive

BaseOrthogonalSpaceMat.name: BaseOrthogonalSpaceMat
BaseOrthogonalSpaceMat.description:
Let V be the row space generated by the rows of mat (over any field that contains all entries of mat). BaseOrthogonalSpaceMat( mat ) computes a base of the orthogonal space of V.

The rows of mat need not be linearly independent.
BaseOrthogonalSpaceMat.title: BaseOrthogonalSpaceMat

SumIntersectionMat.name: SumIntersectionMat
SumIntersectionMat.description:
performs Zassenhaus' algorithm to compute bases for the sum and the intersection of spaces generated by the rows of the matrices M1, M2.

returns a list of length 2, at first position a base of the sum, at second position a base of the intersection. Both bases are in semi-echelon form (see 24.10).
SumIntersectionMat.title: SumIntersectionMat

BaseSteinitzVectors.name: BaseSteinitzVectors
BaseSteinitzVectors.description:
find vectors extending mat to a basis spanning the span of bas. Both bas and mat must be matrices of full (row) rank. It returns a record with the following components:

is a basis of the space spanned by mat in upper triangular form with leading ones at all echelon steps and zeroes above these ones.

is a list of extending vectors in upper triangular form.
BaseSteinitzVectors.title: BaseSteinitzVectors

DiagonalOfMatrix.name: DiagonalOfMatrix
DiagonalOfMatrix.description: return the diagonal of the matrix mat. If mat is not a square matrix, then the result has the same length as the rows of mat, and is padded with zeros if mat has fewer rows than columns.
DiagonalOfMatrix.title: DiagonalOfMatrix

DiagonalOfMat.name: DiagonalOfMat
DiagonalOfMat.description: return the diagonal of the matrix mat. If mat is not a square matrix, then the result has the same length as the rows of mat, and is padded with zeros if mat has fewer rows than columns.
DiagonalOfMat.title: DiagonalOfMatrix

UpperSubdiagonal.name: UpperSubdiagonal
UpperSubdiagonal.description: returns a mutable list containing the entries of the posth upper subdiagonal of the matrix mat.
UpperSubdiagonal.title: UpperSubdiagonal

DepthOfUpperTriangularMatrix.name: DepthOfUpperTriangularMatrix
DepthOfUpperTriangularMatrix.description: If mat is an upper triangular matrix this attribute returns the index of the first nonzero diagonal.
DepthOfUpperTriangularMatrix.title: DepthOfUpperTriangularMatrix

CharacteristicPolynomial.name: CharacteristicPolynomial
CharacteristicPolynomial.description:
For a square matrix mat, CharacteristicPolynomial returns the characteristic polynomial of mat, that is, the StandardAssociate (56.5-5) of the determinant of the matrix mat - X ⋅ I, where X is an indeterminate and I is the appropriate identity matrix.

If fields F and E are given, then F must be a subfield of E, and mat must have entries in E. Then CharacteristicPolynomial returns the characteristic polynomial of the F-linear mapping induced by mat on the underlying E-vector space of mat. In this case, the characteristic polynomial is computed using BlownUpMat (24.13-4) for the field extension of E/F generated by the default field. Thus, if F = E, the result is the same as for the one argument version.

The returned polynomials are expressed in the indeterminate number ind. If ind is not given, it defaults to 1.
CharacteristicPolynomial.title: CharacteristicPolynomial

RationalCanonicalFormTransform.name: RationalCanonicalFormTransform
RationalCanonicalFormTransform.description: For a matrix A, return a matrix P such that A^P is in rational canonical form (also called Frobenius normal form). The algorithm used is the basic textbook version and thus not of optimal complexity.
RationalCanonicalFormTransform.title: RationalCanonicalFormTransform

JordanDecomposition.name: JordanDecomposition
JordanDecomposition.description: JordanDecomposition( mat ) returns a list [S,N] such that S is a semisimple matrix and N is nilpotent. Furthermore, S and N commute and mat=S+N.
JordanDecomposition.title: JordanDecomposition

BlownUpMat.name: BlownUpMat
BlownUpMat.description:
Let B be a basis of a field extension F / K, and mat a matrix whose entries are all in F. (This is not checked.) BlownUpMat returns a matrix over K that is obtained by replacing each entry of mat by its regular representation w.r.t. B.

More precisely, regard mat as the matrix of a linear transformation on the row space F^n w.r.t. the F-basis with vectors (v_1, ..., v_n) and suppose that the basis B consists of the vectors (b_1, ..., b_m); then the returned matrix is the matrix of the linear transformation on the row space K^mn w.r.t. the K-basis whose vectors are (b_1 v_1, ... b_m v_1, ..., b_m v_n).

Note that the linear transformations act on row vectors, i.e., each row of the matrix is a concatenation of vectors of B-coefficients.
BlownUpMat.title: BlownUpMat

BlownUpVector.name: BlownUpVector
BlownUpVector.description:
Let B be a basis of a field extension F / K, and vector a row vector whose entries are all in F. BlownUpVector returns a row vector over K that is obtained by replacing each entry of vector by its coefficients w.r.t. B.

So BlownUpVector and BlownUpMat (24.13-4) are compatible in the sense that for a matrix mat over F, BlownUpVector( B, mat * vector ) is equal to BlownUpMat( B, mat ) * BlownUpVector( B, vector ).
BlownUpVector.title: BlownUpVector

CompanionMatrix.name: CompanionMatrix
CompanionMatrix.description:
Return a fully mutable matrix that is the companion matrix of the polynomial poly. The negatives of the coefficients of poly appear in the last column of the result.

The companion matrix of poly has poly as its minimal polynomial (see MinimalPolynomial (66.8-1)) and as its characteristic polynomial (see CharacteristicPolynomial (24.13-1)).
CompanionMatrix.title: CompanionMatrix

CompanionMatrix.name: CompanionMatrix
CompanionMatrix.description:
Returns: a matrix object

For a monic, univariate polynomial pol whose coefficients lie in the base domain of the matrix object M, CompanionMatrix returns the companion matrix of pol, as a matrix object with the same ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) and BaseDomain (26.3-1) values as M.

We use column convention, that is, the negatives of the coefficients of pol appear in the last column of the result.
CompanionMatrix.title: CompanionMatrix

CompanionMatrix.name: CompanionMatrix
CompanionMatrix.description:
Returns: a matrix object

For a monic, univariate polynomial pol whose coefficients lie in the base domain of the matrix object M, CompanionMatrix returns the companion matrix of pol, as a matrix object with the same ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) and BaseDomain (26.3-1) values as M.

We use column convention, that is, the negatives of the coefficients of pol appear in the last column of the result.
CompanionMatrix.title: CompanionMatrix

CompanionMatrix.name: CompanionMatrix
CompanionMatrix.description:
Returns: a matrix object

For a monic, univariate polynomial pol whose coefficients lie in the base domain of the matrix object M, CompanionMatrix returns the companion matrix of pol, as a matrix object with the same ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) and BaseDomain (26.3-1) values as M.

We use column convention, that is, the negatives of the coefficients of pol appear in the last column of the result.
CompanionMatrix.title: CompanionMatrix

CompanionMat.name: CompanionMat
CompanionMat.description:
Return a fully mutable matrix that is the companion matrix of the polynomial poly. The negatives of the coefficients of poly appear in the last column of the result.

The companion matrix of poly has poly as its minimal polynomial (see MinimalPolynomial (66.8-1)) and as its characteristic polynomial (see CharacteristicPolynomial (24.13-1)).
CompanionMat.title: CompanionMatrix

ImmutableMatrix.name: ImmutableMatrix
ImmutableMatrix.description:
Let matrix be an object for which either IsMatrix (24.2-1) or IsMatrixObj (26.2-2) returns true. In the former case, matrix is a list of lists, and ImmutableMatrix returns an immutable object for which IsMatrix (24.2-1) returns true (in particular again a list of lists), which is equal to matrix, and which is in the optimal (concerning space and runtime) representation for matrices defined over field, provided that the entries of matrix lie in field. In the latter case, ImmutableMatrix returns an immutable object that is equal to the result of ChangedBaseDomain (26.6-3) when this is called with matrix and field.

This means that matrices obtained by several calls of ImmutableMatrix for the same field are compatible for fast arithmetic without need for field conversion.

If the input matrix matrix is in IsMatrix (24.2-1) then it or its rows might change their representation as a side effect of this function. However, one cannot rely on this side effect. Also, if matrix is already immutable and the result of ImmutableMatrix has the same internal representation as matrix, the result is not necessarily identical to matrix.
ImmutableMatrix.title: ImmutableMatrix

ConvertToMatrixRep.name: ConvertToMatrixRep
ConvertToMatrixRep.description:
This function is more technical version of ImmutableMatrix (24.14-1), which will never copy a matrix (or any rows of it) but may fail if it encounters rows locked in the wrong representation, or various other more technical problems. Most users should use ImmutableMatrix (24.14-1) instead. The NC versions of the function do less checking of the argument and may cause unpredictable results or crashes if given unsuitable arguments. Called with one argument list, ConvertToMatrixRep converts list to an internal matrix representation if possible.

Called with a list list and a finite field field, ConvertToMatrixRep converts list to an internal matrix representation appropriate for a matrix over field.

Instead of a field also its size fieldsize may be given.
ConvertToMatrixRep.title: ConvertToMatrixRep

ConvertToMatrixRep.name: ConvertToMatrixRep
ConvertToMatrixRep.description:
This function is more technical version of ImmutableMatrix (24.14-1), which will never copy a matrix (or any rows of it) but may fail if it encounters rows locked in the wrong representation, or various other more technical problems. Most users should use ImmutableMatrix (24.14-1) instead. The NC versions of the function do less checking of the argument and may cause unpredictable results or crashes if given unsuitable arguments. Called with one argument list, ConvertToMatrixRep converts list to an internal matrix representation if possible.

Called with a list list and a finite field field, ConvertToMatrixRep converts list to an internal matrix representation appropriate for a matrix over field.

Instead of a field also its size fieldsize may be given.
ConvertToMatrixRep.title: ConvertToMatrixRep

ConvertToMatrixRepNC.name: ConvertToMatrixRepNC
ConvertToMatrixRepNC.description:
This function is more technical version of ImmutableMatrix (24.14-1), which will never copy a matrix (or any rows of it) but may fail if it encounters rows locked in the wrong representation, or various other more technical problems. Most users should use ImmutableMatrix (24.14-1) instead. The NC versions of the function do less checking of the argument and may cause unpredictable results or crashes if given unsuitable arguments. Called with one argument list, ConvertToMatrixRep converts list to an internal matrix representation if possible.

Called with a list list and a finite field field, ConvertToMatrixRep converts list to an internal matrix representation appropriate for a matrix over field.

Instead of a field also its size fieldsize may be given.
ConvertToMatrixRepNC.title: ConvertToMatrixRep

ConvertToMatrixRepNC.name: ConvertToMatrixRepNC
ConvertToMatrixRepNC.description:
This function is more technical version of ImmutableMatrix (24.14-1), which will never copy a matrix (or any rows of it) but may fail if it encounters rows locked in the wrong representation, or various other more technical problems. Most users should use ImmutableMatrix (24.14-1) instead. The NC versions of the function do less checking of the argument and may cause unpredictable results or crashes if given unsuitable arguments. Called with one argument list, ConvertToMatrixRep converts list to an internal matrix representation if possible.

Called with a list list and a finite field field, ConvertToMatrixRep converts list to an internal matrix representation appropriate for a matrix over field.

Instead of a field also its size fieldsize may be given.
ConvertToMatrixRepNC.title: ConvertToMatrixRep

ProjectiveOrder.name: ProjectiveOrder
ProjectiveOrder.description: Returns an integer n and a finite field element e such that A^n = eI. mat must be a matrix defined over a finite field.
ProjectiveOrder.title: ProjectiveOrder

SimultaneousEigenvalues.name: SimultaneousEigenvalues
SimultaneousEigenvalues.description: The matrices in matlist must be matrices over GF(q) for some prime q. Together, they must generate an abelian p-group of exponent expo. Then the eigenvalues of mat in the splitting field GF(q^r) for some r are powers of an element ξ in the splitting field, which is of order expo. SimultaneousEigenvalues returns a matrix of integers mod expo (a_{i,j}), such that the power ξ^{a_{i,j}} is an eigenvalue of the i-th matrix in matlist and the eigenspaces of the different matrices to the eigenvalues ξ^{a_{i,j}} for fixed j are equal.
SimultaneousEigenvalues.title: SimultaneousEigenvalues

InverseMatMod.name: InverseMatMod
InverseMatMod.description: For a square matrix mat, InverseMatMod returns a matrix inv such that inv * mat is congruent to the identity matrix modulo obj, if such a matrix exists, and fail otherwise.
InverseMatMod.title: InverseMatMod

BasisNullspaceModN.name: BasisNullspaceModN
BasisNullspaceModN.description: M must be a matrix of integers and n a positive integer. Then BasisNullspaceModN returns a set B of vectors such that every vector v of integer modulo n satisfying v M = 0 modulo n can be expressed by a Z-linear combination of elements of B.
BasisNullspaceModN.title: BasisNullspaceModN

NullspaceModQ.name: NullspaceModQ
NullspaceModQ.description:
M must be a matrix of integers and n a positive integer. Then NullspaceModN returns the set of all vectors of integers modulo n, which solve the homogeneous equation system v M = 0 modulo n.

NullspaceModQ is a synonym for NullspaceModN.
NullspaceModQ.title: NullspaceModQ

NullspaceModN.name: NullspaceModN
NullspaceModN.description:
M must be a matrix of integers and n a positive integer. Then NullspaceModN returns the set of all vectors of integers modulo n, which solve the homogeneous equation system v M = 0 modulo n.

NullspaceModQ is a synonym for NullspaceModN.
NullspaceModN.title: NullspaceModQ

PROD_GF2MAT_GF2MAT_SIMPLE.name: PROD_GF2MAT_GF2MAT_SIMPLE
PROD_GF2MAT_GF2MAT_SIMPLE.description: This function performs the standard unblocked and ungreased matrix multiplication for matrices of any size.
PROD_GF2MAT_GF2MAT_SIMPLE.title: PROD_GF2MAT_GF2MAT_SIMPLE

PROD_GF2MAT_GF2MAT_ADVANCED.name: PROD_GF2MAT_GF2MAT_ADVANCED
PROD_GF2MAT_GF2MAT_ADVANCED.description: This function computes the product of m1 and m2, which must be compressed matrices over GF(2) of compatible dimensions, using level g grease and level b blocking.
PROD_GF2MAT_GF2MAT_ADVANCED.title: PROD_GF2MAT_GF2MAT_ADVANCED

AsBlockMatrix.name: AsBlockMatrix
AsBlockMatrix.description: returns a block matrix with nrb row blocks and ncb column blocks which is equal to the ordinary matrix m.
AsBlockMatrix.title: AsBlockMatrix

BlockMatrix.name: BlockMatrix
BlockMatrix.description:
BlockMatrix returns an immutable matrix in the sparse representation IsBlockMatrixRep. The nonzero blocks are described by the list blocks of triples [ i, j, M(i,j) ] each consisting of a matrix M(i,j) and its block coordinates in the block matrix to be constructed. All matrices M(i,j) must have the same dimensions. As usual the first coordinate specifies the row and the second one the column. The resulting matrix has nrb row blocks and ncb column blocks.

If blocks is empty (i.e., if the matrix is a zero matrix) then the dimensions of the blocks must be entered as rpb and cpb, and the zero element as zero.

Note that all blocks must be ordinary matrices (see IsOrdinaryMatrix (24.2-2)), and also the block matrix is an ordinary matrix.
BlockMatrix.title: BlockMatrix

MatrixByBlockMatrix.name: MatrixByBlockMatrix
MatrixByBlockMatrix.description: returns a plain ordinary matrix that is equal to the block matrix blockmat.
MatrixByBlockMatrix.title: MatrixByBlockMatrix

SimplexMethod.name: SimplexMethod
SimplexMethod.description: Find a rational vector x that maximizes xcdotc, subject to the constraint Acdotxleb.
SimplexMethod.title: SimplexMethod

NullspaceIntMat.name: NullspaceIntMat
NullspaceIntMat.description: If mat is a matrix with integral entries, this function returns a list of vectors that forms a basis of the integral nullspace of mat, i.e., of those vectors in the nullspace of mat that have integral entries.
NullspaceIntMat.title: NullspaceIntMat

SolutionIntMat.name: SolutionIntMat
SolutionIntMat.description: If mat is a matrix with integral entries and vec a vector with integral entries, this function returns a vector x with integer entries that is a solution of the equation x * mat = vec. It returns fail if no such vector exists.
SolutionIntMat.title: SolutionIntMat

SolutionNullspaceIntMat.name: SolutionNullspaceIntMat
SolutionNullspaceIntMat.description: This function returns a list of length two, its first entry being the result of a call to SolutionIntMat (25.1-2) with same arguments, the second the result of NullspaceIntMat (25.1-1) applied to the matrix mat. The calculation is performed faster than if two separate calls would be used.
SolutionNullspaceIntMat.title: SolutionNullspaceIntMat

BaseIntMat.name: BaseIntMat
BaseIntMat.description: If mat is a matrix with integral entries, this function returns a list of vectors that forms a basis of the integral row space of mat, i.e. of the set of integral linear combinations of the rows of mat.
BaseIntMat.title: BaseIntMat

BaseIntersectionIntMats.name: BaseIntersectionIntMats
BaseIntersectionIntMats.description: If m and n are matrices with integral entries, this function returns a list of vectors that forms a basis of the intersection of the integral row spaces of m and n.
BaseIntersectionIntMats.title: BaseIntersectionIntMats

ComplementIntMat.name: ComplementIntMat
ComplementIntMat.description:
Let full be a list of integer vectors generating an integral row module M and sub a list of vectors defining a submodule S of M. This function computes a free basis for M that extends S. I.e., if the dimension of S is n it determines a basis B = { b_1, ..., b_m } for M, as well as n integers x_i such that the n vectors s_i:= x_i ⋅ b_i form a basis for S.

It returns a record with the following components:

the vectors b_{n+1} up to b_m (they generate a complement to S).
ComplementIntMat.title: ComplementIntMat

TriangulizedIntegerMat.name: TriangulizedIntegerMat
TriangulizedIntegerMat.description: Computes an upper triangular form of a matrix with integer entries. It returns a mutable matrix in upper triangular form.
TriangulizedIntegerMat.title: TriangulizedIntegerMat

TriangulizedIntegerMatTransform.name: TriangulizedIntegerMatTransform
TriangulizedIntegerMatTransform.description: Computes an upper triangular form of a matrix with integer entries. It returns a record with a component normal (an immutable matrix in upper triangular form) and a component rowtrans that gives the transformations done to the original matrix to bring it into upper triangular form.
TriangulizedIntegerMatTransform.title: TriangulizedIntegerMatTransform

TriangulizeIntegerMat.name: TriangulizeIntegerMat
TriangulizeIntegerMat.description: Changes mat to be in upper triangular form. (The result is the same as that of TriangulizedIntegerMat (25.2-1), but mat will be modified, thus using less memory.) If mat is immutable an error will be triggered.
TriangulizeIntegerMat.title: TriangulizeIntegerMat

HermiteNormalFormIntegerMat.name: HermiteNormalFormIntegerMat
HermiteNormalFormIntegerMat.description: This operation computes the Hermite normal form of a matrix mat with integer entries. It returns a immutable matrix in HNF.
HermiteNormalFormIntegerMat.title: HermiteNormalFormIntegerMat

HermiteNormalFormIntegerMatTransform.name: HermiteNormalFormIntegerMatTransform
HermiteNormalFormIntegerMatTransform.description: This operation computes the Hermite normal form of a matrix mat with integer entries. It returns a record with components normal (a matrix H) and rowtrans (a matrix Q) such that Q A = H.
HermiteNormalFormIntegerMatTransform.title: HermiteNormalFormIntegerMatTransform

SmithNormalFormIntegerMat.name: SmithNormalFormIntegerMat
SmithNormalFormIntegerMat.description: This operation computes the Smith normal form of a matrix mat with integer entries. It returns a new immutable matrix in the Smith normal form.
SmithNormalFormIntegerMat.title: SmithNormalFormIntegerMat

SmithNormalFormIntegerMatTransforms.name: SmithNormalFormIntegerMatTransforms
SmithNormalFormIntegerMatTransforms.description: This operation computes the Smith normal form of a matrix mat with integer entries. It returns a record with components normal (a matrix S), rowtrans (a matrix P), and coltrans (a matrix Q) such that P A Q = S.
SmithNormalFormIntegerMatTransforms.title: SmithNormalFormIntegerMatTransforms

DiagonalizeIntMat.name: DiagonalizeIntMat
DiagonalizeIntMat.description: This function changes mat to its SNF. (The result is the same as that of SmithNormalFormIntegerMat (25.2-6), but mat will be modified, thus using less memory.) If mat is immutable an error will be triggered.
DiagonalizeIntMat.title: DiagonalizeIntMat

NormalFormIntMat.name: NormalFormIntMat
NormalFormIntMat.description:
This general operation for computation of various Normal Forms is probably the most efficient.

Options bit values:

Triangular Form / Smith Normal Form.
NormalFormIntMat.title: NormalFormIntMat

AbelianInvariantsOfList.name: AbelianInvariantsOfList
AbelianInvariantsOfList.description: Given a list of nonnegative integers, this routine returns a sorted list containing the prime power factors of the positive entries in the original list, as well as all zeroes of the original list.
AbelianInvariantsOfList.title: AbelianInvariantsOfList

DeterminantIntMat.name: DeterminantIntMat
DeterminantIntMat.description: Computes the determinant of an integer matrix using the same strategy as NormalFormIntMat (25.2-9). This method is faster in general for matrices greater than 20 × 20 but quite a lot slower for smaller matrices. It therefore passes the work to the more general DeterminantMat (24.4-4) for these smaller matrices.
DeterminantIntMat.title: DeterminantIntMat

Decomposition.name: Decomposition
Decomposition.description:
For a m × n matrix A of cyclotomics that has rank m ≤ n, and a list B of cyclotomic vectors, each of length n, Decomposition tries to find integral solutions of the linear equation systems x * A = B[i], by computing the p-adic series of hypothetical solutions.

Decomposition( A, B, depth ), where depth is a nonnegative integer, computes for each vector B[i] the initial part ∑_{k = 0}^depth x_k p^k, with all x_k vectors of integers with entries bounded by ± (p-1)/2. The prime p is set to 83 first; if the reduction of A modulo p is singular, the next prime is chosen automatically.

A list X is returned. If the computed initial part for x * A = B[i] is a solution, we have X[i] = x, otherwise X[i] = fail.
Decomposition.title: Decomposition

LinearIndependentColumns.name: LinearIndependentColumns
LinearIndependentColumns.description: Called with a matrix mat, LinearIndependentColumns returns a maximal list of column positions such that the restriction of mat to these columns has the same rank as mat.
LinearIndependentColumns.title: LinearIndependentColumns

PadicCoefficients.name: PadicCoefficients
PadicCoefficients.description: Let A be an integral matrix, prime a prime integer, Amodpinv an inverse of A modulo prime, b an integral vector, and depth a nonnegative integer. PadicCoefficients returns the list [ x_0, x_1, ..., x_l, b_{l+1} ] describing the prime-adic approximation of b (see above), where l = depth or l is minimal with the property that b_{l+1} = 0.
PadicCoefficients.title: PadicCoefficients

IntegralizedMat.name: IntegralizedMat
IntegralizedMat.description:
IntegralizedMat returns, for a matrix A of cyclotomics, a record intmat with components mat and inforec. Each family of algebraic conjugate columns of A is encoded in a set of columns of the rational matrix intmat.mat by replacing cyclotomics in A by their coefficients w.r.t. an integral basis. intmat.inforec is a record containing the information how to encode the columns.

If the only argument is A, the value of the component inforec is computed that can be entered as second argument inforec in a later call of IntegralizedMat with a matrix B that shall be encoded compatibly with A.
IntegralizedMat.title: IntegralizedMat

DecompositionInt.name: DecompositionInt
DecompositionInt.description: DecompositionInt does the same as Decomposition (25.4-1), except that A and B must be integral matrices, and depth must be a nonnegative integer.
DecompositionInt.title: DecompositionInt

LLLReducedBasis.name: LLLReducedBasis
LLLReducedBasis.description:
provides an implementation of the LLL algorithm by Lenstra, Lenstra and Lovász (see [LLJL82], [Poh87]). The implementation follows the description in [Coh93, p. 94f.].

LLLReducedBasis returns a record whose component basis is a list of LLL reduced linearly independent vectors spanning the same lattice as the list vectors. L must be a lattice, with scalar product of the vectors v and w given by ScalarProduct( L, v, w ). If no lattice is specified then the scalar product of vectors given by ScalarProduct( v, w ) is used.

In the case of the option "linearcomb", the result record contains also the components relations and transformation, with the following meaning. relations is a basis of the relation space of vectors, i.e., of vectors x such that x * vectors is zero. transformation gives the expression of the new lattice basis in terms of the old, i.e., transformation * vectors equals the basis component of the result.
LLLReducedBasis.title: LLLReducedBasis

LLLReducedGramMat.name: LLLReducedGramMat
LLLReducedGramMat.description:
LLLReducedGramMat provides an implementation of the LLL algorithm by Lenstra, Lenstra and Lovász (see [LLJL82], [Poh87]). The implementation follows the description in [Coh93, p. 94f.].

Let G the Gram matrix of the vectors (b_1, b_2, ..., b_n); this means G is either a square symmetric matrix or lower triangular matrix (only the entries in the lower triangular half are used by the program).

LLLReducedGramMat returns a record whose component remainder is the Gram matrix of the LLL reduced basis corresponding to (b_1, b_2, ..., b_n). If G is a lower triangular matrix then also the remainder component of the result record is a lower triangular matrix.
LLLReducedGramMat.title: LLLReducedGramMat

OrthogonalEmbeddings.name: OrthogonalEmbeddings
OrthogonalEmbeddings.description:
computes all possible orthogonal embeddings of a lattice given by its Gram matrix gram, which must be a regular symmetric matrix of integers. In other words, all integral solutions X of the equation X^tr ⋅ X =gram are calculated. The implementation follows the description in [Ple95].

Usually there are many solutions X but all their rows belong to a small set of vectors, so OrthogonalEmbeddings returns the solutions encoded by a record with the following components.

the list L = [ x_1, x_2, ..., x_n ] of vectors that may be rows of a solution, up to sign; these are exactly the vectors with the property x_i ⋅gram^{-1} ⋅ x_i^tr ≤ 1, see ShortestVectors (25.6-2),
OrthogonalEmbeddings.title: OrthogonalEmbeddings

ShortestVectors.name: ShortestVectors
ShortestVectors.description:
Let G be a regular matrix of a symmetric bilinear form, and m a nonnegative integer. ShortestVectors computes the vectors x that satisfy x ⋅ G ⋅ x^tr ≤ m, and returns a record describing these vectors. The result record has the components

list of the nonzero vectors x, but only one of each pair (x,-x),

list of norms of the vectors according to the Gram matrix G.
ShortestVectors.title: ShortestVectors

IsVectorObj.name: IsVectorObj
IsVectorObj.description:
The idea behind vector objects is that one wants to deal with objects like coefficient lists of fixed length over a given domain R, say, which can be added and can be multiplied from the left with elements from R. A vector object v, say, is always a copyable object (see IsCopyable (12.6-1)) in IsVector (31.14-14), which knows the values of BaseDomain (26.3-1) (with value R) and Length (21.17-5), where R is a domain (see Chapter 12.4) that has methods for Zero (31.10-3), One (31.10-2), \in (30.6-1), Characteristic (31.10-1), IsFinite (30.4-2). We say that v is defined over R. Typically, R will be at least a semiring.

For creating new vector objects compatible with v, NewVector (26.4-1) requires that also the value of ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) is known for v.

Further, entry access v[i] is expected to return a GAP object, for 1 ≤ i ≤ Length( v ), and that these entries of v belong to the base domain R.
IsVectorObj.title: IsVectorObj

IsMatrixObj.name: IsMatrixObj
IsMatrixObj.description:
The idea behind matrix objects is that one wants to deal with objects like m by n arrays over a given domain R, say, which can be added and multiplied and can be multiplied from the left with elements from R. A matrix object M, say, is always a copyable object (see IsCopyable (12.6-1)) in IsVector (31.14-14) and IsScalar (31.14-20), which knows the values of BaseDomain (26.3-1) (with value R), NumberRows (26.3-5) (with value m), NumberColumns (26.3-5) (with value n), where R is a domain (see Chapter 12.4) that has methods for Zero (31.10-3), One (31.10-2), \in (30.6-1), Characteristic (31.10-1), IsFinite (30.4-2). We say that v is defined over R. Typically, R will be at least a semiring.

For creating new matrix objects compatible with M, NewMatrix (26.4-4) requires that also the value of ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) is known for M.

Further, entry access M[i,j] is expected to return a GAP object, for 1 ≤ i ≤ m and 1 ≤ j ≤ n, and that these entries of M belong to the base domain R.
IsMatrixObj.title: IsMatrixObj

IsMatrixOrMatrixObj.name: IsMatrixOrMatrixObj
IsMatrixOrMatrixObj.description: Several functions are defined for objects in IsMatrix (24.2-1) and objects in IsMatrixObj (26.2-2). All these objects lie in the filter IsMatrixOrMatrixObj. It should be used in situations where an object can be either a list of lists in IsMatrix (24.2-1) or a proper matrix object in IsMatrixObj (26.2-2), for example as a requirement in the installation of a method for such an argument.
IsMatrixOrMatrixObj.title: IsMatrixOrMatrixObj

IsRowListMatrix.name: IsRowListMatrix
IsRowListMatrix.description:
A row list matrix object is a matrix object (see IsMatrixObj (26.2-2)) M which admits access to its rows, that is, list access M[i] (see \[\] (21.2-1)) yields the i-th row of M, for 1 ≤ i ≤ NumberRows( M ).

All rows are IsVectorObj (26.2-1) objects in the same representation. Several rows of a row list matrix object can be identical objects, and different row list matrices may share rows. Row access just gives a reference to the row object, without copying the row.

Matrix objects in IsRowListMatrix are not necessarily in IsList (21.1-1), and then they need not obey the general rules for lists.
IsRowListMatrix.title: IsRowListMatrix

BaseDomain.name: BaseDomain
BaseDomain.description:
The vector object vector or matrix object matrix, respectively, is defined over the domain given by its BaseDomain value.

Note that not all entries of the object necessarily lie in its base domain with respect to \in (30.6-1), see Section 26.1.
BaseDomain.title: BaseDomain

BaseDomain.name: BaseDomain
BaseDomain.description:
The vector object vector or matrix object matrix, respectively, is defined over the domain given by its BaseDomain value.

Note that not all entries of the object necessarily lie in its base domain with respect to \in (30.6-1), see Section 26.1.
BaseDomain.title: BaseDomain

ConstructingFilter.name: ConstructingFilter
ConstructingFilter.description:
Returns: a filter

Called with a vector object v or a matrix object M, respectively, ConstructingFilter returns a filter f such that when NewVector (26.4-1) or NewMatrix (26.4-4), respectively, is called with f then a vector object or a matrix object, respectively, in the same representation as the argument is produced.

If the ConstructingFilter value of v or M implies IsCopyable (12.6-1) then mutable versions of v or M can be created, otherwise all vector or matrix objects with this filter are immutable.
ConstructingFilter.title: ConstructingFilter

ConstructingFilter.name: ConstructingFilter
ConstructingFilter.description:
Returns: a filter

Called with a vector object v or a matrix object M, respectively, ConstructingFilter returns a filter f such that when NewVector (26.4-1) or NewMatrix (26.4-4), respectively, is called with f then a vector object or a matrix object, respectively, in the same representation as the argument is produced.

If the ConstructingFilter value of v or M implies IsCopyable (12.6-1) then mutable versions of v or M can be created, otherwise all vector or matrix objects with this filter are immutable.
ConstructingFilter.title: ConstructingFilter

CompatibleVectorFilter.name: CompatibleVectorFilter
CompatibleVectorFilter.description:
Returns: a filter

Called with a matrix object M, CompatibleVectorFilter returns either a filter f such that vector objects with ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) value f are compatible in the sense that M can be multiplied with these vector objects, of fail if no such filter is known.
CompatibleVectorFilter.title: CompatibleVectorFilter

NumberRows.name: NumberRows
NumberRows.description:
For a matrix object M, NumberRows and NumberColumns store the number of rows and columns of M, respectively.

NrRows and NrCols are synonyms of NumberRows and NumberColumns, respectively.
NumberRows.title: NumberRows and NumberColumns

NrRows.name: NrRows
NrRows.description:
For a matrix object M, NumberRows and NumberColumns store the number of rows and columns of M, respectively.

NrRows and NrCols are synonyms of NumberRows and NumberColumns, respectively.
NrRows.title: NumberRows and NumberColumns

NumberColumns.name: NumberColumns
NumberColumns.description:
For a matrix object M, NumberRows and NumberColumns store the number of rows and columns of M, respectively.

NrRows and NrCols are synonyms of NumberRows and NumberColumns, respectively.
NumberColumns.title: NumberRows and NumberColumns

NrCols.name: NrCols
NrCols.description:
For a matrix object M, NumberRows and NumberColumns store the number of rows and columns of M, respectively.

NrRows and NrCols are synonyms of NumberRows and NumberColumns, respectively.
NrCols.title: NumberRows and NumberColumns

NewVector.name: NewVector
NewVector.description:
For a filter filt, a semiring R, and a list list of elements that belong to R, NewVector returns a vector object which has the ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) filt, the BaseDomain (26.3-1) R, and the entries in list. The list list is guaranteed not to be changed by this operation.

If the global option check is set to false then NewVector need not perform consistency checks.

Similarly, NewZeroVector returns a vector object of length n which has filt and R as ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) and BaseDomain (26.3-1) values, and contains the zero of R in each position.
NewVector.title: NewVector and NewZeroVector

NewZeroVector.name: NewZeroVector
NewZeroVector.description:
For a filter filt, a semiring R, and a list list of elements that belong to R, NewVector returns a vector object which has the ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) filt, the BaseDomain (26.3-1) R, and the entries in list. The list list is guaranteed not to be changed by this operation.

If the global option check is set to false then NewVector need not perform consistency checks.

Similarly, NewZeroVector returns a vector object of length n which has filt and R as ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) and BaseDomain (26.3-1) values, and contains the zero of R in each position.
NewZeroVector.title: NewVector and NewZeroVector

Vector.name: Vector
Vector.description:
Returns: a vector object

If a filter filt is given as the first argument then a vector object is returned that has ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) value filt, is defined over the base domain R, and has the entries given by the list list or the vector object v, respectively.

If a semiring R is given as the first argument then a vector object is returned whose ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) value is guessed from R, again with base domain R and entries given by the last argument.
Vector.title: Vector

Vector.name: Vector
Vector.description:
Returns: a vector object

If a filter filt is given as the first argument then a vector object is returned that has ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) value filt, is defined over the base domain R, and has the entries given by the list list or the vector object v, respectively.

If a semiring R is given as the first argument then a vector object is returned whose ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) value is guessed from R, again with base domain R and entries given by the last argument.
Vector.title: Vector

Vector.name: Vector
Vector.description:
Returns: a vector object

If a filter filt is given as the first argument then a vector object is returned that has ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) value filt, is defined over the base domain R, and has the entries given by the list list or the vector object v, respectively.

If a semiring R is given as the first argument then a vector object is returned whose ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) value is guessed from R, again with base domain R and entries given by the last argument.
Vector.title: Vector

Vector.name: Vector
Vector.description:
Returns: a vector object

If a filter filt is given as the first argument then a vector object is returned that has ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) value filt, is defined over the base domain R, and has the entries given by the list list or the vector object v, respectively.

If a semiring R is given as the first argument then a vector object is returned whose ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) value is guessed from R, again with base domain R and entries given by the last argument.

ZeroVector.name: ZeroVector
ZeroVector.description:
Returns: a vector object

For a filter filt, a semiring R and a nonnegative integer len, this operation returns a new vector object of length len over R in the representation filt containing only zeros.

If only R and len are given, then GAP guesses a suitable representation.

NewMatrix.name: NewMatrix
NewMatrix.description:
For a filter filt, a semiring R, a positive integer ncols, and a list list, NewMatrix returns a matrix object which has the ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) filt, the BaseDomain (26.3-1) R, n columns (see NumberColumns (26.3-5)), and the entries described by list, which can be either a plain list of vector objects of length ncols or a plain list of plain lists of length ncols or a plain list of length a multiple of ncols containing the entries in row major order. The list list is guaranteed not to be changed by this operation.

The corresponding entries must be in or compatible with R. If list already contains vector objects, they are copied.

If the global option check is set to false then NewMatrix need not perform consistency checks.
NewMatrix.title: NewMatrix, NewZeroMatrix, NewIdentityMatrix

NewZeroMatrix.description:

The list list is guaranteed not to be changed by this operation.

Matrix.name: Matrix
Matrix.description:
Returns: a matrix object

If a filter filt is given as the first argument then a matrix object is returned that has ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) value filt, is defined over the base domain R, and has the entries given by the list list or the matrix object M, respectively. Here list can be either a list of plain lists that describe the entries of the rows, or a flat list of the entries in row major order, where ncols defines the number of columns.

If a semiring R is given as the first argument then a matrix object is returned whose ConstructingFilter (26.3-2) value is guessed from R, again with base domain R and entries given by the last argument.

Matrix.name: Matrix
Matrix.description:
Returns: A matrix.

If R is a Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup, then MatrixOfReesMatrixSemigroup respectively MatrixOfReesZeroMatrixSemigroup return the matrix used to define multiplication in R. For convenience, one may also abbreviate either to Matrix.

More specifically, if R is a Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup, which is a proper subsemigroup of another such semigroup, then Matrix returns the matrix used to define the Rees matrix (or 0-matrix) semigroup consisting of the whole family to which the elements of R belong. Thus, for example, a 1 by 1 Rees matrix semigroup can have a 65 by 15 matrix.
Matrix.title: Matrix

ZeroMatrix.name: ZeroMatrix
ZeroMatrix.description:
Returns: a matrix object

For a matrix object M and two nonnegative integers m and n, this operation returns a new matrix object with m rows and n columns in the same representation and over the same base domain as M containing only zeros.

If a semiring R and two nonnegative integers m and n are given, the representation of the result is guessed from R.

OneOfBaseDomain.name: OneOfBaseDomain
OneOfBaseDomain.description:
These attributes return the identity element and the zero element of the BaseDomain (26.3-1) value of the vector object v or the matrix object M, respectively.

If v or M, respectively, is a plain list (see IsPlistRep (21.24-2)) then computing its BaseDomain (26.3-1) value can be regarded as expensive, whereas calling OneOfBaseDomain or ZeroOfBaseDomain can be regarded as cheap. If v or M, respectively, is not a plain list then one can also call BaseDomain (26.3-1) first, without loss of performance.
OneOfBaseDomain.title: OneOfBaseDomain and ZeroOfBaseDomain
