IsAlgebraGeneralMapping.title: IsAlgebraGeneralMapping

IsAlgebraHomomorphism.name: IsAlgebraHomomorphism
IsAlgebraHomomorphism.description:
IsAlgebraGeneralMapping specifies whether a general mapping mapp satisfies both properties IsRingGeneralMapping (32.12-1) and (see IsLeftModuleGeneralMapping (32.11-2)).

IsAlgebraHomomorphism is a synonym for the meet of IsAlgebraGeneralMapping and IsMapping (32.3-3).
IsAlgebraHomomorphism.title: IsAlgebraGeneralMapping

IsAlgebraWithOneGeneralMapping.name: IsAlgebraWithOneGeneralMapping
IsAlgebraWithOneGeneralMapping.description:
IsAlgebraWithOneGeneralMapping specifies whether a general mapping mapp satisfies both properties IsAlgebraGeneralMapping (32.12-3) and RespectsOne (32.9-2).

IsAlgebraWithOneHomomorphism is a synonym for the meet of IsAlgebraWithOneGeneralMapping and IsMapping (32.3-3).
IsAlgebraWithOneGeneralMapping.title: IsAlgebraWithOneGeneralMapping

IsAlgebraWithOneHomomorphism.name: IsAlgebraWithOneHomomorphism
IsAlgebraWithOneHomomorphism.description:
IsAlgebraWithOneGeneralMapping specifies whether a general mapping mapp satisfies both properties IsAlgebraGeneralMapping (32.12-3) and RespectsOne (32.9-2).

IsAlgebraWithOneHomomorphism is a synonym for the meet of IsAlgebraWithOneGeneralMapping and IsMapping (32.3-3).
IsAlgebraWithOneHomomorphism.title: IsAlgebraWithOneGeneralMapping

IsFieldHomomorphism.name: IsFieldHomomorphism
IsFieldHomomorphism.description: A general mapping is a field homomorphism if and only if it is a ring homomorphism with source a field.
IsFieldHomomorphism.title: IsFieldHomomorphism

IsGeneralMapping.name: IsGeneralMapping
IsGeneralMapping.description: Each general mapping lies in the category IsGeneralMapping. It implies the categories IsMultiplicativeElementWithInverse (31.14-13) and IsAssociativeElement (31.15-1); for a discussion of these implications, see 32.6.
IsGeneralMapping.title: IsGeneralMapping

IsConstantTimeAccessGeneralMapping.name: IsConstantTimeAccessGeneralMapping
IsConstantTimeAccessGeneralMapping.description:
is true if the underlying relation of the general mapping map knows its AsList (30.3-8) value, and false otherwise.

In the former case, map is allowed to use this list for calls to ImagesElm (32.4-3) etc.
IsConstantTimeAccessGeneralMapping.title: IsConstantTimeAccessGeneralMapping

IsEndoGeneralMapping.name: IsEndoGeneralMapping
IsEndoGeneralMapping.description: If a general mapping has this property then its source and range are equal.
IsEndoGeneralMapping.title: IsEndoGeneralMapping

IsGeneralMappingFamily.name: IsGeneralMappingFamily
IsGeneralMappingFamily.description: The family category of the category of general mappings.
IsGeneralMappingFamily.title: IsGeneralMappingFamily

FamilyRange.name: FamilyRange
FamilyRange.description: is the elements family of the family of the range of each general mapping in the family Fam.
FamilyRange.title: FamilyRange

FamilySource.name: FamilySource
FamilySource.description: is the elements family of the family of the source of each general mapping in the family Fam.
FamilySource.title: FamilySource

FamiliesOfGeneralMappingsAndRanges.name: FamiliesOfGeneralMappingsAndRanges
FamiliesOfGeneralMappingsAndRanges.description: is a list that stores at the odd positions the families of general mappings with source in the family Fam, at the even positions the families of ranges of the general mappings.
FamiliesOfGeneralMappingsAndRanges.title: FamiliesOfGeneralMappingsAndRanges

GeneralMappingsFamily.name: GeneralMappingsFamily
GeneralMappingsFamily.description: All general mappings with same source family FS and same range family FR lie in the family GeneralMappingsFamily( FS, FR ).
GeneralMappingsFamily.title: GeneralMappingsFamily

TypeOfDefaultGeneralMapping.name: TypeOfDefaultGeneralMapping
TypeOfDefaultGeneralMapping.description: is the type of mappings with IsDefaultGeneralMappingRep with source source and range range and additional categories filter.
TypeOfDefaultGeneralMapping.title: TypeOfDefaultGeneralMapping

IsBinaryRelation.name: IsBinaryRelation
IsBinaryRelation.description: is exactly the same category as (i.e. a synonym for) IsEndoGeneralMapping (32.13-3).
IsBinaryRelation.title: IsBinaryRelation

BinaryRelationByElements.name: BinaryRelationByElements
BinaryRelationByElements.description: is the binary relation on domain and with underlying relation consisting of the tuples collection elms. This construction is similar to GeneralMappingByElements (32.2-1) where the source and range are the same set.
BinaryRelationByElements.title: BinaryRelationByElements

IdentityBinaryRelation.name: IdentityBinaryRelation
IdentityBinaryRelation.description: is the binary relation which consists of diagonal pairs, i.e., pairs of the form (x,x). In the first form if a positive integer degree is given then the domain is the set of the integers { 1, ..., degree }. In the second form, the objects x are from the domain domain.
IdentityBinaryRelation.title: IdentityBinaryRelation

IdentityBinaryRelation.name: IdentityBinaryRelation
IdentityBinaryRelation.description: is the binary relation which consists of diagonal pairs, i.e., pairs of the form (x,x). In the first form if a positive integer degree is given then the domain is the set of the integers { 1, ..., degree }. In the second form, the objects x are from the domain domain.
IdentityBinaryRelation.title: IdentityBinaryRelation

EmptyBinaryRelation.name: EmptyBinaryRelation
EmptyBinaryRelation.description: is the relation with R empty. In the first form of the command with degree an integer, the domain is the set of points { 1, ..., degree }. In the second form, the domain is that given by the argument domain.
EmptyBinaryRelation.title: EmptyBinaryRelation

EmptyBinaryRelation.name: EmptyBinaryRelation
EmptyBinaryRelation.description: is the relation with R empty. In the first form of the command with degree an integer, the domain is the set of points { 1, ..., degree }. In the second form, the domain is that given by the argument domain.
EmptyBinaryRelation.title: EmptyBinaryRelation

IsReflexiveBinaryRelation.name: IsReflexiveBinaryRelation
IsReflexiveBinaryRelation.description:
returns true if the binary relation R is reflexive, and false otherwise.

A binary relation R (as a set of pairs) on a set X is reflexive if for all x ∈ X, (x,x) ∈ R. Alternatively, R as a mapping is reflexive if for all x ∈ X, x is an element of the image set R(x).

A reflexive binary relation is necessarily a total endomorphic mapping (tested via IsTotal (32.3-1)).
IsReflexiveBinaryRelation.title: IsReflexiveBinaryRelation

IsSymmetricBinaryRelation.name: IsSymmetricBinaryRelation
IsSymmetricBinaryRelation.description:
returns true if the binary relation R is symmetric, and false otherwise.

A binary relation R (as a set of pairs) on a set X is symmetric if (x,y) ∈ R then (y,x) ∈ R. Alternatively, R as a mapping is symmetric if for all x ∈ X, the preimage set of x under R equals the image set R(x).
IsSymmetricBinaryRelation.title: IsSymmetricBinaryRelation

IsTransitiveBinaryRelation.name: IsTransitiveBinaryRelation
IsTransitiveBinaryRelation.description:
returns true if the binary relation R is transitive, and false otherwise.

A binary relation R (as a set of pairs) on a set X is transitive if (x,y), (y,z) ∈ R implies (x,z) ∈ R. Alternatively, R as a mapping is transitive if for all x ∈ X, the image set R(R(x)) of the image set R(x) of x is a subset of R(x).
IsTransitiveBinaryRelation.title: IsTransitiveBinaryRelation

IsAntisymmetricBinaryRelation.name: IsAntisymmetricBinaryRelation
IsAntisymmetricBinaryRelation.description:
returns true if the binary relation rel is antisymmetric, and false otherwise.

A binary relation R (as a set of pairs) on a set X is antisymmetric if (x,y), (y,x) ∈ R implies x = y. Alternatively, R as a mapping is antisymmetric if for all x ∈ X, the intersection of the preimage set of x under R and the image set R(x) is { x }.
IsAntisymmetricBinaryRelation.title: IsAntisymmetricBinaryRelation

IsPreOrderBinaryRelation.name: IsPreOrderBinaryRelation
IsPreOrderBinaryRelation.description:
returns true if the binary relation rel is a preorder, and false otherwise.

A preorder is a binary relation that is both reflexive and transitive.
IsPreOrderBinaryRelation.title: IsPreOrderBinaryRelation

IsPartialOrderBinaryRelation.name: IsPartialOrderBinaryRelation
IsPartialOrderBinaryRelation.description:
returns true if the binary relation rel is a partial order, and false otherwise.

A partial order is a preorder which is also antisymmetric.
IsPartialOrderBinaryRelation.title: IsPartialOrderBinaryRelation

IsHasseDiagram.name: IsHasseDiagram
IsHasseDiagram.description: returns true if the binary relation rel is a Hasse Diagram of a partial order, i.e., was computed via HasseDiagramBinaryRelation (33.4-4).
IsHasseDiagram.title: IsHasseDiagram

IsEquivalenceRelation.name: IsEquivalenceRelation
IsEquivalenceRelation.description:
returns true if the binary relation R is an equivalence relation, and false otherwise.

Recall, that a relation R is an equivalence relation if it is symmetric, transitive, and reflexive.
IsEquivalenceRelation.title: IsEquivalenceRelation

Successors.name: Successors
Successors.description:
returns the list of images of a binary relation R. If the underlying domain of the relation is not { 1, ..., n }, for some positive integer n, then an error is signalled.

The returned value of Successors is a list of lists where the lists are ordered as the elements according to the sorted order of the underlying set of R. Each list consists of the images of the element whose index is the same as the list with the underlying set in sorted order.

The Successors of a relation is the adjacency list representation of the relation.
Successors.title: Successors

DegreeOfBinaryRelation.name: DegreeOfBinaryRelation
DegreeOfBinaryRelation.description: returns the size of the underlying domain of the binary relation R. This is most natural when working with a binary relation on points.
DegreeOfBinaryRelation.title: DegreeOfBinaryRelation

PartialOrderOfHasseDiagram.name: PartialOrderOfHasseDiagram
PartialOrderOfHasseDiagram.description: is the partial order associated with the Hasse Diagram HD i.e. the partial order generated by the reflexive and transitive closure of HD.
PartialOrderOfHasseDiagram.title: PartialOrderOfHasseDiagram

BinaryRelationOnPoints.name: BinaryRelationOnPoints
BinaryRelationOnPoints.description: Given a list of n lists, each containing elements from the set { 1, ..., n }, this function constructs a binary relation such that 1 is related to list[1], 2 to list[2] and so on. The first version checks whether the list supplied is valid. The NC version skips this check.
BinaryRelationOnPoints.title: BinaryRelationOnPoints

BinaryRelationOnPointsNC.name: BinaryRelationOnPointsNC
BinaryRelationOnPointsNC.description: Given a list of n lists, each containing elements from the set { 1, ..., n }, this function constructs a binary relation such that 1 is related to list[1], 2 to list[2] and so on. The first version checks whether the list supplied is valid. The NC version skips this check.
BinaryRelationOnPointsNC.title: BinaryRelationOnPoints

RandomBinaryRelationOnPoints.name: RandomBinaryRelationOnPoints
RandomBinaryRelationOnPoints.description: creates a relation on points with degree degree.
RandomBinaryRelationOnPoints.title: RandomBinaryRelationOnPoints

AsBinaryRelationOnPoints.name: AsBinaryRelationOnPoints
AsBinaryRelationOnPoints.description:
return the relation on points represented by general relation rel, transformation trans or permutation perm. If rel is already a binary relation on points then rel is returned.

Transformations and permutations are special general endomorphic mappings and have a natural representation as a binary relation on points.

In the last form, an isomorphic relation on points is constructed where the points are indices of the elements of the underlying domain in sorted order.
AsBinaryRelationOnPoints.title: AsBinaryRelationOnPoints

AsBinaryRelationOnPoints.name: AsBinaryRelationOnPoints
AsBinaryRelationOnPoints.description:
return the relation on points represented by general relation rel, transformation trans or permutation perm. If rel is already a binary relation on points then rel is returned.

Transformations and permutations are special general endomorphic mappings and have a natural representation as a binary relation on points.

In the last form, an isomorphic relation on points is constructed where the points are indices of the elements of the underlying domain in sorted order.
AsBinaryRelationOnPoints.title: AsBinaryRelationOnPoints

AsBinaryRelationOnPoints.name: AsBinaryRelationOnPoints
AsBinaryRelationOnPoints.description:
return the relation on points represented by general relation rel, transformation trans or permutation perm. If rel is already a binary relation on points then rel is returned.

Transformations and permutations are special general endomorphic mappings and have a natural representation as a binary relation on points.

In the last form, an isomorphic relation on points is constructed where the points are indices of the elements of the underlying domain in sorted order.
AsBinaryRelationOnPoints.title: AsBinaryRelationOnPoints

ReflexiveClosureBinaryRelation.name: ReflexiveClosureBinaryRelation
ReflexiveClosureBinaryRelation.description: is the smallest binary relation containing the binary relation R which is reflexive. This closure inherits the properties symmetric and transitive from R. E.g., if R is symmetric then its reflexive closure is also.
ReflexiveClosureBinaryRelation.title: ReflexiveClosureBinaryRelation

SymmetricClosureBinaryRelation.name: SymmetricClosureBinaryRelation
SymmetricClosureBinaryRelation.description: is the smallest binary relation containing the binary relation R which is symmetric. This closure inherits the properties reflexive and transitive from R. E.g., if R is reflexive then its symmetric closure is also.
SymmetricClosureBinaryRelation.title: SymmetricClosureBinaryRelation

TransitiveClosureBinaryRelation.name: TransitiveClosureBinaryRelation
TransitiveClosureBinaryRelation.description:
is the smallest binary relation containing the binary relation R which is transitive. This closure inherits the properties reflexive and symmetric from R. E.g., if R is symmetric then its transitive closure is also.

TransitiveClosureBinaryRelation is a modified version of the Floyd-Warshall method of solving the all-pairs shortest-paths problem on a directed graph. Its asymptotic runtime is O(n^3) where n is the size of the vertex set. It only assumes there is an arbitrary (but fixed) ordering of the vertex set.
TransitiveClosureBinaryRelation.title: TransitiveClosureBinaryRelation

HasseDiagramBinaryRelation.name: HasseDiagramBinaryRelation
HasseDiagramBinaryRelation.description: is the smallest relation contained in the partial order partial-order whose reflexive and transitive closure is equal to partial-order.
HasseDiagramBinaryRelation.title: HasseDiagramBinaryRelation

StronglyConnectedComponents.name: StronglyConnectedComponents
StronglyConnectedComponents.description: returns an equivalence relation on the vertices of the binary relation R.
StronglyConnectedComponents.title: StronglyConnectedComponents

PartialOrderByOrderingFunction.name: PartialOrderByOrderingFunction
PartialOrderByOrderingFunction.description: constructs a partial order whose elements are from the domain dom and are ordered using the ordering function orderfunc. The ordering function must be a binary function returning a boolean value. If the ordering function does not describe a partial order then fail is returned.
PartialOrderByOrderingFunction.title: PartialOrderByOrderingFunction

EquivalenceRelationByPartition.name: EquivalenceRelationByPartition
EquivalenceRelationByPartition.description:
constructs the equivalence relation over the set domain which induces the partition represented by list. This representation includes only the non-trivial blocks (or equivalent classes). list is a list of lists, each of these lists contain elements of domain and are pairwise mutually exclusive.

The list of lists do not need to be in any order nor do the elements in the blocks (see EquivalenceRelationPartition (33.6-1)). a list of elements of domain The partition list is a list of lists, each of these is a list of elements of domain that makes up a block (or equivalent class). The domain is the domain over which the relation is defined, and list is a list of lists, each of these is a list of elements of domain which are related to each other. list need only contain the nontrivial blocks and singletons will be ignored. The NC version will not check to see if the lists are pairwise mutually exclusive or that they contain only elements of the domain.
EquivalenceRelationByPartition.title: EquivalenceRelationByPartition

EquivalenceRelationByPartitionNC.name: EquivalenceRelationByPartitionNC
EquivalenceRelationByPartitionNC.description:
constructs the equivalence relation over the set domain which induces the partition represented by list. This representation includes only the non-trivial blocks (or equivalent classes). list is a list of lists, each of these lists contain elements of domain and are pairwise mutually exclusive.

The list of lists do not need to be in any order nor do the elements in the blocks (see EquivalenceRelationPartition (33.6-1)). a list of elements of domain The partition list is a list of lists, each of these is a list of elements of domain that makes up a block (or equivalent class). The domain is the domain over which the relation is defined, and list is a list of lists, each of these is a list of elements of domain which are related to each other. list need only contain the nontrivial blocks and singletons will be ignored. The NC version will not check to see if the lists are pairwise mutually exclusive or that they contain only elements of the domain.
EquivalenceRelationByPartitionNC.title: EquivalenceRelationByPartition

EquivalenceRelationByRelation.name: EquivalenceRelationByRelation
EquivalenceRelationByRelation.description: returns the smallest equivalence relation containing the binary relation rel.
EquivalenceRelationByRelation.title: EquivalenceRelationByRelation

EquivalenceRelationByPairs.name: EquivalenceRelationByPairs
EquivalenceRelationByPairs.description:
return the smallest equivalence relation on the domain D such that every pair in elms is in the relation.

In the NC form, it is not checked that elms are in the domain D.
EquivalenceRelationByPairs.title: EquivalenceRelationByPairs

EquivalenceRelationByPairsNC.name: EquivalenceRelationByPairsNC
EquivalenceRelationByPairsNC.description:
return the smallest equivalence relation on the domain D such that every pair in elms is in the relation.

In the NC form, it is not checked that elms are in the domain D.
EquivalenceRelationByPairsNC.title: EquivalenceRelationByPairs

EquivalenceRelationByProperty.name: EquivalenceRelationByProperty
EquivalenceRelationByProperty.description: creates an equivalence relation on domain whose only defining datum is that of having the property property.
EquivalenceRelationByProperty.title: EquivalenceRelationByProperty

EquivalenceRelationPartition.name: EquivalenceRelationPartition
EquivalenceRelationPartition.description: returns a list of lists of elements of the underlying set of the equivalence relation equiv. The lists are precisely the nonsingleton equivalence classes of the equivalence. This allows us to describe small equivalences on infinite sets.
EquivalenceRelationPartition.title: EquivalenceRelationPartition

GeneratorsOfEquivalenceRelationPartition.name: GeneratorsOfEquivalenceRelationPartition
GeneratorsOfEquivalenceRelationPartition.description: is a set of generating pairs for the equivalence relation equiv. This set is not unique. The equivalence equiv is the smallest equivalence relation over the underlying set which contains the generating pairs.
GeneratorsOfEquivalenceRelationPartition.title: GeneratorsOfEquivalenceRelationPartition

JoinEquivalenceRelations.name: JoinEquivalenceRelations
JoinEquivalenceRelations.description:
JoinEquivalenceRelations returns the smallest equivalence relation containing both the equivalence relations equiv1 and equiv2.

MeetEquivalenceRelations returns the intersection of the two equivalence relations equiv1 and equiv2.
JoinEquivalenceRelations.title: JoinEquivalenceRelations

MeetEquivalenceRelations.name: MeetEquivalenceRelations
MeetEquivalenceRelations.description:
JoinEquivalenceRelations returns the smallest equivalence relation containing both the equivalence relations equiv1 and equiv2.

MeetEquivalenceRelations returns the intersection of the two equivalence relations equiv1 and equiv2.
MeetEquivalenceRelations.title: JoinEquivalenceRelations

IsEquivalenceClass.name: IsEquivalenceClass
IsEquivalenceClass.description:
returns true if the object obj is an equivalence class, and false otherwise.

An equivalence class is a collection of elements which are mutually related to each other in the associated equivalence relation. Note, this is a special category of objects and not just a list of elements.
IsEquivalenceClass.title: IsEquivalenceClass

EquivalenceClassRelation.name: EquivalenceClassRelation
EquivalenceClassRelation.description: returns the equivalence relation of which C is a class.
EquivalenceClassRelation.title: EquivalenceClassRelation

EquivalenceClasses.name: EquivalenceClasses
EquivalenceClasses.description: returns a list of all equivalence classes of the equivalence relation rel. Note that it is possible for different methods to yield the list in different orders, so that for two equivalence relations c1 and c2 we may have c1 = c2 without having EquivalenceClasses( c1 ) =EquivalenceClasses( c2 ).
EquivalenceClasses.title: EquivalenceClasses

EquivalenceClassOfElement.name: EquivalenceClassOfElement
EquivalenceClassOfElement.description: return the equivalence class of elt in the binary relation rel, where elt is an element (i.e. a pair) of the domain of rel. In the NC form, it is not checked that elt is in the domain over which rel is defined.
EquivalenceClassOfElement.title: EquivalenceClassOfElement

EquivalenceClassOfElementNC.name: EquivalenceClassOfElementNC
EquivalenceClassOfElementNC.description: return the equivalence class of elt in the binary relation rel, where elt is an element (i.e. a pair) of the domain of rel. In the NC form, it is not checked that elt is in the domain over which rel is defined.
EquivalenceClassOfElementNC.title: EquivalenceClassOfElement

IsOrdering.name: IsOrdering
IsOrdering.description: returns true if and only if the object ord is an ordering.
IsOrdering.title: IsOrdering

OrderingsFamily.name: OrderingsFamily
OrderingsFamily.description: for a family fam, returns the family of all orderings on elements of fam.
OrderingsFamily.title: OrderingsFamily

OrderingByLessThanFunctionNC.name: OrderingByLessThanFunctionNC
OrderingByLessThanFunctionNC.description:
Called with two arguments, OrderingByLessThanFunctionNC returns the ordering on the elements of the elements of the family fam, according to the LessThanFunction (34.3-5) value given by lt, where lt is a function that takes two arguments in fam and returns true or false.

Called with three arguments, for a family fam, a function lt that takes two arguments in fam and returns true or false, and a list l of properties of orderings, OrderingByLessThanFunctionNC returns the ordering on the elements of fam with LessThanFunction (34.3-5) value given by lt and with the properties from l set to true.
OrderingByLessThanFunctionNC.title: OrderingByLessThanFunctionNC

OrderingByLessThanOrEqualFunctionNC.name: OrderingByLessThanOrEqualFunctionNC
OrderingByLessThanOrEqualFunctionNC.description:
Called with two arguments, OrderingByLessThanOrEqualFunctionNC returns the ordering on the elements of the elements of the family fam according to the LessThanOrEqualFunction (34.3-6) value given by lteq, where lteq is a function that takes two arguments in fam and returns true or false.

Called with three arguments, for a family fam, a function lteq that takes two arguments in fam and returns true or false, and a list l of properties of orderings, OrderingByLessThanOrEqualFunctionNC returns the ordering on the elements of fam with LessThanOrEqualFunction (34.3-6) value given by lteq and with the properties from l set to true.

Notice that these functions do not check whether fam and lt or lteq are compatible, and whether the properties listed in l are indeed satisfied.
OrderingByLessThanOrEqualFunctionNC.title: OrderingByLessThanOrEqualFunctionNC

IsWellFoundedOrdering.name: IsWellFoundedOrdering
IsWellFoundedOrdering.description: for an ordering ord, returns true if and only if the ordering is well founded. An ordering ord is well founded if it admits no infinite descending chains. Normally this property is set at the time of creation of the ordering and there is no general method to check whether a certain ordering is well founded.
IsWellFoundedOrdering.title: IsWellFoundedOrdering

IsTotalOrdering.name: IsTotalOrdering
IsTotalOrdering.description: for an ordering ord, returns true if and only if the ordering is total. An ordering ord is total if any two elements of the family are comparable under ord. Normally this property is set at the time of creation of the ordering and there is no general method to check whether a certain ordering is total.
IsTotalOrdering.title: IsTotalOrdering

IsIncomparableUnder.name: IsIncomparableUnder
IsIncomparableUnder.description: for an ordering ord on the elements of the family of el1 and el2, returns true if el1 ≠ el2 and IsLessThanUnder(ord,el1,el2), IsLessThanUnder(ord,el2,el1) are both false; and returns false otherwise.
IsIncomparableUnder.title: IsIncomparableUnder

FamilyForOrdering.name: FamilyForOrdering
FamilyForOrdering.description: for an ordering ord, returns the family of elements that the ordering ord compares.
FamilyForOrdering.title: FamilyForOrdering

LessThanFunction.name: LessThanFunction
LessThanFunction.description: for an ordering ord, returns a function f which takes two elements el1, el2 in FamilyForOrdering(ord) and returns true if el1 is strictly less than el2 (with respect to ord), and returns false otherwise.
LessThanFunction.title: LessThanFunction

LessThanOrEqualFunction.name: LessThanOrEqualFunction
LessThanOrEqualFunction.description: for an ordering ord, returns a function that takes two elements el1, el2 in FamilyForOrdering(ord) and returns true if el1 is less than or equal to el2 (with respect to ord), and returns false otherwise.
LessThanOrEqualFunction.title: LessThanOrEqualFunction

IsLessThanUnder.name: IsLessThanUnder
IsLessThanUnder.description: for an ordering ord on the elements of the family of el1 and el2, returns true if el1 is (strictly) less than el2 with respect to ord, and false otherwise.
IsLessThanUnder.title: IsLessThanUnder

IsLessThanOrEqualUnder.name: IsLessThanOrEqualUnder
IsLessThanOrEqualUnder.description: for an ordering ord on the elements of the family of el1 and el2, returns true if el1 is less than or equal to el2 with respect to ord, and false otherwise.
IsLessThanOrEqualUnder.title: IsLessThanOrEqualUnder

IsOrderingOnFamilyOfAssocWords.name: IsOrderingOnFamilyOfAssocWords
IsOrderingOnFamilyOfAssocWords.description: for an ordering ord, returns true if ord is an ordering over a family of associative words.
IsOrderingOnFamilyOfAssocWords.title: IsOrderingOnFamilyOfAssocWords

IsTranslationInvariantOrdering.name: IsTranslationInvariantOrdering
IsTranslationInvariantOrdering.description:
for an ordering ord on a family of associative words, returns true if and only if the ordering is translation invariant.

This is a property of orderings on families of associative words. An ordering ord over a family F, with alphabet X is translation invariant if IsLessThanUnder( ord, u, v ) implies that for any a, b ∈ X^*, IsLessThanUnder( ord, a*u*b, a*v*b ).
IsTranslationInvariantOrdering.title: IsTranslationInvariantOrdering

IsReductionOrdering.name: IsReductionOrdering
IsReductionOrdering.description: for an ordering ord on a family of associative words, returns true if and only if the ordering is a reduction ordering. An ordering ord is a reduction ordering if it is well founded and translation invariant.
IsReductionOrdering.title: IsReductionOrdering

OrderingOnGenerators.name: OrderingOnGenerators
OrderingOnGenerators.description: for an ordering ord on a family of associative words, returns a list in which the generators are considered. This could be indeed the ordering of the generators in the ordering, but, for example, if a weight is associated to each generator then this is not true anymore. See the example for WeightLexOrdering (34.4-8).
OrderingOnGenerators.title: OrderingOnGenerators

LexicographicOrdering.name: LexicographicOrdering
LexicographicOrdering.description:
Let D be a free semigroup, a free monoid, or the elements family of such a domain. Called with only argument D, LexicographicOrdering returns the lexicographic ordering on the elements of D.

The optional argument gens can be either the list of free generators of D, in the desired order, or a list of the positions of these generators, in the desired order, and LexicographicOrdering returns the lexicographic ordering on the elements of D with the ordering on the generators as given.
LexicographicOrdering.title: LexicographicOrdering

ShortLexOrdering.name: ShortLexOrdering
ShortLexOrdering.description:
Let D be a free semigroup, a free monoid, or the elements family of such a domain. Called with only argument D, ShortLexOrdering returns the shortlex ordering on the elements of D.

The optional argument gens can be either the list of free generators of D, in the desired order, or a list of the positions of these generators, in the desired order, and ShortLexOrdering returns the shortlex ordering on the elements of D with the ordering on the generators as given.
ShortLexOrdering.title: ShortLexOrdering

IsShortLexOrdering.name: IsShortLexOrdering
IsShortLexOrdering.description: for an ordering ord of a family of associative words, returns true if and only if ord is a shortlex ordering.
IsShortLexOrdering.title: IsShortLexOrdering

WeightLexOrdering.name: WeightLexOrdering
WeightLexOrdering.description: Let D be a free semigroup, a free monoid, or the elements family of such a domain. gens can be either the list of free generators of D, in the desired order, or a list of the positions of these generators, in the desired order. Let wt be a list of weights. WeightLexOrdering returns the weightlex ordering on the elements of D with the ordering on the generators and weights of the generators as given.
WeightLexOrdering.title: WeightLexOrdering

IsWeightLexOrdering.name: IsWeightLexOrdering
IsWeightLexOrdering.description: for an ordering ord on a family of associative words, returns true if and only if ord is a weightlex ordering.
IsWeightLexOrdering.title: IsWeightLexOrdering

WeightOfGenerators.name: WeightOfGenerators
WeightOfGenerators.description: for a weightlex ordering ord, returns a list with length the size of the alphabet of the family. This list gives the weight of each of the letters of the alphabet which are used for weightlex orderings with respect to the ordering given by OrderingOnGenerators (34.4-4).
WeightOfGenerators.title: WeightOfGenerators

BasicWreathProductOrdering.name: BasicWreathProductOrdering
BasicWreathProductOrdering.description:
Let D be a free semigroup, a free monoid, or the elements family of such a domain. Called with only argument D, BasicWreathProductOrdering returns the basic wreath product ordering on the elements of D.

The optional argument gens can be either the list of free generators of D, in the desired order, or a list of the positions of these generators, in the desired order, and BasicWreathProductOrdering returns the lexicographic ordering on the elements of D with the ordering on the generators as given.
BasicWreathProductOrdering.title: BasicWreathProductOrdering

WreathProductOrdering.name: WreathProductOrdering
WreathProductOrdering.description: Let D be a free semigroup, a free monoid, or the elements family of such a domain, let gens be either the list of free generators of D, in the desired order, or a list of the positions of these generators, in the desired order, and let levels be a list of levels for the generators. If gens is omitted then the default ordering is taken. WreathProductOrdering returns the wreath product ordering on the elements of D with the ordering on the generators as given.
WreathProductOrdering.title: WreathProductOrdering

IsWreathProductOrdering.name: IsWreathProductOrdering
IsWreathProductOrdering.description: specifies whether an ordering is a wreath product ordering (see WreathProductOrdering (34.4-13)).
IsWreathProductOrdering.title: IsWreathProductOrdering

LevelsOfGenerators.name: LevelsOfGenerators
LevelsOfGenerators.description: for a wreath product ordering ord, returns the levels of the generators as given at creation (with respect to OrderingOnGenerators (34.4-4)).
LevelsOfGenerators.title: LevelsOfGenerators

IsMagma.name: IsMagma
IsMagma.description: A magma in GAP is a domain M with (not necessarily associative) multiplication *: M × M → M.
IsMagma.title: IsMagma

IsMagmaWithOne.name: IsMagmaWithOne
IsMagmaWithOne.description:
A magma-with-one in GAP is a magma M with an operation ^0 (or One (31.10-2)) that yields the identity of M.

So a magma-with-one M does always contain a unique multiplicatively neutral element e, i.e., e * m = m = m * e holds for all m ∈ M (see MultiplicativeNeutralElement (35.4-10)). This element e can be computed with the operation One (31.10-2) as One( M ), and e is also equal to One( m ) and to m^0 for each element m ∈ M.

Note that a magma may contain a multiplicatively neutral element but not an identity (see One (31.10-2)), and a magma containing an identity may not lie in the category IsMagmaWithOne (see Section 31.6).
IsMagmaWithOne.title: IsMagmaWithOne

IsMagmaWithInversesIfNonzero.name: IsMagmaWithInversesIfNonzero
IsMagmaWithInversesIfNonzero.description:
An object in this GAP category is a magma-with-one M with an operation ^-1: M ∖ Z → M ∖ Z that maps each element m of M ∖ Z to its inverse m^-1 (or Inverse( m ), see Inverse (31.10-8)), where Z is either empty or consists exactly of one element of M.

This category was introduced mainly to describe division rings, since the nonzero elements in a division ring form a group; So an object M in IsMagmaWithInversesIfNonzero will usually have both a multiplicative and an additive structure (see 55), and the set Z, if it is nonempty, contains exactly the zero element (see Zero (31.10-3)) of M.
IsMagmaWithInversesIfNonzero.title: IsMagmaWithInversesIfNonzero

IsMagmaWithInverses.name: IsMagmaWithInverses
IsMagmaWithInverses.description:
A magma-with-inverses in GAP is a magma-with-one M with an operation ^-1: M → M that maps each element m of M to its inverse m^-1 (or Inverse( m ), see Inverse (31.10-8)).

Note that not every trivial magma is a magma-with-one, but every trivial magma-with-one is a magma-with-inverses. This holds also if the identity of the magma-with-one is a zero element. So a magma-with-inverses-if-nonzero can be a magma-with-inverses if either it contains no zero element or consists of a zero element that has itself as zero-th power.
IsMagmaWithInverses.title: IsMagmaWithInverses

Magma.name: Magma
Magma.description: returns the magma M that is generated by the elements in the list gens, that is, the closure of gens under multiplication \* (31.12-1). The family Fam of M can be entered as the first argument; this is obligatory if gens is empty (and hence also M is empty).
Magma.title: Magma

MagmaWithOne.name: MagmaWithOne
MagmaWithOne.description: returns the magma-with-one M that is generated by the elements in the list gens, that is, the closure of gens under multiplication \* (31.12-1) and One (31.10-2). The family Fam of M can be entered as first argument; this is obligatory if gens is empty (and hence M is trivial).
MagmaWithOne.title: MagmaWithOne

MagmaWithInverses.name: MagmaWithInverses
MagmaWithInverses.description: returns the magma-with-inverses M that is generated by the elements in the list gens, that is, the closure of gens under multiplication \* (31.12-1), One (31.10-2), and Inverse (31.10-8). The family Fam of M can be entered as first argument; this is obligatory if gens is empty (and hence M is trivial).
MagmaWithInverses.title: MagmaWithInverses

Submagma.name: Submagma
Submagma.description: Submagma returns the magma generated by the elements in the list gens, with parent the domain D. SubmagmaNC does the same, except that it is not checked whether the elements of gens lie in D.
Submagma.title: Submagma

SubmagmaNC.name: SubmagmaNC
SubmagmaNC.description: Submagma returns the magma generated by the elements in the list gens, with parent the domain D. SubmagmaNC does the same, except that it is not checked whether the elements of gens lie in D.
SubmagmaNC.title: Submagma

SubmagmaWithOne.name: SubmagmaWithOne
SubmagmaWithOne.description: SubmagmaWithOne returns the magma-with-one generated by the elements in the list gens, with parent the domain D. SubmagmaWithOneNC does the same, except that it is not checked whether the elements of gens lie in D.
SubmagmaWithOne.title: SubmagmaWithOne

SubmagmaWithOneNC.name: SubmagmaWithOneNC
SubmagmaWithOneNC.description: SubmagmaWithOne returns the magma-with-one generated by the elements in the list gens, with parent the domain D. SubmagmaWithOneNC does the same, except that it is not checked whether the elements of gens lie in D.
SubmagmaWithOneNC.title: SubmagmaWithOne

SubmagmaWithInverses.name: SubmagmaWithInverses
SubmagmaWithInverses.description: SubmagmaWithInverses returns the magma-with-inverses generated by the elements in the list gens, with parent the domain D. SubmagmaWithInversesNC does the same, except that it is not checked whether the elements of gens lie in D.
SubmagmaWithInverses.title: SubmagmaWithInverses

SubmagmaWithInversesNC.name: SubmagmaWithInversesNC
SubmagmaWithInversesNC.description: SubmagmaWithInverses returns the magma-with-inverses generated by the elements in the list gens, with parent the domain D. SubmagmaWithInversesNC does the same, except that it is not checked whether the elements of gens lie in D.
SubmagmaWithInversesNC.title: SubmagmaWithInverses

AsMagma.name: AsMagma
AsMagma.description: For a collection C whose elements form a magma, AsMagma returns this magma. Otherwise fail is returned.
AsMagma.title: AsMagma

AsSubmagma.name: AsSubmagma
AsSubmagma.description: Let D be a domain and C a collection. If C is a subset of D that forms a magma then AsSubmagma returns this magma, with parent D. Otherwise fail is returned.
AsSubmagma.title: AsSubmagma

IsMagmaWithZeroAdjoined.name: IsMagmaWithZeroAdjoined
IsMagmaWithZeroAdjoined.description:
Returns: true or false.

IsMagmaWithZeroAdjoined returns true if the magma M was created using InjectionZeroMagma (35.2-13) or MagmaWithZeroAdjoined (35.2-13) and returns false if it was not.
IsMagmaWithZeroAdjoined.title: IsMagmaWithZeroAdjoined

InjectionZeroMagma.name: InjectionZeroMagma
InjectionZeroMagma.description:
InjectionZeroMagma returns an embedding from the magma M into a new magma formed from M by adjoining a single new element which is the multiplicative zero of the resulting magma. The elements of the new magma form a family of elements in the category IsMultiplicativeElementWithZero (31.14-12) and the magma itself satisfies IsMagmaWithZeroAdjoined (35.2-12).

MagmaWithZeroAdjoined is just shorthand for Range(InjectionZeroMagma(M))).

If N is a magma with zero adjoined, then the embedding used to create N can be recovered using UnderlyingInjectionZeroMagma (35.2-14).
InjectionZeroMagma.title: InjectionZeroMagma

MagmaWithZeroAdjoined.name: MagmaWithZeroAdjoined
MagmaWithZeroAdjoined.description:
InjectionZeroMagma returns an embedding from the magma M into a new magma formed from M by adjoining a single new element which is the multiplicative zero of the resulting magma. The elements of the new magma form a family of elements in the category IsMultiplicativeElementWithZero (31.14-12) and the magma itself satisfies IsMagmaWithZeroAdjoined (35.2-12).

MagmaWithZeroAdjoined is just shorthand for Range(InjectionZeroMagma(M))).

If N is a magma with zero adjoined, then the embedding used to create N can be recovered using UnderlyingInjectionZeroMagma (35.2-14).
MagmaWithZeroAdjoined.title: InjectionZeroMagma

UnderlyingInjectionZeroMagma.name: UnderlyingInjectionZeroMagma
UnderlyingInjectionZeroMagma.description: UnderlyingInjectionZeroMagma returns the embedding used to create the magma with zero adjoined M.
UnderlyingInjectionZeroMagma.title: UnderlyingInjectionZeroMagma

MagmaByMultiplicationTable.name: MagmaByMultiplicationTable
MagmaByMultiplicationTable.description:
For a square matrix A with n rows such that all entries of A are in the range [ 1 .. n ], MagmaByMultiplicationTable returns a magma M with multiplication * defined by A. That is, M consists of the elements m_1, m_2, ..., m_n, and m_i * m_j = m_k, with k = A[i][j].

The ordering of elements is defined by m_1 < m_2 < ⋯ < m_n, so m_i can be accessed as MagmaElement( M, i ), see MagmaElement (35.3-4).
MagmaByMultiplicationTable.title: MagmaByMultiplicationTable

MagmaWithOneByMultiplicationTable.name: MagmaWithOneByMultiplicationTable
MagmaWithOneByMultiplicationTable.description: The only differences between MagmaByMultiplicationTable (35.3-1) and MagmaWithOneByMultiplicationTable are that the latter returns a magma-with-one (see MagmaWithOne (35.2-2)) if the magma described by the matrix A has an identity, and returns fail if not.
MagmaWithOneByMultiplicationTable.title: MagmaWithOneByMultiplicationTable

MagmaWithInversesByMultiplicationTable.name: MagmaWithInversesByMultiplicationTable
MagmaWithInversesByMultiplicationTable.description: MagmaByMultiplicationTable (35.3-1) and MagmaWithInversesByMultiplicationTable differ only in that the latter returns magma-with-inverses (see MagmaWithInverses (35.2-3)) if each element in the magma described by the matrix A has an inverse, and returns fail if not.
MagmaWithInversesByMultiplicationTable.title: MagmaWithInversesByMultiplicationTable

MagmaElement.name: MagmaElement
MagmaElement.description: For a magma M and a positive integer i, MagmaElement returns the i-th element of M, w.r.t. the ordering <. If M has less than i elements then fail is returned.
MagmaElement.title: MagmaElement

MultiplicationTable.name: MultiplicationTable
MultiplicationTable.description:
For a list elms of elements that form a magma M, MultiplicationTable returns a square matrix A of positive integers such that A[i][j] = k holds if and only if elms[i] * elms[j] = elms[k]. This matrix can be used to construct a magma isomorphic to M, using MagmaByMultiplicationTable (35.3-1).

For a magma M, MultiplicationTable returns the multiplication table w.r.t. the sorted list of elements of M.
MultiplicationTable.title: MultiplicationTable

MultiplicationTable.name: MultiplicationTable
MultiplicationTable.description:
For a list elms of elements that form a magma M, MultiplicationTable returns a square matrix A of positive integers such that A[i][j] = k holds if and only if elms[i] * elms[j] = elms[k]. This matrix can be used to construct a magma isomorphic to M, using MagmaByMultiplicationTable (35.3-1).

For a magma M, MultiplicationTable returns the multiplication table w.r.t. the sorted list of elements of M.
MultiplicationTable.title: MultiplicationTable

GeneratorsOfMagma.name: GeneratorsOfMagma
GeneratorsOfMagma.description:
is a list gens of elements of the magma M that generates M as a magma, that is, the closure of gens under multiplication \* (31.12-1) is M.

For a free magma, each generator can also be accessed using the . operator (see GeneratorsOfDomain (31.9-2)).
GeneratorsOfMagma.title: GeneratorsOfMagma

GeneratorsOfMagmaWithOne.name: GeneratorsOfMagmaWithOne
GeneratorsOfMagmaWithOne.description:
is a list gens of elements of the magma-with-one M that generates M as a magma-with-one, that is, the closure of gens under multiplication \* (31.12-1) and One (31.10-2) is M.

For a free magma with one, each generator can also be accessed using the . operator (see GeneratorsOfDomain (31.9-2)).
GeneratorsOfMagmaWithOne.title: GeneratorsOfMagmaWithOne

GeneratorsOfMagmaWithInverses.name: GeneratorsOfMagmaWithInverses
GeneratorsOfMagmaWithInverses.description: is a list gens of elements of the magma-with-inverses M that generates M as a magma-with-inverses, that is, the closure of gens under multiplication \* (31.12-1) and taking inverses (see Inverse (31.10-8)) is M.
GeneratorsOfMagmaWithInverses.title: GeneratorsOfMagmaWithInverses

Centralizer.name: Centralizer
Centralizer.description:
For an element elm of the magma M this operation returns the centralizer of elm. This is the domain of those elements m ∈ M that commute with elm.

For a submagma S it returns the domain of those elements that commute with all elements s of S.

If class is a class of objects of a magma (this magma then is stored as the ActingDomain of class) such as given by ConjugacyClass (39.10-1), Centralizer returns the centralizer of Representative(class) (which is a slight abuse of the notation).
Centralizer.title: Centralizer

Centralizer.name: Centralizer
Centralizer.description:
For an element elm of the magma M this operation returns the centralizer of elm. This is the domain of those elements m ∈ M that commute with elm.

For a submagma S it returns the domain of those elements that commute with all elements s of S.

If class is a class of objects of a magma (this magma then is stored as the ActingDomain of class) such as given by ConjugacyClass (39.10-1), Centralizer returns the centralizer of Representative(class) (which is a slight abuse of the notation).
Centralizer.title: Centralizer

Centralizer.name: Centralizer
Centralizer.description:
For an element elm of the magma M this operation returns the centralizer of elm. This is the domain of those elements m ∈ M that commute with elm.

For a submagma S it returns the domain of those elements that commute with all elements s of S.

If class is a class of objects of a magma (this magma then is stored as the ActingDomain of class) such as given by ConjugacyClass (39.10-1), Centralizer returns the centralizer of Representative(class) (which is a slight abuse of the notation).
Centralizer.title: Centralizer

Centre.name: Centre
Centre.description:
Centre returns the centre of the magma M, i.e., the domain of those elements m ∈ M that commute and associate with all elements of M. That is, the set { m ∈ M; ∀ a, b ∈ M: ma = am, (ma)b = m(ab), (am)b = a(mb), (ab)m = a(bm) }.

Center is just a synonym for Centre.

For associative magmas we have that Centre( M ) = Centralizer( M, M ), see Centralizer (35.4-4).
Centre.title: Centre

Center.name: Center
Center.description:
Centre returns the centre of the magma M, i.e., the domain of those elements m ∈ M that commute and associate with all elements of M. That is, the set { m ∈ M; ∀ a, b ∈ M: ma = am, (ma)b = m(ab), (am)b = a(mb), (ab)m = a(bm) }.

Center is just a synonym for Centre.

For associative magmas we have that Centre( M ) = Centralizer( M, M ), see Centralizer (35.4-4).
Center.title: Centre

Idempotents.name: Idempotents
Idempotents.description: The set of elements of M which are their own squares.
Idempotents.title: Idempotents

IsAssociative.name: IsAssociative
IsAssociative.description:
A collection M of elements that can be multiplied via \* (31.12-1) is associative if for all elements a, b, c ∈ M the equality (a * b) * c = a * (b * c) holds.

An associative magma is called a semigroup (see 51), an associative magma-with-one is called a monoid (see 51), and an associative magma-with-inverses is called a group (see 39).
IsAssociative.title: IsAssociative

IsCentral.name: IsCentral
IsCentral.description: IsCentral returns true if the object obj, which must either be an element or a magma, commutes with all elements in the magma M.
IsCentral.title: IsCentral

IsCommutative.name: IsCommutative
IsCommutative.description:
A collection M of elements that can be multiplied via \* (31.12-1) is commutative if for all elements a, b ∈ M the equality a * b = b * a holds. IsAbelian is a synonym of IsCommutative.

Note that the commutativity of the addition \+ (31.12-1) in an additive structure can be tested with IsAdditivelyCommutative (55.3-1).
IsCommutative.title: IsCommutative

IsAbelian.name: IsAbelian
IsAbelian.description:
A collection M of elements that can be multiplied via \* (31.12-1) is commutative if for all elements a, b ∈ M the equality a * b = b * a holds. IsAbelian is a synonym of IsCommutative.

Note that the commutativity of the addition \+ (31.12-1) in an additive structure can be tested with IsAdditivelyCommutative (55.3-1).
IsAbelian.title: IsCommutative

IsAbelian.name: IsAbelian
IsAbelian.description: These operations for groups are applicable to character tables and mean the same for a character table as for its underlying group; see Chapter 39 for the definitions. The operations are mainly useful for selecting character tables with certain properties, also for character tables without access to a group.
IsAbelian.title: Group Operations Applicable to Character Tables

MultiplicativeNeutralElement.name: MultiplicativeNeutralElement
MultiplicativeNeutralElement.description:
returns the element e in the magma M with the property that e * m = m = m * e holds for all m ∈ M, if such an element exists. Otherwise fail is returned.

A magma that is not a magma-with-one can have a multiplicative neutral element e; in this case, e cannot be obtained as One( M ), see One (31.10-2).
MultiplicativeNeutralElement.title: MultiplicativeNeutralElement

MultiplicativeZero.name: MultiplicativeZero
MultiplicativeZero.description:
MultiplicativeZero returns the multiplicative zero of the magma M which is the element z in M such that z * m = m * z = z for all m in M.

IsMultiplicativeZero returns true if the element z of the magma M equals the multiplicative zero of M.
MultiplicativeZero.title: MultiplicativeZero

MultiplicativeZero.name: MultiplicativeZero
MultiplicativeZero.description:
Returns: The empty partial permutation.

As described in MultiplicativeZero (35.4-11), Zero returns the multiplicative zero element of the partial permutation f, which is the empty partial permutation.
MultiplicativeZero.title: MultiplicativeZero

IsMultiplicativeZero.name: IsMultiplicativeZero
IsMultiplicativeZero.description:
MultiplicativeZero returns the multiplicative zero of the magma M which is the element z in M such that z * m = m * z = z for all m in M.

IsMultiplicativeZero returns true if the element z of the magma M equals the multiplicative zero of M.
IsMultiplicativeZero.title: MultiplicativeZero

SquareRoots.name: SquareRoots
SquareRoots.description: is the proper set of all elements r in the magma M such that r * r = elm holds.
SquareRoots.title: SquareRoots

TrivialSubmagmaWithOne.name: TrivialSubmagmaWithOne
TrivialSubmagmaWithOne.description: is the magma-with-one that has the identity of the magma-with-one M as only element.
TrivialSubmagmaWithOne.title: TrivialSubmagmaWithOne

IsWord.name: IsWord
IsWord.description:
Given a free multiplicative structure M that is freely generated by a subset X, any expression of an element in M as an iterated product of elements in X is called a word over X.

Interesting cases of free multiplicative structures are those of free semigroups, free monoids, and free groups, where the multiplication is associative (see IsAssociative (35.4-7)), which are described in Chapter 37, and also the case of free magmas, where the multiplication is nonassociative (see IsNonassocWord (36.1-3)).

Elements in free magmas (see FreeMagma (36.4-1)) lie in the category IsWord; similarly, elements in free magmas-with-one (see FreeMagmaWithOne (36.4-2)) lie in the category IsWordWithOne, and so on.
IsWord.title: IsWord

IsWordWithOne.name: IsWordWithOne
IsWordWithOne.description:
Given a free multiplicative structure M that is freely generated by a subset X, any expression of an element in M as an iterated product of elements in X is called a word over X.

Interesting cases of free multiplicative structures are those of free semigroups, free monoids, and free groups, where the multiplication is associative (see IsAssociative (35.4-7)), which are described in Chapter 37, and also the case of free magmas, where the multiplication is nonassociative (see IsNonassocWord (36.1-3)).

Elements in free magmas (see FreeMagma (36.4-1)) lie in the category IsWord; similarly, elements in free magmas-with-one (see FreeMagmaWithOne (36.4-2)) lie in the category IsWordWithOne, and so on.
IsWordWithOne.title: IsWord

IsWordWithInverse.name: IsWordWithInverse
IsWordWithInverse.description:
Given a free multiplicative structure M that is freely generated by a subset X, any expression of an element in M as an iterated product of elements in X is called a word over X.

Interesting cases of free multiplicative structures are those of free semigroups, free monoids, and free groups, where the multiplication is associative (see IsAssociative (35.4-7)), which are described in Chapter 37, and also the case of free magmas, where the multiplication is nonassociative (see IsNonassocWord (36.1-3)).

Elements in free magmas (see FreeMagma (36.4-1)) lie in the category IsWord; similarly, elements in free magmas-with-one (see FreeMagmaWithOne (36.4-2)) lie in the category IsWordWithOne, and so on.
IsWordWithInverse.title: IsWord

IsWordCollection.name: IsWordCollection
IsWordCollection.description: IsWordCollection is the collections category (see CategoryCollections (30.2-4)) of IsWord (36.1-1).
IsWordCollection.title: IsWordCollection

IsNonassocWord.name: IsNonassocWord
IsNonassocWord.description:
A nonassociative word in GAP is an element in a free magma or a free magma-with-one (see 36.4).

The default methods for ViewObj (6.3-5) and PrintObj (6.3-5) show nonassociative words as products of letters, where the succession of multiplications is determined by round brackets.

In this sense each nonassociative word describes a program to form a product of generators. (Also associative words can be interpreted as such programs, except that the exact succession of multiplications is not prescribed due to the associativity.) The function MappedWord (36.3-1) implements a way to apply such a program. A more general way is provided by straight line programs (see 37.8).
IsNonassocWord.title: IsNonassocWord

IsNonassocWordWithOne.name: IsNonassocWordWithOne
IsNonassocWordWithOne.description:
A nonassociative word in GAP is an element in a free magma or a free magma-with-one (see 36.4).

The default methods for ViewObj (6.3-5) and PrintObj (6.3-5) show nonassociative words as products of letters, where the succession of multiplications is determined by round brackets.

In this sense each nonassociative word describes a program to form a product of generators. (Also associative words can be interpreted as such programs, except that the exact succession of multiplications is not prescribed due to the associativity.) The function MappedWord (36.3-1) implements a way to apply such a program. A more general way is provided by straight line programs (see 37.8).
IsNonassocWordWithOne.title: IsNonassocWord

IsNonassocWordCollection.name: IsNonassocWordCollection
IsNonassocWordCollection.description: IsNonassocWordCollection is the collections category (see CategoryCollections (30.2-4)) of IsNonassocWord (36.1-3), and IsNonassocWordWithOneCollection is the collections category of IsNonassocWordWithOne (36.1-3).
IsNonassocWordCollection.title: IsNonassocWordCollection

IsNonassocWordWithOneCollection.name: IsNonassocWordWithOneCollection
IsNonassocWordWithOneCollection.description: IsNonassocWordCollection is the collections category (see CategoryCollections (30.2-4)) of IsNonassocWord (36.1-3), and IsNonassocWordWithOneCollection is the collections category of IsNonassocWordWithOne (36.1-3).
IsNonassocWordWithOneCollection.title: IsNonassocWordCollection

MappedWord.name: MappedWord
MappedWord.description:
MappedWord returns the object that is obtained by replacing each occurrence in the word w of a generator in the list gens by the corresponding object in the list imgs. The lists gens and imgs must of course have the same length.

MappedWord needs to do some preprocessing to get internal generator numbers etc. When mapping many (several thousand) words, a dedicated loop might be faster.

For example, if the elements in imgs are all associative words (see Chapter 37) in the same family as the elements in gens, and some of them are equal to the corresponding generators in gens, then those may be omitted from gens and imgs. In this situation, the special case that the lists gens and imgs have only length 1 is handled more efficiently by EliminatedWord (37.4-6).
MappedWord.title: MappedWord

FreeMagma.name: FreeMagma
FreeMagma.description:
FreeMagma returns a free magma. The number of generators, and the labels given to the generators, can be specified in several different ways. Warning: the labels of generators are only an aid for printing, and do not necessarily distinguish generators; see the examples at the end of FreeSemigroup (51.1-10) for more information.

Called with a positive integer rank, FreeMagma returns a free magma on rank generators. The optional argument name must be a string; its default value is "x".

If name is not given but the generatorNames option is, then this option is respected as described in Section 50.1-16.
FreeMagma.title: FreeMagma

IsAssocWord.name: IsAssocWord
IsAssocWord.description: IsAssocWord is the category of associative words in free semigroups, IsAssocWordWithOne is the category of associative words in free monoids (which admit the operation One (31.10-2) to compute an identity), IsAssocWordWithInverse is the category of associative words in free groups (which have an inverse). See IsWord (36.1-1) for more general categories of words.
IsAssocWord.title: IsAssocWord

IsAssocWordWithOne.name: IsAssocWordWithOne
IsAssocWordWithOne.description: IsAssocWord is the category of associative words in free semigroups, IsAssocWordWithOne is the category of associative words in free monoids (which admit the operation One (31.10-2) to compute an identity), IsAssocWordWithInverse is the category of associative words in free groups (which have an inverse). See IsWord (36.1-1) for more general categories of words.
IsAssocWordWithOne.title: IsAssocWord

IsAssocWordWithInverse.name: IsAssocWordWithInverse
IsAssocWordWithInverse.description: IsAssocWord is the category of associative words in free semigroups, IsAssocWordWithOne is the category of associative words in free monoids (which admit the operation One (31.10-2) to compute an identity), IsAssocWordWithInverse is the category of associative words in free groups (which have an inverse). See IsWord (36.1-1) for more general categories of words.
IsAssocWordWithInverse.title: IsAssocWord

FreeGroup.name: FreeGroup
FreeGroup.description:
FreeGroup returns a free group. The number of generators, and the labels given to the generators, can be specified in several different ways. Warning: the labels of generators are only an aid for printing, and do not necessarily distinguish generators; see the examples at the end of FreeSemigroup (51.1-10) for more information.

Called with a nonnegative integer rank, FreeGroup returns a free group on rank generators. The optional argument name must be a string; its default value is "f".

If name is not given but the generatorNames option is, then this option is respected as described in Section 50.1-16.
FreeGroup.title: FreeGroup

FreeGroup.description:

IsFreeGroup.name: IsFreeGroup
IsFreeGroup.description:
Any group consisting of elements in IsAssocWordWithInverse (37.1-1) lies in the filter IsFreeGroup; this holds in particular for any group created with FreeGroup (37.2-1), or any subgroup of such a group.

Also see Chapter 47.
IsFreeGroup.title: IsFreeGroup

AssignGeneratorVariables.name: AssignGeneratorVariables
AssignGeneratorVariables.description:
If G is a group, whose generators are represented by symbols (for example a free group, a finitely presented group or a pc group) this function assigns these generators to global variables with the same names.

The aim of this function is to make it easy in interactive use to work with (for example) a free group. It is a shorthand for a sequence of assignments of the form

However, since overwriting global variables can be very dangerous, it is not permitted to use this function within a function. (If –despite this warning– this is done, the result is undefined.)
AssignGeneratorVariables.title: AssignGeneratorVariables

IsShortLexLessThanOrEqual.name: IsShortLexLessThanOrEqual
IsShortLexLessThanOrEqual.description: returns IsLessThanOrEqualUnder(ord, u, v) where ord is the short less ordering for the family of u and v. (This is here for compatibility with GAP 4.2.)
IsShortLexLessThanOrEqual.title: IsShortLexLessThanOrEqual

IsBasicWreathLessThanOrEqual.name: IsBasicWreathLessThanOrEqual
IsBasicWreathLessThanOrEqual.description: returns IsLessThanOrEqualUnder(ord, u, v) where ord is the basic wreath product ordering for the family of u and v. (This is here for compatibility with GAP 4.2.)
IsBasicWreathLessThanOrEqual.title: IsBasicWreathLessThanOrEqual

ExponentSumWord.name: ExponentSumWord
ExponentSumWord.description: For an associative word w and a generator gen, ExponentSumWord returns the number of times gen appears in w minus the number of times its inverse appears in w. If both gen and its inverse do not occur in w then 0 is returned. gen may also be the inverse of a generator.
ExponentSumWord.title: ExponentSumWord

Subword.name: Subword
Subword.description: For an associative word w and two positive integers from and to, Subword returns the subword of w that begins at position from and ends at position to. Indexing is done with origin 1.
Subword.title: Subword

PositionWord.name: PositionWord
PositionWord.description:
Let w and sub be associative words, and from a positive integer. PositionWord returns the position of the first occurrence of sub as a subword of w, starting at position from. If there is no such occurrence, fail is returned. Indexing is done with origin 1.

In other words, PositionWord( w, sub, from ) is the smallest integer i larger than or equal to from such that Subword( w, i, i+Length( sub )-1 ) = sub, see Subword (37.4-3).
PositionWord.title: PositionWord

SubstitutedWord.name: SubstitutedWord
SubstitutedWord.description:
Let w be an associative word.

In the first form, SubstitutedWord returns the associative word obtained by replacing the subword of w that begins at position from and ends at position to by the associative word by. from and to must be positive integers, indexing is done with origin 1. In other words, SubstitutedWord( w, from, to, by ) is the product of the three words Subword( w, 1, from-1 ), by, and Subword( w, to+1, Length( w ) ), see Subword (37.4-3).

In the second form, SubstitutedWord returns the associative word obtained by replacing the first occurrence of the associative word sub of w, starting at position from, by the associative word by; if there is no such occurrence, fail is returned.
SubstitutedWord.title: SubstitutedWord

SubstitutedWord.name: SubstitutedWord
SubstitutedWord.description:
Let w be an associative word.

In the first form, SubstitutedWord returns the associative word obtained by replacing the subword of w that begins at position from and ends at position to by the associative word by. from and to must be positive integers, indexing is done with origin 1. In other words, SubstitutedWord( w, from, to, by ) is the product of the three words Subword( w, 1, from-1 ), by, and Subword( w, to+1, Length( w ) ), see Subword (37.4-3).

In the second form, SubstitutedWord returns the associative word obtained by replacing the first occurrence of the associative word sub of w, starting at position from, by the associative word by; if there is no such occurrence, fail is returned.
SubstitutedWord.title: SubstitutedWord

EliminatedWord.name: EliminatedWord
EliminatedWord.description: For an associative word w, a generator gen, and an associative word by, EliminatedWord returns the associative word obtained by replacing each occurrence of gen in w by by.
EliminatedWord.title: EliminatedWord

NumberSyllables.name: NumberSyllables
NumberSyllables.description: NumberSyllables returns the number of syllables of the associative word w.
NumberSyllables.title: NumberSyllables

ExponentSyllable.name: ExponentSyllable
ExponentSyllable.description: ExponentSyllable returns the exponent of the i-th syllable of the associative word w.
ExponentSyllable.title: ExponentSyllable

GeneratorSyllable.name: GeneratorSyllable
GeneratorSyllable.description: GeneratorSyllable returns the number of the generator that is involved in the i-th syllable of the associative word w.
GeneratorSyllable.title: GeneratorSyllable

SubSyllables.name: SubSyllables
SubSyllables.description: SubSyllables returns the subword of the associative word w that consists of the syllables from positions from to to, where from and to must be positive integers, and indexing is done with origin 1.
SubSyllables.title: SubSyllables

IsLetterAssocWordRep.name: IsLetterAssocWordRep
IsLetterAssocWordRep.description: A word in letter representation stores a list of generator/inverses numbers (as given by LetterRepAssocWord (37.6-8)). Letter access is fast, syllable access is slow for such words.
IsLetterAssocWordRep.title: IsLetterAssocWordRep

IsLetterWordsFamily.name: IsLetterWordsFamily
IsLetterWordsFamily.description:
A letter word family stores words by default in letter form.

Internally, there are letter representations that use integers (4 Byte) to represent a generator and letter representations that use single bytes to represent a character. The latter are more memory efficient, but can only be used if there are less than 128 generators (in which case they are used by default).
IsLetterWordsFamily.title: IsLetterWordsFamily

IsBLetterAssocWordRep.name: IsBLetterAssocWordRep
IsBLetterAssocWordRep.description: these two subrepresentations of IsLetterAssocWordRep (37.6-1) indicate whether the word is stored as a list of bytes (in a string) or as a list of integers).
IsBLetterAssocWordRep.title: IsBLetterAssocWordRep

IsWLetterAssocWordRep.name: IsWLetterAssocWordRep
IsWLetterAssocWordRep.description: these two subrepresentations of IsLetterAssocWordRep (37.6-1) indicate whether the word is stored as a list of bytes (in a string) or as a list of integers).
IsWLetterAssocWordRep.title: IsBLetterAssocWordRep

IsBLetterWordsFamily.name: IsBLetterWordsFamily
IsBLetterWordsFamily.description: These two subcategories of IsLetterWordsFamily (37.6-2) specify the type of letter representation to be used.
IsBLetterWordsFamily.title: IsBLetterWordsFamily

IsWLetterWordsFamily.name: IsWLetterWordsFamily
IsWLetterWordsFamily.description: These two subcategories of IsLetterWordsFamily (37.6-2) specify the type of letter representation to be used.
IsWLetterWordsFamily.title: IsBLetterWordsFamily

IsSyllableAssocWordRep.name: IsSyllableAssocWordRep
IsSyllableAssocWordRep.description: A word in syllable representation stores generator/exponents pairs (as given by ExtRepOfObj (79.8-1). Syllable access is fast, letter access is slow for such words.
IsSyllableAssocWordRep.title: IsSyllableAssocWordRep

IsSyllableWordsFamily.name: IsSyllableWordsFamily
IsSyllableWordsFamily.description: A syllable word family stores words by default in syllable form. There are also different versions of syllable representations, which compress a generator exponent pair in 8, 16 or 32 bits or use a pair of integers. Internal mechanisms try to make this as memory efficient as possible.
IsSyllableWordsFamily.title: IsSyllableWordsFamily

Is16BitsFamily.name: Is16BitsFamily
Is16BitsFamily.description: Regardless of the internal representation used, it is possible to convert a word in a list of numbers in letter or syllable representation and vice versa.
Is16BitsFamily.title: Is16BitsFamily

Is32BitsFamily.name: Is32BitsFamily
Is32BitsFamily.description: Regardless of the internal representation used, it is possible to convert a word in a list of numbers in letter or syllable representation and vice versa.
Is32BitsFamily.title: Is16BitsFamily

IsInfBitsFamily.name: IsInfBitsFamily
IsInfBitsFamily.description: Regardless of the internal representation used, it is possible to convert a word in a list of numbers in letter or syllable representation and vice versa.
IsInfBitsFamily.title: Is16BitsFamily

LetterRepAssocWord.name: LetterRepAssocWord
LetterRepAssocWord.description:
The letter representation of an associated word is as a list of integers, each entry corresponding to a group generator. Inverses of the generators are represented by negative numbers. The generator numbers are as associated to the family.

This operation returns the letter representation of the associative word w.

In the call with two arguments, the generator numbers correspond to the generator order given in the list gens.
LetterRepAssocWord.title: LetterRepAssocWord

AssocWordByLetterRep.name: AssocWordByLetterRep
AssocWordByLetterRep.description:
takes a letter representation lrep (see LetterRepAssocWord (37.6-8)) and returns an associative word in family fam corresponding to this letter representation.

If gens is given, the numbers in the letter representation correspond to gens.

The external representation (see section 37.7) can be used if a syllable representation is needed.
AssocWordByLetterRep.title: AssocWordByLetterRep

IsStraightLineProgram.name: IsStraightLineProgram
IsStraightLineProgram.description: Each straight line program in GAP lies in the category IsStraightLineProgram.
IsStraightLineProgram.title: IsStraightLineProgram

StraightLineProgram.name: StraightLineProgram
StraightLineProgram.description:
In the first form, lines must be a nonempty list of lists that defines a unique straight line program (see IsStraightLineProgram (37.8-1)); in this case StraightLineProgram returns this program, otherwise an error is signalled. The optional argument nrgens specifies the number of input generators of the program; if a line of form 1. (that is, a list of integers) occurs in lines except in the last position, this number is not determined by lines and therefore must be specified by the argument nrgens; if not then StraightLineProgram returns fail.

In the second form, string must be a nonempty string describing an arithmetic expression in terms of the strings in the list gens, where multiplication is denoted by concatenation, powering is denoted by ^, and round brackets (, ) may be used. Each entry in gens must consist only of uppercase or lowercase letters (i.e., letters in IsAlphaChar (27.5-4)) such that no entry is an initial part of another one. Called with this input, StraightLineProgram returns a straight line program that evaluates to the word corresponding to string when called with generators corresponding to gens.

The NC variant does the same, except that the internal consistency of the program is not checked.
StraightLineProgram.title: StraightLineProgram

StraightLineProgram.description:
