LinesOfStraightLineProgram.name: LinesOfStraightLineProgram
LinesOfStraightLineProgram.description: For a straight line program prog, LinesOfStraightLineProgram returns the list of program lines. There is no default method to compute these lines if they are not stored.
LinesOfStraightLineProgram.title: LinesOfStraightLineProgram

NrInputsOfStraightLineProgram.name: NrInputsOfStraightLineProgram
NrInputsOfStraightLineProgram.description:
For a straight line program prog, NrInputsOfStraightLineProgram returns the number of generators that are needed as input.

If a line of form 1. (that is, a list of integers) occurs in the lines of prog except the last line then the number of generators is not determined by the lines, and must be set in the construction of the straight line program (see StraightLineProgram (37.8-2)). So if prog contains a line of form 1. other than the last line and does not store the number of generators then NrInputsOfStraightLineProgram signals an error.
NrInputsOfStraightLineProgram.title: NrInputsOfStraightLineProgram

ResultOfStraightLineProgram.name: ResultOfStraightLineProgram
ResultOfStraightLineProgram.description:
ResultOfStraightLineProgram evaluates the straight line program (see IsStraightLineProgram (37.8-1)) prog at the group elements in the list gens.

The result of a straight line program with lines p_1, p_2, ..., p_k when applied to gens is defined as follows.

First a list r of intermediate results is initialized with a shallow copy of gens.
ResultOfStraightLineProgram.title: ResultOfStraightLineProgram

StringOfResultOfStraightLineProgram.name: StringOfResultOfStraightLineProgram
StringOfResultOfStraightLineProgram.description: StringOfResultOfStraightLineProgram returns a string that describes the result of the straight line program (see IsStraightLineProgram (37.8-1)) prog as word(s) in terms of the strings in the list gensnames. If the result of prog is a single element then the return value of StringOfResultOfStraightLineProgram is a string consisting of the entries of gensnames, opening and closing brackets ( and ), and powering by integers via ^. If the result of prog is a list of elements then the return value of StringOfResultOfStraightLineProgram is a comma separated concatenation of the strings of the single elements, enclosed in square brackets [, ].
StringOfResultOfStraightLineProgram.title: StringOfResultOfStraightLineProgram

CompositionOfStraightLinePrograms.name: CompositionOfStraightLinePrograms
CompositionOfStraightLinePrograms.description:
For two straight line programs prog1 and prog2, CompositionOfStraightLinePrograms returns a straight line program prog with the properties that prog1 and prog have the same number of inputs, and the result of prog when applied to given generators gens equals the result of prog2 when this is applied to the output of prog1 applied to gens.

(Of course the number of outputs of prog1 must be the same as the number of inputs of prog2.)
CompositionOfStraightLinePrograms.title: CompositionOfStraightLinePrograms

IntegratedStraightLineProgram.name: IntegratedStraightLineProgram
IntegratedStraightLineProgram.description: For a nonempty dense list listofprogs of straight line programs p_1, p_2, ..., p_m that have the same number n of inputs (see NrInputsOfStraightLineProgram (37.8-4)), IntegratedStraightLineProgram returns a straight line program prog with n inputs such that for each n-tuple gens of generators, ResultOfStraightLineProgram( prog, gens ) is the concatenation of the lists r_1, r_2, ..., r_m, where r_i is equal to ResultOfStraightLineProgram( p_i, gens ) if this result is a list of elements, and otherwise r_i is equal to the list of length one that contains this result.
IntegratedStraightLineProgram.title: IntegratedStraightLineProgram

RestrictOutputsOfSLP.name: RestrictOutputsOfSLP
RestrictOutputsOfSLP.description: slp must be a straight line program returning a tuple of values. This function returns a new slp that calculates only those outputs specified by k. The argument k may be an integer or a list of integers. If k is an integer, the resulting slp calculates only the result with that number in the original output tuple. If k is a list of integers, the resulting slp calculates those results with indices k in the original output tuple. In both cases the resulting slp does only what is necessary. Obviously, the slp must have a line with enough expressions (lists) for the supplied k as its last line. slp is either an slp or a pair where the first entry are the lines of the slp and the second is the number of inputs.
RestrictOutputsOfSLP.title: RestrictOutputsOfSLP

IntermediateResultOfSLP.name: IntermediateResultOfSLP
IntermediateResultOfSLP.description: Returns a new slp that calculates only the value of slot k at the end of slp doing only what is necessary. slp is either an slp or a pair where the first entry are the lines of the slp and the second is the number of inputs. Note that this assumes a general SLP with possible overwriting. If you know that your SLP does not overwrite slots, please use IntermediateResultOfSLPWithoutOverwrite (37.8-11), which is much faster in this case.
IntermediateResultOfSLP.title: IntermediateResultOfSLP

IntermediateResultOfSLPWithoutOverwrite.name: IntermediateResultOfSLPWithoutOverwrite
IntermediateResultOfSLPWithoutOverwrite.description: Returns a new slp that calculates only the value of slot k, which must be an integer. Note that slp must not overwrite slots but only append!!! Use IntermediateResultOfSLP (37.8-10) in the other case! slp is either an slp or a pair where the first entry is the list of lines of the slp and the second is the number of its inputs.
IntermediateResultOfSLPWithoutOverwrite.title: IntermediateResultOfSLPWithoutOverwrite

IntermediateResultsOfSLPWithoutOverwrite.name: IntermediateResultsOfSLPWithoutOverwrite
IntermediateResultsOfSLPWithoutOverwrite.description: Returns a new slp that calculates only the values of slots contained in the list k. Note that slp must not overwrite slots but only append!!! Use IntermediateResultOfSLP (37.8-10) in the other case! slp is either a slp or a pair where the first entry is the list of lines of the slp and the second is the number of its inputs.
IntermediateResultsOfSLPWithoutOverwrite.title: IntermediateResultsOfSLPWithoutOverwrite

ProductOfStraightLinePrograms.name: ProductOfStraightLinePrograms
ProductOfStraightLinePrograms.description: s1 and s2 must be two slps that return a single element with the same number of inputs. This function constructs an slp that returns the product of the two results the slps s1 and s2 would produce with the same input.
ProductOfStraightLinePrograms.title: ProductOfStraightLinePrograms

SlotUsagePattern.name: SlotUsagePattern
SlotUsagePattern.description: Analyses the straight line program s for more efficient evaluation. This means in particular two things, when this attribute is known: First of all, intermediate results which are not actually needed later on are not computed at all, and once an intermediate result is used for the last time in this SLP, it is discarded. The latter leads to the fact that the evaluation of the SLP needs less memory.
SlotUsagePattern.title: SlotUsagePattern

IsStraightLineProgElm.name: IsStraightLineProgElm
IsStraightLineProgElm.description: A straight line program element is a group element given (for memory reasons) as a straight line program. Straight line program elements are positional objects, the first component is a record with a component seeds, the second component the straight line program.
IsStraightLineProgElm.title: IsStraightLineProgElm

StraightLineProgElm.name: StraightLineProgElm
StraightLineProgElm.description: Creates a straight line program element for seed seed and program prog.
StraightLineProgElm.title: StraightLineProgElm

StraightLineProgGens.name: StraightLineProgGens
StraightLineProgGens.description: returns a set of straight line program elements corresponding to the generators in gens. If gens is a set of permutations then base can be given which must be a base for the group generated by gens. (Such a base will be used to speed up equality tests.)
StraightLineProgGens.title: StraightLineProgGens

EvalStraightLineProgElm.name: EvalStraightLineProgElm
EvalStraightLineProgElm.description: evaluates a straight line program element slpel from its seeds.
EvalStraightLineProgElm.title: EvalStraightLineProgElm

StretchImportantSLPElement.name: StretchImportantSLPElement
StretchImportantSLPElement.description:
If elm is a straight line program element whose straight line representation is very long, this operation changes the representation of elm to a straight line program element, equal to elm, whose seed contains the evaluation of elm and whose straight line program has length 1.

For other objects nothing happens.

This operation permits to designate important elements within an algorithm (elements that will be referred to often), which will be represented by guaranteed short straight line program elements.
StretchImportantSLPElement.title: StretchImportantSLPElement

IsRewritingSystem.name: IsRewritingSystem
IsRewritingSystem.description: This is the category in which all rewriting systems lie.
IsRewritingSystem.title: IsRewritingSystem

Rules.name: Rules
Rules.description: The rules comprising the rewriting system. Note that these may change through the life of the rewriting system, however they will always be a set of defining relations of the algebra described by the rewriting system.
Rules.title: Rules

OrderOfRewritingSystem.name: OrderOfRewritingSystem
OrderOfRewritingSystem.description: return the ordering of the rewriting system rws.
OrderOfRewritingSystem.title: OrderOfRewritingSystem

OrderingOfRewritingSystem.name: OrderingOfRewritingSystem
OrderingOfRewritingSystem.description: return the ordering of the rewriting system rws.
OrderingOfRewritingSystem.title: OrderOfRewritingSystem

ReducedForm.name: ReducedForm
ReducedForm.description: Given an element u in the free (or term) algebra T over which rws is defined, rewrite u by successive applications of the rules of rws until no further rewriting is possible, and return the resulting element of T.
ReducedForm.title: ReducedForm

IsConfluent.name: IsConfluent
IsConfluent.description:
For a rewriting system rws, IsConfluent returns true if and only if rws is confluent. A rewriting system is confluent if, for every two words u and v in the free algebra T which represent the same element of the algebra A defined by rws, ReducedForm( rws, u ) = ReducedForm( rws, v) as words in the free algebra T. This element is the unique normal form of the element represented by u.

For an algebra A with a canonical rewriting system associated with it, IsConfluent checks whether that rewriting system is confluent.

Also see IsConfluent (46.4-7).
IsConfluent.title: IsConfluent

IsConfluent.name: IsConfluent
IsConfluent.description:
For a rewriting system rws, IsConfluent returns true if and only if rws is confluent. A rewriting system is confluent if, for every two words u and v in the free algebra T which represent the same element of the algebra A defined by rws, ReducedForm( rws, u ) = ReducedForm( rws, v) as words in the free algebra T. This element is the unique normal form of the element represented by u.

For an algebra A with a canonical rewriting system associated with it, IsConfluent checks whether that rewriting system is confluent.

Also see IsConfluent (46.4-7).
IsConfluent.title: IsConfluent

IsConfluent.name: IsConfluent
IsConfluent.description: checks whether the pc group G has been built from a collector with a confluent power-commutator presentation.
IsConfluent.title: IsConfluent

ConfluentRws.name: ConfluentRws
ConfluentRws.description: Return a new rewriting system defining the same algebra as rws which is confluent.
ConfluentRws.title: ConfluentRws

IsReduced.name: IsReduced
IsReduced.description: A rewriting system is reduced if for each rule (l, r), l and r are both reduced.
IsReduced.title: IsReduced

ReduceRules.name: ReduceRules
ReduceRules.description: Reduce rules and remove redundant rules to make rws reduced.
ReduceRules.title: ReduceRules

AddRule.name: AddRule
AddRule.description: Add rule to a rewriting system rws.
AddRule.title: AddRule

AddRuleReduced.name: AddRuleReduced
AddRuleReduced.description: Add rule to rewriting system rws. Performs a reduction operation on the resulting system, so that if rws is reduced it will remain reduced.
AddRuleReduced.title: AddRuleReduced

MakeConfluent.name: MakeConfluent
MakeConfluent.description: Add rules (and perhaps reduce) in order to make rws confluent
MakeConfluent.title: MakeConfluent

GeneratorsOfRws.name: GeneratorsOfRws
GeneratorsOfRws.description: Returns the list of generators of the rewriting system rws.
GeneratorsOfRws.title: GeneratorsOfRws

ReducedProduct.name: ReducedProduct
ReducedProduct.description:
The result of ReducedProduct is w where [w] equals [u][v] in A and w is in reduced form.

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedProduct.title: ReducedProduct

ReducedSum.name: ReducedSum
ReducedSum.description:
The result of ReducedProduct is w where [w] equals [u][v] in A and w is in reduced form.

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedSum.title: ReducedProduct

ReducedOne.name: ReducedOne
ReducedOne.description:
The result of ReducedProduct is w where [w] equals [u][v] in A and w is in reduced form.

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedOne.title: ReducedProduct

ReducedAdditiveInverse.description:
The result of ReducedProduct is w where [w] equals [u][v] in A and w is in reduced form.

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedAdditiveInverse.title: ReducedProduct

ReducedComm.description:

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedComm.title: ReducedProduct

ReducedConjugate.description:
The result of ReducedProduct is w where [w] equals [u][v] in A and w is in reduced form.

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedConjugate.title: ReducedProduct

ReducedDifference.description:

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedDifference.title: ReducedProduct

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedInverse.title: ReducedProduct

ReducedLeftQuotient.name: ReducedLeftQuotient
ReducedLeftQuotient.description:
The result of ReducedProduct is w where [w] equals [u][v] in A and w is in reduced form.

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedLeftQuotient.title: ReducedProduct

ReducedPower.description:

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedPower.title: ReducedProduct

ReducedQuotient.name: ReducedQuotient
ReducedQuotient.description:
The result of ReducedProduct is w where [w] equals [u][v] in A and w is in reduced form.

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedQuotient.title: ReducedProduct

ReducedScalarProduct.name: ReducedScalarProduct
ReducedScalarProduct.description:
The result of ReducedProduct is w where [w] equals [u][v] in A and w is in reduced form.

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedScalarProduct.title: ReducedProduct

ReducedZero.name: ReducedZero
ReducedZero.description:
The result of ReducedProduct is w where [w] equals [u][v] in A and w is in reduced form.

The remaining operations are defined similarly when they are defined (as determined by the signature of the term algebra).
ReducedZero.title: ReducedProduct

IsBuiltFromAdditiveMagmaWithInverses.name: IsBuiltFromAdditiveMagmaWithInverses
IsBuiltFromAdditiveMagmaWithInverses.description: These properties may be used to identify the type of term algebra over which the rewriting system is defined.
IsBuiltFromAdditiveMagmaWithInverses.title: IsBuiltFromAdditiveMagmaWithInverses

IsBuiltFromMagma.name: IsBuiltFromMagma
IsBuiltFromMagma.description: These properties may be used to identify the type of term algebra over which the rewriting system is defined.
IsBuiltFromMagma.title: IsBuiltFromAdditiveMagmaWithInverses

IsBuiltFromMagmaWithOne.name: IsBuiltFromMagmaWithOne
IsBuiltFromMagmaWithOne.description: These properties may be used to identify the type of term algebra over which the rewriting system is defined.
IsBuiltFromMagmaWithOne.title: IsBuiltFromAdditiveMagmaWithInverses

IsBuiltFromMagmaWithInverses.name: IsBuiltFromMagmaWithInverses
IsBuiltFromMagmaWithInverses.description: These properties may be used to identify the type of term algebra over which the rewriting system is defined.
IsBuiltFromMagmaWithInverses.title: IsBuiltFromAdditiveMagmaWithInverses

IsBuiltFromSemigroup.name: IsBuiltFromSemigroup
IsBuiltFromSemigroup.description: These properties may be used to identify the type of term algebra over which the rewriting system is defined.
IsBuiltFromSemigroup.title: IsBuiltFromAdditiveMagmaWithInverses

IsBuiltFromGroup.name: IsBuiltFromGroup
IsBuiltFromGroup.description: These properties may be used to identify the type of term algebra over which the rewriting system is defined.
IsBuiltFromGroup.title: IsBuiltFromAdditiveMagmaWithInverses

Group.name: Group
Group.description:
Group( gen, ... ) is the group generated by the arguments gen, ...

If the only argument gens is a list that is not a matrix then Group( gens ) is the group generated by the elements of that list.

If there are two arguments, a list gens and an element id, then Group( gens, id ) is the group generated by the elements of gens, with identity id.
Group.title: Group

Group.name: Group
Group.description:
Group( gen, ... ) is the group generated by the arguments gen, ...

If the only argument gens is a list that is not a matrix then Group( gens ) is the group generated by the elements of that list.

If there are two arguments, a list gens and an element id, then Group( gens, id ) is the group generated by the elements of gens, with identity id.
Group.title: Group

GroupByGenerators.name: GroupByGenerators
GroupByGenerators.description:
GroupByGenerators returns the group G generated by the list gens. If a second argument id is present then this is stored as the identity element of the group.

The value of the attribute GeneratorsOfGroup (39.2-4) of G need not be equal to gens. GroupByGenerators is the underlying operation called by Group (39.2-1).
GroupByGenerators.title: GroupByGenerators

GroupByGenerators.name: GroupByGenerators
GroupByGenerators.description:
GroupByGenerators returns the group G generated by the list gens. If a second argument id is present then this is stored as the identity element of the group.

The value of the attribute GeneratorsOfGroup (39.2-4) of G need not be equal to gens. GroupByGenerators is the underlying operation called by Group (39.2-1).
GroupByGenerators.title: GroupByGenerators

GroupWithGenerators.name: GroupWithGenerators
GroupWithGenerators.description: GroupWithGenerators returns the group G generated by the list gens. If a second argument id is present then this is stored as the identity element of the group. The value of the attribute GeneratorsOfGroup (39.2-4) of G is equal to gens.
GroupWithGenerators.title: GroupWithGenerators

GeneratorsOfGroup.name: GeneratorsOfGroup
GeneratorsOfGroup.description:
returns a list of generators of the group G. If G has been created by the command GroupWithGenerators (39.2-3) with argument gens, then the list returned by GeneratorsOfGroup will be equal to gens. For such a group, each generator can also be accessed using the . operator (see GeneratorsOfDomain (31.9-2)): for a positive integer i, G.i returns the i-th element of the list returned by GeneratorsOfGroup. Moreover, if G is a free group, and name is the name of a generator of G then G.name also returns this generator.

While in this example GAP displays the group via the generating set stored in the attribute GeneratorsOfGroup, the methods installed for View (6.3-3) will in general display only some information about the group which may even be just the fact that it is a group.
GeneratorsOfGroup.title: GeneratorsOfGroup

AsGroup.name: AsGroup
AsGroup.description: if the elements of the collection D form a group the command returns this group, otherwise it returns fail.
AsGroup.title: AsGroup

ConjugateGroup.name: ConjugateGroup
ConjugateGroup.description:
returns the conjugate group of G, obtained by applying the conjugating element obj.

To form a conjugate (group) by any object acting via ^, one can also use the infix operator ^.
ConjugateGroup.title: ConjugateGroup

IsGroup.name: IsGroup
IsGroup.description:
A group is a magma-with-inverses (see IsMagmaWithInverses (35.1-4)) and associative (see IsAssociative (35.4-7)) multiplication.

IsGroup tests whether the object obj fulfills these conditions, it does not test whether obj is a set of elements that forms a group under multiplication; use AsGroup (39.2-5) if you want to perform such a test. (See 13.3 for details about categories.)
IsGroup.title: IsGroup

InfoGroup.name: InfoGroup
InfoGroup.description: is the info class for the generic group theoretic functions (see 7.4).
InfoGroup.title: InfoGroup

Subgroup.name: Subgroup
Subgroup.description:
creates the subgroup U of G generated by gens. The Parent (31.7-1) value of U will be G. The NC version does not check, whether the elements in gens actually lie in G.

The unary version of Subgroup creates a (shell) subgroup that does not even know generators but can be used to collect information about a particular subgroup over time.
Subgroup.title: Subgroup

Subgroup.name: Subgroup
Subgroup.description:
creates the subgroup U of G generated by gens. The Parent (31.7-1) value of U will be G. The NC version does not check, whether the elements in gens actually lie in G.

The unary version of Subgroup creates a (shell) subgroup that does not even know generators but can be used to collect information about a particular subgroup over time.
Subgroup.title: Subgroup

SubgroupNC.name: SubgroupNC
SubgroupNC.description:
creates the subgroup U of G generated by gens. The Parent (31.7-1) value of U will be G. The NC version does not check, whether the elements in gens actually lie in G.

The unary version of Subgroup creates a (shell) subgroup that does not even know generators but can be used to collect information about a particular subgroup over time.
SubgroupNC.title: Subgroup

Index.name: Index
Index.description: For a subgroup U of the group G, Index returns the index [G:U] = |G| / |U| of U in G. The NC version does not test whether U is contained in G.
Index.title: Index (GAP operation)

Index.name: Index
Index.description: For two character tables tbl and subtbl, Index returns the quotient of the Size (71.8-5) values of tbl and subtbl. The containment of the underlying groups of subtbl and tbl is not checked; so the distinction between Index (39.3-2) and IndexNC (39.3-2) is not made for character tables.
Index.title: Index

IndexNC.name: IndexNC
IndexNC.description: For a subgroup U of the group G, Index returns the index [G:U] = |G| / |U| of U in G. The NC version does not test whether U is contained in G.
IndexNC.title: Index (GAP operation)

IndexInWholeGroup.name: IndexInWholeGroup
IndexInWholeGroup.description: If the family of elements of G itself forms a group P, this attribute returns the index of G in P. It is used primarily for free groups or finitely presented groups.
IndexInWholeGroup.title: IndexInWholeGroup

AsSubgroup.name: AsSubgroup
AsSubgroup.description: creates a subgroup of G which contains the same elements as U
AsSubgroup.title: AsSubgroup

IsSubgroup.name: IsSubgroup
IsSubgroup.description: IsSubgroup returns true if U is a group that is a subset of the domain G. This is actually checked by calling IsGroup( U ) and IsSubset( G, U ); note that special methods for IsSubset (30.5-1) are available that test only generators of U if G is closed under the group operations. So in most cases, for example whenever one knows already that U is a group, it is better to call only IsSubset (30.5-1).
IsSubgroup.title: IsSubgroup

IsNormal.name: IsNormal
IsNormal.description:
returns true if the group G normalizes the group U and false otherwise.

A group G normalizes a group U if and only if for every g ∈ G and u ∈ U the element u^g is a member of U. Note that U need not be a subgroup of G.
IsNormal.title: IsNormal

IsCharacteristicSubgroup.name: IsCharacteristicSubgroup
IsCharacteristicSubgroup.description: tests whether N is invariant under all automorphisms of G.
IsCharacteristicSubgroup.title: IsCharacteristicSubgroup

ConjugateSubgroup.name: ConjugateSubgroup
ConjugateSubgroup.description:
For a group G which has a parent group P (see Parent (31.7-1)), returns the subgroup of P, obtained by conjugating G using the conjugating element g.

If G has no parent group, it just delegates to the call to ConjugateGroup (39.2-6) with the same arguments.

To form a conjugate (subgroup) by any object acting via ^, one can also use the infix operator ^.
ConjugateSubgroup.title: ConjugateSubgroup

ConjugateSubgroups.name: ConjugateSubgroups
ConjugateSubgroups.description: returns a list of all images of the group U under conjugation action by G.
ConjugateSubgroups.title: ConjugateSubgroups

IsSubnormal.name: IsSubnormal
IsSubnormal.description: A subgroup U of the group G is subnormal if it is contained in a subnormal series of G.
IsSubnormal.title: IsSubnormal

SubgroupByProperty.name: SubgroupByProperty
SubgroupByProperty.description:
creates a subgroup of G consisting of those elements fulfilling prop (which is a tester function). No test is done whether the property actually defines a subgroup.

Note that currently very little functionality beyond an element test exists for groups created this way.
SubgroupByProperty.title: SubgroupByProperty

SubgroupShell.name: SubgroupShell
SubgroupShell.description: creates a subgroup of G which at this point is not yet specified further (but will be later, for example by assigning a generating set).
SubgroupShell.title: SubgroupShell

ClosureGroup.name: ClosureGroup
ClosureGroup.description: creates the group generated by the elements of G and obj. obj can be either an element or a collection of elements, in particular another group.
ClosureGroup.title: ClosureGroup

ClosureGroupAddElm.name: ClosureGroupAddElm
ClosureGroupAddElm.description: These three functions together with ClosureGroupDefault (39.4-3) implement the main methods for ClosureGroup (39.4-1). In the ordering given, they just add elm to the generators, remove duplicates and identity elements, and test whether elm is already contained in G.
ClosureGroupAddElm.title: ClosureGroupAddElm

ClosureGroupCompare.name: ClosureGroupCompare
ClosureGroupCompare.description: These three functions together with ClosureGroupDefault (39.4-3) implement the main methods for ClosureGroup (39.4-1). In the ordering given, they just add elm to the generators, remove duplicates and identity elements, and test whether elm is already contained in G.
ClosureGroupCompare.title: ClosureGroupAddElm

ClosureGroupIntest.name: ClosureGroupIntest
ClosureGroupIntest.description: These three functions together with ClosureGroupDefault (39.4-3) implement the main methods for ClosureGroup (39.4-1). In the ordering given, they just add elm to the generators, remove duplicates and identity elements, and test whether elm is already contained in G.
ClosureGroupIntest.title: ClosureGroupAddElm

ClosureGroupDefault.name: ClosureGroupDefault
ClosureGroupDefault.description: This functions returns the closure of the group G with the element elm. If G has the attribute AsSSortedList (30.3-10) then also the result has this attribute. This is used to implement the default method for Enumerator (30.3-2) and EnumeratorSorted (30.3-3).
ClosureGroupDefault.title: ClosureGroupDefault

ClosureSubgroup.name: ClosureSubgroup
ClosureSubgroup.description: For a group G that stores a parent group (see 31.7), ClosureSubgroup calls ClosureGroup (39.4-1) with the same arguments; if the result is a subgroup of the parent of G then the parent of G is set as parent of the result, otherwise an error is raised. The check whether the result is contained in the parent of G is omitted by the NC version. As a wrong parent might imply wrong properties this version should be used with care.
ClosureSubgroup.title: ClosureSubgroup

ClosureSubgroupNC.name: ClosureSubgroupNC
ClosureSubgroupNC.description: For a group G that stores a parent group (see 31.7), ClosureSubgroup calls ClosureGroup (39.4-1) with the same arguments; if the result is a subgroup of the parent of G then the parent of G is set as parent of the result, otherwise an error is raised. The check whether the result is contained in the parent of G is omitted by the NC version. As a wrong parent might imply wrong properties this version should be used with care.
ClosureSubgroupNC.title: ClosureSubgroup

EpimorphismFromFreeGroup.name: EpimorphismFromFreeGroup
EpimorphismFromFreeGroup.description:
For a group G with a known generating set, this attribute returns a homomorphism from a free group that maps the free generators to the generators of G.

The option names can be used to prescribe a (print) name for the free generators.

The following example shows how to decompose elements of S_4 in the generators (1,2,3,4) and (1,2):
EpimorphismFromFreeGroup.title: EpimorphismFromFreeGroup

Factorization.name: Factorization
Factorization.description:
returns a factorization of elm as word in the generators of the group G given in the attribute GeneratorsOfGroup (39.2-4). The attribute EpimorphismFromFreeGroup (39.5-1) of G will contain a map from the free group in which the word is expressed to the group G. The attribute MappingGeneratorsImages (40.10-2) of this map gives a list of generators and corresponding letters.

The algorithm used forms all elements of the group to ensure a short word is found. Therefore this function should not be used when the group G has more than a few million elements. Because of this, one should not call this function within algorithms, but use homomorphisms instead.
Factorization.title: Factorization

GrowthFunctionOfGroup.name: GrowthFunctionOfGroup
GrowthFunctionOfGroup.description: For a group G with a generating set given in GeneratorsOfGroup (39.2-4), this function calculates the number of elements whose shortest expression as words in the generating set is of a particular length. It returns a list L, whose i+1 entry counts the number of elements whose shortest word expression has length i. If a maximal length radius is given, only words up to length radius are counted. Otherwise the group must be finite and all elements are enumerated.
GrowthFunctionOfGroup.title: GrowthFunctionOfGroup

GrowthFunctionOfGroup.name: GrowthFunctionOfGroup
GrowthFunctionOfGroup.description: For a group G with a generating set given in GeneratorsOfGroup (39.2-4), this function calculates the number of elements whose shortest expression as words in the generating set is of a particular length. It returns a list L, whose i+1 entry counts the number of elements whose shortest word expression has length i. If a maximal length radius is given, only words up to length radius are counted. Otherwise the group must be finite and all elements are enumerated.
GrowthFunctionOfGroup.title: GrowthFunctionOfGroup

StructureDescription.name: StructureDescription
StructureDescription.description:
The method for StructureDescription exhibits a structure of the given group G to some extent, using the strategy outlined below. The idea is to return a possibly short string which gives some insight in the structure of the considered group. It is intended primarily for small groups (order less than 100) or groups with few normal subgroups, in other cases, in particular large p-groups, it can be very costly. Furthermore, the string returned is -- as the action on chief factors is not described -- often not the most useful way to describe a group.

The string returned by StructureDescription is not an isomorphism invariant: non-isomorphic groups can have the same string value, and two isomorphic groups in different representations can produce different strings. The value returned by StructureDescription is a string of the following form:

Note that the StructureDescription is only one possible way of building up the given group from smaller pieces.
StructureDescription.title: StructureDescription

RightCoset.name: RightCoset
RightCoset.description:
returns the right coset of U with representative g, which is the set of all elements of the form ug for all u ∈ U. g must be an element of a larger group G which contains U. For element operations such as in a right coset behaves like a set of group elements.

Right cosets are external orbits for the action of U which acts via OnLeftInverse (41.2-3). Of course the action of a larger group G on right cosets is via OnRight (41.2-2).
RightCoset.title: RightCoset

RightCosets.name: RightCosets
RightCosets.description: computes a duplicate free list of right cosets U g for g ∈ G. A set of representatives for the elements in this list forms a right transversal of U in G. (By inverting the representatives one obtains a list of representatives of the left cosets of U.) The NC version does not check whether U is a subgroup of G.
RightCosets.title: RightCosets

RightCosetsNC.name: RightCosetsNC
RightCosetsNC.description: computes a duplicate free list of right cosets U g for g ∈ G. A set of representatives for the elements in this list forms a right transversal of U in G. (By inverting the representatives one obtains a list of representatives of the left cosets of U.) The NC version does not check whether U is a subgroup of G.
RightCosetsNC.title: RightCosets

CanonicalRightCosetElement.name: CanonicalRightCosetElement
CanonicalRightCosetElement.description:
returns a canonical representative of the right coset U g which is independent of the given representative g. This can be used to compare cosets by comparing their canonical representatives.

The representative chosen to be the canonical one is representation dependent and only guaranteed to remain the same within one GAP session.
CanonicalRightCosetElement.title: CanonicalRightCosetElement

IsRightCoset.name: IsRightCoset
IsRightCoset.description:
The category of right cosets.

GAP does not provide left cosets as a separate data type, but as the left coset gU consists of exactly the inverses of the elements of the right coset Ug^{-1} calculations with left cosets can be emulated using right cosets by inverting the representatives.
IsRightCoset.title: IsRightCoset

IsBiCoset.name: IsBiCoset
IsBiCoset.description: A (right) coset Ug is considered a bicoset if its set of elements simultaneously forms a left coset for the same subgroup. This is the case if and only if the coset representative g normalizes the subgroup U.
IsBiCoset.title: IsBiCoset

CosetDecomposition.name: CosetDecomposition
CosetDecomposition.description: For a finite group G and a subgroup S ≤ G this function returns a partition of the elements of G according to the (right) cosets of S. The result is a list of lists, each sublist corresponding to one coset. The first sublist is the elements list of the subgroup, the other lists are arranged accordingly.
CosetDecomposition.title: CosetDecomposition

RightTransversal.name: RightTransversal
RightTransversal.description:
A right transversal t is a list of representatives for the set U ∖ G of right cosets (consisting of cosets Ug) of U in G.

The object returned by RightTransversal is not a plain list, but an object that behaves like an immutable list of length [G:U], except if U is the trivial subgroup of G in which case RightTransversal may return the sorted plain list of coset representatives.

The operation PositionCanonical (21.16-3), called for a transversal t and an element g of G, will return the position of the representative in t that lies in the same coset of U as the element g does. (In comparison, Position (21.16-1) will return fail if the element is not equal to the representative.) Functions that implement group actions such as Action (41.7-2) or Permutation (41.9-1) (see Chapter 41) use PositionCanonical (21.16-3), therefore it is possible to act on a right transversal to implement the action on the cosets. This is often much more efficient than acting on cosets.
RightTransversal.title: RightTransversal

DoubleCoset.name: DoubleCoset
DoubleCoset.description: The groups U and V must be subgroups of a common supergroup G of which g is an element. This command constructs the double coset U g V which is the set of all elements of the form ugv for any u ∈ U, v ∈ V. For element operations such as in, a double coset behaves like a set of group elements. The double coset stores U in the attribute LeftActingGroup, g as Representative (30.4-7), and V as RightActingGroup.
DoubleCoset.title: DoubleCoset

RepresentativesContainedRightCosets.name: RepresentativesContainedRightCosets
RepresentativesContainedRightCosets.description: A double coset D = U g V can be considered as a union of right cosets U h_i. (It is the union of the orbit of U g under right multiplication by V.) For a double coset D this function returns a set of representatives h_i such that D = ⋃_{h_i} U h_i. The representatives returned are canonical for U (see CanonicalRightCosetElement (39.7-3)) and form a set.
RepresentativesContainedRightCosets.title: RepresentativesContainedRightCosets

DoubleCosets.name: DoubleCosets
DoubleCosets.description: computes a duplicate free list of all double cosets U g V for g ∈ G. The groups U and V must be subgroups of the group G. The NC version does not check whether U and V are subgroups of G.
DoubleCosets.title: DoubleCosets

DoubleCosetsNC.name: DoubleCosetsNC
DoubleCosetsNC.description: computes a duplicate free list of all double cosets U g V for g ∈ G. The groups U and V must be subgroups of the group G. The NC version does not check whether U and V are subgroups of G.
DoubleCosetsNC.title: DoubleCosets

DoubleCosetRepsAndSizes.name: DoubleCosetRepsAndSizes
DoubleCosetRepsAndSizes.description: returns a list of double coset representatives and their sizes, the entries are lists of the form [ r, n ] where r and n are an element of the double coset and the size of the coset, respectively. This operation is faster than DoubleCosetsNC (39.9-3) because no double coset objects have to be created.
DoubleCosetRepsAndSizes.title: DoubleCosetRepsAndSizes

InfoCoset.name: InfoCoset
InfoCoset.description: The information function for coset and double coset operations is InfoCoset.
InfoCoset.title: InfoCoset

ConjugacyClass.name: ConjugacyClass
ConjugacyClass.description:
creates the conjugacy class in G with representative g. This class is an external set, so functions such as Representative (30.4-7) (which returns g), ActingDomain (41.12-3) (which returns G), StabilizerOfExternalSet (41.12-10) (which returns the centralizer of g) and AsList (30.3-8) work for it.

A conjugacy class is an external orbit (see ExternalOrbit (41.12-9)) of group elements with the group acting by conjugation on it. Thus element tests or operation representatives can be computed. The attribute Centralizer (35.4-4) gives the centralizer of the representative (which is the same result as StabilizerOfExternalSet (41.12-10)). (This is a slight abuse of notation: This is not the centralizer of the class as a set which would be the standard behaviour of Centralizer (35.4-4).)
ConjugacyClass.title: ConjugacyClass

ConjugacyClasses.name: ConjugacyClasses
ConjugacyClasses.description:
returns the conjugacy classes of elements of G as a list of class objects of G (see ConjugacyClass (39.10-1) for details). It is guaranteed that the class of the identity is in the first position, the further arrangement depends on the method chosen (and might be different for equal but not identical groups).

For very small groups (of size up to 500) the classes will be computed by the conjugation action of G on itself (see ConjugacyClassesByOrbits (39.10-4)). This can be deliberately switched off using the noaction option shown below.

For solvable groups, the default method to compute the classes is by homomorphic lift (see section 45.17).
ConjugacyClasses.title: ConjugacyClasses

ConjugacyClasses.name: ConjugacyClasses
ConjugacyClasses.description:
For a character table tbl with known underlying group G, the ConjugacyClasses value of tbl is a list of conjugacy classes of G. All those lists stored in the table that are related to the ordering of conjugacy classes (such as sizes of centralizers and conjugacy classes, orders of representatives, power maps, and all class functions) refer to the ordering of this list.

This ordering need not coincide with the ordering of conjugacy classes as stored in the underlying group of the table (see 71.21). One reason for this is that otherwise we would not be allowed to use a library table as the character table of a group for which the conjugacy classes are stored already. (Another, less important reason is that we can use the same group as underlying group of character tables that differ only w.r.t. the ordering of classes.)

The class of the identity element must be the first class (see 71.5).
ConjugacyClasses.title: ConjugacyClasses

ConjugacyClassesByRandomSearch.name: ConjugacyClassesByRandomSearch
ConjugacyClassesByRandomSearch.description:
computes the classes of the group G by random search. This works very efficiently for almost simple groups.

This function is also accessible via the option random to the function ConjugacyClass (39.10-1).
ConjugacyClassesByRandomSearch.title: ConjugacyClassesByRandomSearch

ConjugacyClassesByOrbits.name: ConjugacyClassesByOrbits
ConjugacyClassesByOrbits.description:
computes the classes of the group G as orbits of G on its elements. This can be quick but unsurprisingly may also take a lot of memory if G becomes larger. All the classes will store their element list and thus a membership test will be quick as well.

This function is also accessible via the option action to the function ConjugacyClass (39.10-1).

Typically, for small groups (roughly of order up to 10^3) the computation of classes as orbits under the action is fastest; memory restrictions (and the increasing cost of eliminating duplicates) make this less efficient for larger groups.
ConjugacyClassesByOrbits.title: ConjugacyClassesByOrbits

NrConjugacyClasses.name: NrConjugacyClasses
NrConjugacyClasses.description: returns the number of conjugacy classes of G.
NrConjugacyClasses.title: NrConjugacyClasses

NrConjugacyClasses.name: NrConjugacyClasses
NrConjugacyClasses.description: These operations for groups are applicable to character tables and mean the same for a character table as for its underlying group; see Chapter 39 for the definitions. The operations are mainly useful for selecting character tables with certain properties, also for character tables without access to a group.
NrConjugacyClasses.title: Group Operations Applicable to Character Tables

RationalClass.name: RationalClass
RationalClass.description: creates the rational class in G with representative g. A rational class consists of all elements that are conjugate to g or to an i-th power of g where i is coprime to the order of g. Thus a rational class can be interpreted as a conjugacy class of cyclic subgroups. A rational class is an external set (IsExternalSet (41.12-1)) of group elements with the group acting by conjugation on it, but not an external orbit.
RationalClass.title: RationalClass

RationalClasses.name: RationalClasses
RationalClasses.description: returns a list of the rational classes of the group G. (See RationalClass (39.10-6).)
RationalClasses.title: RationalClasses

GaloisGroup.name: GaloisGroup
GaloisGroup.description: Suppose that ratcl is a rational class of a group G with representative g. The exponents i for which g^i lies already in the ordinary conjugacy class of g, form a subgroup of the prime residue class group P_n (see PrimitiveRootMod (15.3-4)), the so-called Galois group of the rational class. The prime residue class group P_n is obtained in GAP as Units( Integers mod n ), the unit group of a residue class ring. The Galois group of a rational class ratcl is stored in the attribute GaloisGroup as a subgroup of this group.
GaloisGroup.title: GaloisGroup

GaloisGroup.name: GaloisGroup
GaloisGroup.description:
The Galois group of a field F is the group of all field automorphisms of F that fix the subfield K =LeftActingDomain( F ) pointwise.

Note that the field extension F > K need not be a Galois extension.
GaloisGroup.title: GaloisGroup

GaloisGroup.name: GaloisGroup
GaloisGroup.description:
The Galois group Gal( ℚ_n, ℚ ) of the field extension ℚ_n / ℚ is isomorphic to the group (ℤ / n ℤ)^* of prime residues modulo n, via the isomorphism (ℤ / n ℤ)^* → Gal( ℚ_n, ℚ ) that is defined by k + n ℤ ↦ ( z ↦ z^*k ).

The Galois group of the field extension ℚ_n / L with any abelian number field L ⊆ ℚ_n is simply the factor group of Gal( ℚ_n, ℚ ) modulo the stabilizer of L, and the Galois group of L / L', with L' an abelian number field contained in L, is the subgroup in this group that stabilizes L'. These groups are easily described in terms of (ℤ / n ℤ)^*. Generators of (ℤ / n ℤ)^* can be computed using GeneratorsPrimeResidues (15.2-4).

In GAP, a field extension L / L' is given by the field object L with LeftActingDomain (57.1-11) value L' (see 60.3).
GaloisGroup.title: GaloisGroup

IsConjugate.name: IsConjugate
IsConjugate.description:
tests whether the elements x and y or the subgroups U and V are conjugate under the action of G. (They do not need to be contained in G.) This command is only a shortcut to RepresentativeAction (41.6-1).

RepresentativeAction (41.6-1) can be used to obtain conjugating elements.
IsConjugate.title: IsConjugate

IsConjugate.name: IsConjugate
IsConjugate.description:
tests whether the elements x and y or the subgroups U and V are conjugate under the action of G. (They do not need to be contained in G.) This command is only a shortcut to RepresentativeAction (41.6-1).

RepresentativeAction (41.6-1) can be used to obtain conjugating elements.
IsConjugate.title: IsConjugate

NthRootsInGroup.name: NthRootsInGroup
NthRootsInGroup.description: Let e be an element in the group G. This function returns a list of all those elements in G whose n-th power is e.
NthRootsInGroup.title: NthRootsInGroup

Normalizer.name: Normalizer
Normalizer.description:
For two groups G, U, Normalizer computes the normalizer N_G(U), that is, the stabilizer of U under the conjugation action of G.

For a group G and a group element g, Normalizer computes N_G(⟨ g ⟩).
Normalizer.title: Normalizer

Normalizer.name: Normalizer
Normalizer.description:
For two groups G, U, Normalizer computes the normalizer N_G(U), that is, the stabilizer of U under the conjugation action of G.

For a group G and a group element g, Normalizer computes N_G(⟨ g ⟩).
Normalizer.title: Normalizer

Core.name: Core
Core.description: If S and U are groups of elements in the same family, this operation returns the core of U in S, that is the intersection of all S-conjugates of U.
Core.title: Core

PCore.name: PCore
PCore.description: The p-core of G is the largest normal p-subgroup of G. It is the core of a Sylow p-subgroup of G, see Core (39.11-2).
PCore.title: PCore

NormalClosure.name: NormalClosure
NormalClosure.description:
The normal closure of U in G is the smallest normal subgroup of the closure of G and U which contains U.

The second argument may also be a list of group elements, in which case the normal closure of the group generated by these elements is computed.
NormalClosure.title: NormalClosure

NormalClosure.name: NormalClosure
NormalClosure.description:
The normal closure of U in G is the smallest normal subgroup of the closure of G and U which contains U.

The second argument may also be a list of group elements, in which case the normal closure of the group generated by these elements is computed.
NormalClosure.title: NormalClosure

NormalIntersection.name: NormalIntersection
NormalIntersection.description: computes the intersection of G and U, assuming that G is normalized by U. This works faster than Intersection, but will not produce the intersection if G is not normalized by U.
NormalIntersection.title: NormalIntersection

ComplementClassesRepresentatives.name: ComplementClassesRepresentatives
ComplementClassesRepresentatives.description:
Let N be a normal subgroup of G. This command returns a set of representatives for the conjugacy classes of complements of N in G. Complements are subgroups of G which intersect trivially with N and together with N generate G.

At the moment methods are available only for the case that N or G/N is solvable.
ComplementClassesRepresentatives.title: ComplementClassesRepresentatives

InfoComplement.name: InfoComplement
InfoComplement.description: Info class for the complement routines.
InfoComplement.title: InfoComplement

CommutatorSubgroup.name: CommutatorSubgroup
CommutatorSubgroup.description: If G and H are two groups of elements in the same family, this operation returns the group generated by all commutators [ g, h ] = g^{-1} h^{-1} g h (see Comm (31.12-3)) of elements g ∈ G and h ∈ H, that is the group ⟨ [ g, h ] ∣ g ∈ G, h ∈ H ⟩.
CommutatorSubgroup.title: CommutatorSubgroup

DerivedSubgroup.name: DerivedSubgroup
DerivedSubgroup.description: The derived subgroup G' of G is the subgroup generated by all commutators of pairs of elements of G. It is normal in G and the factor group G/G' is the largest abelian factor group of G.
DerivedSubgroup.title: DerivedSubgroup

CommutatorLength.name: CommutatorLength
CommutatorLength.description: returns the minimal number n such that each element in the derived subgroup (see DerivedSubgroup (39.12-3)) of the group G can be written as a product of (at most) n commutators of elements in G.
CommutatorLength.title: CommutatorLength

CommutatorLength.name: CommutatorLength
CommutatorLength.description: These operations for groups are applicable to character tables and mean the same for a character table as for its underlying group; see Chapter 39 for the definitions. The operations are mainly useful for selecting character tables with certain properties, also for character tables without access to a group.
CommutatorLength.title: Group Operations Applicable to Character Tables

FittingSubgroup.name: FittingSubgroup
FittingSubgroup.description: The Fitting subgroup of a group G is its largest nilpotent normal subgroup.
FittingSubgroup.title: FittingSubgroup

FrattiniSubgroup.name: FrattiniSubgroup
FrattiniSubgroup.description: The Frattini subgroup of a group G is the intersection of all maximal subgroups of G.
FrattiniSubgroup.title: FrattiniSubgroup

PrefrattiniSubgroup.name: PrefrattiniSubgroup
PrefrattiniSubgroup.description:
returns a Prefrattini subgroup of the finite solvable group G.

A factor M/N of G is called a Frattini factor if M/N is contained in the Frattini subgroup of G/N. A subgroup P is a Prefrattini subgroup of G if P covers each Frattini chief factor of G, and if for each maximal subgroup of G there exists a conjugate maximal subgroup, which contains P. In a finite solvable group G the Prefrattini subgroups form a characteristic conjugacy class of subgroups and the intersection of all these subgroups is the Frattini subgroup of G.
PrefrattiniSubgroup.title: PrefrattiniSubgroup

PerfectResiduum.name: PerfectResiduum
PerfectResiduum.description: is the smallest normal subgroup of G that has a solvable factor group.
PerfectResiduum.title: PerfectResiduum

SolvableRadical.name: SolvableRadical
SolvableRadical.description: is the solvable radical of the group G, i.e., the largest solvable normal subgroup of G.
SolvableRadical.title: SolvableRadical

Socle.name: Socle
Socle.description: The socle of the group G is the subgroup generated by all minimal normal subgroups.
Socle.title: Socle

SupersolvableResiduum.name: SupersolvableResiduum
SupersolvableResiduum.description: is the supersolvable residuum of the group G, that is, its smallest normal subgroup N such that the factor group G / N is supersolvable.
SupersolvableResiduum.title: SupersolvableResiduum

PRump.name: PRump
PRump.description: For a prime p, the p-rump of a group G is the subgroup G' G^p. Unless it equals G itself (which is the e.g. the case if G is perfect), it is equal to the second term of the p-central series of G, see PCentralSeries (39.17-13).
PRump.title: PRump

SylowSubgroup.name: SylowSubgroup
SylowSubgroup.description: returns a Sylow p-subgroup of the finite group G. This is a p-subgroup of G whose index in G is coprime to p. SylowSubgroup computes Sylow subgroups via the operation SylowSubgroupOp.
SylowSubgroup.title: SylowSubgroup

SylowComplement.name: SylowComplement
SylowComplement.description:
returns a Sylow p-complement of the finite group G. This is a subgroup U of order coprime to p such that the index [G:U] is a p-power.

At the moment methods exist only if G is solvable and GAP will issue an error if G is not solvable.
SylowComplement.title: SylowComplement

HallSubgroup.name: HallSubgroup
HallSubgroup.description:
computes a P-Hall subgroup for a set P of primes. This is a subgroup the order of which is only divisible by primes in P and whose index is coprime to all primes in P. Such a subgroup is unique up to conjugacy if G is solvable. The function computes Hall subgroups via the operation HallSubgroupOp.

If G is solvable this function always returns a subgroup. If G is not solvable this function might return a subgroup (if it is unique up to conjugacy), a list of subgroups (which are representatives of the conjugacy classes in case there are several such classes) or fail if no such subgroup exists.
HallSubgroup.title: HallSubgroup

SylowSystem.name: SylowSystem
SylowSystem.description: A Sylow system of a group G is a set of Sylow subgroups of G such that every pair of subgroups from this set commutes as subgroups. Sylow systems exist only for solvable groups. The operation returns fail if the group G is not solvable.
SylowSystem.title: SylowSystem

ComplementSystem.name: ComplementSystem
ComplementSystem.description: A complement system of a group G is a set of Hall p'-subgroups of G, where p' runs through the subsets of prime factors of |G| that omit exactly one prime. Every pair of subgroups from this set commutes as subgroups. Complement systems exist only for solvable groups, therefore ComplementSystem returns fail if the group G is not solvable.
ComplementSystem.title: ComplementSystem

HallSystem.name: HallSystem
HallSystem.description: returns a list containing one Hall P-subgroup for each set P of prime divisors of the order of G. Hall systems exist only for solvable groups. The operation returns fail if the group G is not solvable.
HallSystem.title: HallSystem

Omega.name: Omega
Omega.description: For a p-group G, one defines Ω_n(G) = ⟨ g ∈ G ∣ g^{p^n} = 1 ⟩. The default value for n is 1.
Omega.title: Omega

Omega.name: Omega
Omega.description:
constructs a group isomorphic to the group Ω( e, d, q ) of those d × d matrices over the field with q elements that respect a non-singular quadratic form (see InvariantQuadraticForm (44.5-5)) specified by e, and that have square spinor norm in odd characteristic or Dickson invariant 0 in even characteristic, respectively, in the category given by the filter filt.

For odd q and d ≥ 2, this group has always index two in the corresponding special orthogonal group, which will be conjugate in GL(d,q) to the group returned by SO( e, d, q ), see SpecialOrthogonalGroup (50.2-7), but may fix a different form (see 50.2).

The value of e must be 0 for odd d (and can optionally be omitted in this case), respectively one of 1 or -1 for even d. If filt is not given it defaults to IsMatrixGroup (44.1-1), and the returned group is the group Ω( e, d, q ) itself.
Omega.title: Omega

Omega.name: Omega
Omega.description:
constructs a group isomorphic to the group Ω( e, d, q ) of those d × d matrices over the field with q elements that respect a non-singular quadratic form (see InvariantQuadraticForm (44.5-5)) specified by e, and that have square spinor norm in odd characteristic or Dickson invariant 0 in even characteristic, respectively, in the category given by the filter filt.

For odd q and d ≥ 2, this group has always index two in the corresponding special orthogonal group, which will be conjugate in GL(d,q) to the group returned by SO( e, d, q ), see SpecialOrthogonalGroup (50.2-7), but may fix a different form (see 50.2).

The value of e must be 0 for odd d (and can optionally be omitted in this case), respectively one of 1 or -1 for even d. If filt is not given it defaults to IsMatrixGroup (44.1-1), and the returned group is the group Ω( e, d, q ) itself.
Omega.title: Omega

Agemo.name: Agemo
Agemo.description: For a p-group G, one defines ℧_n(G) = ⟨ g^{p^n} ∣ g ∈ G ⟩. The default value for n is 1.
Agemo.title: Agemo

IsCyclic.name: IsCyclic
IsCyclic.description: A group is cyclic if it can be generated by one element. For a cyclic group, one can compute a generating set consisting of only one element using MinimalGeneratingSet (39.22-3).
IsCyclic.title: IsCyclic

IsCyclic.name: IsCyclic
IsCyclic.description: These operations for groups are applicable to character tables and mean the same for a character table as for its underlying group; see Chapter 39 for the definitions. The operations are mainly useful for selecting character tables with certain properties, also for character tables without access to a group.
IsCyclic.title: Group Operations Applicable to Character Tables

IsElementaryAbelian.name: IsElementaryAbelian
IsElementaryAbelian.description: A group G is elementary abelian if it is commutative and if there is a prime p such that the order of each element in G divides p.
IsElementaryAbelian.title: IsElementaryAbelian

IsElementaryAbelian.name: IsElementaryAbelian
IsElementaryAbelian.description: These operations for groups are applicable to character tables and mean the same for a character table as for its underlying group; see Chapter 39 for the definitions. The operations are mainly useful for selecting character tables with certain properties, also for character tables without access to a group.
IsElementaryAbelian.title: Group Operations Applicable to Character Tables

IsNilpotentGroup.name: IsNilpotentGroup
IsNilpotentGroup.description: A group is nilpotent if the lower central series (see LowerCentralSeriesOfGroup (39.17-11) for a definition) reaches the trivial subgroup in a finite number of steps.
IsNilpotentGroup.title: IsNilpotentGroup

NilpotencyClassOfGroup.name: NilpotencyClassOfGroup
NilpotencyClassOfGroup.description:
The nilpotency class of a nilpotent group G is the number of steps in the lower central series of G (see LowerCentralSeriesOfGroup (39.17-11));

If G is not nilpotent an error is issued.
NilpotencyClassOfGroup.title: NilpotencyClassOfGroup

IsPerfectGroup.name: IsPerfectGroup
IsPerfectGroup.description: A group is perfect if it equals its derived subgroup (see DerivedSubgroup (39.12-3)).
IsPerfectGroup.title: IsPerfectGroup

IsSolvableGroup.name: IsSolvableGroup
IsSolvableGroup.description:
A group is solvable if the derived series (see DerivedSeriesOfGroup (39.17-7) for a definition) reaches the trivial subgroup in a finite number of steps.

For finite groups this is the same as being polycyclic (see IsPolycyclicGroup (39.15-7)), and each polycyclic group is solvable, but there are infinite solvable groups that are not polycyclic.
IsSolvableGroup.title: IsSolvableGroup

IsPolycyclicGroup.name: IsPolycyclicGroup
IsPolycyclicGroup.description: A group is polycyclic if it has a subnormal series with cyclic factors. For finite groups this is the same as if the group is solvable (see IsSolvableGroup (39.15-6)).
IsPolycyclicGroup.title: IsPolycyclicGroup

IsSupersolvableGroup.name: IsSupersolvableGroup
IsSupersolvableGroup.description: A finite group is supersolvable if it has a normal series with cyclic factors.
IsSupersolvableGroup.title: IsSupersolvableGroup

IsMonomialGroup.name: IsMonomialGroup
IsMonomialGroup.description: A finite group is monomial if every irreducible complex character is induced from a linear character of a subgroup.
IsMonomialGroup.title: IsMonomialGroup

IsSimpleGroup.name: IsSimpleGroup
IsSimpleGroup.description: A group is simple if it is nontrivial and has no nontrivial normal subgroups. A nonabelian simple group is simple and not abelian.
IsSimpleGroup.title: IsSimpleGroup

IsNonabelianSimpleGroup.name: IsNonabelianSimpleGroup
IsNonabelianSimpleGroup.description: A group is simple if it is nontrivial and has no nontrivial normal subgroups. A nonabelian simple group is simple and not abelian.
IsNonabelianSimpleGroup.title: IsSimpleGroup

IsAlmostSimpleGroup.name: IsAlmostSimpleGroup
IsAlmostSimpleGroup.description:
A group G is almost simple if a nonabelian simple group S exists such that G is isomorphic to a subgroup of the automorphism group of S that contains all inner automorphisms of S.

Equivalently, G is almost simple if and only if it has a unique minimal normal subgroup N and if N is a nonabelian simple group.

Note that an almost simple group is not defined as an extension of a simple group by outer automorphisms, since we want to exclude extensions of groups of prime order. In particular, a simple group is almost simple if and only if it is nonabelian.
IsAlmostSimpleGroup.title: IsAlmostSimpleGroup

IsQuasisimpleGroup.name: IsQuasisimpleGroup
IsQuasisimpleGroup.description: A group G is quasisimple if G is perfect (see IsPerfectGroup (39.15-5)) and if G/Z(G) is simple (see IsSimpleGroup (39.15-10)), where Z(G) is the centre of G (see Centre (35.4-5)).
IsQuasisimpleGroup.title: IsQuasisimpleGroup

IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup.name: IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup
IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup.description:
For a finite simple group G, IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup returns a record with the components name, shortname, series, and possibly parameter, describing the isomorphism type of G.

The values of the components name, shortname, and series are strings, name gives name(s) for G, shortname gives one name for G that is compatible with the naming scheme used in the GAP packages CTblLib and AtlasRep (and in the Atlas of Finite Groups [CCN+85]), and series describes the following series.

(If different characterizations of G are possible only one is given by series and parameter, while name may give several names.)
IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup.title: IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup

IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup.name: IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup
IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup.description:
For a finite simple group G, IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup returns a record with the components name, shortname, series, and possibly parameter, describing the isomorphism type of G.

The values of the components name, shortname, and series are strings, name gives name(s) for G, shortname gives one name for G that is compatible with the naming scheme used in the GAP packages CTblLib and AtlasRep (and in the Atlas of Finite Groups [CCN+85]), and series describes the following series.

(If different characterizations of G are possible only one is given by series and parameter, while name may give several names.)
IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup.title: IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup

IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup.name: IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup
IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup.description: These operations for groups are applicable to character tables and mean the same for a character table as for its underlying group; see Chapter 39 for the definitions. The operations are mainly useful for selecting character tables with certain properties, also for character tables without access to a group.
IsomorphismTypeInfoFiniteSimpleGroup.title: Group Operations Applicable to Character Tables

SimpleGroup.name: SimpleGroup
SimpleGroup.description: This function will construct an instance of the specified nonabelian simple group. Groups are specified via their name in ATLAS style notation, with parameters added if necessary. The intelligence applied to parsing the name is limited, and at the moment no proper extensions can be constructed. For groups which do not have a permutation representation of small degree the AtlasRep package might need to be installed to construct these groups.
SimpleGroup.title: SimpleGroup

SimpleGroupsIterator.name: SimpleGroupsIterator
SimpleGroupsIterator.description: This function returns an iterator that will run over all nonabelian simple groups, starting at order start if specified, up to order 10^27 (or -- if specified -- order end). If the option NOPSL2 is given, groups of type PSL_2(q) are omitted.
SimpleGroupsIterator.title: SimpleGroupsIterator

SmallSimpleGroup.name: SmallSimpleGroup
SmallSimpleGroup.description:
Returns: The ith simple group of order order in the stored list, given in a small-degree permutation representation, or fail (20.2-1) if no such simple group exists.

If i is not given, it defaults to 1. Currently, all simple groups of order less than 10^6 are available via this function.
SmallSimpleGroup.title: SmallSimpleGroup

AllSmallNonabelianSimpleGroups.name: AllSmallNonabelianSimpleGroups
AllSmallNonabelianSimpleGroups.description:
Returns: A list of all nonabelian simple groups whose order lies in the range orders.

The groups are given in small-degree permutation representations. The returned list is sorted by ascending group order. Currently, all simple groups of order less than 10^6 are available via this function.
AllSmallNonabelianSimpleGroups.title: AllSmallNonabelianSimpleGroups

IsFinitelyGeneratedGroup.name: IsFinitelyGeneratedGroup
IsFinitelyGeneratedGroup.description:
tests whether the group G can be generated by a finite number of generators. (This property is mainly used to obtain finiteness conditions.)

Note that this is a pure existence statement. Even if a group is known to be generated by a finite number of elements, it can be very hard or even impossible to obtain such a generating set if it is not known.
IsFinitelyGeneratedGroup.title: IsFinitelyGeneratedGroup

IsSubsetLocallyFiniteGroup.name: IsSubsetLocallyFiniteGroup
IsSubsetLocallyFiniteGroup.description: A group is called locally finite if every finitely generated subgroup is finite. This property checks whether the group U is a subset of a locally finite group. This is used to check whether finite generation will imply finiteness, as it does for example for permutation groups.
IsSubsetLocallyFiniteGroup.title: IsSubsetLocallyFiniteGroup

IsPGroup.name: IsPGroup
IsPGroup.description:
A p-group is a group in which the order (see Order (31.10-10)) of every element is of the form p^n for a prime integer p and a nonnegative integer n. IsPGroup returns true if G is a p-group, and false otherwise.

Finite p-groups are precisely those groups whose order (see Size (30.4-6)) is 1 or a prime power (see IsPrimePowerInt (14.4-4), and are always nilpotent.

Note that p-groups can also be infinite, and in that case, need not be nilpotent.
IsPGroup.title: IsPGroup

IsPowerfulPGroup.name: IsPowerfulPGroup
IsPowerfulPGroup.description: A finite p-group G is said to be a powerful p-group if the commutator subgroup [G,G] is contained in G^p if the prime p is odd, or if [G,G] is contained in G^4 if p = 2. The subgroup G^p is called the first Agemo subgroup, (see Agemo (39.14-2)). IsPowerfulPGroup returns true if G is a powerful p-group, and false otherwise. Note: This function returns true if G is the trivial group.
IsPowerfulPGroup.title: IsPowerfulPGroup

IsRegularPGroup.name: IsRegularPGroup
IsRegularPGroup.description: A finite p-group G is a regular p-group if for all a,b in G, one has a^p b^p = (a b)^p c^p where c is an element of the derived subgroup of the group generated by a and b (see [Hal34]). IsRegularPGroup returns true if G is a regular p-group, and false otherwise. Note: This function returns true if G is the trivial group.
IsRegularPGroup.title: IsRegularPGroup

PrimePGroup.name: PrimePGroup
PrimePGroup.description:
If G is a nontrivial p-group (see IsPGroup (39.15-20)), PrimePGroup returns the prime integer p; if G is trivial then PrimePGroup returns fail. Otherwise an error is issued.

(One should avoid a common error of writing if IsPGroup(g) then ... PrimePGroup(g) ... where the code represented by dots assumes that PrimePGroup(g) is an integer.)
PrimePGroup.title: PrimePGroup

PClassPGroup.name: PClassPGroup
PClassPGroup.description: The p-class of a p-group G (see IsPGroup (39.15-20)) is the length of the lower p-central series (see PCentralSeries (39.17-13)) of G. If G is not a p-group then an error is issued.
PClassPGroup.title: PClassPGroup

RankPGroup.name: RankPGroup
RankPGroup.description: For a p-group G (see IsPGroup (39.15-20)), RankPGroup returns the rank of G, which is defined as the minimal size of a generating system of G. If G is not a p-group then an error is issued.
RankPGroup.title: RankPGroup

IsPSolvable.name: IsPSolvable
IsPSolvable.description: A finite group is p-solvable if every chief factor either has order not divisible by p, or is solvable.
IsPSolvable.title: IsPSolvable

IsPNilpotent.name: IsPNilpotent
IsPNilpotent.description: A group is p-nilpotent if it possesses a normal p-complement.
IsPNilpotent.title: IsPNilpotent

AbelianInvariants.name: AbelianInvariants
AbelianInvariants.description:
returns the abelian invariants (also sometimes called primary decomposition) of the commutator factor group of the group G. These are given as a list of prime-powers or zeroes and describe the structure of G/G' as a direct product of cyclic groups of prime power (or infinite) order.

(See IndependentGeneratorsOfAbelianGroup (39.22-5) to obtain actual generators).
AbelianInvariants.title: AbelianInvariants

AbelianInvariants.name: AbelianInvariants
AbelianInvariants.description: These operations for groups are applicable to character tables and mean the same for a character table as for its underlying group; see Chapter 39 for the definitions. The operations are mainly useful for selecting character tables with certain properties, also for character tables without access to a group.
AbelianInvariants.title: Group Operations Applicable to Character Tables

Exponent.name: Exponent
Exponent.description: The exponent e of a group G is the lcm of the orders of its elements, that is, e is the smallest integer such that g^e = 1 for all g ∈ G.
Exponent.title: Exponent

Exponent.name: Exponent
Exponent.description: These operations for groups are applicable to character tables and mean the same for a character table as for its underlying group; see Chapter 39 for the definitions. The operations are mainly useful for selecting character tables with certain properties, also for character tables without access to a group.
Exponent.title: Group Operations Applicable to Character Tables

EulerianFunction.name: EulerianFunction
EulerianFunction.description:
returns the number of n-tuples (g_1, g_2, ..., g_n) of elements of the group G that generate the whole group G. The elements of such an n-tuple need not be different.

In [Hal36], the notation ϕ_n(G) is used for the value returned by EulerianFunction, and the quotient of ϕ_n(G) by the order of the automorphism group of G is called d_n(G). If G is a nonabelian simple group then d_n(G) is the greatest number d for which the direct product of d groups isomorphic with G can be generated by n elements.

If the Library of Tables of Marks (see Chapter 70) covers the group G, you may also use EulerianFunctionByTom (70.9-9).
EulerianFunction.title: EulerianFunction

ChiefSeries.name: ChiefSeries
ChiefSeries.description: is a series of normal subgroups of G which cannot be refined further. That is there is no normal subgroup N of G with U_i > N > U_{i+1}. This attribute returns one chief series (of potentially many possibilities).
ChiefSeries.title: ChiefSeries

ChiefSeriesThrough.name: ChiefSeriesThrough
ChiefSeriesThrough.description: is a chief series of the group G going through the normal subgroups in the list l, which must be a list of normal subgroups of G contained in each other, sorted by descending size. This attribute returns one chief series (of potentially many possibilities).
ChiefSeriesThrough.title: ChiefSeriesThrough

ChiefSeriesUnderAction.name: ChiefSeriesUnderAction
ChiefSeriesUnderAction.description: returns a series of normal subgroups of G which are invariant under H such that the series cannot be refined any further. G must be a subgroup of H. This attribute returns one such series (of potentially many possibilities).
ChiefSeriesUnderAction.title: ChiefSeriesUnderAction

SubnormalSeries.name: SubnormalSeries
SubnormalSeries.description: If U is a subgroup of G this operation returns a subnormal series that descends from G to a subnormal subgroup V ≥U. If U is subnormal, V = U.
SubnormalSeries.title: SubnormalSeries

CompositionSeries.name: CompositionSeries
CompositionSeries.description: A composition series is a subnormal series which cannot be refined. This attribute returns one composition series (of potentially many possibilities). The variant CompositionSeriesThrough takes as second argument a list normals of normal subgroups of the group, and returns a composition series that incorporates these normal subgroups.
CompositionSeries.title: CompositionSeries

CompositionSeriesThrough.name: CompositionSeriesThrough
CompositionSeriesThrough.description: A composition series is a subnormal series which cannot be refined. This attribute returns one composition series (of potentially many possibilities). The variant CompositionSeriesThrough takes as second argument a list normals of normal subgroups of the group, and returns a composition series that incorporates these normal subgroups.
CompositionSeriesThrough.title: CompositionSeries

DisplayCompositionSeries.name: DisplayCompositionSeries
DisplayCompositionSeries.description: Displays a composition series of G in a nice way, identifying the simple factors.
DisplayCompositionSeries.title: DisplayCompositionSeries

DerivedSeriesOfGroup.name: DerivedSeriesOfGroup
DerivedSeriesOfGroup.description: The derived series of a group is obtained by U_{i+1} = U_i'. It stops if U_i is perfect.
DerivedSeriesOfGroup.title: DerivedSeriesOfGroup

DerivedLength.name: DerivedLength
DerivedLength.description: The derived length of a group is the number of steps in the derived series. (As there is always the group, it is the series length minus 1.)
DerivedLength.title: DerivedLength

ElementaryAbelianSeries.name: ElementaryAbelianSeries
ElementaryAbelianSeries.description:
returns a series of normal subgroups of G such that all factors are elementary abelian. If the group is not solvable (and thus no such series exists) it returns fail.

The variant ElementaryAbelianSeriesLargeSteps tries to make the steps in this series large (by eliminating intermediate subgroups if possible) at a small additional cost.

In the third variant, an elementary abelian series through the given series of normal subgroups in the list list is constructed.
ElementaryAbelianSeries.title: ElementaryAbelianSeries

ElementaryAbelianSeries.name: ElementaryAbelianSeries
ElementaryAbelianSeries.description:
returns a series of normal subgroups of G such that all factors are elementary abelian. If the group is not solvable (and thus no such series exists) it returns fail.

The variant ElementaryAbelianSeriesLargeSteps tries to make the steps in this series large (by eliminating intermediate subgroups if possible) at a small additional cost.

In the third variant, an elementary abelian series through the given series of normal subgroups in the list list is constructed.
ElementaryAbelianSeries.title: ElementaryAbelianSeries

ElementaryAbelianSeriesLargeSteps.name: ElementaryAbelianSeriesLargeSteps
ElementaryAbelianSeriesLargeSteps.description:
returns a series of normal subgroups of G such that all factors are elementary abelian. If the group is not solvable (and thus no such series exists) it returns fail.

The variant ElementaryAbelianSeriesLargeSteps tries to make the steps in this series large (by eliminating intermediate subgroups if possible) at a small additional cost.

In the third variant, an elementary abelian series through the given series of normal subgroups in the list list is constructed.
ElementaryAbelianSeriesLargeSteps.title: ElementaryAbelianSeries

InvariantElementaryAbelianSeries.name: InvariantElementaryAbelianSeries
InvariantElementaryAbelianSeries.description:
For a (solvable) group G and a list of automorphisms morph of G, this command finds a normal series of G with elementary abelian factors such that every group in this series is invariant under every automorphism in morph.

If a normal subgroup N of G which is invariant under morph is given, this series is chosen to contain N. No tests are performed to check the validity of the arguments.

The series obtained will be constructed to prefer large steps unless fine is given as true.
InvariantElementaryAbelianSeries.title: InvariantElementaryAbelianSeries

LowerCentralSeriesOfGroup.name: LowerCentralSeriesOfGroup
LowerCentralSeriesOfGroup.description: The lower central series of a group G is defined as U_{i+1}:= [G, U_i]. It is a central series of normal subgroups. The name derives from the fact that U_i is contained in the i-th step subgroup of any central series.
LowerCentralSeriesOfGroup.title: LowerCentralSeriesOfGroup

UpperCentralSeriesOfGroup.name: UpperCentralSeriesOfGroup
UpperCentralSeriesOfGroup.description: The upper central series of a group G is defined as an ending series U_i / U_{i+1}:= Z(G/U_{i+1}). It is a central series of normal subgroups. The name derives from the fact that U_i contains every i-th step subgroup of a central series.
UpperCentralSeriesOfGroup.title: UpperCentralSeriesOfGroup

PCentralSeries.name: PCentralSeries
PCentralSeries.description: The p-central series of G is defined by U_1:= G, U_i:= [G, U_{i-1}] U_{i-1}^p.
PCentralSeries.title: PCentralSeries

JenningsSeries.name: JenningsSeries
JenningsSeries.description: For a p-group G, this function returns its Jennings series. This series is defined by setting G_1 = G and for i ≥ 0, G_{i+1} = [G_i,G] G_j^p, where j is the smallest integer > i/p.
JenningsSeries.title: JenningsSeries

DimensionsLoewyFactors.name: DimensionsLoewyFactors
DimensionsLoewyFactors.description:
This operation computes the dimensions of the factors of the Loewy series of G. (See [HB82, p. 157] for the slightly complicated definition of the Loewy Series.)
