The dimensions are computed via the JenningsSeries (39.17-14) without computing the Loewy series itself.
DimensionsLoewyFactors.title: DimensionsLoewyFactors

AscendingChain.name: AscendingChain
AscendingChain.description:
This function computes an ascending chain of subgroups from U to G. This chain is given as a list whose first entry is U and the last entry is G. The function tries to make the links in this chain small.

The option refineIndex can be used to give a bound for refinements of steps to avoid GAP trying to enforce too small steps. The option cheap (if set to true) will overall limit the amount of heuristic searches.
AscendingChain.title: AscendingChain

IntermediateGroup.name: IntermediateGroup
IntermediateGroup.description: This routine tries to find a subgroup E of G, such that G > E > U holds. If U is maximal in G, the function returns fail. This is done by finding minimal blocks for the operation of G on the right cosets of U.
IntermediateGroup.title: IntermediateGroup

IntermediateSubgroups.name: IntermediateSubgroups
IntermediateSubgroups.description: returns a list of all subgroups of G that properly contain U; that is all subgroups between G and U. It returns a record with a component subgroups, which is a list of these subgroups, as well as a component inclusions, which lists all maximality inclusions among these subgroups. A maximality inclusion is given as a list [i, j] indicating that the subgroup number i is a maximal subgroup of the subgroup number j, the numbers 0 and 1 + Length(subgroups) are used to denote U and G, respectively.
IntermediateSubgroups.title: IntermediateSubgroups

StructuralSeriesOfGroup.name: StructuralSeriesOfGroup
StructuralSeriesOfGroup.description: The structural series of a finite group G is a descending series of characteristic subgroups which goes through the derived series of the solvable radical of $G$, refined into elementary abelian factors, as well as the socle and the Pker (kernel of the action on socle components) of the radical factor
StructuralSeriesOfGroup.title: StructuralSeriesOfGroup

NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroup.name: NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroup
NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroup.description: returns a homomorphism from G to another group whose kernel is N. GAP will try to select the image group as to make computations in it as efficient as possible. As the factor group G/N can be identified with the image of G this permits efficient computations in the factor group. The homomorphism returned is not necessarily surjective, so ImagesSource (32.4-1) should be used instead of Range (32.3-7) to get a group isomorphic to the factor group. The NC variant does not check whether N is normal in G.
NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroup.title: NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroup

NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroupNC.name: NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroupNC
NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroupNC.description: returns a homomorphism from G to another group whose kernel is N. GAP will try to select the image group as to make computations in it as efficient as possible. As the factor group G/N can be identified with the image of G this permits efficient computations in the factor group. The homomorphism returned is not necessarily surjective, so ImagesSource (32.4-1) should be used instead of Range (32.3-7) to get a group isomorphic to the factor group. The NC variant does not check whether N is normal in G.
NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroupNC.title: NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroup

FactorGroup.name: FactorGroup
FactorGroup.description: returns the image of the NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroup(G,N). This function is provided for compatibility with older code, but if a connection between group and factor is desired, users need to start by obtaining the NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroup in the first place. The NC version does not test whether N is normal in G.
FactorGroup.title: FactorGroup

FactorGroupNC.name: FactorGroupNC
FactorGroupNC.description: returns the image of the NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroup(G,N). This function is provided for compatibility with older code, but if a connection between group and factor is desired, users need to start by obtaining the NaturalHomomorphismByNormalSubgroup in the first place. The NC version does not test whether N is normal in G.
FactorGroupNC.title: FactorGroup

CommutatorFactorGroup.name: CommutatorFactorGroup
CommutatorFactorGroup.description: computes the commutator factor group G/G' of the group G.
CommutatorFactorGroup.title: CommutatorFactorGroup

MaximalAbelianQuotient.name: MaximalAbelianQuotient
MaximalAbelianQuotient.description: returns an epimorphism from G onto the maximal abelian quotient of G. The kernel of this epimorphism is the derived subgroup of G, see DerivedSubgroup (39.12-3).
MaximalAbelianQuotient.title: MaximalAbelianQuotient

HasAbelianFactorGroup.name: HasAbelianFactorGroup
HasAbelianFactorGroup.description: tests whether G / N is abelian (without explicitly constructing the factor group and without testing whether N is in fact a normal subgroup).
HasAbelianFactorGroup.title: HasAbelianFactorGroup

HasElementaryAbelianFactorGroup.name: HasElementaryAbelianFactorGroup
HasElementaryAbelianFactorGroup.description: tests whether G / N is elementary abelian (without explicitly constructing the factor group and without testing whether N is in fact a normal subgroup).
HasElementaryAbelianFactorGroup.title: HasElementaryAbelianFactorGroup

CentralizerModulo.name: CentralizerModulo
CentralizerModulo.description: Computes the full preimage of the centralizer C_{G/N}(elm ⋅ N) in G (without necessarily constructing the factor group).
CentralizerModulo.title: CentralizerModulo

ConjugacyClassSubgroups.name: ConjugacyClassSubgroups
ConjugacyClassSubgroups.description:
generates the conjugacy class of subgroups of G with representative U. This class is an external set, so functions such as Representative (30.4-7), (which returns U), ActingDomain (41.12-3) (which returns G), StabilizerOfExternalSet (41.12-10) (which returns the normalizer of U), and AsList (30.3-8) work for it.

(The use of the [] list access to select elements of the class is considered obsolescent and will be removed in future versions. Use ClassElementLattice (39.20-2) instead.)
ConjugacyClassSubgroups.title: ConjugacyClassSubgroups

IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsRep.name: IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsRep
IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsRep.description: Is the representation GAP uses for conjugacy classes of subgroups. It can be used to check whether an object is a class of subgroups. The second representation IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsByStabilizerRep in addition is an external orbit by stabilizer and will compute its elements via a transversal of the stabilizer.
IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsRep.title: IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsRep

IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsByStabilizerRep.name: IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsByStabilizerRep
IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsByStabilizerRep.description: Is the representation GAP uses for conjugacy classes of subgroups. It can be used to check whether an object is a class of subgroups. The second representation IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsByStabilizerRep in addition is an external orbit by stabilizer and will compute its elements via a transversal of the stabilizer.
IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsByStabilizerRep.title: IsConjugacyClassSubgroupsRep

ConjugacyClassesSubgroups.name: ConjugacyClassesSubgroups
ConjugacyClassesSubgroups.description: This attribute returns a list of all conjugacy classes of subgroups of the group G. It also is applicable for lattices of subgroups (see LatticeSubgroups (39.20-1)). The order in which the classes are listed depends on the method chosen by GAP. For each class of subgroups, a representative can be accessed using Representative (30.4-7).
ConjugacyClassesSubgroups.title: ConjugacyClassesSubgroups

ConjugacyClassesMaximalSubgroups.name: ConjugacyClassesMaximalSubgroups
ConjugacyClassesMaximalSubgroups.description: returns the conjugacy classes of maximal subgroups of G. Representatives of the classes can be computed directly by MaximalSubgroupClassReps (39.19-5).
ConjugacyClassesMaximalSubgroups.title: ConjugacyClassesMaximalSubgroups

MaximalSubgroupClassReps.name: MaximalSubgroupClassReps
MaximalSubgroupClassReps.description: returns a list of conjugacy representatives of the maximal subgroups of G.
MaximalSubgroupClassReps.title: MaximalSubgroupClassReps

LowIndexSubgroups.name: LowIndexSubgroups
LowIndexSubgroups.description:
The operation LowIndexSubgroups computes representatives of the conjugacy classes of subgroups of the group G that have index less than or equal to index.

For finitely presented groups this operation simply defaults to LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroup (47.10-1). In other cases, it uses repeated calculation of maximal subgroups.

The function LowLayerSubgroups (39.20-6) works similar but does not bound the index, but instead considers up to layer-th maximal subgroups.
LowIndexSubgroups.title: LowIndexSubgroups

AllSubgroups.name: AllSubgroups
AllSubgroups.description: For a finite group G AllSubgroups returns a list of all subgroups of G, intended primarily for use in class for small examples. This list will quickly get very long and in general use of ConjugacyClassesSubgroups (39.19-3) is recommended.
AllSubgroups.title: AllSubgroups

MaximalSubgroups.name: MaximalSubgroups
MaximalSubgroups.description: returns a list of all maximal subgroups of G. This may take up much space, therefore the command should be avoided if possible. See ConjugacyClassesMaximalSubgroups (39.19-4).
MaximalSubgroups.title: MaximalSubgroups

NormalSubgroups.name: NormalSubgroups
NormalSubgroups.description:
returns a list of all normal subgroups of G.

The algorithm for the computation of normal subgroups is described in [Hul98].
NormalSubgroups.title: NormalSubgroups

MaximalNormalSubgroups.name: MaximalNormalSubgroups
MaximalNormalSubgroups.description: is a list containing those proper normal subgroups of the group G that are maximal among the proper normal subgroups. Gives error if G/G' is infinite, yielding infinitely many maximal normal subgroups. Note, that the maximal normal subgroups of a group G can be computed more efficiently if the character table of G is known or if G is known to be abelian or solvable (even if infinite). So if the character table is needed, anyhow, or G is suspected to be abelian or solvable, then these should be computed before computing the maximal normal subgroups.
MaximalNormalSubgroups.title: MaximalNormalSubgroups

MinimalNormalSubgroups.name: MinimalNormalSubgroups
MinimalNormalSubgroups.description: is a list containing those nontrivial normal subgroups of the group G that are minimal among the nontrivial normal subgroups.
MinimalNormalSubgroups.title: MinimalNormalSubgroups

CharacteristicSubgroups.name: CharacteristicSubgroups
CharacteristicSubgroups.description: returns a list of all characteristic subgroups of G, that is subgroups that are invariant under all automorphisms.
CharacteristicSubgroups.title: CharacteristicSubgroups

LatticeSubgroups.name: LatticeSubgroups
LatticeSubgroups.description: computes the lattice of subgroups of the group G. This lattice has the conjugacy classes of subgroups as attribute ConjugacyClassesSubgroups (39.19-3) and permits one to test maximality/minimality relations.
LatticeSubgroups.title: LatticeSubgroups

ClassElementLattice.name: ClassElementLattice
ClassElementLattice.description:
For a class C of subgroups, obtained by a lattice computation, this operation returns the n-th conjugate subgroup in the class.

Because of other methods installed, calling AsList (30.3-8) with C can give a different arrangement of the class elements!

The GAP package XGAP permits a graphical display of the lattice of subgroups in a nice way.
ClassElementLattice.title: ClassElementLattice

DotFileLatticeSubgroups.name: DotFileLatticeSubgroups
DotFileLatticeSubgroups.description:
This function produces a graphical representation of the subgroup lattice L in file file. The output is in .dot (also known as GraphViz format). For details on the format, and information about how to display or edit this format see https://www.graphviz.org. (On the Macintosh, the program OmniGraffle is also able to read this format.)

Subgroups are labelled in the form i-j where i is the number of the class of subgroups and j the number within this class. Normal subgroups are represented by a box.
DotFileLatticeSubgroups.title: DotFileLatticeSubgroups

MaximalSubgroupsLattice.name: MaximalSubgroupsLattice
MaximalSubgroupsLattice.description:
For a lattice lat of subgroups this attribute contains the maximal subgroup relations among the subgroups of the lattice. It is a list corresponding to the ConjugacyClassesSubgroups (39.19-3) value of the lattice, each entry giving a list of the maximal subgroups of the representative of this class. Every maximal subgroup is indicated by a list of the form [ c, n ] which means that the n-th subgroup in class number c is a maximal subgroup of the representative.

The number n corresponds to access via ClassElementLattice (39.20-2) and not necessarily the AsList (30.3-8) arrangement! See also MinimalSupergroupsLattice (39.20-5).
MaximalSubgroupsLattice.title: MaximalSubgroupsLattice

MinimalSupergroupsLattice.name: MinimalSupergroupsLattice
MinimalSupergroupsLattice.description:
For a lattice lat of subgroups this attribute contains the minimal supergroup relations among the subgroups of the lattice. It is a list corresponding to the ConjugacyClassesSubgroups (39.19-3) value of the lattice, each entry giving a list of the minimal supergroups of the representative of this class. Every minimal supergroup is indicated by a list of the form [ c, n ], which means that the n-th subgroup in class number c is a minimal supergroup of the representative.

The number n corresponds to access via ClassElementLattice (39.20-2) and not necessarily the AsList (30.3-8) arrangement! See also MaximalSubgroupsLattice (39.20-4).
MinimalSupergroupsLattice.title: MinimalSupergroupsLattice

LowLayerSubgroups.name: LowLayerSubgroups
LowLayerSubgroups.description: This function computes representatives of the conjugacy classes of subgroups of the finite group G such that the subgroups can be obtained as lim-fold iterated maximal subgroups. If a function cond is given, only subgroups for which this function returns true (also for their intermediate overgroups) is returned. If also a function dosub is given, maximal subgroups are only attempted if this function returns true (this is separated for performance reasons). In the example below, the result would be the same with leaving out the fourth function, but calculation this way is slightly faster. If an initial argument act is given, it must be a group containing and normalizing G, and representatives for classes under the action of this group are chosen.
LowLayerSubgroups.title: LowLayerSubgroups

ContainedConjugates.name: ContainedConjugates
ContainedConjugates.description: For A,B ≤ G this operation returns representatives of the A conjugacy classes of subgroups that are conjugate to B under G. The function returns a list of pairs of subgroup and conjugating element. If the optional fourth argument onlyone is given as true, then only one pair (or fail if none exists) is returned.
ContainedConjugates.title: ContainedConjugates

ContainingConjugates.name: ContainingConjugates
ContainingConjugates.description: For A,B ≤ G this operation returns all G conjugates of A that contain B. The function returns a list of pairs of subgroup and conjugating element.
ContainingConjugates.title: ContainingConjugates

MinimalFaithfulPermutationDegree.name: MinimalFaithfulPermutationDegree
MinimalFaithfulPermutationDegree.description: For a finite group G, MinimalFaithfulPermutationDegree calculates the least positive integer n=μ(G) such that G is isomorphic to a subgroup of the symmetric group of degree n. This can require calculating the whole subgroup lattice. The operation MinimalFaithfulPermutationRepresentation returns a corresponding isomorphism.
MinimalFaithfulPermutationDegree.title: MinimalFaithfulPermutationDegree

MinimalFaithfulPermutationRepresentation.name: MinimalFaithfulPermutationRepresentation
MinimalFaithfulPermutationRepresentation.description: For a finite group G, MinimalFaithfulPermutationDegree calculates the least positive integer n=μ(G) such that G is isomorphic to a subgroup of the symmetric group of degree n. This can require calculating the whole subgroup lattice. The operation MinimalFaithfulPermutationRepresentation returns a corresponding isomorphism.
MinimalFaithfulPermutationRepresentation.title: MinimalFaithfulPermutationDegree

RepresentativesPerfectSubgroups.name: RepresentativesPerfectSubgroups
RepresentativesPerfectSubgroups.description: returns a list of conjugacy representatives of perfect (respectively simple) subgroups of G. This uses the library of perfect groups (see PerfectGroup (50.6-2)), thus it will issue an error if the library is insufficient to determine all perfect subgroups.
RepresentativesPerfectSubgroups.title: RepresentativesPerfectSubgroups

RepresentativesSimpleSubgroups.name: RepresentativesSimpleSubgroups
RepresentativesSimpleSubgroups.description: returns a list of conjugacy representatives of perfect (respectively simple) subgroups of G. This uses the library of perfect groups (see PerfectGroup (50.6-2)), thus it will issue an error if the library is insufficient to determine all perfect subgroups.
RepresentativesSimpleSubgroups.title: RepresentativesPerfectSubgroups

ConjugacyClassesPerfectSubgroups.name: ConjugacyClassesPerfectSubgroups
ConjugacyClassesPerfectSubgroups.description: returns a list of the conjugacy classes of perfect subgroups of G. (see RepresentativesPerfectSubgroups (39.20-10).)
ConjugacyClassesPerfectSubgroups.title: ConjugacyClassesPerfectSubgroups

Zuppos.name: Zuppos
Zuppos.description: The Zuppos of a group are the cyclic subgroups of prime power order. (The name Zuppo derives from the German abbreviation for zyklische Untergruppen von Primzahlpotenzordnung.) This attribute gives generators of all such subgroups of a group G. That is all elements of G of prime power order up to the equivalence that they generate the same cyclic subgroup.
Zuppos.title: Zuppos

InfoLattice.name: InfoLattice
InfoLattice.description: is the information class used by the cyclic extension methods for subgroup lattice calculations.
InfoLattice.title: InfoLattice

LatticeByCyclicExtension.name: LatticeByCyclicExtension
LatticeByCyclicExtension.description:
computes the lattice of G using the cyclic extension algorithm. If the function func is given, the algorithm will discard all subgroups not fulfilling func (and will also not extend them), returning a partial lattice. This can be useful to compute only subgroups with certain properties. Note however that this will not necessarily yield all subgroups that fulfill func, but the subgroups whose subgroups are used for the construction must also fulfill func as well. (In fact the filter func will simply discard subgroups in the cyclic extension algorithm. Therefore the trivial subgroup will always be included.) Also note, that for such a partial lattice maximality/minimality inclusion relations cannot be computed. (If func is a list of length 2, its first entry is such a discarding function, the second a function for discarding zuppos.)

The cyclic extension algorithm requires the perfect subgroups of G. However GAP cannot analyze the function func for its implication but can only apply it. If it is known that func implies solvability, the computation of the perfect subgroups can be avoided by giving a third parameter noperf set to true.

The total number of classes in this example is much bigger, as the following example shows:
LatticeByCyclicExtension.title: LatticeByCyclicExtension

InvariantSubgroupsElementaryAbelianGroup.name: InvariantSubgroupsElementaryAbelianGroup
InvariantSubgroupsElementaryAbelianGroup.description: Let G be an elementary abelian group and homs be a set of automorphisms of G. Then this function computes all subspaces of G which are invariant under all automorphisms in homs. When considering G as a module for the algebra generated by homs, these are all submodules. If homs is empty, it computes all subgroups. If the optional parameter dims is given, only submodules of this dimension are computed.
InvariantSubgroupsElementaryAbelianGroup.title: InvariantSubgroupsElementaryAbelianGroup

SubgroupsSolvableGroup.name: SubgroupsSolvableGroup
SubgroupsSolvableGroup.description:
This function (implementing the algorithm published in [Hul99]) computes subgroups of a solvable group G, using the homomorphism principle. It returns a list of representatives up to G-conjugacy.

The optional argument opt is a record, which may be used to suggest restrictions on the subgroups computed. The following record components of opt are recognized and have the following effects. Note that all of the following restrictions to subgroups with particular properties are only used to speed up the calculation, but the result might still contain subgroups (that had to be computed in any case) that do not satisfy the properties. If this is not desired, the calculation must be followed by an explicit test for the desired properties (which is not done by default, as it would be a general slowdown). The function guarantees that representatives of all subgroups that satisfy the properties are found, i.e. there can be only false positives.

must be a list of automorphisms of G. If given, only groups which are invariant under all these automorphisms are computed. The algorithm must know the normalizer in G of the group generated by actions (defined formally by embedding in the semidirect product of G with actions). This can be given in the component funcnorm and will be computed if this component is not given.
SubgroupsSolvableGroup.title: SubgroupsSolvableGroup

SizeConsiderFunction.name: SizeConsiderFunction
SizeConsiderFunction.description:
This function returns a function consider of five arguments that can be used in SubgroupsSolvableGroup (39.21-3) for the option consider to compute subgroups whose sizes are divisible by size.

This example shows that in general the consider function does not provide a perfect filter. It is guaranteed that all subgroups fulfilling the condition are returned, but not all subgroups returned necessarily fulfill the condition.
SizeConsiderFunction.title: SizeConsiderFunction

ExactSizeConsiderFunction.name: ExactSizeConsiderFunction
ExactSizeConsiderFunction.description:
This function returns a function consider of five arguments that can be used in SubgroupsSolvableGroup (39.21-3) for the option consider to compute subgroups whose sizes are exactly size.

Again, the consider function does not provide a perfect filter. It is guaranteed that all subgroups fulfilling the condition are returned, but not all subgroups returned necessarily fulfill the condition.
ExactSizeConsiderFunction.title: ExactSizeConsiderFunction

InfoPcSubgroup.name: InfoPcSubgroup
InfoPcSubgroup.description: Information function for the subgroup lattice functions using pcgs.
InfoPcSubgroup.title: InfoPcSubgroup

GeneratorsSmallest.name: GeneratorsSmallest
GeneratorsSmallest.description: returns a smallest generating set for the group G. This is the lexicographically (using GAPs order of group elements) smallest list l of elements of G such that G = ⟨ l ⟩ and l_i not ∈ ⟨ l_1, ..., l_{i-1} ⟩ (in particular l_1 is not the identity element of the group). The comparison of two groups via lexicographic comparison of their sorted element lists yields the same relation as lexicographic comparison of their smallest generating sets.
GeneratorsSmallest.title: GeneratorsSmallest

LargestElementGroup.name: LargestElementGroup
LargestElementGroup.description: returns the largest element of G with respect to the ordering < of the elements family.
LargestElementGroup.title: LargestElementGroup

MinimalGeneratingSet.name: MinimalGeneratingSet
MinimalGeneratingSet.description:
returns a generating set of G of minimal possible length.

Note that only methods for finite groups, solvable groups, or finitely generated nilpotent groups are available (the latter through the Polycyclic package) and that calculations for nonsolvable finite groups of higher rank can be expensive.

If you do not really need a minimal generating set, but are satisfied with getting a reasonably small set of generators, you better use SmallGeneratingSet (39.22-4).
MinimalGeneratingSet.title: MinimalGeneratingSet

SmallGeneratingSet.name: SmallGeneratingSet
SmallGeneratingSet.description: returns a generating set of G which has few elements. As neither irredundancy, nor minimal length is proven it runs much faster than MinimalGeneratingSet (39.22-3). It can be used whenever a short generating set is desired which not necessarily needs to be optimal.
SmallGeneratingSet.title: SmallGeneratingSet

IndependentGeneratorsOfAbelianGroup.name: IndependentGeneratorsOfAbelianGroup
IndependentGeneratorsOfAbelianGroup.description: returns a list of generators a_1, a_2, ... of prime power order or infinite order of the abelian group A such that A is the direct product of the cyclic groups generated by the a_i. The list of orders of the returned generators must match the result of AbelianInvariants (39.16-1) (taking into account that zero and infinity (18.2-1) are identified).
IndependentGeneratorsOfAbelianGroup.title: IndependentGeneratorsOfAbelianGroup

IndependentGeneratorExponents.name: IndependentGeneratorExponents
IndependentGeneratorExponents.description: For an abelian group G, with IndependentGeneratorsOfAbelianGroup (39.22-5) value the list [ a_1, ..., a_n ], this operation returns the exponent vector [ e_1, ..., e_n ] to represent g = ∏_i a_i^{e_i}.
IndependentGeneratorExponents.title: IndependentGeneratorExponents

OneCocycles.name: OneCocycles
OneCocycles.description: Computes the group of 1-cocycles Z^1(G/M,M). The normal subgroup M may be given by a (Modulo)Pcgs mpcgs. In this case the whole calculation is performed modulo the normal subgroup defined by DenominatorOfModuloPcgs(mpcgs) (see 45.1). Similarly the group G may instead be specified by a set of elements gens that are representatives for a generating system for the factor group G/M. If this is done the 1-cocycles are computed with respect to these generators (otherwise the routines try to select suitable generators themselves). The current version of the code assumes that G is a permutation group or a pc group.
OneCocycles.title: OneCocycles

OneCocycles.name: OneCocycles
OneCocycles.description: Computes the group of 1-cocycles Z^1(G/M,M). The normal subgroup M may be given by a (Modulo)Pcgs mpcgs. In this case the whole calculation is performed modulo the normal subgroup defined by DenominatorOfModuloPcgs(mpcgs) (see 45.1). Similarly the group G may instead be specified by a set of elements gens that are representatives for a generating system for the factor group G/M. If this is done the 1-cocycles are computed with respect to these generators (otherwise the routines try to select suitable generators themselves). The current version of the code assumes that G is a permutation group or a pc group.
OneCocycles.title: OneCocycles

OneCocycles.name: OneCocycles
OneCocycles.description: Computes the group of 1-cocycles Z^1(G/M,M). The normal subgroup M may be given by a (Modulo)Pcgs mpcgs. In this case the whole calculation is performed modulo the normal subgroup defined by DenominatorOfModuloPcgs(mpcgs) (see 45.1). Similarly the group G may instead be specified by a set of elements gens that are representatives for a generating system for the factor group G/M. If this is done the 1-cocycles are computed with respect to these generators (otherwise the routines try to select suitable generators themselves). The current version of the code assumes that G is a permutation group or a pc group.
OneCocycles.title: OneCocycles

OneCocycles.name: OneCocycles
OneCocycles.description: Computes the group of 1-cocycles Z^1(G/M,M). The normal subgroup M may be given by a (Modulo)Pcgs mpcgs. In this case the whole calculation is performed modulo the normal subgroup defined by DenominatorOfModuloPcgs(mpcgs) (see 45.1). Similarly the group G may instead be specified by a set of elements gens that are representatives for a generating system for the factor group G/M. If this is done the 1-cocycles are computed with respect to these generators (otherwise the routines try to select suitable generators themselves). The current version of the code assumes that G is a permutation group or a pc group.
OneCocycles.title: OneCocycles

OneCoboundaries.name: OneCoboundaries
OneCoboundaries.description:
computes the group of 1-coboundaries. Syntax of input and output otherwise is the same as with OneCocycles (39.23-1) except that entries that refer to cocycles are not computed.

The operations OneCocycles (39.23-1) and OneCoboundaries return a record with (at least) the components:

Is a list of representatives for a generating set of G/M. Cocycles are represented with respect to these generators.
OneCoboundaries.title: OneCoboundaries

OCOneCocycles.name: OCOneCocycles
OCOneCocycles.description:
is the more technical function to compute 1-cocycles. It takes a record ocr as first argument which must contain at least the components group for the group and modulePcgs for a (modulo) pcgs of the module. This record will also be returned with components as described under OneCocycles (39.23-1) (with the exception of isSplitExtension which is indicated by the existence of a complement) but components such as oneCoboundaries will only be computed if not already present.

If onlySplit is true, OCOneCocycles returns false as soon as possible if the extension does not split.
OCOneCocycles.title: OCOneCocycles

ComplementClassesRepresentativesEA.name: ComplementClassesRepresentativesEA
ComplementClassesRepresentativesEA.description: computes complement classes to an elementary abelian normal subgroup N via 1-Cohomology. Normally, a user program should call ComplementClassesRepresentatives (39.11-6) instead, which also works for a solvable (not necessarily elementary abelian) N.
ComplementClassesRepresentativesEA.title: ComplementClassesRepresentativesEA

InfoCoh.name: InfoCoh
InfoCoh.description: The info class for the cohomology calculations is InfoCoh.
InfoCoh.title: InfoCoh

EpimorphismSchurCover.name: EpimorphismSchurCover
EpimorphismSchurCover.description: returns an epimorphism epi from a group D onto G. The group D is one (of possibly several) Schur covers of G. The group D can be obtained as the Source (32.3-8) value of epi. The kernel of epi is the Schur multiplier of G. If pl is given as a list of primes, only the multiplier part for these primes is realized. At the moment, D is represented as a finitely presented group.
EpimorphismSchurCover.title: EpimorphismSchurCover

SchurCover.name: SchurCover
SchurCover.description:
returns one (of possibly several) Schur covers of the group G.

At the moment this cover is represented as a finitely presented group and IsomorphismPermGroup (43.3-1) would be needed to convert it to a permutation group.

If also the relation to G is needed, EpimorphismSchurCover (39.24-1) should be used.
SchurCover.title: SchurCover

AbelianInvariantsMultiplier.name: AbelianInvariantsMultiplier
AbelianInvariantsMultiplier.description:
returns a list of the abelian invariants of the Schur multiplier of G.

At the moment, this operation will not give any information about how to extend the multiplier to a Schur Cover.

(Note that the last command from the example will take some time.)
AbelianInvariantsMultiplier.title: AbelianInvariantsMultiplier

Epicentre.name: Epicentre
Epicentre.description: There are various ways of describing the epicentre of a group G. It is the smallest normal subgroup N of G such that G/N is a central quotient of a group. It is also equal to the Exterior Center of G, see [Ell98].
Epicentre.title: Epicentre

ExteriorCentre.name: ExteriorCentre
ExteriorCentre.description: There are various ways of describing the epicentre of a group G. It is the smallest normal subgroup N of G such that G/N is a central quotient of a group. It is also equal to the Exterior Center of G, see [Ell98].
ExteriorCentre.title: Epicentre

NonabelianExteriorSquare.name: NonabelianExteriorSquare
NonabelianExteriorSquare.description: Computes the nonabelian exterior square G ∧ G of the group G, which for a finitely presented group is the derived subgroup of any Schur cover of G (see [BJR87]).
NonabelianExteriorSquare.title: NonabelianExteriorSquare

EpimorphismNonabelianExteriorSquare.name: EpimorphismNonabelianExteriorSquare
EpimorphismNonabelianExteriorSquare.description: Computes the mapping G ∧ G → G. The kernel of this mapping is equal to the Schur multiplier of G.
EpimorphismNonabelianExteriorSquare.title: EpimorphismNonabelianExteriorSquare

IsCentralFactor.name: IsCentralFactor
IsCentralFactor.description: This function determines if there exists a group H such that G is isomorphic to the quotient H/Z(H). A group with this property is called in literature capable. A group being capable is equivalent to the epicentre of G being trivial, see [BFS79].
IsCentralFactor.title: IsCentralFactor

BasicSpinRepresentationOfSymmetricGroup.name: BasicSpinRepresentationOfSymmetricGroup
BasicSpinRepresentationOfSymmetricGroup.description: Constructs the image of the Coxeter generators in the basic spin (projective) representation of the symmetric group of degree n over a field of characteristic p ≥ 0. There are two such representations and sign controls which is returned: +1 gives a group where the preimage of an adjacent transposition (i,i+1) has order 4, -1 gives a group where the preimage of an adjacent transposition (i,i+1) has order 2. If no sign is specified, +1 is used by default. If no p is specified, 3 is used by default. (Note that the convention of which cover is labelled as +1 is inconsistent in the literature.)
BasicSpinRepresentationOfSymmetricGroup.title: BasicSpinRepresentationOfSymmetricGroup

SchurCoverOfSymmetricGroup.name: SchurCoverOfSymmetricGroup
SchurCoverOfSymmetricGroup.description: Constructs a Schur cover of SymmetricGroup(n) as a faithful, irreducible matrix group in characteristic p (p ≠ 2). For n ≥ 4, there are two such covers, and sign determines which is returned: +1 gives a group where the preimage of an adjacent transposition (i,i+1) has order 4, -1 gives a group where the preimage of an adjacent transposition (i,i+1) has order 2. If no sign is specified, +1 is used by default. If no p is specified, 3 is used by default. (Note that the convention of which cover is labelled as +1 is inconsistent in the literature.) For n ≤ 3, the symmetric group is its own Schur cover and sign is ignored. For p = 2, there is no faithful, irreducible representation of the Schur cover unless n = 1 or n = 3, so fail is returned if p = 2. For p = 3, n = 3, the representation is indecomposable, but reducible. The field of the matrix group is generally GF(p^2) if p > 0, and an abelian number field if p = 0.
SchurCoverOfSymmetricGroup.title: SchurCoverOfSymmetricGroup

DoubleCoverOfAlternatingGroup.name: DoubleCoverOfAlternatingGroup
DoubleCoverOfAlternatingGroup.description: Constructs a double cover of AlternatingGroup(n) as a faithful, completely reducible matrix group in characteristic p (p ≠ 2) for n ≥ 4. For n ≤ 3, the alternating group is its own Schur cover, and fail is returned. For p = 2, there is no faithful, completely reducible representation of the double cover, so fail is returned. The field of the matrix group is generally GF(p^2) if p>0, and an abelian number field if p=0. If p is omitted, the default is 3.
DoubleCoverOfAlternatingGroup.title: DoubleCoverOfAlternatingGroup

TwoCohomologyGeneric.name: TwoCohomologyGeneric
TwoCohomologyGeneric.description: This function computes the second cohomology group for an arbitrary finite group G. The generators of the module M must correspond to the generators of G. It returns a record with components coboundaries, cocycles and cohomology which are lists of vectors that form a basis of the respective group. cohomology is chosen as a vector space complement to coboundaries in the cocycles. These vectors are representing tails in M with respect to the relators of the presentation presentation of G. (Note that this presentation is on a generating set chosen by the routine, this generating system corresponds to the components group and module of the record returned. The extension corresponding to a cocyle c can be constructed as FpGroupCocycle(r,c) where r is the cohomology record. This is currently done as a finitely presented group.
TwoCohomologyGeneric.title: TwoCohomologyGeneric

FpGroupCocycle.name: FpGroupCocycle
FpGroupCocycle.description:
For a record r as returned by TwoCohomologyGeneric (39.25-1) and a vector c in the space of two-cocycles, this operation returns a finitely presented group that is an extension corresponding to the cocycle c. If the underlying module has dimension d, the last d generators generate the normal subgroup. If the optional parameter doperm is given as true, a faithful permutation representation is computed and stored in the attribute IsomorphismPermGroup (43.3-1) of the computed group. If the option normalform is given as true, arithmetic in the resulting finitely presented group will bring words into normal form.

Also see Section 46.8 for operations and methods specific for Pc groups.
FpGroupCocycle.title: FpGroupCocycle

CanEasilyTestMembership.name: CanEasilyTestMembership
CanEasilyTestMembership.description: This filter indicates whether GAP can test membership of elements in the group G (via the operation \in (30.6-1)) in reasonable time. It is used by the method selection to decide whether an algorithm that relies on membership tests may be used.
CanEasilyTestMembership.title: CanEasilyTestMembership

CanEasilyComputeWithIndependentGensAbeli.name: CanEasilyComputeWithIndependentGensAbelianGroup
CanEasilyComputeWithIndependentGensAbeli.description: This filter indicates whether GAP can in reasonable time compute independent abelian generators of the group G (via IndependentGeneratorsOfAbelianGroup (39.22-5)) and then can decompose arbitrary group elements with respect to these generators using IndependentGeneratorExponents (39.22-6). It is used by the method selection to decide whether an algorithm that relies on these two operations may be used.
CanEasilyComputeWithIndependentGensAbeli.title: CanEasilyComputeWithIndependentGensAbelianGroup

CanComputeSize.name: CanComputeSize
CanComputeSize.description: This filter indicates that we know that the size of the domain dom (which might be infinity (18.2-1)) can be computed reasonably easily. It doesn't imply as quick a computation as HasSize would but its absence does not imply that the size cannot be computed.
CanComputeSize.title: CanComputeSize

CanComputeSizeAnySubgroup.name: CanComputeSizeAnySubgroup
CanComputeSizeAnySubgroup.description: This filter indicates whether GAP can easily compute the size of any subgroup of the group G. (This is for example advantageous if one can test that a stabilizer index equals the length of the orbit computed so far to stop early.)
CanComputeSizeAnySubgroup.title: CanComputeSizeAnySubgroup

CanComputeIndex.name: CanComputeIndex
CanComputeIndex.description: This function indicates whether the index [G:H] (which might be infinity (18.2-1)) can be computed. It assumes that H ≤ G (see CanComputeIsSubset (39.26-6)).
CanComputeIndex.title: CanComputeIndex

CanComputeIsSubset.name: CanComputeIsSubset
CanComputeIsSubset.description: This filter indicates that GAP can test (via IsSubset (30.5-1)) whether B is a subset of A.
CanComputeIsSubset.title: CanComputeIsSubset

KnowsHowToDecompose.name: KnowsHowToDecompose
KnowsHowToDecompose.description:
Tests whether the group G can decompose elements in the generators gens. If gens is not given it tests, whether it can decompose in the generators given in the GeneratorsOfGroup (39.2-4) value of G.

This property can be used for example to check whether a group homomorphism by images (see GroupHomomorphismByImages (40.1-1)) can be reasonably defined from this group.
KnowsHowToDecompose.title: KnowsHowToDecompose

NormalizerViaRadical.name: NormalizerViaRadical
NormalizerViaRadical.description:
This function implements a particular approach, following the SolvableRadical paradigm, for calculating the normalizer of a subgroup S in G. It is at the moment provided only as a separate function, and not as method for the operation Normalizer, as it can often be slower than other built-in routines. In certain hard cases (non-solvable groups with nontrivial radical), however its performance is substantially superior. The function thus is provided as a non-automated tool for advanced users.

Note that this example only demonstrates usage, but that in this case in fact the ordinary Normalizer routine performs faster.
NormalizerViaRadical.title: NormalizerViaRadical

GroupHomomorphismByImages.name: GroupHomomorphismByImages
GroupHomomorphismByImages.description:
GroupHomomorphismByImages returns the group homomorphism with source G and range H that is defined by mapping the list gens of generators of G to the list imgs of images in H.

If omitted, the arguments gens and imgs default to the GeneratorsOfGroup (39.2-4) value of G and H, respectively. If H is not given the mapping is automatically considered as surjective.

If gens does not generate G or if the mapping of the generators does not extend to a homomorphism (i.e., if mapping the generators describes only a multi-valued mapping) then fail is returned.
GroupHomomorphismByImages.title: GroupHomomorphismByImages

GroupHomomorphismByImagesNC.name: GroupHomomorphismByImagesNC
GroupHomomorphismByImagesNC.description:
GroupHomomorphismByImagesNC creates a homomorphism as GroupHomomorphismByImages (40.1-1) does, however it does not test whether gens generates G and that the mapping of gens to imgs indeed defines a group homomorphism. Because these tests can be expensive it can be substantially faster than GroupHomomorphismByImages (40.1-1). Results are unpredictable if the conditions do not hold.

If omitted, the arguments gens and imgs default to the GeneratorsOfGroup (39.2-4) value of G and H, respectively.

(For creating a possibly multi-valued mapping from G to H that respects multiplication and inverses, GroupGeneralMappingByImages (40.1-3) can be used.)
GroupHomomorphismByImagesNC.title: GroupHomomorphismByImagesNC

GroupGeneralMappingByImages.name: GroupGeneralMappingByImages
GroupGeneralMappingByImages.description: returns a general mapping defined by extending the mapping from gens to imgs homomorphically. If the range H is not given the mapping will be made automatically surjective. The NC version does not test whether gens are contained in G or imgs are contained in H. (GroupHomomorphismByImages (40.1-1) creates a group general mapping by images and tests whether it is in IsMapping (32.3-3).)
GroupGeneralMappingByImages.title: GroupGeneralMappingByImages

GroupGeneralMappingByImages.name: GroupGeneralMappingByImages
GroupGeneralMappingByImages.description: returns a general mapping defined by extending the mapping from gens to imgs homomorphically. If the range H is not given the mapping will be made automatically surjective. The NC version does not test whether gens are contained in G or imgs are contained in H. (GroupHomomorphismByImages (40.1-1) creates a group general mapping by images and tests whether it is in IsMapping (32.3-3).)
GroupGeneralMappingByImages.title: GroupGeneralMappingByImages

GroupGeneralMappingByImagesNC.name: GroupGeneralMappingByImagesNC
GroupGeneralMappingByImagesNC.description: returns a general mapping defined by extending the mapping from gens to imgs homomorphically. If the range H is not given the mapping will be made automatically surjective. The NC version does not test whether gens are contained in G or imgs are contained in H. (GroupHomomorphismByImages (40.1-1) creates a group general mapping by images and tests whether it is in IsMapping (32.3-3).)
GroupGeneralMappingByImagesNC.title: GroupGeneralMappingByImages

GroupGeneralMappingByImagesNC.name: GroupGeneralMappingByImagesNC
GroupGeneralMappingByImagesNC.description: returns a general mapping defined by extending the mapping from gens to imgs homomorphically. If the range H is not given the mapping will be made automatically surjective. The NC version does not test whether gens are contained in G or imgs are contained in H. (GroupHomomorphismByImages (40.1-1) creates a group general mapping by images and tests whether it is in IsMapping (32.3-3).)
GroupGeneralMappingByImagesNC.title: GroupGeneralMappingByImages

GroupHomomorphismByFunction.name: GroupHomomorphismByFunction
GroupHomomorphismByFunction.description:
GroupHomomorphismByFunction returns a group homomorphism hom with source S and range R, such that each element s of S is mapped to the element fun( s ), where fun is a GAP function.

If the argument invfun is bound then hom is a bijection between S and R, and the preimage of each element r of R is given by invfun( r ), where invfun is a GAP function.

If five arguments are given and the fourth argument is false then the GAP function prefun can be used to compute a single preimage also if hom is not bijective.
GroupHomomorphismByFunction.title: GroupHomomorphismByFunction

GroupHomomorphismByFunction.name: GroupHomomorphismByFunction
GroupHomomorphismByFunction.description:
GroupHomomorphismByFunction returns a group homomorphism hom with source S and range R, such that each element s of S is mapped to the element fun( s ), where fun is a GAP function.

If the argument invfun is bound then hom is a bijection between S and R, and the preimage of each element r of R is given by invfun( r ), where invfun is a GAP function.

If five arguments are given and the fourth argument is false then the GAP function prefun can be used to compute a single preimage also if hom is not bijective.
GroupHomomorphismByFunction.title: GroupHomomorphismByFunction

AsGroupGeneralMappingByImages.name: AsGroupGeneralMappingByImages
AsGroupGeneralMappingByImages.description: If map is a mapping from one group to another this attribute returns a group general mapping that which implements the same abstract mapping. (Some operations can be performed more effective in this representation, see also IsGroupGeneralMappingByAsGroupGeneralMappingByImages (40.10-3).)
AsGroupGeneralMappingByImages.title: AsGroupGeneralMappingByImages

ImagesSmallestGenerators.name: ImagesSmallestGenerators
ImagesSmallestGenerators.description: returns the list of images of GeneratorsSmallest(Source(map)). This list can be used to compare group homomorphisms. (The standard comparison is to compare the image lists on the set of elements of the source. If however x and y have the same images under a and b, certainly all their products have. Therefore it is sufficient to test this on the images of the smallest generators.)
ImagesSmallestGenerators.title: ImagesSmallestGenerators

IsHandledByNiceMonomorphism.name: IsHandledByNiceMonomorphism
IsHandledByNiceMonomorphism.description: If this property is true, high-valued methods that translate all calculations in obj in the image under the NiceMonomorphism (40.5-2) value of obj become available for obj.
IsHandledByNiceMonomorphism.title: IsHandledByNiceMonomorphism

NiceMonomorphism.name: NiceMonomorphism
NiceMonomorphism.description:
is a homomorphism that is defined (at least) on the whole of obj and whose restriction to obj is injective. The concrete morphism (and also the image group) will depend on the representation of obj.

WARNING: The domain of the homomorphism may be larger than obj. To obtain the image of obj under the homomorphism, use NiceObject (40.5-3); see there for an example where it matters.
NiceMonomorphism.title: NiceMonomorphism

NiceObject.name: NiceObject
NiceObject.description:
The NiceObject value of obj is the image of obj under the mapping stored as the value of NiceMonomorphism (40.5-2) for obj.

A typical example are finite matrix groups, which use a faithful action on vectors to translate all calculations in a permutation group.
NiceObject.title: NiceObject

IsCanonicalNiceMonomorphism.name: IsCanonicalNiceMonomorphism
IsCanonicalNiceMonomorphism.description: A nice monomorphism (see NiceMonomorphism (40.5-2) nhom is canonical if the image set will only depend on the set of group elements but not on the generating set and \< (31.11-1) comparison of group elements translates through the nice monomorphism. This implies that equal objects will always have equal NiceObject (40.5-3) values. In some situations however this condition would be expensive to achieve, therefore it is not guaranteed for every nice monomorphism.
IsCanonicalNiceMonomorphism.title: IsCanonicalNiceMonomorphism

ConjugatorIsomorphism.name: ConjugatorIsomorphism
ConjugatorIsomorphism.description:
Let G be a group, and g an element in the same family as the elements of G. ConjugatorIsomorphism returns the isomorphism from G to G^g defined by h ↦ h^g for all h ∈ G.

If g normalizes G then ConjugatorIsomorphism does the same as ConjugatorAutomorphismNC (40.6-2).
ConjugatorIsomorphism.title: ConjugatorIsomorphism

ConjugatorAutomorphism.name: ConjugatorAutomorphism
ConjugatorAutomorphism.description:
Let G be a group, and g an element in the same family as the elements of G such that g normalizes G. ConjugatorAutomorphism returns the automorphism of G defined by h ↦ h^g for all h ∈ G.

If conjugation by g does not leave G invariant, ConjugatorAutomorphism returns fail; in this case, the isomorphism from G to G^g induced by conjugation with g can be constructed with ConjugatorIsomorphism (40.6-1).

ConjugatorAutomorphismNC does the same as ConjugatorAutomorphism, except that the check is omitted whether g normalizes G and it is assumed that g is chosen to be in G if possible.
ConjugatorAutomorphism.title: ConjugatorAutomorphism

ConjugatorAutomorphismNC.name: ConjugatorAutomorphismNC
ConjugatorAutomorphismNC.description:
Let G be a group, and g an element in the same family as the elements of G such that g normalizes G. ConjugatorAutomorphism returns the automorphism of G defined by h ↦ h^g for all h ∈ G.

If conjugation by g does not leave G invariant, ConjugatorAutomorphism returns fail; in this case, the isomorphism from G to G^g induced by conjugation with g can be constructed with ConjugatorIsomorphism (40.6-1).

ConjugatorAutomorphismNC does the same as ConjugatorAutomorphism, except that the check is omitted whether g normalizes G and it is assumed that g is chosen to be in G if possible.
ConjugatorAutomorphismNC.title: ConjugatorAutomorphism

InnerAutomorphism.name: InnerAutomorphism
InnerAutomorphism.description:
Let G be a group, and g ∈ G. InnerAutomorphism returns the automorphism of G defined by h ↦ h^g for all h ∈ G.

If g is not an element of G, InnerAutomorphism returns fail; in this case, the isomorphism from G to G^g induced by conjugation with g can be constructed with ConjugatorIsomorphism (40.6-1) or with ConjugatorAutomorphism (40.6-2).

InnerAutomorphismNC does the same as InnerAutomorphism, except that the check is omitted whether g ∈ G.
InnerAutomorphism.title: InnerAutomorphism

InnerAutomorphismNC.name: InnerAutomorphismNC
InnerAutomorphismNC.description:
Let G be a group, and g ∈ G. InnerAutomorphism returns the automorphism of G defined by h ↦ h^g for all h ∈ G.

If g is not an element of G, InnerAutomorphism returns fail; in this case, the isomorphism from G to G^g induced by conjugation with g can be constructed with ConjugatorIsomorphism (40.6-1) or with ConjugatorAutomorphism (40.6-2).

InnerAutomorphismNC does the same as InnerAutomorphism, except that the check is omitted whether g ∈ G.
InnerAutomorphismNC.title: InnerAutomorphism

IsConjugatorIsomorphism.name: IsConjugatorIsomorphism
IsConjugatorIsomorphism.description:
Let hom be a group general mapping (see IsGroupGeneralMapping (32.9-4)) with source G. IsConjugatorIsomorphism returns true if hom is induced by conjugation of G by an element g that lies in G or in a group into which G is naturally embedded in the sense described below, and false otherwise.

Natural embeddings are dealt with in the case that G is a permutation group (see Chapter 43), a matrix group (see Chapter 44), a finitely presented group (see Chapter 47), or a group given w.r.t. a polycyclic presentation (see Chapter 46). In all other cases, IsConjugatorIsomorphism may return false if hom is induced by conjugation but is not an inner automorphism.

If IsConjugatorIsomorphism returns true for hom then an element g that induces hom can be accessed as value of the attribute ConjugatorOfConjugatorIsomorphism (40.6-5).
IsConjugatorIsomorphism.title: IsConjugatorIsomorphism

IsConjugatorAutomorphism.name: IsConjugatorAutomorphism
IsConjugatorAutomorphism.description:
Let hom be a group general mapping (see IsGroupGeneralMapping (32.9-4)) with source G. IsConjugatorIsomorphism returns true if hom is induced by conjugation of G by an element g that lies in G or in a group into which G is naturally embedded in the sense described below, and false otherwise.

Natural embeddings are dealt with in the case that G is a permutation group (see Chapter 43), a matrix group (see Chapter 44), a finitely presented group (see Chapter 47), or a group given w.r.t. a polycyclic presentation (see Chapter 46). In all other cases, IsConjugatorIsomorphism may return false if hom is induced by conjugation but is not an inner automorphism.

If IsConjugatorIsomorphism returns true for hom then an element g that induces hom can be accessed as value of the attribute ConjugatorOfConjugatorIsomorphism (40.6-5).
IsConjugatorAutomorphism.title: IsConjugatorIsomorphism

IsInnerAutomorphism.name: IsInnerAutomorphism
IsInnerAutomorphism.description:
Let hom be a group general mapping (see IsGroupGeneralMapping (32.9-4)) with source G. IsConjugatorIsomorphism returns true if hom is induced by conjugation of G by an element g that lies in G or in a group into which G is naturally embedded in the sense described below, and false otherwise.

Natural embeddings are dealt with in the case that G is a permutation group (see Chapter 43), a matrix group (see Chapter 44), a finitely presented group (see Chapter 47), or a group given w.r.t. a polycyclic presentation (see Chapter 46). In all other cases, IsConjugatorIsomorphism may return false if hom is induced by conjugation but is not an inner automorphism.

If IsConjugatorIsomorphism returns true for hom then an element g that induces hom can be accessed as value of the attribute ConjugatorOfConjugatorIsomorphism (40.6-5).
IsInnerAutomorphism.title: IsConjugatorIsomorphism

ConjugatorOfConjugatorIsomorphism.name: ConjugatorOfConjugatorIsomorphism
ConjugatorOfConjugatorIsomorphism.description:
For a conjugator isomorphism hom (see ConjugatorIsomorphism (40.6-1)), ConjugatorOfConjugatorIsomorphism returns an element g such that mapping under hom is induced by conjugation with g.

To avoid problems with IsInnerAutomorphism (40.6-4), it is guaranteed that the conjugator is taken from the source of hom if possible.
ConjugatorOfConjugatorIsomorphism.title: ConjugatorOfConjugatorIsomorphism

AutomorphismGroup.name: AutomorphismGroup
AutomorphismGroup.description:
returns the full automorphism group of the group G. The automorphisms act on G by the caret operator ^. The automorphism group often stores a NiceMonomorphism (40.5-2) value whose image is a permutation group, obtained by the action on a subset of G.

Note that current methods for the calculation of the automorphism group of a group G require G to be a permutation group or a pc group to be efficient. For groups in other representations the calculation is likely very slow.

Also, the AutPGrp package installs enhanced methods for AutomorphismGroup for finite p-groups, and the FGA package - for finitely generated subgroups of free groups.
AutomorphismGroup.title: AutomorphismGroup

IsGroupOfAutomorphisms.name: IsGroupOfAutomorphisms
IsGroupOfAutomorphisms.description: indicates whether G consists of automorphisms of another group H. The group H can be obtained from G via the attribute AutomorphismDomain (40.7-3).
IsGroupOfAutomorphisms.title: IsGroupOfAutomorphisms

AutomorphismDomain.name: AutomorphismDomain
AutomorphismDomain.description: If G consists of automorphisms of H, this attribute returns H.
AutomorphismDomain.title: AutomorphismDomain

IsAutomorphismGroup.name: IsAutomorphismGroup
IsAutomorphismGroup.description: indicates whether G, which must be IsGroupOfAutomorphisms (40.7-2), is the full automorphism group of another group H, this group is given as AutomorphismDomain (40.7-3) value of G.
IsAutomorphismGroup.title: IsAutomorphismGroup

InnerAutomorphismsAutomorphismGroup.name: InnerAutomorphismsAutomorphismGroup
InnerAutomorphismsAutomorphismGroup.description: For an automorphism group autgroup of a group this attribute stores the subgroup of inner automorphisms (automorphisms induced by conjugation) of the original group.
InnerAutomorphismsAutomorphismGroup.title: InnerAutomorphismsAutomorphismGroup

InnerAutomorphismGroup.name: InnerAutomorphismGroup
InnerAutomorphismGroup.description: For a group G this attribute stores the group of inner automorphisms (automorphisms induced by conjugation) of the original group.
InnerAutomorphismGroup.title: InnerAutomorphismGroup

InducedAutomorphism.name: InducedAutomorphism
InducedAutomorphism.description: Let aut be an automorphism of a group G and epi be a homomorphism from G to a group H such that the kernel of epi is fixed under aut. Let U be the image of epi. This command returns the automorphism of U induced by aut via epi, that is, the automorphism of U which maps g^epi to (g^aut)^epi, for g ∈ G.
InducedAutomorphism.title: InducedAutomorphism

AssignNiceMonomorphismAutomorphismGroup.name: AssignNiceMonomorphismAutomorphismGroup
AssignNiceMonomorphismAutomorphismGroup.description:
computes a nice monomorphism for autgroup acting on group and stores it as NiceMonomorphism (40.5-2) value of autgrp.

If the centre of AutomorphismDomain (40.7-3) of autgrp is trivial, the operation will first try to represent all automorphisms by conjugation (in group or in a natural parent of group).

If this fails the operation tries to find a small subset of group on which the action will be faithful.
AssignNiceMonomorphismAutomorphismGroup.title: AssignNiceMonomorphismAutomorphismGroup

NiceMonomorphismAutomGroup.name: NiceMonomorphismAutomGroup
NiceMonomorphismAutomGroup.description: This function creates a monomorphism for an automorphism group autgrp of a group by permuting the group elements in the list elms. This list must be chosen to yield a faithful representation. elmsgens is a list of generators which are a subset of elms. (They can differ from the group's original generators.) It does not yet assign it as NiceMonomorphism (40.5-2) value.
NiceMonomorphismAutomGroup.title: NiceMonomorphismAutomGroup

IsomorphismGroups.name: IsomorphismGroups
IsomorphismGroups.description:
computes an isomorphism between the groups G and H if they are isomorphic and returns fail otherwise.

With the existing methods the amount of time needed grows with the size of a generating system of G. (Thus in particular for p-groups calculations can be slow.) If you do only need to know whether groups are isomorphic, you might want to consider IdGroup (smallgrp: IdGroup) or the random isomorphism test (see RandomIsomorphismTest (46.10-1)).
IsomorphismGroups.title: IsomorphismGroups

AllHomomorphismClasses.name: AllHomomorphismClasses
AllHomomorphismClasses.description: For two finite groups G and H, this function returns representatives of all homomorphisms G to H up to H-conjugacy.
AllHomomorphismClasses.title: AllHomomorphismClasses

AllHomomorphisms.name: AllHomomorphisms
AllHomomorphisms.description: For two finite groups G and H, this function returns all homomorphisms G to H. Since this number will grow quickly, AllHomomorphismClasses (40.9-2) should be used in most cases. AllEndomorphisms returns all homomorphisms from G to itself, AllAutomorphisms returns all bijective endomorphisms.
AllHomomorphisms.title: AllHomomorphisms

AllEndomorphisms.name: AllEndomorphisms
AllEndomorphisms.description: For two finite groups G and H, this function returns all homomorphisms G to H. Since this number will grow quickly, AllHomomorphismClasses (40.9-2) should be used in most cases. AllEndomorphisms returns all homomorphisms from G to itself, AllAutomorphisms returns all bijective endomorphisms.
AllEndomorphisms.title: AllHomomorphisms

AllAutomorphisms.name: AllAutomorphisms
AllAutomorphisms.description: For two finite groups G and H, this function returns all homomorphisms G to H. Since this number will grow quickly, AllHomomorphismClasses (40.9-2) should be used in most cases. AllEndomorphisms returns all homomorphisms from G to itself, AllAutomorphisms returns all bijective endomorphisms.
AllAutomorphisms.title: AllHomomorphisms

GQuotients.name: GQuotients
GQuotients.description:
computes all epimorphisms from F onto G up to automorphisms of G. This classifies all factor groups of F which are isomorphic to G.

With the existing methods the amount of time needed grows with the size of a generating system of G. (Thus in particular for p-groups calculations can be slow.)

If the findall option is set to false, the algorithm will stop once one homomorphism has been found (this can be faster and might be sufficient if not all homomorphisms are needed).
GQuotients.title: GQuotients

IsomorphicSubgroups.name: IsomorphicSubgroups
IsomorphicSubgroups.description:
computes all monomorphisms from H into G up to G-conjugacy of the image groups. This classifies all G-classes of subgroups of G which are isomorphic to H.

With the existing methods, the amount of time needed grows with the size of a generating system of G. (Thus in particular for p-groups calculations can be slow.) A main use of IsomorphicSubgroups therefore is to find nonsolvable subgroups (which often can be generated by 2 elements).

(To find p-subgroups it is often faster to compute the subgroup lattice of the Sylow subgroup and to use IdGroup (smallgrp: IdGroup) to identify the type of the subgroups.)
IsomorphicSubgroups.title: IsomorphicSubgroups

MorClassLoop.name: MorClassLoop
MorClassLoop.description:
This function loops over element tuples taken from classes and checks these for properties such as generating a given group, or fulfilling relations. This can be used to find small generating sets or all types of Morphisms. The element tuples are used only up to inner automorphisms as all images can be obtained easily from them by conjugation while running through all of them usually would take too long.

range is a group from which these elements are taken. The classes are given in a list classes which is a list of records with the following components.

list of conjugacy classes
MorClassLoop.title: MorClassLoop

IsGroupGeneralMappingByImages.name: IsGroupGeneralMappingByImages
IsGroupGeneralMappingByImages.description: Representation for mappings from one group to another that are defined by extending a mapping of group generators homomorphically. Instead of record components, the attribute MappingGeneratorsImages (40.10-2) is used to store generators and their images.
IsGroupGeneralMappingByImages.title: IsGroupGeneralMappingByImages

MappingGeneratorsImages.name: MappingGeneratorsImages
MappingGeneratorsImages.description: This attribute contains a list of length 2, the first entry being a list of generators of the source of map and the second entry a list of their images. This attribute is used, for example, by GroupHomomorphismByImages (40.1-1) to store generators and images.
MappingGeneratorsImages.title: MappingGeneratorsImages

IsGroupGeneralMappingByAsGroupGeneralMap.name: IsGroupGeneralMappingByAsGroupGeneralMappingByImages
IsGroupGeneralMappingByAsGroupGeneralMap.description: Representation for mappings that delegate work on a GroupHomomorphismByImages (40.1-1).
IsGroupGeneralMappingByAsGroupGeneralMap.title: IsGroupGeneralMappingByAsGroupGeneralMappingByImages

IsPreimagesByAsGroupGeneralMappingByImag.name: IsPreimagesByAsGroupGeneralMappingByImages
IsPreimagesByAsGroupGeneralMappingByImag.description: Representation for mappings that delegate work for preimages to a mapping created with GroupHomomorphismByImages (40.1-1).
IsPreimagesByAsGroupGeneralMappingByImag.title: IsPreimagesByAsGroupGeneralMappingByImages

IsPermGroupGeneralMapping.name: IsPermGroupGeneralMapping
IsPermGroupGeneralMapping.description: are the representations for mappings that map from a perm group
IsPermGroupGeneralMapping.title: IsPermGroupGeneralMapping

IsPermGroupGeneralMappingByImages.name: IsPermGroupGeneralMappingByImages
IsPermGroupGeneralMappingByImages.description: are the representations for mappings that map from a perm group
IsPermGroupGeneralMappingByImages.title: IsPermGroupGeneralMapping

IsPermGroupHomomorphism.name: IsPermGroupHomomorphism
IsPermGroupHomomorphism.description: are the representations for mappings that map from a perm group
IsPermGroupHomomorphism.title: IsPermGroupGeneralMapping

IsPermGroupHomomorphismByImages.name: IsPermGroupHomomorphismByImages
IsPermGroupHomomorphismByImages.description: are the representations for mappings that map from a perm group
IsPermGroupHomomorphismByImages.title: IsPermGroupGeneralMapping

IsToPermGroupGeneralMappingByImages.name: IsToPermGroupGeneralMappingByImages
IsToPermGroupGeneralMappingByImages.description: is the representation for mappings that map to a perm group
IsToPermGroupGeneralMappingByImages.title: IsToPermGroupGeneralMappingByImages

IsToPermGroupHomomorphismByImages.name: IsToPermGroupHomomorphismByImages
IsToPermGroupHomomorphismByImages.description: is the representation for mappings that map to a perm group
IsToPermGroupHomomorphismByImages.title: IsToPermGroupGeneralMappingByImages

IsGroupGeneralMappingByPcgs.name: IsGroupGeneralMappingByPcgs
IsGroupGeneralMappingByPcgs.description: is the representations for mappings that map a pcgs to images and thus may use exponents to decompose generators.
IsGroupGeneralMappingByPcgs.title: IsGroupGeneralMappingByPcgs

IsPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages.name: IsPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages
IsPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages.description: is the representation for mappings from a pc group
IsPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages.title: IsPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages

IsPcGroupHomomorphismByImages.name: IsPcGroupHomomorphismByImages
IsPcGroupHomomorphismByImages.description: is the representation for mappings from a pc group
IsPcGroupHomomorphismByImages.title: IsPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages

IsToPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages.name: IsToPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages
IsToPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages.description: is the representation for mappings to a pc group
IsToPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages.title: IsToPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages

IsToPcGroupHomomorphismByImages.name: IsToPcGroupHomomorphismByImages
IsToPcGroupHomomorphismByImages.description: is the representation for mappings to a pc group
IsToPcGroupHomomorphismByImages.title: IsToPcGroupGeneralMappingByImages

IsFromFpGroupGeneralMappingByImages.name: IsFromFpGroupGeneralMappingByImages
IsFromFpGroupGeneralMappingByImages.description: is the representation of mappings from an fp group.
IsFromFpGroupGeneralMappingByImages.title: IsFromFpGroupGeneralMappingByImages

IsFromFpGroupHomomorphismByImages.name: IsFromFpGroupHomomorphismByImages
IsFromFpGroupHomomorphismByImages.description: is the representation of mappings from an fp group.
IsFromFpGroupHomomorphismByImages.title: IsFromFpGroupGeneralMappingByImages

IsFromFpGroupStdGensGeneralMappingByImag.name: IsFromFpGroupStdGensGeneralMappingByImages
IsFromFpGroupStdGensGeneralMappingByImag.description: is the representation of total mappings from an fp group that give images of the standard generators.
IsFromFpGroupStdGensGeneralMappingByImag.title: IsFromFpGroupStdGensGeneralMappingByImages

IsFromFpGroupStdGensHomomorphismByImages.name: IsFromFpGroupStdGensHomomorphismByImages
IsFromFpGroupStdGensHomomorphismByImages.description: is the representation of total mappings from an fp group that give images of the standard generators.
IsFromFpGroupStdGensHomomorphismByImages.title: IsFromFpGroupStdGensGeneralMappingByImages

OnPoints.name: OnPoints
OnPoints.description: returns pnt ^ g. This is for example the action of a permutation group on points, or the action of a group on its elements via conjugation, that is, if both pnt and g are elements from a common group then pnt ^ g is equal to g^{-1}*pnt*g. The action of a matrix group on vectors from the right is described by both OnPoints and OnRight (41.2-2).
OnPoints.title: OnPoints

OnRight.name: OnRight
OnRight.description: returns pnt * g. This is for example the action of a group on its elements via right multiplication, or the action of a group on the cosets of a subgroup. The action of a matrix group on vectors from the right is described by both OnPoints (41.2-1) and OnRight.
OnRight.title: OnRight

OnLeftInverse.name: OnLeftInverse
OnLeftInverse.description:
returns g^{-1} * pnt. Forming the inverse is necessary to make this a proper action, as in GAP groups always act from the right.

OnLeftInverse is used for example in the representation of a right coset as an external set (see 41.12), that is, a right coset Ug is an external set for the group U acting on it via OnLeftInverse.)
OnLeftInverse.title: OnLeftInverse

OnSets.name: OnSets
OnSets.description:
Let set be a proper set (see 21.19). OnSets returns the proper set formed by the images of all points x of set via the action function OnPoints (41.2-1), applied to x and g.

OnSets is for example used to compute the action of a permutation group on blocks.

(OnTuples (41.2-5) is an action on lists that preserves the ordering of entries.)
OnSets.title: OnSets

OnTuples.name: OnTuples
OnTuples.description:
Let tup be a list. OnTuples returns the list formed by the images of all points x of tup via the action function OnPoints (41.2-1), applied to x and g.

(OnSets (41.2-4) is an action on lists that additionally sorts the entries of the result.)
OnTuples.title: OnTuples

OnPairs.name: OnPairs
OnPairs.description: is a special case of OnTuples (41.2-5) for lists tup of length 2.
OnPairs.title: OnPairs

OnSetsSets.name: OnSetsSets
OnSetsSets.description: implements the action on sets of sets. For the special case that the sets are pairwise disjoint, it is possible to use OnSetsDisjointSets (41.2-8). set must be a sorted list whose entries are again sorted lists, otherwise an error is triggered (see 41.3).
OnSetsSets.title: OnSetsSets

OnSetsDisjointSets.name: OnSetsDisjointSets
OnSetsDisjointSets.description: implements the action on sets of pairwise disjoint sets (see also OnSetsSets (41.2-7)). set must be a sorted list whose entries are again sorted lists, otherwise an error is triggered (see 41.3).
OnSetsDisjointSets.title: OnSetsDisjointSets

OnSetsTuples.name: OnSetsTuples
OnSetsTuples.description: implements the action on sets of tuples. set must be a sorted list, otherwise an error is triggered (see 41.3).
OnSetsTuples.title: OnSetsTuples

OnTuplesSets.name: OnTuplesSets
OnTuplesSets.description: implements the action on tuples of sets. set must be a list whose entries are again sorted lists, otherwise an error is triggered (see 41.3).
OnTuplesSets.title: OnTuplesSets

OnTuplesTuples.name: OnTuplesTuples
OnTuplesTuples.description: implements the action on tuples of tuples.
OnTuplesTuples.title: OnTuplesTuples

OnLines.name: OnLines
OnLines.description:
Let vec be a normed row vector, that is, its first nonzero entry is normed to the identity of the relevant field, see NormedRowVector (23.2-1). The function OnLines returns the row vector obtained from first multiplying vec from the right with g (via OnRight (41.2-2)) and then normalizing the resulting row vector by scalar multiplication from the left.

This action corresponds to the projective action of a matrix group on one-dimensional subspaces.

If vec is a zero vector or is not normed then an error is triggered (see 41.3).
OnLines.title: OnLines

OnIndeterminates.name: OnIndeterminates
OnIndeterminates.description: A permutation perm acts on the multivariate polynomial poly by permuting the indeterminates as it permutes points.
OnIndeterminates.title: OnIndeterminates

OnSubspacesByCanonicalBasis.name: OnSubspacesByCanonicalBasis
OnSubspacesByCanonicalBasis.description:
implements the operation of a matrix group on subspaces of a vector space. bas must be a list of (linearly independent) vectors which forms a basis of the subspace in Hermite normal form. mat is an element of the acting matrix group. The function returns a mutable matrix which gives the basis of the image of the subspace in Hermite normal form. (In other words: it triangulizes the product of bas with mat.)

bas must be given in Hermite normal form, otherwise an error is triggered (see 41.3).
OnSubspacesByCanonicalBasis.title: OnSubspacesByCanonicalBasis

OnSubspacesByCanonicalBasisConcatenation.name: OnSubspacesByCanonicalBasisConcatenations
OnSubspacesByCanonicalBasisConcatenation.description:
implements the operation of a matrix group on subspaces of a vector space. bas must be a list of (linearly independent) vectors which forms a basis of the subspace in Hermite normal form. mat is an element of the acting matrix group. The function returns a mutable matrix which gives the basis of the image of the subspace in Hermite normal form. (In other words: it triangulizes the product of bas with mat.)

bas must be given in Hermite normal form, otherwise an error is triggered (see 41.3).
OnSubspacesByCanonicalBasisConcatenation.title: OnSubspacesByCanonicalBasis

Orbit.name: Orbit
Orbit.description:
The orbit of the point pnt is the list of all images of pnt under the action of the group G w.r.t. the action function act or OnPoints (41.2-1) if no action function is given.

(Note that the arrangement of points in this list is not defined by the operation.)

The orbit of pnt will always contain one element that is equal to pnt, however for performance reasons this element is not necessarily identical to pnt, in particular if pnt is mutable.
Orbit.title: Orbit

Orbits.name: Orbits
Orbits.description:
returns a duplicate-free list of the orbits of the elements in seeds under the action act of G or under OnPoints (41.2-1) if no action function is given.

For a permutation group G, one may also invoke this as Orbits(G), which returns all the orbits of its natural action on the set of points moved by it. For example the group ⟨ (1,2,3), (4,5) ⟩ has the orbits {1,2,3} and {4,5}.

(Note that the arrangement of orbits or of points within one orbit is not defined by the operation.)
Orbits.title: Orbits

Orbits.name: Orbits
Orbits.description:
returns a duplicate-free list of the orbits of the elements in seeds under the action act of G or under OnPoints (41.2-1) if no action function is given.

For a permutation group G, one may also invoke this as Orbits(G), which returns all the orbits of its natural action on the set of points moved by it. For example the group ⟨ (1,2,3), (4,5) ⟩ has the orbits {1,2,3} and {4,5}.

(Note that the arrangement of orbits or of points within one orbit is not defined by the operation.)
Orbits.title: Orbits

Orbits.name: Orbits
Orbits.description:
returns a duplicate-free list of the orbits of the elements in seeds under the action act of G or under OnPoints (41.2-1) if no action function is given.

For a permutation group G, one may also invoke this as Orbits(G), which returns all the orbits of its natural action on the set of points moved by it. For example the group ⟨ (1,2,3), (4,5) ⟩ has the orbits {1,2,3} and {4,5}.

(Note that the arrangement of orbits or of points within one orbit is not defined by the operation.)
Orbits.title: Orbits

OrbitsDomain.name: OrbitsDomain
OrbitsDomain.description:
returns a list of the orbits of G on the domain Omega (given as lists) under the action act or under OnPoints (41.2-1) if no action function is given.

This operation is often faster than Orbits (41.4-2). The domain Omega must be closed under the action of G, otherwise an error can occur.

For a permutation group G, one may also invoke this as OrbitsDomain(G), which returns all the orbits of its natural action on the set of points moved by it.
OrbitsDomain.title: OrbitsDomain

OrbitsDomain.name: OrbitsDomain
OrbitsDomain.description:
returns a list of the orbits of G on the domain Omega (given as lists) under the action act or under OnPoints (41.2-1) if no action function is given.

This operation is often faster than Orbits (41.4-2). The domain Omega must be closed under the action of G, otherwise an error can occur.

For a permutation group G, one may also invoke this as OrbitsDomain(G), which returns all the orbits of its natural action on the set of points moved by it.
OrbitsDomain.title: OrbitsDomain

OrbitsDomain.name: OrbitsDomain
OrbitsDomain.description:
returns a list of the orbits of G on the domain Omega (given as lists) under the action act or under OnPoints (41.2-1) if no action function is given.

This operation is often faster than Orbits (41.4-2). The domain Omega must be closed under the action of G, otherwise an error can occur.

For a permutation group G, one may also invoke this as OrbitsDomain(G), which returns all the orbits of its natural action on the set of points moved by it.
OrbitsDomain.title: OrbitsDomain

OrbitLength.name: OrbitLength
OrbitLength.description: computes the length of the orbit of pnt under the action function act or OnPoints (41.2-1) if no action function is given.
OrbitLength.title: OrbitLength

OrbitLengths.name: OrbitLengths
OrbitLengths.description:
computes the lengths of all the orbits of the elements in seeds under the action act of G.

For a permutation group G, one may also invoke this as OrbitLengths(G), which returns the lengths of all the orbits of its natural action on the set of points moved by it. For example the group ⟨ (1,2,3), (5,6) ⟩ has the orbit lengths 2 and 3.
OrbitLengths.title: OrbitLengths

OrbitLengths.name: OrbitLengths
OrbitLengths.description:
computes the lengths of all the orbits of the elements in seeds under the action act of G.

For a permutation group G, one may also invoke this as OrbitLengths(G), which returns the lengths of all the orbits of its natural action on the set of points moved by it. For example the group ⟨ (1,2,3), (5,6) ⟩ has the orbit lengths 2 and 3.
OrbitLengths.title: OrbitLengths

OrbitLengths.name: OrbitLengths
OrbitLengths.description:
computes the lengths of all the orbits of the elements in seeds under the action act of G.

For a permutation group G, one may also invoke this as OrbitLengths(G), which returns the lengths of all the orbits of its natural action on the set of points moved by it. For example the group ⟨ (1,2,3), (5,6) ⟩ has the orbit lengths 2 and 3.
OrbitLengths.title: OrbitLengths

OrbitLengthsDomain.name: OrbitLengthsDomain
OrbitLengthsDomain.description:
computes the lengths of all the orbits of G on Omega.
