The value of CrystGroupDefaultAction can be changed with the function SetCrystGroupDefaultAction (44.7-2).
CrystGroupDefaultAction.title: CrystGroupDefaultAction

SetCrystGroupDefaultAction.name: SetCrystGroupDefaultAction
SetCrystGroupDefaultAction.description: allows one to set the value of the global variable CrystGroupDefaultAction (44.7-1). Only the arguments RightAction and LeftAction are allowed. Initially, the value of CrystGroupDefaultAction (44.7-1) is RightAction.
SetCrystGroupDefaultAction.title: SetCrystGroupDefaultAction

Pcgs.name: Pcgs
Pcgs.description: returns a pcgs for the group G. If grp is not polycyclic it returns fail and this result is not stored as attribute value, in particular in this case the filter HasPcgs is not set for G!
Pcgs.title: Pcgs

CanEasilyComputePcgs.name: CanEasilyComputePcgs
CanEasilyComputePcgs.description: This filter indicates whether it is possible to compute a pcgs for grp cheaply. Clearly, grp must be polycyclic in this case. However, not for every polycyclic group there is a method to compute a pcgs at low costs. This filter is used in the method selection mainly. Note that this filter may change its value from false to true.
CanEasilyComputePcgs.title: CanEasilyComputePcgs

PcgsByPcSequence.name: PcgsByPcSequence
PcgsByPcSequence.description: constructs a pcgs for the elements family fam from the elements in the list pcs. The elements must lie in the family fam. PcgsByPcSequence and its NC variant will always create a new pcgs which is not induced by any other pcgs (cf. InducedPcgsByPcSequence (45.7-2)).
PcgsByPcSequence.title: PcgsByPcSequence

PcgsByPcSequenceNC.name: PcgsByPcSequenceNC
PcgsByPcSequenceNC.description: constructs a pcgs for the elements family fam from the elements in the list pcs. The elements must lie in the family fam. PcgsByPcSequence and its NC variant will always create a new pcgs which is not induced by any other pcgs (cf. InducedPcgsByPcSequence (45.7-2)).
PcgsByPcSequenceNC.title: PcgsByPcSequence

RelativeOrders.name: RelativeOrders
RelativeOrders.description: returns the list of relative orders of the pcgs pcgs.
RelativeOrders.title: RelativeOrders

IsFiniteOrdersPcgs.name: IsFiniteOrdersPcgs
IsFiniteOrdersPcgs.description: tests whether the relative orders of pcgs are all finite.
IsFiniteOrdersPcgs.title: IsFiniteOrdersPcgs

IsPrimeOrdersPcgs.name: IsPrimeOrdersPcgs
IsPrimeOrdersPcgs.description: tests whether the relative orders of pcgs are prime numbers. Many algorithms require a pcgs to have this property. The operation IsomorphismRefinedPcGroup (46.4-8) can be of help here.
IsPrimeOrdersPcgs.title: IsPrimeOrdersPcgs

PcSeries.name: PcSeries
PcSeries.description: returns the subnormal series determined by pcgs.
PcSeries.title: PcSeries

OneOfPcgs.name: OneOfPcgs
OneOfPcgs.description: The identity of the group generated by pcgs.
OneOfPcgs.title: OneOfPcgs

RelativeOrderOfPcElement.name: RelativeOrderOfPcElement
RelativeOrderOfPcElement.description: The relative order of elm with respect to the prime order pcgs pcgs.
RelativeOrderOfPcElement.title: RelativeOrderOfPcElement

ExponentOfPcElement.name: ExponentOfPcElement
ExponentOfPcElement.description: returns the pos-th exponent of elm with respect to pcgs.
ExponentOfPcElement.title: ExponentOfPcElement

ExponentsOfPcElement.name: ExponentsOfPcElement
ExponentsOfPcElement.description: returns the exponents of elm with respect to pcgs. The three argument version returns the exponents in the positions given in posran.
ExponentsOfPcElement.title: ExponentsOfPcElement

DepthOfPcElement.name: DepthOfPcElement
DepthOfPcElement.description: returns the depth of the element elm with respect to pcgs.
DepthOfPcElement.title: DepthOfPcElement

LeadingExponentOfPcElement.name: LeadingExponentOfPcElement
LeadingExponentOfPcElement.description: returns the leading exponent of elm with respect to pcgs.
LeadingExponentOfPcElement.title: LeadingExponentOfPcElement

PcElementByExponents.name: PcElementByExponents
PcElementByExponents.description:
returns the element corresponding to the exponent vector list with respect to pcgs. The exponents in list must be in the range of permissible exponents for pcgs. It is not guaranteed that PcElementByExponents will reduce the exponents modulo the relative orders. (You should use the operation LinearCombinationPcgs (45.5-7) for this purpose.) The NC version does not check that the lengths of the lists fit together and does not check the exponent range.

The three argument version gives exponents only w.r.t. the generators in pcgs indexed by basisind.
PcElementByExponents.title: PcElementByExponents

PcElementByExponentsNC.name: PcElementByExponentsNC
PcElementByExponentsNC.description:
returns the element corresponding to the exponent vector list with respect to pcgs. The exponents in list must be in the range of permissible exponents for pcgs. It is not guaranteed that PcElementByExponents will reduce the exponents modulo the relative orders. (You should use the operation LinearCombinationPcgs (45.5-7) for this purpose.) The NC version does not check that the lengths of the lists fit together and does not check the exponent range.

The three argument version gives exponents only w.r.t. the generators in pcgs indexed by basisind.
PcElementByExponentsNC.title: PcElementByExponents

LinearCombinationPcgs.name: LinearCombinationPcgs
LinearCombinationPcgs.description: returns the product ∏_i pcgs[i]^{list[i]}. In contrast to PcElementByExponents (45.5-6) this permits negative exponents. pcgs might be a list of group elements. In this case, an appropriate identity element one must be given. list can be empty.
LinearCombinationPcgs.title: LinearCombinationPcgs

SiftedPcElement.name: SiftedPcElement
SiftedPcElement.description: sifts elm through pcgs, reducing it if the depth is the same as the depth of one of the generators in pcgs. Thus the identity is returned if elm lies in the group generated by pcgs. pcgs must be an induced pcgs (see section 45.7) and elm must lie in the span of the parent of pcgs.
SiftedPcElement.title: SiftedPcElement

CanonicalPcElement.name: CanonicalPcElement
CanonicalPcElement.description: reduces elm at the induces pcgs ipcgs such that the exponents of the reduced result r are zero at the depths for which there are generators in ipcgs. Elements, whose quotient lies in the group generated by ipcgs yield the same canonical element.
CanonicalPcElement.title: CanonicalPcElement

ReducedPcElement.name: ReducedPcElement
ReducedPcElement.description: reduces the element x by dividing off (from the left) a power of y such that the leading coefficient of the result with respect to pcgs becomes zero. The elements x and y therefore have to have the same depth.
ReducedPcElement.title: ReducedPcElement

CleanedTailPcElement.name: CleanedTailPcElement
CleanedTailPcElement.description:
returns an element in the span of pcgs whose exponents for indices 1 to dep-1 with respect to pcgs are the same as those of elm, the remaining exponents are undefined. This can be used to obtain more simple elements if only representatives in a factor are required, see 45.9.

The difference to HeadPcElementByNumber (45.5-12) is that this function is guaranteed to zero out trailing coefficients while CleanedTailPcElement will only do this if it can be done cheaply.
CleanedTailPcElement.title: CleanedTailPcElement

HeadPcElementByNumber.name: HeadPcElementByNumber
HeadPcElementByNumber.description: returns an element in the span of pcgs whose exponents for indices 1 to dep-1 with respect to pcgs are the same as those of elm, the remaining exponents are zero. This can be used to obtain more simple elements if only representatives in a factor are required.
HeadPcElementByNumber.title: HeadPcElementByNumber

ExponentsConjugateLayer.name: ExponentsConjugateLayer
ExponentsConjugateLayer.description: Computes the exponents of elm^e with respect to mpcgs; elm must be in the span of mpcgs, e a pc element in the span of the parent pcgs of mpcgs and mpcgs must be the modulo pcgs for an abelian layer. (This is the usual case when acting on a chief factor). In this case if mpcgs is induced by the family pcgs (see section 45.7), the exponents can be computed directly by looking up exponents without having to compute in the group and having to collect a potential tail.
ExponentsConjugateLayer.title: ExponentsConjugateLayer

ExponentsOfRelativePower.name: ExponentsOfRelativePower
ExponentsOfRelativePower.description: For p = pcgs[i] this function returns the exponent vector with respect to pcgs of the element p^e where e is the relative order of p in pcgs. For the family pcgs or pcgs induced by it (see section 45.7), this might be faster than computing the element and computing its exponent vector.
ExponentsOfRelativePower.title: ExponentsOfRelativePower

ExponentsOfConjugate.name: ExponentsOfConjugate
ExponentsOfConjugate.description: returns the exponents of pcgs[i]^pcgs[j] with respect to pcgs. For the family pcgs or pcgs induced by it (see section 45.7), this might be faster than computing the element and computing its exponent vector.
ExponentsOfConjugate.title: ExponentsOfConjugate

ExponentsOfCommutator.name: ExponentsOfCommutator
ExponentsOfCommutator.description: returns the exponents of the commutator Comm( pcgs[i], pcgs[j] ) with respect to pcgs. For the family pcgs or pcgs induced by it, (see section 45.7), this might be faster than computing the element and computing its exponent vector.
ExponentsOfCommutator.title: ExponentsOfCommutator

IsInducedPcgs.name: IsInducedPcgs
IsInducedPcgs.description: The category of induced pcgs. This a subcategory of pcgs.
IsInducedPcgs.title: IsInducedPcgs

InducedPcgsByPcSequence.name: InducedPcgsByPcSequence
InducedPcgsByPcSequence.description:
If pcs is a list of elements that form an induced pcgs with respect to pcgs this operation returns an induced pcgs with these elements.

In the third version, the depths of pcs with respect to pcgs can be given (they are computed anew otherwise).
InducedPcgsByPcSequence.title: InducedPcgsByPcSequence

InducedPcgsByPcSequenceNC.name: InducedPcgsByPcSequenceNC
InducedPcgsByPcSequenceNC.description:
If pcs is a list of elements that form an induced pcgs with respect to pcgs this operation returns an induced pcgs with these elements.

In the third version, the depths of pcs with respect to pcgs can be given (they are computed anew otherwise).
InducedPcgsByPcSequenceNC.title: InducedPcgsByPcSequence

ParentPcgs.name: ParentPcgs
ParentPcgs.description: returns the pcgs by which pcgs was induced. If pcgs was not induced, it simply returns pcgs.
ParentPcgs.title: ParentPcgs

InducedPcgs.name: InducedPcgs
InducedPcgs.description:
computes a pcgs for grp which is induced by pcgs. If pcgs has a parent pcgs, then the result is induced with respect to this parent pcgs.

InducedPcgs is a wrapper function only. Therefore, methods for computing an induced pcgs should be installed for the operation InducedPcgsOp.
InducedPcgs.title: InducedPcgs

InducedPcgsByGenerators.name: InducedPcgsByGenerators
InducedPcgsByGenerators.description: returns an induced pcgs with respect to pcgs for the subgroup generated by gens.
InducedPcgsByGenerators.title: InducedPcgsByGenerators

InducedPcgsByGeneratorsNC.name: InducedPcgsByGeneratorsNC
InducedPcgsByGeneratorsNC.description: returns an induced pcgs with respect to pcgs for the subgroup generated by gens.
InducedPcgsByGeneratorsNC.title: InducedPcgsByGenerators

InducedPcgsByPcSequenceAndGenerators.name: InducedPcgsByPcSequenceAndGenerators
InducedPcgsByPcSequenceAndGenerators.description: returns an induced pcgs with respect to pcgs of the subgroup generated by ind and gens. Here ind must be an induced pcgs with respect to pcgs (or a list of group elements that form such an igs) and it will be used as initial sequence for the computation.
InducedPcgsByPcSequenceAndGenerators.title: InducedPcgsByPcSequenceAndGenerators

LeadCoeffsIGS.name: LeadCoeffsIGS
LeadCoeffsIGS.description: This attribute is used to store leading coefficients with respect to the parent pcgs. the i-th entry –if bound– is the leading exponent of the element of igs that has depth i in the parent. (It cannot be assigned to a component in the object created by InducedPcgsByPcSequenceNC (45.7-2) as the permutation group methods call it from within the postprocessing, before this postprocessing however no coefficients may be computed.)
LeadCoeffsIGS.title: LeadCoeffsIGS

ExtendedPcgs.name: ExtendedPcgs
ExtendedPcgs.description: extends the pcgs N (induced w.r.t. home) to a new induced pcgs by prepending gens. No checks are performed that this really yields an induced pcgs.
ExtendedPcgs.title: ExtendedPcgs

SubgroupByPcgs.name: SubgroupByPcgs
SubgroupByPcgs.description: returns a subgroup of G generated by the elements of pcgs.
SubgroupByPcgs.title: SubgroupByPcgs

IsCanonicalPcgs.name: IsCanonicalPcgs
IsCanonicalPcgs.description: An induced pcgs is canonical if the matrix of the exponent vectors of the elements of pcgs with respect to the ParentPcgs (45.7-3) value of pcgs is in Hermite normal form (see [LNS84]). While a subgroup can have various induced pcgs with respect to a parent pcgs a canonical pcgs is unique.
IsCanonicalPcgs.title: IsCanonicalPcgs

CanonicalPcgs.name: CanonicalPcgs
CanonicalPcgs.description: returns the canonical pcgs corresponding to the induced pcgs pcgs.
CanonicalPcgs.title: CanonicalPcgs

ModuloPcgs.name: ModuloPcgs
ModuloPcgs.description: returns a modulo pcgs for the factor G/N which must be solvable, while N may be non-solvable. ModuloPcgs will return a pcgs for the factor, there is no guarantee that it will be compatible with any other pcgs. If this is required, the mod operator must be used on induced pcgs, see \mod (45.9-5).
ModuloPcgs.title: ModuloPcgs

IsModuloPcgs.name: IsModuloPcgs
IsModuloPcgs.description: The category of modulo pcgs. Note that each pcgs is a modulo pcgs for the trivial subgroup.
IsModuloPcgs.title: IsModuloPcgs

NumeratorOfModuloPcgs.name: NumeratorOfModuloPcgs
NumeratorOfModuloPcgs.description: returns a generating set for the numerator of the modulo pcgs pcgs.
NumeratorOfModuloPcgs.title: NumeratorOfModuloPcgs

DenominatorOfModuloPcgs.name: DenominatorOfModuloPcgs
DenominatorOfModuloPcgs.description: returns a generating set for the denominator of the modulo pcgs pcgs.
DenominatorOfModuloPcgs.title: DenominatorOfModuloPcgs

CorrespondingGeneratorsByModuloPcgs.name: CorrespondingGeneratorsByModuloPcgs
CorrespondingGeneratorsByModuloPcgs.description:
Let mpcgs be a modulo pcgs for a factor of a group G and let U be a subgroup of G generated by imgs such that U covers the factor for the modulo pcgs. Then this function computes elements in U corresponding to the generators of the modulo pcgs.

Note that the computation of induced generating sets is not possible for some modulo pcgs.
CorrespondingGeneratorsByModuloPcgs.title: CorrespondingGeneratorsByModuloPcgs

CanonicalPcgsByGeneratorsWithImages.name: CanonicalPcgsByGeneratorsWithImages
CanonicalPcgsByGeneratorsWithImages.description: computes a canonical, pcgs-induced pcgs for the span of gens and simultaneously does the same transformations on imgs, preserving thus a correspondence between gens and imgs. This operation is used to represent homomorphisms from a pc group.
CanonicalPcgsByGeneratorsWithImages.title: CanonicalPcgsByGeneratorsWithImages

ProjectedPcElement.name: ProjectedPcElement
ProjectedPcElement.description: returns the image in H of an element elm of G.
ProjectedPcElement.title: ProjectedPcElement

ProjectedInducedPcgs.name: ProjectedInducedPcgs
ProjectedInducedPcgs.description: ipcgs must be an induced pcgs with respect to pcgs. This operation returns an induced pcgs with respect to fpcgs consisting of the nontrivial images of ipcgs.
ProjectedInducedPcgs.title: ProjectedInducedPcgs

LiftedPcElement.name: LiftedPcElement
LiftedPcElement.description: returns a preimage in G of an element elm of H.
LiftedPcElement.title: LiftedPcElement

LiftedInducedPcgs.name: LiftedInducedPcgs
LiftedInducedPcgs.description: ipcgs must be an induced pcgs with respect to fpcgs. This operation returns an induced pcgs with respect to pcgs consisting of the preimages of ipcgs, appended by the elements in ker (assuming there is a bijection of pcgs mod ker to fpcgs). ker might be a simple element list.
LiftedInducedPcgs.title: LiftedInducedPcgs

IsPcgsElementaryAbelianSeries.name: IsPcgsElementaryAbelianSeries
IsPcgsElementaryAbelianSeries.description: returns true if the pcgs pcgs refines an elementary abelian series. IndicesEANormalSteps (45.11-3) then gives the indices in the Pcgs, at which the subgroups of this series start.
IsPcgsElementaryAbelianSeries.title: IsPcgsElementaryAbelianSeries

PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries.name: PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries
PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries.description: computes a pcgs for G that refines an elementary abelian series. IndicesEANormalSteps (45.11-3) gives the indices in the pcgs, at which the normal subgroups of this series start. The second variant returns a pcgs that runs through the normal subgroups in the list list.
PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries.title: PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries

PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries.name: PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries
PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries.description: computes a pcgs for G that refines an elementary abelian series. IndicesEANormalSteps (45.11-3) gives the indices in the pcgs, at which the normal subgroups of this series start. The second variant returns a pcgs that runs through the normal subgroups in the list list.
PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries.title: PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries

IndicesEANormalSteps.name: IndicesEANormalSteps
IndicesEANormalSteps.description:
Let pcgs be a pcgs obtained as corresponding to a series of normal subgroups with elementary abelian factors (for example from calling PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries (45.11-2)) Then IndicesEANormalSteps returns a sorted list of integers, indicating the tails of pcgs which generate these normal subgroup of G. If i is an element of this list, (g_i, ..., g_n) is a normal subgroup of G. The list always starts with 1 and ends with n+1. (These indices form one series with elementary abelian subfactors, not necessarily the most refined one.)

The attribute EANormalSeriesByPcgs (45.11-4) returns the actual series of subgroups.

For arbitrary pcgs not obtained as belonging to a special series such a set of indices not necessarily exists, and IndicesEANormalSteps is not guaranteed to work in this situation.
IndicesEANormalSteps.title: IndicesEANormalSteps

IndicesEANormalStepsBounded.name: IndicesEANormalStepsBounded
IndicesEANormalStepsBounded.description:
Let pcgs be a pcgs obtained as corresponding to a series of normal subgroups with elementary abelian factors (for example from calling PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries (45.11-2)) Then IndicesEANormalSteps returns a sorted list of integers, indicating the tails of pcgs which generate these normal subgroup of G. If i is an element of this list, (g_i, ..., g_n) is a normal subgroup of G. The list always starts with 1 and ends with n+1. (These indices form one series with elementary abelian subfactors, not necessarily the most refined one.)

The attribute EANormalSeriesByPcgs (45.11-4) returns the actual series of subgroups.

For arbitrary pcgs not obtained as belonging to a special series such a set of indices not necessarily exists, and IndicesEANormalSteps is not guaranteed to work in this situation.
IndicesEANormalStepsBounded.title: IndicesEANormalSteps

EANormalSeriesByPcgs.name: EANormalSeriesByPcgs
EANormalSeriesByPcgs.description: Let pcgs be a pcgs obtained as corresponding to a series of normal subgroups with elementary abelian factors (for example from calling PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries (45.11-2)). This attribute returns the actual series of normal subgroups, corresponding to IndicesEANormalSteps (45.11-3).
EANormalSeriesByPcgs.title: EANormalSeriesByPcgs

IsPcgsCentralSeries.name: IsPcgsCentralSeries
IsPcgsCentralSeries.description: returns true if the pcgs pcgs refines an central elementary abelian series. IndicesCentralNormalSteps (45.11-7) then gives the indices in the pcgs, at which the subgroups of this series start.
IsPcgsCentralSeries.title: IsPcgsCentralSeries

PcgsCentralSeries.name: PcgsCentralSeries
PcgsCentralSeries.description: computes a pcgs for G that refines a central elementary abelian series. IndicesCentralNormalSteps (45.11-7) gives the indices in the pcgs, at which the normal subgroups of this series start.
PcgsCentralSeries.title: PcgsCentralSeries

IndicesCentralNormalSteps.name: IndicesCentralNormalSteps
IndicesCentralNormalSteps.description:
Let pcgs be a pcgs obtained as corresponding to a series of normal subgroups with central elementary abelian factors (for example from calling PcgsCentralSeries (45.11-6)). Then IndicesCentralNormalSteps returns a sorted list of integers, indicating the tails of pcgs which generate these normal subgroups of G. If i is an element of this list, (g_i, ..., g_n) is a normal subgroup of G. The list always starts with 1 and ends with n+1. (These indices form one series with central elementary abelian subfactors, not necessarily the most refined one.)

The attribute CentralNormalSeriesByPcgs (45.11-8) returns the actual series of subgroups.

For arbitrary pcgs not obtained as belonging to a special series such a set of indices not necessarily exists, and IndicesCentralNormalSteps is not guaranteed to work in this situation.
IndicesCentralNormalSteps.title: IndicesCentralNormalSteps

CentralNormalSeriesByPcgs.name: CentralNormalSeriesByPcgs
CentralNormalSeriesByPcgs.description: Let pcgs be a pcgs obtained as corresponding to a series of normal subgroups with central elementary abelian factors (for example from calling PcgsCentralSeries (45.11-6)). This attribute returns the actual series of normal subgroups, corresponding to IndicesCentralNormalSteps (45.11-7).
CentralNormalSeriesByPcgs.title: CentralNormalSeriesByPcgs

IsPcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup.name: IsPcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup
IsPcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup.description: returns true if the pcgs pcgs refines a p-central elementary abelian series for a p-group. IndicesPCentralNormalStepsPGroup (45.11-11) then gives the indices in the pcgs, at which the subgroups of this series start.
IsPcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup.title: IsPcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup

PcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup.name: PcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup
PcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup.description: computes a pcgs for the p-group G that refines a p-central elementary abelian series. IndicesPCentralNormalStepsPGroup (45.11-11) gives the indices in the pcgs, at which the normal subgroups of this series start.
PcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup.title: PcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup

IndicesPCentralNormalStepsPGroup.name: IndicesPCentralNormalStepsPGroup
IndicesPCentralNormalStepsPGroup.description:
Let pcgs be a pcgs obtained as corresponding to a series of normal subgroups with p-central elementary abelian factors (for example from calling PcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup (45.11-10)). Then IndicesPCentralNormalStepsPGroup returns a sorted list of integers, indicating the tails of pcgs which generate these normal subgroups of G. If i is an element of this list, (g_i, ..., g_n) is a normal subgroup of G. The list always starts with 1 and ends with n+1. (These indices form one series with central elementary abelian subfactors, not necessarily the most refined one.)

The attribute PCentralNormalSeriesByPcgsPGroup (45.11-12) returns the actual series of subgroups.

For arbitrary pcgs not obtained as belonging to a special series such a set of indices not necessarily exists, and IndicesPCentralNormalStepsPGroup is not guaranteed to work in this situation.
IndicesPCentralNormalStepsPGroup.title: IndicesPCentralNormalStepsPGroup

PCentralNormalSeriesByPcgsPGroup.name: PCentralNormalSeriesByPcgsPGroup
PCentralNormalSeriesByPcgsPGroup.description: Let pcgs be a pcgs obtained as corresponding to a series of normal subgroups with p-central elementary abelian factors (for example from calling PcgsPCentralSeriesPGroup (45.11-10)). This attribute returns the actual series of normal subgroups, corresponding to IndicesPCentralNormalStepsPGroup (45.11-11).
PCentralNormalSeriesByPcgsPGroup.title: PCentralNormalSeriesByPcgsPGroup

IsPcgsChiefSeries.name: IsPcgsChiefSeries
IsPcgsChiefSeries.description: returns true if the pcgs pcgs refines a chief series. IndicesChiefNormalSteps (45.11-15) then gives the indices in the pcgs, at which the subgroups of this series start.
IsPcgsChiefSeries.title: IsPcgsChiefSeries

PcgsChiefSeries.name: PcgsChiefSeries
PcgsChiefSeries.description: computes a pcgs for G that refines a chief series. IndicesChiefNormalSteps (45.11-15) gives the indices in the pcgs, at which the normal subgroups of this series start.
PcgsChiefSeries.title: PcgsChiefSeries

IndicesChiefNormalSteps.name: IndicesChiefNormalSteps
IndicesChiefNormalSteps.description:
Let pcgs be a pcgs obtained as corresponding to a chief series for example from calling PcgsChiefSeries (45.11-14)). Then IndicesChiefNormalSteps returns a sorted list of integers, indicating the tails of pcgs which generate these normal subgroups of G. If i is an element of this list, (g_i, ..., g_n) is a normal subgroup of G. The list always starts with 1 and ends with n+1. (These indices form one series with elementary abelian subfactors, not necessarily the most refined one.)

The attribute ChiefNormalSeriesByPcgs (45.11-16) returns the actual series of subgroups.

For arbitrary pcgs not obtained as belonging to a special series such a set of indices not necessarily exists, and IndicesChiefNormalSteps is not guaranteed to work in this situation.
IndicesChiefNormalSteps.title: IndicesChiefNormalSteps

ChiefNormalSeriesByPcgs.name: ChiefNormalSeriesByPcgs
ChiefNormalSeriesByPcgs.description: Let pcgs be a pcgs obtained as corresponding to a chief series (for example from calling PcgsChiefSeries (45.11-14)). This attribute returns the actual series of normal subgroups, corresponding to IndicesChiefNormalSteps (45.11-15).
ChiefNormalSeriesByPcgs.title: ChiefNormalSeriesByPcgs

IndicesNormalSteps.name: IndicesNormalSteps
IndicesNormalSteps.description:
returns the indices of all steps in the pc series, which are normal in the group defined by the pcgs.

(In general, this function yields a slower performance than the more specialized index functions for elementary abelian series etc.)
IndicesNormalSteps.title: IndicesNormalSteps

NormalSeriesByPcgs.name: NormalSeriesByPcgs
NormalSeriesByPcgs.description:
returns the subgroups the pc series, which are normal in the group defined by the pcgs.

(In general, this function yields a slower performance than the more specialized index functions for elementary abelian series etc.)
NormalSeriesByPcgs.title: NormalSeriesByPcgs

SumFactorizationFunctionPcgs.name: SumFactorizationFunctionPcgs
SumFactorizationFunctionPcgs.description:
computes the sum and intersection of the lists n and u whose elements form modulo pcgs induced by parentpcgs for two subgroups modulo a kernel given by kerpcgs. If kerpcgs is a tail if the parent-pcgs it is sufficient to give the starting depth, this can be more efficient than to construct an explicit pcgs. The factor group modulo kerpcgs generated by n must be elementary abelian and normal under u.

The function returns a record with components

elements that form a modulo pcgs for the span of both subgroups.
SumFactorizationFunctionPcgs.title: SumFactorizationFunctionPcgs

IsSpecialPcgs.name: IsSpecialPcgs
IsSpecialPcgs.description: tests whether obj is a special pcgs.
IsSpecialPcgs.title: IsSpecialPcgs

SpecialPcgs.name: SpecialPcgs
SpecialPcgs.description: computes a special pcgs for the group defined by pcgs or for G.
SpecialPcgs.title: SpecialPcgs

SpecialPcgs.name: SpecialPcgs
SpecialPcgs.description: computes a special pcgs for the group defined by pcgs or for G.
SpecialPcgs.title: SpecialPcgs

LGWeights.name: LGWeights
LGWeights.description: returns the LGWeights of the special pcgs pcgs.
LGWeights.title: LGWeights

LGLayers.name: LGLayers
LGLayers.description: returns the layers of the special pcgs pcgs.
LGLayers.title: LGLayers

LGFirst.name: LGFirst
LGFirst.description: returns the first indices for each layer of the special pcgs pcgs.
LGFirst.title: LGFirst

LGLength.name: LGLength
LGLength.description:
returns the length of the LG-series of the group G, if G is solvable, and fail otherwise.

Thus the first group, SmallGroup(96, 220), has a lower nilpotent series of length 1; that is, the group is nilpotent. It is a direct product of its Sylow subgroups. Moreover the Sylow 2-subgroup is generated by the elements f1, f2, f3, f4, f6, and the Sylow 3-subgroup is generated by f5. The lower 2-central series of the Sylow 2-subgroup has length 2 and the second subgroup in this series is generated by f6.

The second group, SmallGroup(96, 120), has a lower nilpotent series of length 2 and hence is not nilpotent. The second subgroup in this series is just the Sylow 3-subgroup and it is generated by f6. The subgroup generated by f1, ..., f5 is a Sylow 2-subgroup of the group and also a head complement to the second head of the group. Its lower 2-central series has length 2.
LGLength.title: LGLength

IsInducedPcgsWrtSpecialPcgs.name: IsInducedPcgsWrtSpecialPcgs
IsInducedPcgsWrtSpecialPcgs.description: tests whether pcgs is induced with respect to a special pcgs.
IsInducedPcgsWrtSpecialPcgs.title: IsInducedPcgsWrtSpecialPcgs

InducedPcgsWrtSpecialPcgs.name: InducedPcgsWrtSpecialPcgs
InducedPcgsWrtSpecialPcgs.description:
computes an induced pcgs with respect to the special pcgs of the parent of G.

InducedPcgsWrtSpecialPcgs will return a pcgs induced by a special pcgs (which might differ from the one you had in mind). If you need an induced pcgs compatible with a given special pcgs use InducedPcgs (45.7-4) for this special pcgs.
InducedPcgsWrtSpecialPcgs.title: InducedPcgsWrtSpecialPcgs

VectorSpaceByPcgsOfElementaryAbelianGrou.name: VectorSpaceByPcgsOfElementaryAbelianGroup
VectorSpaceByPcgsOfElementaryAbelianGrou.description: returns the vector space over fld corresponding to the modulo pcgs mpcgs. Note that mpcgs has to define an elementary abelian p-group where p is the characteristic of fld.
VectorSpaceByPcgsOfElementaryAbelianGrou.title: VectorSpaceByPcgsOfElementaryAbelianGroup

LinearAction.name: LinearAction
LinearAction.description: returns a list of matrices, one for each element of gens, which corresponds to the matrix action of the elements in gens on the basis basisvectors via linear.
LinearAction.title: LinearAction

LinearOperation.name: LinearOperation
LinearOperation.description: returns a list of matrices, one for each element of gens, which corresponds to the matrix action of the elements in gens on the basis basisvectors via linear.
LinearOperation.title: LinearAction

LinearActionLayer.name: LinearActionLayer
LinearActionLayer.description: returns a list of matrices, one for each element of gens, which corresponds to the matrix action of G on the vector space corresponding to the modulo pcgs pcgs.
LinearActionLayer.title: LinearActionLayer

LinearOperationLayer.name: LinearOperationLayer
LinearOperationLayer.description: returns a list of matrices, one for each element of gens, which corresponds to the matrix action of G on the vector space corresponding to the modulo pcgs pcgs.
LinearOperationLayer.title: LinearActionLayer

AffineAction.name: AffineAction
AffineAction.description: return a list of matrices, one for each element of gens, which corresponds to the affine action of the elements in gens on the basis basisvectors via linear with translation transl.
AffineAction.title: AffineAction

AffineActionLayer.name: AffineActionLayer
AffineActionLayer.description: returns a list of matrices, one for each element of gens, which corresponds to the affine action of G on the vector space corresponding to the modulo pcgs pcgs with translation transl.
AffineActionLayer.title: AffineActionLayer

StabilizerPcgs.name: StabilizerPcgs
StabilizerPcgs.description: computes the stabilizer in the group generated by pcgs of the point pnt. If given, acts are elements by which pcgs acts, act is the acting function. This function returns a pcgs for the stabilizer which is induced by the ParentPcgs of pcgs, that is it is compatible with pcgs.
StabilizerPcgs.title: StabilizerPcgs

Pcgs_OrbitStabilizer.name: Pcgs_OrbitStabilizer
Pcgs_OrbitStabilizer.description: runs a solvable group orbit-stabilizer algorithm on pnt with pcgs acting via the images oprs and the operation function opr. The domain domain can be used to speed up search, if it is not known, false can be given instead. The function returns a record with components orbit, stabpcgs and lengths, the latter indicating the lengths of the orbit whenever it got extended. This can be used to recompute transversal elements. This function should be used only inside algorithms when speed is essential.
Pcgs_OrbitStabilizer.title: Pcgs_OrbitStabilizer

ClassesSolvableGroup.name: ClassesSolvableGroup
ClassesSolvableGroup.description:
computes conjugacy classes and centralizers in solvable groups. G is the acting group. mode indicates the type of the calculation:

0 Conjugacy classes

4 Conjugacy test for the two elements in opt.candidates
ClassesSolvableGroup.title: ClassesSolvableGroup

CentralizerSizeLimitConsiderFunction.name: CentralizerSizeLimitConsiderFunction
CentralizerSizeLimitConsiderFunction.description:
returns a function (with arguments fhome, rep, cen, K, L) that can be used in ClassesSolvableGroup (45.17-1) as the consider component of the options record. It will restrict the lifting to those classes, for which the size of the centralizer (in the factor) is at most sz.

See also SubgroupsSolvableGroup (39.21-3).
CentralizerSizeLimitConsiderFunction.title: CentralizerSizeLimitConsiderFunction

FamilyPcgs.name: FamilyPcgs
FamilyPcgs.description:
returns, for a pc group grp, a natural pcgs of some group G which contains grp and is maximal with this property.

The pcgs operations described in Chapter 45 are particularly efficient with respect to this pcgs.
FamilyPcgs.title: FamilyPcgs

IsFamilyPcgs.name: IsFamilyPcgs
IsFamilyPcgs.description: specifies whether the pcgs is a FamilyPcgs (46.1-1) of a pc group.
IsFamilyPcgs.title: IsFamilyPcgs

InducedPcgsWrtFamilyPcgs.name: InducedPcgsWrtFamilyPcgs
InducedPcgsWrtFamilyPcgs.description: returns the pcgs which induced with respect to a family pcgs (see IsParentPcgsFamilyPcgs (46.1-4) for further details).
InducedPcgsWrtFamilyPcgs.title: InducedPcgsWrtFamilyPcgs

IsParentPcgsFamilyPcgs.name: IsParentPcgsFamilyPcgs
IsParentPcgsFamilyPcgs.description:
This property indicates that the pcgs pcgs is induced with respect to a family pcgs.

This property is needed to distinguish between different independent polycyclic generating sequences which a pc group may have, since the elementary operations for a non-family pcgs may not be as efficient as the elementary operations for the family pcgs.

This can have a significant influence on the performance of algorithms for polycyclic groups. Many algorithms require a pcgs that corresponds to an elementary abelian series (see PcgsElementaryAbelianSeries (45.11-2)) or even a special pcgs (see 45.13). If the family pcgs has the required properties, it will be used for these purposes, if not GAP has to work with respect to a new pcgs which is not the family pcgs and thus takes longer for elementary calculations like ExponentsOfPcElement (45.5-3).
IsParentPcgsFamilyPcgs.title: IsParentPcgsFamilyPcgs

IsPcGroup.name: IsPcGroup
IsPcGroup.description: tests whether G is a pc group.
IsPcGroup.title: IsPcGroup

IsomorphismFpGroupByPcgs.name: IsomorphismFpGroupByPcgs
IsomorphismFpGroupByPcgs.description: It is possible to convert a pc group to a fp group in GAP. The function IsomorphismFpGroupByPcgs computes the power-commutator presentation defined by pcgs. The string str can be used to give a name to the generators of the fp group.
IsomorphismFpGroupByPcgs.title: IsomorphismFpGroupByPcgs

PcGroupFpGroup.name: PcGroupFpGroup
PcGroupFpGroup.description: creates a pc group P from an fp group (see Chapter 47) G whose presentation is polycyclic. The resulting group P has generators corresponding to the generators of G. They are printed in the same way as generators of G, but they lie in a different family. If the pc presentation of G is not confluent, an error message occurs.
PcGroupFpGroup.title: PcGroupFpGroup

SingleCollector.name: SingleCollector
SingleCollector.description:
initializes a single collector or a combinatorial collector, where fgrp must be a free group and relorders must be a list of the relative orders of the pc series.

A combinatorial collector can only be set up for a finite p-group. Here, the relative orders relorders must all be equal and a prime.
SingleCollector.title: SingleCollector

CombinatorialCollector.name: CombinatorialCollector
CombinatorialCollector.description:
initializes a single collector or a combinatorial collector, where fgrp must be a free group and relorders must be a list of the relative orders of the pc series.

A combinatorial collector can only be set up for a finite p-group. Here, the relative orders relorders must all be equal and a prime.
CombinatorialCollector.title: SingleCollector

SetConjugate.name: SetConjugate
SetConjugate.description:
Let f_1, ..., f_n be the generators of the underlying free group of the collector coll.

For i < j, SetConjugate sets the conjugate f_j^{f_i} to equal w, which is assumed to be a canonical word in f_{i+1}, ..., f_n. No check of the arguments is performed.
SetConjugate.title: SetConjugate

SetCommutator.name: SetCommutator
SetCommutator.description:
Let f_1, ..., f_n be the generators of the underlying free group of the collector coll.

For i < j, SetCommutator sets the commutator of f_j and f_i to equal w, which is assumed to be a canonical word in f_{i+1}, ..., f_n. No check of the arguments is performed.
SetCommutator.title: SetCommutator

SetPower.name: SetPower
SetPower.description:
Let f_1, ..., f_n be the generators of the underlying free group of the collector coll, and let r_i be the corresponding relative orders.

SetPower sets the power f_i^{r_i} to equal w, which is assumed to be a canonical word in f_{i+1}, ..., f_n. No check of the arguments is performed.
SetPower.title: SetPower

GroupByRws.name: GroupByRws
GroupByRws.description: creates a group from a rewriting system. In the first version it is checked whether the rewriting system is confluent, in the second version this is assumed to be true.
GroupByRws.title: GroupByRws

GroupByRwsNC.name: GroupByRwsNC
GroupByRwsNC.description: creates a group from a rewriting system. In the first version it is checked whether the rewriting system is confluent, in the second version this is assumed to be true.
GroupByRwsNC.title: GroupByRws

IsomorphismRefinedPcGroup.name: IsomorphismRefinedPcGroup
IsomorphismRefinedPcGroup.description: returns an isomorphism from G onto an isomorphic pc group whose family pcgs is a prime order pcgs.
IsomorphismRefinedPcGroup.title: IsomorphismRefinedPcGroup

RefinedPcGroup.name: RefinedPcGroup
RefinedPcGroup.description: returns the range of the IsomorphismRefinedPcGroup (46.4-8) value of G.
RefinedPcGroup.title: RefinedPcGroup

PcGroupWithPcgs.name: PcGroupWithPcgs
PcGroupWithPcgs.description:
creates a new pc group G whose family pcgs is isomorphic to the (modulo) pcgs mpcgs.

If a pcgs is only given by a list of pc elements, PcgsByPcSequence (45.3-1) can be used:
PcGroupWithPcgs.title: PcGroupWithPcgs

IsomorphismPcGroup.name: IsomorphismPcGroup
IsomorphismPcGroup.description: returns an isomorphism from G onto an isomorphic pc group. The series chosen for this pc representation depends on the method chosen. G must be a polycyclic group of any kind, for example a solvable permutation group.
IsomorphismPcGroup.title: IsomorphismPcGroup

IsomorphismSpecialPcGroup.name: IsomorphismSpecialPcGroup
IsomorphismSpecialPcGroup.description: returns an isomorphism from G onto an isomorphic pc group whose family pcgs is a special pcgs. (This can be beneficial to the runtime of calculations.) G may be a polycyclic group of any kind, for example a solvable permutation group.
IsomorphismSpecialPcGroup.title: IsomorphismSpecialPcGroup

TwoCoboundaries.name: TwoCoboundaries
TwoCoboundaries.description: returns the group of 2-coboundaries of a pc group G by the G-module M. The generators of M must correspond to the Pcgs (45.2-1) value of G. The group of coboundaries is given as vector space over the field underlying M.
TwoCoboundaries.title: TwoCoboundaries

TwoCocycles.name: TwoCocycles
TwoCocycles.description: returns the 2-cocycles of a pc group G by the G-module M. The generators of M must correspond to the Pcgs (45.2-1) value of G. The operation returns a list of vectors over the field underlying M and the additive group generated by these vectors is the group of 2-cocyles.
TwoCocycles.title: TwoCocycles

TwoCohomology.name: TwoCohomology
TwoCohomology.description: This operation computes the second cohomology group for the special case of a Pc Group. It returns a record defining the second cohomology group as factor space of the space of cocycles by the space of coboundaries. G must be a pc group and the generators of M must correspond to the pcgs of G.
TwoCohomology.title: TwoCohomology

Extensions.name: Extensions
Extensions.description: returns all extensions of G by the G-module M up to equivalence as pc groups.
Extensions.title: Extensions

Extension.name: Extension
Extension.description: returns the extension of G by the G-module M via the cocycle c as pc groups. The NC version does not check the resulting group for consistence.
Extension.title: Extension

ExtensionNC.name: ExtensionNC
ExtensionNC.description: returns the extension of G by the G-module M via the cocycle c as pc groups. The NC version does not check the resulting group for consistence.
ExtensionNC.title: Extension

SplitExtension.name: SplitExtension
SplitExtension.description: returns the split extension of G by the G-module M. See also SplitExtension (46.8-10) for its 3-argument version.
SplitExtension.title: SplitExtension

SplitExtension.name: SplitExtension
SplitExtension.description:
returns the split extensions of the pc group G by the pc group N. aut should be a homomorphism from G into Aut(N).

In the following example we construct the holomorph of Q_8 as split extension of Q_8 by S_4.
SplitExtension.title: SplitExtension

ModuleOfExtension.name: ModuleOfExtension
ModuleOfExtension.description:
returns the module of an extension E of G by M. This is the normal subgroup of E which corresponds to M.

Note that the extensions returned by Extensions (46.8-4) are computed up to equivalence, but not up to isomorphism.
ModuleOfExtension.title: ModuleOfExtension

CompatiblePairs.name: CompatiblePairs
CompatiblePairs.description: returns the group of compatible pairs of the group G with the G-module M as subgroup of the direct product Aut(G) × Aut(M). Here Aut(M) is considered as subgroup of a general linear group. The optional argument D should be a subgroup of Aut(G) × Aut(M). If it is given, then only the compatible pairs in D are computed. If a group A of automorphisms of G is given as optional first argument, it is used in place of the full automorphism group of G, avoiding the need to compute this automorphism group.
CompatiblePairs.title: CompatiblePairs

ExtensionRepresentatives.name: ExtensionRepresentatives
ExtensionRepresentatives.description: returns all extensions of G by the G-module M up to equivalence under action of P where P has to be a subgroup of the group of compatible pairs of G with M.
ExtensionRepresentatives.title: ExtensionRepresentatives

CodePcgs.name: CodePcgs
CodePcgs.description: returns the code corresponding to pcgs.
CodePcgs.title: CodePcgs

CodePcGroup.name: CodePcGroup
CodePcGroup.description: returns the code for a pcgs of G.
CodePcGroup.title: CodePcGroup

PcGroupCode.name: PcGroupCode
PcGroupCode.description: returns a pc group of size size corresponding to code. The argument code must be a valid code for a pcgs, otherwise anything may happen. Valid codes are usually obtained by one of the functions CodePcgs (46.9-1) or CodePcGroup (46.9-2).
PcGroupCode.title: PcGroupCode

RandomIsomorphismTest.name: RandomIsomorphismTest
RandomIsomorphismTest.description:
The first argument is a list coderecs containing records describing groups, and the second argument is a non-negative integer n.

The test returns a sublist of coderecs where isomorphic copies detected by the probabilistic test have been removed.

The list coderecs should contain records with two components, code and order, describing a group via PcGroupCode( code, order ) (see PcGroupCode (46.9-3)).
RandomIsomorphismTest.title: RandomIsomorphismTest

IsSubgroupFpGroup.name: IsSubgroupFpGroup
IsSubgroupFpGroup.description: is the category for finitely presented groups or subgroups of a finitely presented group.
IsSubgroupFpGroup.title: IsSubgroupFpGroup

IsFpGroup.name: IsFpGroup
IsFpGroup.description:
is a synonym for IsSubgroupFpGroup(G) and IsGroupOfFamily(G).

Free groups are a special case of finitely presented groups, namely finitely presented groups with no relators.

Note that FreeGroup(infinity) (which exists e.g. for purposes of rewriting presentations with further generators) satisfies this filter, though of course it is not finitely generated (and thus not finitely presented). IsFpGroup thus is not a proper property test and slightly misnamed for the sake of its most prominent uses.
IsFpGroup.title: IsFpGroup

InfoFpGroup.name: InfoFpGroup
InfoFpGroup.description: The info class for functions dealing with finitely presented groups is InfoFpGroup.
InfoFpGroup.title: InfoFpGroup

FactorGroupFpGroupByRels.name: FactorGroupFpGroupByRels
FactorGroupFpGroupByRels.description: returns the factor group G/N of G by the normal closure N of elts where elts is expected to be a list of elements of G.
FactorGroupFpGroupByRels.title: FactorGroupFpGroupByRels

ParseRelators.name: ParseRelators
ParseRelators.description: Will translate a list of relations as given in print, e.g. x y^2 = (x y^3 x)^2 xy = yzx into relators. gens must be a list of generators of a free group, each being displayed by a single letter. rels is a string that lists a sequence of equalities. These must be written in the letters which are the names of the generators in gens. Change of upper/lower case is interpreted to indicate inverses.
ParseRelators.title: ParseRelators

StringFactorizationWord.name: StringFactorizationWord
StringFactorizationWord.description: returns a string that expresses a given word w in compact form written as a string. Inverses are expressed by changing the upper/lower case of the generators, recurring expressions are written as products.
StringFactorizationWord.title: StringFactorizationWord

FpElmComparisonMethod.name: FpElmComparisonMethod
FpElmComparisonMethod.description: If fam is the elements family of a finitely presented group this attribute returns a function smaller(left, right) that will be used to compare elements in fam.
FpElmComparisonMethod.title: FpElmComparisonMethod

SetReducedMultiplication.name: SetReducedMultiplication
SetReducedMultiplication.description: For an FpGroup obj, an element obj of it or the family obj of its elements, this function will force immediate reduction when multiplying, keeping words short at extra cost per multiplication.
SetReducedMultiplication.title: SetReducedMultiplication

FreeGroupOfFpGroup.name: FreeGroupOfFpGroup
FreeGroupOfFpGroup.description: returns the underlying free group for the finitely presented group G. This is the group generated by the free generators provided by the FreeGeneratorsOfFpGroup (47.4-2) value of G.
FreeGroupOfFpGroup.title: FreeGroupOfFpGroup

FreeGeneratorsOfFpGroup.name: FreeGeneratorsOfFpGroup
FreeGeneratorsOfFpGroup.description:
FreeGeneratorsOfFpGroup returns the underlying free generators corresponding to the generators of the finitely presented group G which must be a full FpGroup.

FreeGeneratorsOfWholeGroup also works for subgroups of an FpGroup and returns the free generators of the full group that defines the family.
FreeGeneratorsOfFpGroup.title: FreeGeneratorsOfFpGroup

FreeGeneratorsOfWholeGroup.name: FreeGeneratorsOfWholeGroup
FreeGeneratorsOfWholeGroup.description:
FreeGeneratorsOfFpGroup returns the underlying free generators corresponding to the generators of the finitely presented group G which must be a full FpGroup.

FreeGeneratorsOfWholeGroup also works for subgroups of an FpGroup and returns the free generators of the full group that defines the family.
FreeGeneratorsOfWholeGroup.title: FreeGeneratorsOfFpGroup

RelatorsOfFpGroup.name: RelatorsOfFpGroup
RelatorsOfFpGroup.description:
returns the relators of the finitely presented group G as words in the free generators provided by the FreeGeneratorsOfFpGroup (47.4-2) value of G.

Note that these attributes are only available for the full finitely presented group. It is possible (for example by using Subgroup (39.3-1)) to construct a subgroup of index 1 which is not identical to the whole group. The latter one can be obtained in this situation via Parent (31.7-1).

Elements of a finitely presented group are not words, but are represented using a word from the free group as representative. The following two commands obtain this representative, respectively create an element in the finitely presented group.
RelatorsOfFpGroup.title: RelatorsOfFpGroup

UnderlyingElement.name: UnderlyingElement
UnderlyingElement.description: Let elm be an element of a group whose elements are represented as words with further properties. Then UnderlyingElement returns the word from the free group that is used as a representative for elm.
UnderlyingElement.title: UnderlyingElement

UnderlyingElement.name: UnderlyingElement
UnderlyingElement.description: for an element elm of a finitely presented semigroup or monoid. Returns the word from the free semigroup or free monoid that is used as a representative for elm.
UnderlyingElement.title: UnderlyingElement

ElementOfFpGroup.name: ElementOfFpGroup
ElementOfFpGroup.description: If fam is the elements family of a finitely presented group and word is a word in the free generators underlying this finitely presented group, this operation creates the element with the representative word in the free group.
ElementOfFpGroup.title: ElementOfFpGroup

CosetTable.name: CosetTable
CosetTable.description:
returns the coset table of the finitely presented group G on the cosets of the subgroup H.

Basically a coset table is the permutation representation of the finitely presented group on the cosets of a subgroup (which need not be faithful if the subgroup has a nontrivial core). Most of the set theoretic and group functions use the regular representation of G, i.e., the coset table of G over the trivial subgroup.

The coset table is returned as a list of lists. For each generator of G and its inverse the table contains a generator list. A generator list is simply a list of integers. If l is the generator list for the generator g and if l[i] = j then generator g takes the coset i to the coset j by multiplication from the right. Thus the permutation representation of G on the cosets of H is obtained by applying PermList (42.5-2) to each generator list.
CosetTable.title: CosetTable

TracedCosetFpGroup.name: TracedCosetFpGroup
TracedCosetFpGroup.description: Traces the coset number pt under the word word through the coset table tab. (Note: word must be in the free group, use UnderlyingElement (47.4-4) if in doubt.)
TracedCosetFpGroup.title: TracedCosetFpGroup

CosetTableBySubgroup.name: CosetTableBySubgroup
CosetTableBySubgroup.description: returns a coset table for the action of G on the cosets of H. The columns of the table correspond to the GeneratorsOfGroup (39.2-4) value of G.
CosetTableBySubgroup.title: CosetTableBySubgroup

CosetTableFromGensAndRels.name: CosetTableFromGensAndRels
CosetTableFromGensAndRels.description:
is an internal function which is called by the functions CosetTable (47.6-1), CosetTableInWholeGroup (47.8-1) and others. It is, in fact, the workhorse that performs a Todd-Coxeter coset enumeration. fgens must be a set of free generators and grels a set of relators in these generators. fsgens are subgroup generators expressed as words in these generators. The function returns a coset table with respect to fgens.

CosetTableFromGensAndRels will call TCENUM.CosetTableFromGensAndRels. This makes it possible to replace the built-in coset enumerator with another one by assigning TCENUM to another record.

The library version which is used by default performs a standard Felsch strategy coset enumeration. You can call this function explicitly as GAPTCENUM.CosetTableFromGensAndRels even if other coset enumerators are installed.
CosetTableFromGensAndRels.title: CosetTableFromGensAndRels

CosetTableDefaultMaxLimit.name: CosetTableDefaultMaxLimit
CosetTableDefaultMaxLimit.description:
is the default limit for the number of cosets allowed in a coset enumeration.

A coset enumeration will not finish if the subgroup does not have finite index, and even if it has it may take many more intermediate cosets than the actual index of the subgroup is. To avoid a coset enumeration running away therefore GAP has a safety stop built in. This is controlled by the global variable CosetTableDefaultMaxLimit.

If this number of cosets is reached, GAP will issue an error message and prompt the user to either continue the calculation or to stop it. The default value is 4096000.
CosetTableDefaultMaxLimit.title: CosetTableDefaultMaxLimit

CosetTableDefaultLimit.name: CosetTableDefaultLimit
CosetTableDefaultLimit.description:
is the default number of cosets with which any coset table is initialized before doing a coset enumeration.

The function performing this coset enumeration will automatically extend the table whenever necessary (as long as the number of cosets does not exceed the value of CosetTableDefaultMaxLimit (47.6-6)), but this is an expensive operation. Thus, if you change the value of CosetTableDefaultLimit, you should set it to a number of cosets that you expect to be sufficient for your subsequent coset enumerations. On the other hand, if you make it too large, your job will unnecessarily waste a lot of space.

The default value of CosetTableDefaultLimit is 1000.
CosetTableDefaultLimit.title: CosetTableDefaultLimit

MostFrequentGeneratorFpGroup.name: MostFrequentGeneratorFpGroup
MostFrequentGeneratorFpGroup.description: is an internal function which is used in some applications of coset table methods. It returns the first of those generators of the given finitely presented group G which occur most frequently in the relators.
MostFrequentGeneratorFpGroup.title: MostFrequentGeneratorFpGroup

IndicesInvolutaryGenerators.name: IndicesInvolutaryGenerators
IndicesInvolutaryGenerators.description: returns the indices of those generators of the finitely presented group G which are known to be involutions. This knowledge is used by internal functions to improve the performance of coset enumerations.
IndicesInvolutaryGenerators.title: IndicesInvolutaryGenerators

CosetTableStandard.name: CosetTableStandard
CosetTableStandard.description: specifies the definition of a standard coset table. It is used whenever coset tables or augmented coset tables are created. Its value may be "lenlex" or "semilenlex". If it is "lenlex" coset tables will be standardized using all their columns as defined in Charles Sims' book (this is the new default standard of GAP). If it is "semilenlex" they will be standardized using only their generator columns (this was the original GAP standard). The default value of CosetTableStandard is "lenlex".
CosetTableStandard.title: CosetTableStandard

StandardizeTable.name: StandardizeTable
StandardizeTable.description: standardizes the given coset table table. The second argument is optional. It defines the standard to be used, its values may be "lenlex" or "semilenlex" specifying the new or the old convention, respectively. If no value for the parameter standard is provided the function will use the global variable CosetTableStandard (47.7-1) instead. Note that the function alters the given table, it does not create a copy.
StandardizeTable.title: StandardizeTable

CosetTableInWholeGroup.name: CosetTableInWholeGroup
CosetTableInWholeGroup.description:
is equivalent to CosetTable(G,H) where G is the (unique) finitely presented group such that H is a subgroup of G. It overrides a silent option (see CosetTableFromGensAndRels (47.6-5)) with false.

The variant TryCosetTableInWholeGroup does not override the silent option with false in case a coset table is only wanted if not too expensive. It will store a result that is not fail in the attribute CosetTableInWholeGroup.
CosetTableInWholeGroup.title: CosetTableInWholeGroup

TryCosetTableInWholeGroup.name: TryCosetTableInWholeGroup
TryCosetTableInWholeGroup.description:
is equivalent to CosetTable(G,H) where G is the (unique) finitely presented group such that H is a subgroup of G. It overrides a silent option (see CosetTableFromGensAndRels (47.6-5)) with false.

The variant TryCosetTableInWholeGroup does not override the silent option with false in case a coset table is only wanted if not too expensive. It will store a result that is not fail in the attribute CosetTableInWholeGroup.
TryCosetTableInWholeGroup.title: CosetTableInWholeGroup

SubgroupOfWholeGroupByCosetTable.name: SubgroupOfWholeGroupByCosetTable
SubgroupOfWholeGroupByCosetTable.description: takes a family fpfam of an FpGroup and a standardized coset table tab and returns the subgroup of fpfam!.wholeGroup defined by this coset table. The function will not check whether the coset table is standardized. See also CosetTableBySubgroup (47.6-4).
SubgroupOfWholeGroupByCosetTable.title: SubgroupOfWholeGroupByCosetTable

AugmentedCosetTableInWholeGroup.name: AugmentedCosetTableInWholeGroup
AugmentedCosetTableInWholeGroup.description:
For a subgroup H of a finitely presented group, this function returns an augmented coset table. If a generator set gens is given, it is guaranteed that gens will be a subset of the primary and secondary subgroup generators of this coset table.

It is mutable so we are permitted to add further entries. However existing entries may not be changed. Any entries added however should correspond to the subgroup only and not to a homomorphism.
AugmentedCosetTableInWholeGroup.title: AugmentedCosetTableInWholeGroup

AugmentedCosetTableMtc.name: AugmentedCosetTableMtc
AugmentedCosetTableMtc.description:
is an internal function used by the subgroup presentation functions described in 48.2. It applies a Modified Todd-Coxeter coset representative enumeration to construct an augmented coset table (see 48.2) for the given subgroup H of G. The subgroup generators will be named string1, string2, ....

The function accepts the options max and silent as described for the function CosetTableFromGensAndRels (47.6-5).
AugmentedCosetTableMtc.title: AugmentedCosetTableMtc

AugmentedCosetTableRrs.name: AugmentedCosetTableRrs
AugmentedCosetTableRrs.description: is an internal function used by the subgroup presentation functions described in 48.2. It applies the Reduced Reidemeister-Schreier method to construct an augmented coset table for the subgroup of G which is defined by the given coset table table. The new subgroup generators will be named string1, string2, ....
AugmentedCosetTableRrs.title: AugmentedCosetTableRrs

RewriteWord.name: RewriteWord
RewriteWord.description:
RewriteWord rewrites word (which must be a word in the underlying free group with respect to which the augmented coset table aug is given) in the subgroup generators given by the augmented coset table aug. It returns a Tietze-type word (i.e. a list of integers), referring to the primary and secondary generators of aug.

If word is not contained in the subgroup, fail is returned.
RewriteWord.title: RewriteWord

LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroupIterator.name: LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroupIterator
LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroupIterator.description:
These functions compute representatives of the conjugacy classes of subgroups of the finitely presented group G that contain the subgroup H of G and that have index less than or equal to index.

LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroupIterator returns an iterator (see 30.8) that can be used to run over these subgroups, and LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroup returns the list of these subgroups. If one is interested only in one or a few subgroups up to a given index then preferably the iterator should be used.

If the optional argument excluded has been specified, then it is expected to be a list of words in the free generators of the underlying free group of G, and LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroup returns only those subgroups of index at most index that contain H, but do not contain any conjugate of any of the group elements defined by these words.
LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroupIterator.title: LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroupIterator

LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroup.name: LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroup
LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroup.description:
These functions compute representatives of the conjugacy classes of subgroups of the finitely presented group G that contain the subgroup H of G and that have index less than or equal to index.

LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroupIterator returns an iterator (see 30.8) that can be used to run over these subgroups, and LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroup returns the list of these subgroups. If one is interested only in one or a few subgroups up to a given index then preferably the iterator should be used.

If the optional argument excluded has been specified, then it is expected to be a list of words in the free generators of the underlying free group of G, and LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroup returns only those subgroups of index at most index that contain H, but do not contain any conjugate of any of the group elements defined by these words.
LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroup.title: LowIndexSubgroupsFpGroupIterator

IsomorphismFpGroup.name: IsomorphismFpGroup
IsomorphismFpGroup.description: returns an isomorphism from the given finite group G to a finitely presented group isomorphic to G. The function first chooses a set of generators of G and then computes a presentation in terms of these generators.
IsomorphismFpGroup.title: IsomorphismFpGroup

IsomorphismFpGroupByGenerators.name: IsomorphismFpGroupByGenerators
IsomorphismFpGroupByGenerators.description:
returns an isomorphism from a finite group G to a finitely presented group F isomorphic to G. The generators of F correspond to the generators of G given in the list gens. If string is given it is used to name the generators of the finitely presented group.

The NC version will avoid testing whether the elements in gens generate G.

The main task of the function IsomorphismFpGroupByGenerators is to find a presentation of G in the provided generators gens. In the case of a permutation group G it does this by first constructing a stabilizer chain of G and then it works through that chain from the bottom to the top, recursively computing a presentation for each of the involved stabilizers. The method used is essentially an implementation of John Cannon's multi-stage relations-finding algorithm as described in [Neu82] (see also [Can73] for a more graph theoretical description). Moreover, it makes heavy use of Tietze transformations in each stage to avoid an explosion of the total length of the relators.
IsomorphismFpGroupByGenerators.title: IsomorphismFpGroupByGenerators

IsomorphismFpGroupByGeneratorsNC.name: IsomorphismFpGroupByGeneratorsNC
IsomorphismFpGroupByGeneratorsNC.description:
returns an isomorphism from a finite group G to a finitely presented group F isomorphic to G. The generators of F correspond to the generators of G given in the list gens. If string is given it is used to name the generators of the finitely presented group.

The NC version will avoid testing whether the elements in gens generate G.

The main task of the function IsomorphismFpGroupByGenerators is to find a presentation of G in the provided generators gens. In the case of a permutation group G it does this by first constructing a stabilizer chain of G and then it works through that chain from the bottom to the top, recursively computing a presentation for each of the involved stabilizers. The method used is essentially an implementation of John Cannon's multi-stage relations-finding algorithm as described in [Neu82] (see also [Can73] for a more graph theoretical description). Moreover, it makes heavy use of Tietze transformations in each stage to avoid an explosion of the total length of the relators.
IsomorphismFpGroupByGeneratorsNC.title: IsomorphismFpGroupByGenerators

IsomorphismSimplifiedFpGroup.name: IsomorphismSimplifiedFpGroup
IsomorphismSimplifiedFpGroup.description:
applies Tietze transformations to a copy of the presentation of the given finitely presented group G in order to reduce it with respect to the number of generators, the number of relators, and the relator lengths.

The operation returns an isomorphism with source G, range a group H isomorphic to G, so that the presentation of H has been simplified using Tietze transformations.

IsomorphismSimplifiedFpGroup uses Tietze transformations to simplify the presentation, see 48.1-6.
IsomorphismSimplifiedFpGroup.title: IsomorphismSimplifiedFpGroup

SubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotientSubgroup.name: SubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotientSubgroup
SubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotientSubgroup.description: takes a FpGroup family fpfam, a finitely generated group Q such that the fp generators of fpfam can be mapped by an epimorphism phi onto the GeneratorsOfGroup (39.2-4) value of Q, and a subgroup U of Q. It returns the subgroup of fpfam!.wholeGroup which is the full preimage of U under phi.
SubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotientSubgroup.title: SubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotientSubgroup

IsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotientRep.name: IsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotientRep
IsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotientRep.description: is the representation for subgroups of an FpGroup, given by a quotient subgroup. The components G!.quot and G!.sub hold quotient, respectively subgroup.
IsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotientRep.title: IsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotientRep

AsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotient.name: AsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotient
AsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotient.description:
returns the same subgroup in the representation AsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotient.

See also SubgroupOfWholeGroupByCosetTable (47.8-2) and CosetTableBySubgroup (47.6-4).

This technique is used by GAP for example to represent the derived subgroup, which is obtained from the quotient G/G'.
AsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotient.title: AsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotient

DefiningQuotientHomomorphism.name: DefiningQuotientHomomorphism
DefiningQuotientHomomorphism.description: if U is a subgroup in quotient representation (IsSubgroupOfWholeGroupByQuotientRep (47.13-2)), this function returns the defining homomorphism from the whole group to U!.quot.
DefiningQuotientHomomorphism.title: DefiningQuotientHomomorphism

PQuotient.name: PQuotient
PQuotient.description:
computes a factor p-group of a finitely presented group F in form of a quotient system. The quotient system can be converted into an epimorphism from F onto the p-group computed by the function EpimorphismQuotientSystem (47.14-2).

For a group G define the exponent-p central series of G inductively by cal P_1(G) = G and cal P_{i+1}(G) = [cal P_i(G),G]cal P_{i+1}(G)^p. The factor groups modulo the terms of the lower exponent-p central series are p-groups. The group G has p-class c if cal P_c(G) ≠ cal P_{c+1}(G) = 1.

The algorithm computes successive quotients modulo the terms of the exponent-p central series of F. If the parameter c is present, then the factor group modulo the (c+1)-th term of the exponent-p central series of F is returned. If c is not present, then the algorithm attempts to compute the largest factor p-group of F. In case F does not have a largest factor p-group, the algorithm will not terminate.
PQuotient.title: PQuotient

EpimorphismQuotientSystem.name: EpimorphismQuotientSystem
EpimorphismQuotientSystem.description:
For a quotient system quotsys obtained from the function PQuotient (47.14-1), this operation returns an epimorphism F → P where F is the finitely presented group of which quotsys is a quotient system and P is a pc group isomorphic to the quotient of F determined by quotsys.

Different calls to this operation will create different groups P, each with its own family.
EpimorphismQuotientSystem.title: EpimorphismQuotientSystem

EpimorphismPGroup.name: EpimorphismPGroup
EpimorphismPGroup.description: computes an epimorphism from the finitely presented group fpgrp to the largest p-group of p-class cl which is a quotient of fpgrp. If cl is omitted, the largest finite p-group quotient (of p-class up to 1000) is determined.
EpimorphismPGroup.title: EpimorphismPGroup

EpimorphismNilpotentQuotient.name: EpimorphismNilpotentQuotient
EpimorphismNilpotentQuotient.description:
returns an epimorphism on the class n finite nilpotent quotient of the finitely presented group fpgrp. If n is omitted, the largest finite nilpotent quotient (of p-class up to 1000) is taken.

A related operation which is also applicable to finitely presented groups is GQuotients (40.9-4), which computes all epimorphisms from a (finitely presented) group F onto a given (finite) group G.
EpimorphismNilpotentQuotient.title: EpimorphismNilpotentQuotient

SolvableQuotient.name: SolvableQuotient
SolvableQuotient.description:
This routine calls the solvable quotient algorithm for a finitely presented group F. The quotient to be found can be specified in the following ways: Specifying an integer size finds a quotient of size up to size (if such large quotients exist). Specifying a list of primes in primes finds the largest quotient involving the given primes. Finally tuples can be used to prescribe a chief series.

SQ can be used as a synonym for SolvableQuotient.
SolvableQuotient.title: SolvableQuotient

SolvableQuotient.name: SolvableQuotient
SolvableQuotient.description:
This routine calls the solvable quotient algorithm for a finitely presented group F. The quotient to be found can be specified in the following ways: Specifying an integer size finds a quotient of size up to size (if such large quotients exist). Specifying a list of primes in primes finds the largest quotient involving the given primes. Finally tuples can be used to prescribe a chief series.

SQ can be used as a synonym for SolvableQuotient.
SolvableQuotient.title: SolvableQuotient

SolvableQuotient.name: SolvableQuotient
SolvableQuotient.description:
This routine calls the solvable quotient algorithm for a finitely presented group F. The quotient to be found can be specified in the following ways: Specifying an integer size finds a quotient of size up to size (if such large quotients exist). Specifying a list of primes in primes finds the largest quotient involving the given primes. Finally tuples can be used to prescribe a chief series.

SQ can be used as a synonym for SolvableQuotient.
SolvableQuotient.title: SolvableQuotient

SQ.name: SQ
SQ.description:
This routine calls the solvable quotient algorithm for a finitely presented group F. The quotient to be found can be specified in the following ways: Specifying an integer size finds a quotient of size up to size (if such large quotients exist). Specifying a list of primes in primes finds the largest quotient involving the given primes. Finally tuples can be used to prescribe a chief series.

SQ can be used as a synonym for SolvableQuotient.
SQ.title: SolvableQuotient

EpimorphismSolvableQuotient.name: EpimorphismSolvableQuotient
EpimorphismSolvableQuotient.description: computes an epimorphism from the finitely presented group fpgrp to the largest solvable quotient given by param (specified as in SolvableQuotient (47.14-5)).
EpimorphismSolvableQuotient.title: EpimorphismSolvableQuotient

LargerQuotientBySubgroupAbelianization.name: LargerQuotientBySubgroupAbelianization
LargerQuotientBySubgroupAbelianization.description: Let hom a homomorphism from a finitely presented group G to a finite group H and U ≤ H. This function will –if it exists– return a subgroup S ≤ G, such that the core of S is properly contained in the kernel of hom as well as in the derived subgroup of V, where V is the pre-image of U under hom. Thus S exposes a larger quotient of G. If no such subgroup exists, fail is returned.
LargerQuotientBySubgroupAbelianization.title: LargerQuotientBySubgroupAbelianization

AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroup.name: AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroup
AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroup.description: AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroup is a synonym for AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupRrs (47.15-3).
AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroup.title: AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroup

AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupMtc.name: AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupMtc
AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupMtc.description: uses the Modified Todd-Coxeter method to compute the abelian invariants of a subgroup H of a finitely presented group G.
AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupMtc.title: AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupMtc

AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupRrs.name: AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupRrs
AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupRrs.description:
uses the Reduced Reidemeister-Schreier method to compute the abelian invariants of a subgroup H of a finitely presented group G.

Alternatively to the subgroup H, its coset table table in G may be given as second argument.
AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupRrs.title: AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupRrs

AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupRrs.name: AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupRrs
AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupRrs.description:
uses the Reduced Reidemeister-Schreier method to compute the abelian invariants of a subgroup H of a finitely presented group G.

Alternatively to the subgroup H, its coset table table in G may be given as second argument.
AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupRrs.title: AbelianInvariantsSubgroupFpGroupRrs

AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroup.name: AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroup
AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroup.description: AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroup is a synonym for AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroupRrs (47.15-5).
AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroup.title: AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroup

AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroupRrs.name: AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroupRrs
AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroupRrs.description:
uses the Reduced Reidemeister-Schreier method to compute the abelian invariants of the normal closure of a subgroup H of a finitely presented group G. See 48.2 for details on the different strategies.

The following example shows a calculation for the Coxeter group B_1. This calculation and a similar one for B_0 have been used to prove that B_1' / B_1'' ≅ Z_2^9 × Z^3 and B_0' / B_0'' ≅ Z_2^91 × Z^27 as stated in in [FJNT95, Proposition 5].
AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroupRrs.title: AbelianInvariantsNormalClosureFpGroupRrs

IsInfiniteAbelianizationGroup.name: IsInfiniteAbelianizationGroup
IsInfiniteAbelianizationGroup.description: returns true if the commutator factor group G/G' is infinite. This might be done without computing the full structure of the commutator factor group.
IsInfiniteAbelianizationGroup.title: IsInfiniteAbelianizationGroup

NewmanInfinityCriterion.name: NewmanInfinityCriterion
NewmanInfinityCriterion.description:
Let G be a finitely presented group and p a prime that divides the order of the commutator factor group of G. This function applies an infinity criterion due to M. F. Newman [New90] to G. (See [Joh97, chapter 16] for a more explicit description.) It returns true if the criterion succeeds in proving that G is infinite and fail otherwise.

Note that the criterion uses the number of generators and relations in the presentation of G. Reduction of the presentation via Tietze transformations (IsomorphismSimplifiedFpGroup (47.12-1)) therefore might produce an isomorphic group, for which the criterion will work better.

This proves that the subgroup k (and thus the whole group g) is infinite. (This is the original example from [New90].)
NewmanInfinityCriterion.title: NewmanInfinityCriterion

PresentationFpGroup.name: PresentationFpGroup
PresentationFpGroup.description:
creates a presentation, i. e., a Tietze object, for the given finitely presented group G. This presentation will be exactly as the presentation of G and no initial Tietze transformations are applied to it.

The optional printlevel parameter can be used to restrict or to extend the amount of output provided by Tietze transformation commands when being applied to the created presentation. The default value 1 is designed for interactive use and implies explicit messages to be displayed by most of these commands. A printlevel value of 0 will suppress these messages, whereas a printlevel value of 2 will enforce some additional output.
PresentationFpGroup.title: PresentationFpGroup

TzSort.name: TzSort
TzSort.description: sorts the relators of the given presentation P by increasing lengths. There is no particular ordering defined for the relators of equal length. Note that TzSort does not return a new object. It changes the given presentation.
TzSort.title: TzSort

GeneratorsOfPresentation.name: GeneratorsOfPresentation
GeneratorsOfPresentation.description: returns a list of free generators that is a shallow copy (see ShallowCopy (12.7-1)) of the current generators of the presentation P.
GeneratorsOfPresentation.title: GeneratorsOfPresentation

FpGroupPresentation.name: FpGroupPresentation
FpGroupPresentation.description: constructs an f. p. group as defined by the given Tietze presentation P.
FpGroupPresentation.title: FpGroupPresentation

PresentationViaCosetTable.name: PresentationViaCosetTable
PresentationViaCosetTable.description:
constructs a presentation for a given concrete finite group. It applies the relations finding algorithm which has been described in [Can73] and [Neu82]. It automatically applies Tietze transformations to the presentation found.

If only a group G has been specified, the single stage algorithm is applied.

The operation IsomorphismFpGroup (47.11-1) in contrast uses a multiple-stage algorithm using a chief series and stabilizer chains. It usually should be used rather than PresentationViaCosetTable. (It does not apply Tietze transformations automatically.)
PresentationViaCosetTable.title: PresentationViaCosetTable

SimplifiedFpGroup.name: SimplifiedFpGroup
SimplifiedFpGroup.description:
applies Tietze transformations to a copy of the presentation of the given finitely presented group G in order to reduce it with respect to the number of generators, the number of relators, and the relator lengths.

SimplifiedFpGroup returns a group isomorphic to the given one with a presentation which has been tried to simplify via Tietze transformations.

If the connection to the original group is important, then the operation IsomorphismSimplifiedFpGroup (47.12-1) should be used instead.
SimplifiedFpGroup.title: SimplifiedFpGroup

PresentationSubgroup.name: PresentationSubgroup
PresentationSubgroup.description: PresentationSubgroup is a synonym for PresentationSubgroupRrs (48.2-2).
PresentationSubgroup.title: PresentationSubgroup

PresentationSubgroupRrs.name: PresentationSubgroupRrs
PresentationSubgroupRrs.description:
