InverseSemigroup.name: InverseSemigroup
InverseSemigroup.description:
Returns: An inverse semigroup.

If obj1, obj2, ... are (any combination) of associative elements with unique semigroup inverses, semigroups of such elements, or collections of such elements, then InverseSemigroup returns the inverse semigroup generated by the union of obj1, obj2, .... This equals the semigroup generated by the union of obj1, obj2, ... and their inverses.

For example if S and T are inverse semigroups, then InverseSemigroup(S, f, Idempotents(T)); is the inverse semigroup generated by Union(GeneratorsOfInverseSemigroup(S), [f], Idempotents(T)));.
InverseSemigroup.title: InverseSemigroup

InverseMonoid.name: InverseMonoid
InverseMonoid.description:
Returns: An inverse monoid.

If obj1, obj2, ... are (any combination) of associative elements with unique semigroup inverses, semigroups of such elements, or collections of such elements, then InverseMonoid returns the inverse monoid generated by the union of obj1, obj2, .... This equals the monoid generated by the union of obj1, obj2, ... and their inverses.

As present, the only associative elements with unique semigroup inverses are partial permutations; see Chapter 54.
InverseMonoid.title: InverseMonoid

GeneratorsOfInverseSemigroup.name: GeneratorsOfInverseSemigroup
GeneratorsOfInverseSemigroup.description:
Returns: The generators of an inverse semigroup.

If S is an inverse semigroup, then GeneratorsOfInverseSemigroup returns the generators used to define S, i.e. an inverse semigroup generating set for S.

The value of GeneratorsOfSemigroup(S), for an inverse semigroup S, is the union of inverse semigroup generator and their inverses. So, S is the semigroup, as opposed to inverse semigroup, generated by the elements of GeneratorsOfInverseSemigroup(S) and their inverses.
GeneratorsOfInverseSemigroup.title: GeneratorsOfInverseSemigroup

GeneratorsOfInverseMonoid.name: GeneratorsOfInverseMonoid
GeneratorsOfInverseMonoid.description:
Returns: The generators of an inverse monoid.

If S is an inverse monoid, then GeneratorsOfInverseMonoid returns the generators used to define S, i.e. an inverse monoid generating set for S.

There are four different possible generating sets which define an inverse monoid. More precisely, an inverse monoid can be generated as an inverse monoid, inverse semigroup, monoid, or semigroup. The different generating sets in each case can be obtained using GeneratorsOfInverseMonoid, GeneratorsOfInverseSemigroup (51.3-3), GeneratorsOfMonoid (51.2-7), and GeneratorsOfSemigroup (51.1-8), respectively.
GeneratorsOfInverseMonoid.title: GeneratorsOfInverseMonoid

IsInverseSubsemigroup.name: IsInverseSubsemigroup
IsInverseSubsemigroup.description:
Returns: true or false.

If the semigroup T is an inverse subsemigroup of the semigroup S, then this operation returns true.
IsInverseSubsemigroup.title: IsInverseSubsemigroup

IsRegularSemigroup.name: IsRegularSemigroup
IsRegularSemigroup.description: returns true if S is regular, i.e., if every \(\mathcal{D}\)-class of S is regular.
IsRegularSemigroup.title: IsRegularSemigroup

IsRegularSemigroupElement.name: IsRegularSemigroupElement
IsRegularSemigroupElement.description: returns true if x has a general inverse in S, i.e., there is an element y ∈ S such that x y x = x and y x y = y.
IsRegularSemigroupElement.title: IsRegularSemigroupElement

InversesOfSemigroupElement.name: InversesOfSemigroupElement
InversesOfSemigroupElement.description:
Returns: A list of the inverses of an element of a semigroup.

InversesOfSemigroupElement returns a list of the inverses of the element x in the semigroup S.

An element y in S is an inverse of x if x*y*x=x and y*x*y=y. The element x has an inverse if and only if x is a regular element of S.
InversesOfSemigroupElement.title: InversesOfSemigroupElement

IsSimpleSemigroup.name: IsSimpleSemigroup
IsSimpleSemigroup.description: is true if and only if the semigroup S has no proper ideals.
IsSimpleSemigroup.title: IsSimpleSemigroup

IsZeroSimpleSemigroup.name: IsZeroSimpleSemigroup
IsZeroSimpleSemigroup.description: is true if and only if the semigroup has no proper ideals except for 0, where S is a semigroup with zero. If the semigroup does not find its zero, then a break-loop is entered.
IsZeroSimpleSemigroup.title: IsZeroSimpleSemigroup

IsZeroGroup.name: IsZeroGroup
IsZeroGroup.description: is true if and only if the semigroup S is a group with zero adjoined.
IsZeroGroup.title: IsZeroGroup

IsReesCongruenceSemigroup.name: IsReesCongruenceSemigroup
IsReesCongruenceSemigroup.description: returns true if S is a Rees Congruence semigroup, that is, if all congruences of S are Rees Congruences.
IsReesCongruenceSemigroup.title: IsReesCongruenceSemigroup

IsInverseSemigroup.name: IsInverseSemigroup
IsInverseSemigroup.description:
Returns: true or false.

A semigroup S is an inverse semigroup if every element x in S has a unique semigroup inverse, that is, a unique element y in S such that x*y*x=x and y*x*y=y.

A monoid that happens to be an inverse semigroup is called an inverse monoid; see IsMonoid (51.2-1).
IsInverseSemigroup.title: IsInverseSemigroup

IsInverseMonoid.name: IsInverseMonoid
IsInverseMonoid.description:
Returns: true or false.

A semigroup S is an inverse semigroup if every element x in S has a unique semigroup inverse, that is, a unique element y in S such that x*y*x=x and y*x*y=y.

A monoid that happens to be an inverse semigroup is called an inverse monoid; see IsMonoid (51.2-1).
IsInverseMonoid.title: IsInverseSemigroup

SemigroupIdealByGenerators.name: SemigroupIdealByGenerators
SemigroupIdealByGenerators.description: S is a semigroup, gens is a list of elements of S. Returns the two-sided ideal of S generated by gens.
SemigroupIdealByGenerators.title: SemigroupIdealByGenerators

ReesCongruenceOfSemigroupIdeal.name: ReesCongruenceOfSemigroupIdeal
ReesCongruenceOfSemigroupIdeal.description: A two sided ideal I of a semigroup S defines a congruence on S given by ∆ ∪ I × I.
ReesCongruenceOfSemigroupIdeal.title: ReesCongruenceOfSemigroupIdeal

IsSemigroupCongruence.name: IsSemigroupCongruence
IsSemigroupCongruence.description: a magma congruence c on a semigroup.
IsSemigroupCongruence.title: IsSemigroupCongruence

IsReesCongruence.name: IsReesCongruence
IsReesCongruence.description: returns true if and only if the congruence c has at most one nonsingleton congruence class.
IsReesCongruence.title: IsReesCongruence

IsQuotientSemigroup.name: IsQuotientSemigroup
IsQuotientSemigroup.description: is the category of semigroups constructed from another semigroup and a congruence on it.
IsQuotientSemigroup.title: IsQuotientSemigroup

HomomorphismQuotientSemigroup.name: HomomorphismQuotientSemigroup
HomomorphismQuotientSemigroup.description: for a congruence cong and a semigroup S. Returns the homomorphism from S to the quotient of S by cong.
HomomorphismQuotientSemigroup.title: HomomorphismQuotientSemigroup

GreensRClasses.name: GreensRClasses
GreensRClasses.description:
If S is a semigroup, then these attributes return the Green's R-, L-, H-, J-, or D-classes, respectively for the semigroup S.

Additionally, if S is a Green's D-class of a semigroup, then GreensRClasses and GreensLClasses return the Green's R- or L-classes of the semigroup, respectively, contained in the D-class S; if S is a Green's D-, R-, or L-class of a semigroup, then GreensHClasses returns the Green's H-classes of the semigroup contained in the Green's class S.

EquivalenceClasses (33.7-3) for a Green's relation lead to one of these functions.
GreensRClasses.title: GreensRClasses

GreensLClasses.name: GreensLClasses
GreensLClasses.description:
If S is a semigroup, then these attributes return the Green's R-, L-, H-, J-, or D-classes, respectively for the semigroup S.

GreensHClasses.description:

GroupHClassOfGreensDClass.name: GroupHClassOfGreensDClass
GroupHClassOfGreensDClass.description: for a D class Dclass of a semigroup, returns a group H class of the D class, or fail if there is no group H class.
GroupHClassOfGreensDClass.title: GroupHClassOfGreensDClass

IsGroupHClass.name: IsGroupHClass
IsGroupHClass.description: returns true if the Green's H class Hclass is a group, which in turn is true if and only if Hclass^2 intersects Hclass.
IsGroupHClass.title: IsGroupHClass

IsRegularDClass.name: IsRegularDClass
IsRegularDClass.description: returns true if the Greens D class Dclass is regular. A D class is regular if and only if each of its elements is regular, which in turn is true if and only if any one element of Dclass is regular. Idempotents are regular since eee = e so it follows that a Green's D class containing an idempotent is regular. Conversely, it is true that a regular D class must contain at least one idempotent. (See [How76, Prop. 3.2].)
IsRegularDClass.title: IsRegularDClass

DisplaySemigroup.name: DisplaySemigroup
DisplaySemigroup.description:
Produces a convenient display of a transformation semigroup's D-Class structure. Let S be a transformation semigroup of degree n. Then for each r≤ n, we show all D-classes of rank r.

A regular D-class with a single H-class of size 120 appears as

(the * denoting regularity).
DisplaySemigroup.title: DisplaySemigroup

ReesMatrixSemigroup.name: ReesMatrixSemigroup
ReesMatrixSemigroup.description:
Returns: A Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup.

When S is a semigroup and mat is an m by n matrix with entries in S, the function ReesMatrixSemigroup returns the n by m Rees matrix semigroup over S with multiplication defined by mat.

The arguments of ReesZeroMatrixSemigroup should be a semigroup S and an m by n matrix mat with entries in S or equal to the integer 0. ReesZeroMatrixSemigroup returns the n by m Rees 0-matrix semigroup over S with multiplication defined by mat. In GAP a Rees 0-matrix semigroup always contains a multiplicative zero element, regardless of whether there are any entries in mat which are equal to 0.
ReesMatrixSemigroup.title: ReesMatrixSemigroup

ReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.name: ReesZeroMatrixSemigroup
ReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.description:
Returns: A Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup.

When S is a semigroup and mat is an m by n matrix with entries in S, the function ReesMatrixSemigroup returns the n by m Rees matrix semigroup over S with multiplication defined by mat.

The arguments of ReesZeroMatrixSemigroup should be a semigroup S and an m by n matrix mat with entries in S or equal to the integer 0. ReesZeroMatrixSemigroup returns the n by m Rees 0-matrix semigroup over S with multiplication defined by mat. In GAP a Rees 0-matrix semigroup always contains a multiplicative zero element, regardless of whether there are any entries in mat which are equal to 0.
ReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.title: ReesMatrixSemigroup

ReesMatrixSubsemigroup.name: ReesMatrixSubsemigroup
ReesMatrixSubsemigroup.description:
Returns: A Rees matrix or 0-matrix subsemigroup.

The arguments of ReesMatrixSubsemigroup should be a Rees matrix semigroup R, subsets I and J of the rows and columns of R, respectively, and a subsemigroup U of the underlying semigroup of R. ReesMatrixSubsemigroup returns the subsemigroup of R generated by the direct product of I, U, and J.

The usage and returned value of ReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup is analogous when R is a Rees 0-matrix semigroup.
ReesMatrixSubsemigroup.title: ReesMatrixSubsemigroup

ReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup.name: ReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup
ReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup.description:
Returns: A Rees matrix or 0-matrix subsemigroup.

The arguments of ReesMatrixSubsemigroup should be a Rees matrix semigroup R, subsets I and J of the rows and columns of R, respectively, and a subsemigroup U of the underlying semigroup of R. ReesMatrixSubsemigroup returns the subsemigroup of R generated by the direct product of I, U, and J.

The usage and returned value of ReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup is analogous when R is a Rees 0-matrix semigroup.
ReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup.title: ReesMatrixSubsemigroup

IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup.name: IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup
IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup.description:
Returns: An isomorphism.

Every finite simple semigroup is isomorphic to a Rees matrix semigroup over a group, and every finite 0-simple semigroup is isomorphic to a Rees 0-matrix semigroup over a group.

If the argument S is a simple semigroup, then IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup returns an isomorphism to a Rees matrix semigroup over a group. If S is not simple, then IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup returns an error.
IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup.title: IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup

IsomorphismReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.name: IsomorphismReesZeroMatrixSemigroup
IsomorphismReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.description:
Returns: An isomorphism.

Every finite simple semigroup is isomorphic to a Rees matrix semigroup over a group, and every finite 0-simple semigroup is isomorphic to a Rees 0-matrix semigroup over a group.

If the argument S is a simple semigroup, then IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup returns an isomorphism to a Rees matrix semigroup over a group. If S is not simple, then IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup returns an error.
IsomorphismReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.title: IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup

IsReesMatrixSemigroupElement.name: IsReesMatrixSemigroupElement
IsReesMatrixSemigroupElement.description:
Returns: true or false.

Every element of a Rees matrix semigroup belongs to the category IsReesMatrixSemigroupElement, and every element of a Rees 0-matrix semigroup belongs to the category IsReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement.
IsReesMatrixSemigroupElement.title: IsReesMatrixSemigroupElement

IsReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement.name: IsReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement
IsReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement.description:
Returns: true or false.

Every element of a Rees matrix semigroup belongs to the category IsReesMatrixSemigroupElement, and every element of a Rees 0-matrix semigroup belongs to the category IsReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement.
IsReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement.title: IsReesMatrixSemigroupElement

ReesMatrixSemigroupElement.name: ReesMatrixSemigroupElement
ReesMatrixSemigroupElement.description:
Returns: An element of a Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup.

The arguments of ReesMatrixSemigroupElement should be a Rees matrix subsemigroup R, elements i and j of the the rows and columns of R, respectively, and an element x of the underlying semigroup of R. ReesMatrixSemigroupElement returns the element of R with row index i, underlying element x in the underlying semigroup of R, and column index j, if such an element exist, if such an element exists.

The usage of ReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement is analogous to that of ReesMatrixSemigroupElement, when R is a Rees 0-matrix semigroup.
ReesMatrixSemigroupElement.title: ReesMatrixSemigroupElement

ReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement.name: ReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement
ReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement.description:
Returns: An element of a Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup.

The arguments of ReesMatrixSemigroupElement should be a Rees matrix subsemigroup R, elements i and j of the the rows and columns of R, respectively, and an element x of the underlying semigroup of R. ReesMatrixSemigroupElement returns the element of R with row index i, underlying element x in the underlying semigroup of R, and column index j, if such an element exist, if such an element exists.

The usage of ReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement is analogous to that of ReesMatrixSemigroupElement, when R is a Rees 0-matrix semigroup.
ReesZeroMatrixSemigroupElement.title: ReesMatrixSemigroupElement

IsReesMatrixSubsemigroup.name: IsReesMatrixSubsemigroup
IsReesMatrixSubsemigroup.description:
Returns: true or false.

Every semigroup consisting of elements of a Rees matrix semigroup satisfies the property IsReesMatrixSubsemigroup and every semigroup of Rees 0-matrix semigroup elements satisfies IsReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup.

Note that a subsemigroup of a Rees matrix semigroup is not necessarily a Rees matrix semigroup.
IsReesMatrixSubsemigroup.title: IsReesMatrixSubsemigroup

IsReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup.name: IsReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup
IsReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup.description:
Returns: true or false.

Every semigroup consisting of elements of a Rees matrix semigroup satisfies the property IsReesMatrixSubsemigroup and every semigroup of Rees 0-matrix semigroup elements satisfies IsReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup.

Note that a subsemigroup of a Rees matrix semigroup is not necessarily a Rees matrix semigroup.
IsReesZeroMatrixSubsemigroup.title: IsReesMatrixSubsemigroup

IsReesMatrixSemigroup.name: IsReesMatrixSemigroup
IsReesMatrixSemigroup.description:
Returns: true or false.

A subsemigroup of a Rees matrix semigroup I× U× J satisfies IsReesMatrixSemigroup if and only if it is equal to I'× U'× J' where I'⊆ I, J'⊆ J, and U' is a subsemigroup of U. It can be costly to check that a subsemigroup defined by generators satisfies IsReesMatrixSemigroup. The analogous statements holds for Rees 0-matrix semigroups.

It is not necessarily the case that a simple subsemigroups of Rees matrix semigroups satisfies IsReesMatrixSemigroup. A Rees matrix semigroup is simple if and only if its underlying semigroup is simple. A finite semigroup is simple if and only if it is isomorphic to a Rees matrix semigroup over a group; this isomorphism can be obtained explicitly using IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup (51.9-3).
IsReesMatrixSemigroup.title: IsReesMatrixSemigroup

IsReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.name: IsReesZeroMatrixSemigroup
IsReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.description:
Returns: true or false.

A subsemigroup of a Rees matrix semigroup I× U× J satisfies IsReesMatrixSemigroup if and only if it is equal to I'× U'× J' where I'⊆ I, J'⊆ J, and U' is a subsemigroup of U. It can be costly to check that a subsemigroup defined by generators satisfies IsReesMatrixSemigroup. The analogous statements holds for Rees 0-matrix semigroups.

It is not necessarily the case that a simple subsemigroups of Rees matrix semigroups satisfies IsReesMatrixSemigroup. A Rees matrix semigroup is simple if and only if its underlying semigroup is simple. A finite semigroup is simple if and only if it is isomorphic to a Rees matrix semigroup over a group; this isomorphism can be obtained explicitly using IsomorphismReesMatrixSemigroup (51.9-3).
IsReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.title: IsReesMatrixSemigroup

MatrixOfReesMatrixSemigroup.name: MatrixOfReesMatrixSemigroup
MatrixOfReesMatrixSemigroup.description:
Returns: A matrix.

If R is a Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup, then MatrixOfReesMatrixSemigroup respectively MatrixOfReesZeroMatrixSemigroup return the matrix used to define multiplication in R. For convenience, one may also abbreviate either to Matrix.

More specifically, if R is a Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup, which is a proper subsemigroup of another such semigroup, then Matrix returns the matrix used to define the Rees matrix (or 0-matrix) semigroup consisting of the whole family to which the elements of R belong. Thus, for example, a 1 by 1 Rees matrix semigroup can have a 65 by 15 matrix.
MatrixOfReesMatrixSemigroup.title: Matrix

MatrixOfReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.name: MatrixOfReesZeroMatrixSemigroup
MatrixOfReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.description:
Returns: A matrix.

If R is a Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup, then MatrixOfReesMatrixSemigroup respectively MatrixOfReesZeroMatrixSemigroup return the matrix used to define multiplication in R. For convenience, one may also abbreviate either to Matrix.

More specifically, if R is a Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup, which is a proper subsemigroup of another such semigroup, then Matrix returns the matrix used to define the Rees matrix (or 0-matrix) semigroup consisting of the whole family to which the elements of R belong. Thus, for example, a 1 by 1 Rees matrix semigroup can have a 65 by 15 matrix.
MatrixOfReesZeroMatrixSemigroup.title: Matrix

Rows.name: Rows
Rows.description:
Returns: The rows or columns of R.

Rows returns the rows of the Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup R. Note that the rows of the semigroup correspond to the columns of the matrix used to define multiplication in R.

Columns returns the columns of the Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup R. Note that the columns of the semigroup correspond to the rows of the matrix used to define multiplication in R.
Rows.title: Rows and columns

Columns.name: Columns
Columns.description:
Returns: The rows or columns of R.

Rows returns the rows of the Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup R. Note that the rows of the semigroup correspond to the columns of the matrix used to define multiplication in R.

Columns returns the columns of the Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup R. Note that the columns of the semigroup correspond to the rows of the matrix used to define multiplication in R.
Columns.title: Rows and columns

UnderlyingSemigroup.name: UnderlyingSemigroup
UnderlyingSemigroup.description:
Returns: A semigroup.

UnderlyingSemigroup returns the underlying semigroup of the Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup R.

Arbitrary subsemigroups of Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroups do not have an underlying semigroup. Such a subsemigroup R has an underlying semigroup if and only if it satisfies IsReesMatrixSemigroup (51.9-7) or IsReesZeroMatrixSemigroup (51.9-7).
UnderlyingSemigroup.title: UnderlyingSemigroup

AssociatedReesMatrixSemigroupOfDClass.name: AssociatedReesMatrixSemigroupOfDClass
AssociatedReesMatrixSemigroupOfDClass.description:
Returns: A Rees matrix or 0-matrix semigroup.

If D is a regular \(\mathcal{D}\)-class of a finite semigroup S, then there is a standard way of associating a Rees matrix semigroup to D. If D is a subsemigroup of S, then D is simple and hence is isomorphic to a Rees matrix semigroup. In this case, the associated Rees matrix semigroup of D is just the Rees matrix semigroup isomorphic to D.

If D is not a subsemigroup of S, then we define a semigroup with elements D and a new element 0 with multiplication of x,y∈ D defined by:
AssociatedReesMatrixSemigroupOfDClass.title: AssociatedReesMatrixSemigroupOfDClass

IsSubsemigroupFpSemigroup.name: IsSubsemigroupFpSemigroup
IsSubsemigroupFpSemigroup.description:
The first function returns true if t is a finitely presented semigroup or a subsemigroup of a finitely presented semigroup. The second function does the equivalent thing for monoids. (Generally speaking, such a subsemigroup or monoid can be constructed with Semigroup(gens) or Monoid(gens), where gens is a list of elements of a finitely presented semigroup or monoid.)

A submonoid of a monoid has the same identity as the monoid.
IsSubsemigroupFpSemigroup.title: IsSubsemigroupFpSemigroup

IsSubmonoidFpMonoid.name: IsSubmonoidFpMonoid
IsSubmonoidFpMonoid.description:
The first function returns true if t is a finitely presented semigroup or a subsemigroup of a finitely presented semigroup. The second function does the equivalent thing for monoids. (Generally speaking, such a subsemigroup or monoid can be constructed with Semigroup(gens) or Monoid(gens), where gens is a list of elements of a finitely presented semigroup or monoid.)

A submonoid of a monoid has the same identity as the monoid.
IsSubmonoidFpMonoid.title: IsSubsemigroupFpSemigroup

IsFpSemigroup.name: IsFpSemigroup
IsFpSemigroup.description:
The first function is a synonym for IsSubsemigroupFpSemigroup(s) and IsWholeFamily(s) (this is because a subsemigroup of a finitely presented semigroup is not necessarily finitely presented).

Similarly, the second function is a synonym for IsSubmonoidFpMonoid(m) and IsWholeFamily(m).
IsFpSemigroup.title: IsFpSemigroup

IsFpMonoid.name: IsFpMonoid
IsFpMonoid.description:
The first function is a synonym for IsSubsemigroupFpSemigroup(s) and IsWholeFamily(s) (this is because a subsemigroup of a finitely presented semigroup is not necessarily finitely presented).

Similarly, the second function is a synonym for IsSubmonoidFpMonoid(m) and IsWholeFamily(m).
IsFpMonoid.title: IsFpSemigroup

IsElementOfFpSemigroup.name: IsElementOfFpSemigroup
IsElementOfFpSemigroup.description: returns true if elm is an element of a finitely presented semigroup or monoid.
IsElementOfFpSemigroup.title: IsElementOfFpSemigroup

IsElementOfFpMonoid.name: IsElementOfFpMonoid
IsElementOfFpMonoid.description: returns true if elm is an element of a finitely presented semigroup or monoid.
IsElementOfFpMonoid.title: IsElementOfFpSemigroup

FpGrpMonSmgOfFpGrpMonSmgElement.name: FpGrpMonSmgOfFpGrpMonSmgElement
FpGrpMonSmgOfFpGrpMonSmgElement.description: returns the finitely presented group, monoid or semigroup to which elm belongs.
FpGrpMonSmgOfFpGrpMonSmgElement.title: FpGrpMonSmgOfFpGrpMonSmgElement

FactorFreeSemigroupByRelations.name: FactorFreeSemigroupByRelations
FactorFreeSemigroupByRelations.description:
for a free semigroup or free monoid f and a list rels of pairs of elements of f. Returns the finitely presented semigroup or monoid which is the quotient of f by the least congruence on f generated by the pairs in rels.

Users should be aware that much of the code described in this chapter is in need of substantial revision. In particular, the two functions described here are not called by the operation \/ of the previous subsection, and so are liable to be removed in due course.
FactorFreeSemigroupByRelations.title: FactorFreeSemigroupByRelations

FactorFreeMonoidByRelations.name: FactorFreeMonoidByRelations
FactorFreeMonoidByRelations.description:
for a free semigroup or free monoid f and a list rels of pairs of elements of f. Returns the finitely presented semigroup or monoid which is the quotient of f by the least congruence on f generated by the pairs in rels.

Users should be aware that much of the code described in this chapter is in need of substantial revision. In particular, the two functions described here are not called by the operation \/ of the previous subsection, and so are liable to be removed in due course.
FactorFreeMonoidByRelations.title: FactorFreeSemigroupByRelations

IsomorphismFpSemigroup.name: IsomorphismFpSemigroup
IsomorphismFpSemigroup.description: for a finitely presented monoid m or a finitely presented group g. Returns an isomorphism from m or g to a finitely presented semigroup or monoid.
IsomorphismFpSemigroup.title: IsomorphismFpSemigroup

IsomorphismFpMonoid.name: IsomorphismFpMonoid
IsomorphismFpMonoid.description: for a finitely presented monoid m or a finitely presented group g. Returns an isomorphism from m or g to a finitely presented semigroup or monoid.
IsomorphismFpMonoid.title: IsomorphismFpSemigroup

ElementOfFpSemigroup.name: ElementOfFpSemigroup
ElementOfFpSemigroup.description: for a family fam of elements of a finitely presented semigroup or monoid and a word word in the free generators underlying this finitely presented semigroup or monoid. Returns the element of the finitely presented semigroup or monoid with the representative word in the free semigroup or free monoid. These operations are inverse to UnderlyingElement.
ElementOfFpSemigroup.title: ElementOfFpSemigroup

ElementOfFpMonoid.name: ElementOfFpMonoid
ElementOfFpMonoid.description: for a family fam of elements of a finitely presented semigroup or monoid and a word word in the free generators underlying this finitely presented semigroup or monoid. Returns the element of the finitely presented semigroup or monoid with the representative word in the free semigroup or free monoid. These operations are inverse to UnderlyingElement.
ElementOfFpMonoid.title: ElementOfFpSemigroup

FreeSemigroupOfFpSemigroup.name: FreeSemigroupOfFpSemigroup
FreeSemigroupOfFpSemigroup.description: returns the underlying free semigroup or free monoid for the finitely presented semigroup s or monoid m, i.e. the free semigroup or free monoid over which s or m is defined as a quotient. (This is the free semigroup or free monoid generated by the free generators provided by FreeGeneratorsOfFpSemigroup(s) or FreeGeneratorsOfFpMonoid(m)).
FreeSemigroupOfFpSemigroup.title: FreeSemigroupOfFpSemigroup

FreeMonoidOfFpMonoid.name: FreeMonoidOfFpMonoid
FreeMonoidOfFpMonoid.description: returns the underlying free semigroup or free monoid for the finitely presented semigroup s or monoid m, i.e. the free semigroup or free monoid over which s or m is defined as a quotient. (This is the free semigroup or free monoid generated by the free generators provided by FreeGeneratorsOfFpSemigroup(s) or FreeGeneratorsOfFpMonoid(m)).
FreeMonoidOfFpMonoid.title: FreeSemigroupOfFpSemigroup

FreeGeneratorsOfFpSemigroup.name: FreeGeneratorsOfFpSemigroup
FreeGeneratorsOfFpSemigroup.description: returns the underlying free generators corresponding to the generators of the finitely presented semigroup s or monoid m.
FreeGeneratorsOfFpSemigroup.title: FreeGeneratorsOfFpSemigroup

FreeGeneratorsOfFpMonoid.name: FreeGeneratorsOfFpMonoid
FreeGeneratorsOfFpMonoid.description: returns the underlying free generators corresponding to the generators of the finitely presented semigroup s or monoid m.
FreeGeneratorsOfFpMonoid.title: FreeGeneratorsOfFpSemigroup

RelationsOfFpSemigroup.name: RelationsOfFpSemigroup
RelationsOfFpSemigroup.description: returns the relations of the finitely presented semigroup s or monoid m as pairs of words in the free generators provided by FreeGeneratorsOfFpSemigroup(s) or FreeGeneratorsOfFpMonoid(m).
RelationsOfFpSemigroup.title: RelationsOfFpSemigroup

RelationsOfFpMonoid.name: RelationsOfFpMonoid
RelationsOfFpMonoid.description: returns the relations of the finitely presented semigroup s or monoid m as pairs of words in the free generators provided by FreeGeneratorsOfFpSemigroup(s) or FreeGeneratorsOfFpMonoid(m).
RelationsOfFpMonoid.title: RelationsOfFpSemigroup

ReducedConfluentRewritingSystem.name: ReducedConfluentRewritingSystem
ReducedConfluentRewritingSystem.description:
returns a reduced confluent rewriting system of the finitely presented semigroup or monoid S with respect to the reduction ordering ordering (see 34).

The default for ordering is the length plus lexicographic ordering on words, also called the shortlex ordering; for the definition see for example [Sim94].

Notice that this might not terminate. In particular, if the semigroup or monoid S does not have a solvable word problem then it this will certainly never end. Also, in this case, the object returned is an immutable rewriting system, because once we have a confluent rewriting system for a finitely presented semigroup or monoid we do not want to allow it to change (as it was most probably very time consuming to get it in the first place). Furthermore, this is also an attribute storing object (see 13.4).
ReducedConfluentRewritingSystem.title: ReducedConfluentRewritingSystem

KB_REW.name: KB_REW
KB_REW.description: KB_REW is a global record variable whose components contain functions used for Knuth-Bendix. By default KB_REW is assigned to GAPKB_REW, which contains the KB functions provided by the GAP library.
KB_REW.title: KB_REW

GAPKB_REW.name: GAPKB_REW
GAPKB_REW.description: KB_REW is a global record variable whose components contain functions used for Knuth-Bendix. By default KB_REW is assigned to GAPKB_REW, which contains the KB functions provided by the GAP library.
GAPKB_REW.title: KB_REW

KnuthBendixRewritingSystem.name: KnuthBendixRewritingSystem
KnuthBendixRewritingSystem.description: in the first form, for a semigroup s and a reduction ordering for the underlying free semigroup, it returns the Knuth-Bendix rewriting system of the finitely presented semigroup s using the reduction ordering wordord. In the second form, for a monoid m and a reduction ordering for the underlying free monoid, it returns the Knuth-Bendix rewriting system of the finitely presented monoid m using the reduction ordering wordord.
KnuthBendixRewritingSystem.title: KnuthBendixRewritingSystem

KnuthBendixRewritingSystem.name: KnuthBendixRewritingSystem
KnuthBendixRewritingSystem.description: in the first form, for a semigroup s and a reduction ordering for the underlying free semigroup, it returns the Knuth-Bendix rewriting system of the finitely presented semigroup s using the reduction ordering wordord. In the second form, for a monoid m and a reduction ordering for the underlying free monoid, it returns the Knuth-Bendix rewriting system of the finitely presented monoid m using the reduction ordering wordord.
KnuthBendixRewritingSystem.title: KnuthBendixRewritingSystem

SemigroupOfRewritingSystem.name: SemigroupOfRewritingSystem
SemigroupOfRewritingSystem.description: returns the semigroup or monoid over which rws is a rewriting system.
SemigroupOfRewritingSystem.title: SemigroupOfRewritingSystem

MonoidOfRewritingSystem.name: MonoidOfRewritingSystem
MonoidOfRewritingSystem.description: returns the semigroup or monoid over which rws is a rewriting system.
MonoidOfRewritingSystem.title: SemigroupOfRewritingSystem

FreeSemigroupOfRewritingSystem.name: FreeSemigroupOfRewritingSystem
FreeSemigroupOfRewritingSystem.description:
returns the free semigroup or monoid over which rws is a rewriting system.

As mentioned before, having a confluent rewriting system, one can decide whether two words represent the same element of a finitely presented semigroup (or finitely presented monoid).
FreeSemigroupOfRewritingSystem.title: FreeSemigroupOfRewritingSystem

FreeMonoidOfRewritingSystem.name: FreeMonoidOfRewritingSystem
FreeMonoidOfRewritingSystem.description:
returns the free semigroup or monoid over which rws is a rewriting system.

As mentioned before, having a confluent rewriting system, one can decide whether two words represent the same element of a finitely presented semigroup (or finitely presented monoid).
FreeMonoidOfRewritingSystem.title: FreeSemigroupOfRewritingSystem

CosetTableOfFpSemigroup.name: CosetTableOfFpSemigroup
CosetTableOfFpSemigroup.description:
r is a right congruence of an fp-semigroup S. This attribute is the coset table of FP semigroup S on a right congruence r. Given a right congruence r we represent S as a set of transformations of the congruence classes of r.

The images of the cosets under the generators are compiled in a list table such that table[i][s] contains the image of coset s under generator i.
CosetTableOfFpSemigroup.title: CosetTableOfFpSemigroup

IsTransformation.name: IsTransformation
IsTransformation.description: Every transformation in GAP belongs to the category IsTransformation. Basic operations for transformations are ImageListOfTransformation (53.5-2), ImageSetOfTransformation (53.5-3), KernelOfTransformation (53.5-12), FlatKernelOfTransformation (53.5-11), RankOfTransformation (53.5-4), DegreeOfTransformation (53.5-1), multiplication of two transformations via *, and exponentiation with the first argument a positive integer i and second argument a transformation f where the result is the image i ^ f of the point i under f.
IsTransformation.title: IsTransformation

IsTransformationCollection.name: IsTransformationCollection
IsTransformationCollection.description: Every collection of transformations belongs to the category IsTransformationCollection. For example, transformation semigroups belong to IsTransformationCollection.
IsTransformationCollection.title: IsTransformationCollection

TransformationFamily.name: TransformationFamily
TransformationFamily.description: The family of all transformations is TransformationFamily.
TransformationFamily.title: TransformationFamily

Transformation.name: Transformation
Transformation.description:
Returns: A transformation.

TransformationList returns the transformation f such that i ^ f = list[i] if i is between 1 and the length of list and i ^ f = i if i is larger than the length of list. An error will occur in TransformationList if list is not dense, if list contains an element which is not a positive integer, or if list contains an integer not in [ 1 .. Length( list ) ].

TransformationList is the analogue in the context of transformations of PermList (42.5-2). Transformation is a synonym of TransformationList when the argument is a list.
Transformation.title: Transformation

Transformation.name: Transformation
Transformation.description:
Returns: A transformation.

TransformationList returns the transformation f such that i ^ f = list[i] if i is between 1 and the length of list and i ^ f = i if i is larger than the length of list. An error will occur in TransformationList if list is not dense, if list contains an element which is not a positive integer, or if list contains an integer not in [ 1 .. Length( list ) ].

TransformationList is the analogue in the context of transformations of PermList (42.5-2). Transformation is a synonym of TransformationList when the argument is a list.
Transformation.title: Transformation

Transformation.name: Transformation
Transformation.description:
Returns: A transformation.

If src and dst are lists of positive integers of the same length, such that src contains no element twice, then TransformationListList( src, dst ) returns a transformation f such that src[i] ^ f = dst[i]. The transformation f fixes all points larger than the maximum of the entries in src and dst.

TransformationListList is the analogue in the context of transformations of MappingPermListList (42.5-3). Transformation is a synonym of TransformationListList when its arguments are two lists of positive integers.
Transformation.title: Transformation

TransformationList.name: TransformationList
TransformationList.description:
Returns: A transformation.

TransformationList returns the transformation f such that i ^ f = list[i] if i is between 1 and the length of list and i ^ f = i if i is larger than the length of list. An error will occur in TransformationList if list is not dense, if list contains an element which is not a positive integer, or if list contains an integer not in [ 1 .. Length( list ) ].

TransformationList is the analogue in the context of transformations of PermList (42.5-2). Transformation is a synonym of TransformationList when the argument is a list.
TransformationList.title: Transformation

TransformationListList.name: TransformationListList
TransformationListList.description:
Returns: A transformation.

If src and dst are lists of positive integers of the same length, such that src contains no element twice, then TransformationListList( src, dst ) returns a transformation f such that src[i] ^ f = dst[i]. The transformation f fixes all points larger than the maximum of the entries in src and dst.

TransformationListList is the analogue in the context of transformations of MappingPermListList (42.5-3). Transformation is a synonym of TransformationListList when its arguments are two lists of positive integers.
TransformationListList.title: Transformation

TransformationByImageAndKernel.name: TransformationByImageAndKernel
TransformationByImageAndKernel.description:
Returns: A transformation or fail.

This operation returns the transformation f where i ^ f = im[ker[i]] for i in the range [ 1 .. Length( ker ) ]. This transformation has flat kernel equal to ker and image set equal to Set( im ).

The argument im should be a duplicate free list of positive integers and ker should be the flat kernel of a transformation with rank equal to the length of im. If the arguments do not fulfil these conditions, then fail is returned.
TransformationByImageAndKernel.title: TransformationByImageAndKernel

Idempotent.name: Idempotent
Idempotent.description:
Returns: A transformation or fail.

Idempotent returns the idempotent transformation with image set im and flat kernel ker if such a transformation exists and fail if it does not. More specifically, a transformation is returned when the argument im is a set of positive integers and ker is the flat kernel of a transformation with rank equal to the length of im and where im has one element in every class of the kernel corresponding to ker.

Note that this is function does not always return the same transformation as TransformationByImageAndKernel with the same arguments.
Idempotent.title: Idempotent

TransformationOp.name: TransformationOp
TransformationOp.description:
Returns: A transformation or fail.

TransformationOp returns the transformation that corresponds to the action of the object obj on the domain or list list via the function func. If the optional third argument func is not specified, then the action OnPoints (41.2-1) is used by default. Note that the returned transformation refers to the positions in list even if list itself consists of integers.

This function is the analogue in the context of transformations of Permutation (41.9-1).
TransformationOp.title: TransformationOp

TransformationOpNC.name: TransformationOpNC
TransformationOpNC.description:
Returns: A transformation or fail.

TransformationOp returns the transformation that corresponds to the action of the object obj on the domain or list list via the function func. If the optional third argument func is not specified, then the action OnPoints (41.2-1) is used by default. Note that the returned transformation refers to the positions in list even if list itself consists of integers.

This function is the analogue in the context of transformations of Permutation (41.9-1).
TransformationOpNC.title: TransformationOp

TransformationNumber.name: TransformationNumber
TransformationNumber.description:
Returns: A transformation or a number.

These functions implement a bijection from the transformations with degree at most n to the numbers [ 1 .. n ^ n ].

More precisely, if m and n are positive integers such that m is at most n ^ n, then TransformationNumber returns the mth transformation with degree at most n.
TransformationNumber.title: TransformationNumber

NumberTransformation.name: NumberTransformation
NumberTransformation.description:
Returns: A transformation or a number.

These functions implement a bijection from the transformations with degree at most n to the numbers [ 1 .. n ^ n ].

More precisely, if m and n are positive integers such that m is at most n ^ n, then TransformationNumber returns the mth transformation with degree at most n.
NumberTransformation.title: TransformationNumber

RandomTransformation.name: RandomTransformation
RandomTransformation.description:
Returns: A random transformation.

If n is a positive integer, then RandomTransformation returns a random transformation with degree at most n.
RandomTransformation.title: RandomTransformation

IdentityTransformation.name: IdentityTransformation
IdentityTransformation.description: This variable is bound to the identity transformation, which has degree 0.
IdentityTransformation.title: IdentityTransformation

ConstantTransformation.name: ConstantTransformation
ConstantTransformation.description:
Returns: A transformation.

This function returns a constant transformation f such that i ^ f = n for all i less than or equal to m, when n and m are positive integers.
ConstantTransformation.title: ConstantTransformation

AsTransformation.name: AsTransformation
AsTransformation.description:
Returns: A transformation.

AsTransformation returns the permutation, transformation, partial permutation or binary relation f as a transformation.

If f is a permutation and n is a non-negative integer, then AsTransformation( f, n ) returns the transformation g such that i ^ g = i ^ f for all i in the range [ 1 .. n ].
AsTransformation.title: AsTransformation

RestrictedTransformation.name: RestrictedTransformation
RestrictedTransformation.description:
Returns: A transformation.

RestrictedTransformation returns the new transformation g such that i ^ g = i ^ f for all i in list and such that i ^ g = i for all i not in list.
RestrictedTransformation.title: RestrictedTransformation

PermutationOfImage.name: PermutationOfImage
PermutationOfImage.description:
Returns: A permutation or fail.

If the transformation f is a permutation of the points in its image, then PermutationOfImage returns this permutation. If f does not permute its image, then fail is returned.

If f happens to be a permutation, then PermutationOfImage with argument f returns the same value as AsPermutation with argument f.
PermutationOfImage.title: PermutationOfImage

PermLeftQuoTransformation.name: PermLeftQuoTransformation
PermLeftQuoTransformation.description:
Returns: A permutation.

Returns the permutation on the image set of f induced by f ^ -1 * g when the transformations f and g have equal kernel and image set.

PermLeftQuoTransformation verifies that f and g have equal kernels and image sets, and returns an error if they do not. PermLeftQuoTransformationNC does no checks.
PermLeftQuoTransformation.title: PermLeftQuoTransformation

PermLeftQuoTransformationNC.name: PermLeftQuoTransformationNC
PermLeftQuoTransformationNC.description:
Returns: A permutation.

Returns the permutation on the image set of f induced by f ^ -1 * g when the transformations f and g have equal kernel and image set.

PermLeftQuoTransformation verifies that f and g have equal kernels and image sets, and returns an error if they do not. PermLeftQuoTransformationNC does no checks.
PermLeftQuoTransformationNC.title: PermLeftQuoTransformation

IsInjectiveListTrans.name: IsInjectiveListTrans
IsInjectiveListTrans.description:
Returns: true or false.

The argument obj should be a transformation or the list of images of a transformation and list should be a list of positive integers. IsInjectiveListTrans checks if obj is injective on list.

More precisely, if obj is a transformation, then we define f := obj and if obj is the image list of a transformation we define f := Transformation( obj ). IsInjectiveListTrans returns true if f is injective on list and false if it is not. If list is not duplicate free, then false is returned.
IsInjectiveListTrans.title: IsInjectiveListTrans

ComponentTransformationInt.name: ComponentTransformationInt
ComponentTransformationInt.description:
Returns: A list of positive integers.

If f is a transformation and n is a positive integer, then ComponentTransformationInt returns those elements i such that n ^ f ^ j = i for some positive integer j, i.e. the elements of the component of f containing n that can be obtained by applying powers of f to n.
ComponentTransformationInt.title: ComponentTransformationInt

PreImagesOfTransformation.name: PreImagesOfTransformation
PreImagesOfTransformation.description:
Returns: A set of positive integers.

Returns the preimages of the positive integer n under the transformation f, i.e. the positive integers i such that i ^ f = n.
PreImagesOfTransformation.title: PreImagesOfTransformation

DegreeOfTransformation.name: DegreeOfTransformation
DegreeOfTransformation.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

The degree of a transformation f is the largest value such that n ^ f <> n or i ^ f = n for some i <> n. Equivalently, the degree of a transformation is the least value n such that [ n + 1, n + 2, ... ] is fixed pointwise by f.

The degree of a collection of transformations coll is the maximum degree of any transformation in coll.
DegreeOfTransformation.title: DegreeOfTransformation

DegreeOfTransformationCollection.name: DegreeOfTransformationCollection
DegreeOfTransformationCollection.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

The degree of a transformation f is the largest value such that n ^ f <> n or i ^ f = n for some i <> n. Equivalently, the degree of a transformation is the least value n such that [ n + 1, n + 2, ... ] is fixed pointwise by f.

The degree of a collection of transformations coll is the maximum degree of any transformation in coll.
DegreeOfTransformationCollection.title: DegreeOfTransformation

ImageListOfTransformation.name: ImageListOfTransformation
ImageListOfTransformation.description:
Returns: The list of images of a transformation.

Returns the list of images of [ 1 .. n ] under the transformation f, which is [ 1 ^ f .. n ^ f ]. If the optional second argument n is not present, then the degree of f is used by default.

This is the analogue for transformations of ListPerm (42.5-1) for permutations.
ImageListOfTransformation.title: ImageListOfTransformation

ListTransformation.name: ListTransformation
ListTransformation.description:
Returns: The list of images of a transformation.

Returns the list of images of [ 1 .. n ] under the transformation f, which is [ 1 ^ f .. n ^ f ]. If the optional second argument n is not present, then the degree of f is used by default.

This is the analogue for transformations of ListPerm (42.5-1) for permutations.
ListTransformation.title: ImageListOfTransformation

ImageSetOfTransformation.name: ImageSetOfTransformation
ImageSetOfTransformation.description:
Returns: The set of images of the transformation.

Returns the set of points in the list of images of [ 1 .. n ] under f, i.e. the sorted list of images with duplicates removed. If the optional second argument n is not given, then the degree of f is used.
ImageSetOfTransformation.title: ImageSetOfTransformation

RankOfTransformation.name: RankOfTransformation
RankOfTransformation.description:
Returns: The rank of a transformation.

When the arguments are a transformation f and a positive integer n, RankOfTransformation returns the size of the set of images of the transformation f in the range [ 1 .. n ]. If the optional second argument n is not specified, then the degree of f is used.

When the arguments are a transformation f and a list list of positive integers, this function returns the size of the set of images of the transformation f on list.
RankOfTransformation.title: RankOfTransformation

RankOfTransformation.name: RankOfTransformation
RankOfTransformation.description:
Returns: The rank of a transformation.

When the arguments are a transformation f and a positive integer n, RankOfTransformation returns the size of the set of images of the transformation f in the range [ 1 .. n ]. If the optional second argument n is not specified, then the degree of f is used.

When the arguments are a transformation f and a list list of positive integers, this function returns the size of the set of images of the transformation f on list.
RankOfTransformation.title: RankOfTransformation

SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.name: SmallestImageOfMovedPoint
SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.description:
Returns: A positive integer or infinity.

SmallestImageOfMovedPoint returns the smallest positive integer i ^ f such that i ^ f <> i if such an i exists. If f is the identity transformation, then infinity is returned.

If the argument is a collection of transformations coll, then the smallest integer which is the image a point moved by at least one element of coll is returned, if such a point exists. If coll only contains identity transformations, then SmallestImageOfMovedPoint returns infinity.
SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.title: SmallestImageOfMovedPoint

SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.name: SmallestImageOfMovedPoint
SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.description:
Returns: A positive integer or infinity.

SmallestImageOfMovedPoint returns the smallest positive integer i ^ f such that i ^ f <> i if such an i exists. If f is the identity transformation, then infinity is returned.

If the argument is a collection of transformations coll, then the smallest integer which is the image a point moved by at least one element of coll is returned, if such a point exists. If coll only contains identity transformations, then SmallestImageOfMovedPoint returns infinity.
SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.title: SmallestImageOfMovedPoint

SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.name: SmallestImageOfMovedPoint
SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.description:
Returns: A positive integer or infinity.

SmallestImageOfMovedPoint returns the smallest positive integer i^f such that i^f<>i if such an i exists. If f is the identity partial permutation, then infinity is returned.

If the argument is a collection of partial permutations coll, then the smallest integer which is the image a point moved by at least one element of coll is returned, if such a point exists. If coll only contains identity partial permutations, then SmallestImageOfMovedPoint returns infinity.
SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.title: SmallestImageOfMovedPoint

SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.name: SmallestImageOfMovedPoint
SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.description:
Returns: A positive integer or infinity.

SmallestImageOfMovedPoint returns the smallest positive integer i^f such that i^f<>i if such an i exists. If f is the identity partial permutation, then infinity is returned.

If the argument is a collection of partial permutations coll, then the smallest integer which is the image a point moved by at least one element of coll is returned, if such a point exists. If coll only contains identity partial permutations, then SmallestImageOfMovedPoint returns infinity.
SmallestImageOfMovedPoint.title: SmallestImageOfMovedPoint

LargestImageOfMovedPoint.name: LargestImageOfMovedPoint
LargestImageOfMovedPoint.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

LargestImageOfMovedPoint returns the largest positive integer i ^ f such that i ^ f <> i if such an i exists. If f is the identity transformation, then 0 is returned.

If the argument is a collection of transformations coll, then the largest integer which is the image a point moved by at least one element of coll is returned, if such a point exists. If coll only contains identity transformations, then LargestImageOfMovedPoint returns 0.
LargestImageOfMovedPoint.title: LargestImageOfMovedPoint

LargestImageOfMovedPoint.name: LargestImageOfMovedPoint
LargestImageOfMovedPoint.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

LargestImageOfMovedPoint returns the largest positive integer i ^ f such that i ^ f <> i if such an i exists. If f is the identity transformation, then 0 is returned.

If the argument is a collection of transformations coll, then the largest integer which is the image a point moved by at least one element of coll is returned, if such a point exists. If coll only contains identity transformations, then LargestImageOfMovedPoint returns 0.
LargestImageOfMovedPoint.title: LargestImageOfMovedPoint

LargestImageOfMovedPoint.name: LargestImageOfMovedPoint
LargestImageOfMovedPoint.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

LargestImageOfMovedPoint returns the largest positive integer i^f such that i^f<>i if such an i exists. If f is an identity partial permutation, then 0 is returned.

If the argument is a collection of partial permutations coll, then the largest integer which is the image of a point moved by at least one element of coll is returned, if such a point exists. If coll only contains identity partial permutations, then LargestImageOfMovedPoint returns 0.
LargestImageOfMovedPoint.title: LargestImageOfMovedPoint

LargestImageOfMovedPoint.name: LargestImageOfMovedPoint
LargestImageOfMovedPoint.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

LargestImageOfMovedPoint returns the largest positive integer i^f such that i^f<>i if such an i exists. If f is an identity partial permutation, then 0 is returned.

If the argument is a collection of partial permutations coll, then the largest integer which is the image of a point moved by at least one element of coll is returned, if such a point exists. If coll only contains identity partial permutations, then LargestImageOfMovedPoint returns 0.
LargestImageOfMovedPoint.title: LargestImageOfMovedPoint

FlatKernelOfTransformation.name: FlatKernelOfTransformation
FlatKernelOfTransformation.description:
Returns: The flat kernel of a transformation.

If the kernel classes of the transformation f on [ 1 .. n ] are K_1, dots, K_r, then FlatKernelOfTransformation returns a list L such that L[i] = j for all i in K_j. For a given transformation and positive integer n, there is a unique such list.

If the optional second argument n is not present, then the degree of f is used by default.
FlatKernelOfTransformation.title: FlatKernelOfTransformation

KernelOfTransformation.name: KernelOfTransformation
KernelOfTransformation.description:
Returns: The kernel of a transformation.

When the arguments are a transformation f, a positive integer n, and true, KernelOfTransformation returns the kernel of the transformation f on [ 1 .. n ] as a set of sets of positive integers. If the argument bool is false, then only the non-singleton classes are returned.

The second and third arguments are optional, the default values are the degree of f and true.
KernelOfTransformation.title: KernelOfTransformation

InverseOfTransformation.name: InverseOfTransformation
InverseOfTransformation.description:
Returns: A transformation.

InverseOfTransformation returns a semigroup inverse of the transformation f in the full transformation semigroup. An inverse of f is any transformation g such that f * g * f = f and g * f * g = g. Every transformation has at least one inverse.
InverseOfTransformation.title: InverseOfTransformation

IndexPeriodOfTransformation.name: IndexPeriodOfTransformation
IndexPeriodOfTransformation.description:
Returns: A pair of positive integers.

Returns the least positive integers m and r such that f ^ (m + r) = f ^ m, which are known as the index and period of the transformation f.
IndexPeriodOfTransformation.title: IndexPeriodOfTransformation

SmallestIdempotentPower.name: SmallestIdempotentPower
SmallestIdempotentPower.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

This function returns the least positive integer n such that the transformation f ^ n is an idempotent. The smallest idempotent power of f is the least multiple of the period of f that is greater than or equal to the index of f; see IndexPeriodOfTransformation (53.5-15).
SmallestIdempotentPower.title: SmallestIdempotentPower

SmallestIdempotentPower.name: SmallestIdempotentPower
SmallestIdempotentPower.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

This function returns the least positive integer n such that the partial permutation f^n is an idempotent. The smallest idempotent power of f is the least multiple of the period of f that is greater than or equal to the index of f; see IndexPeriodOfPartialPerm (54.3-16).
SmallestIdempotentPower.title: SmallestIdempotentPower

ComponentsOfTransformation.name: ComponentsOfTransformation
ComponentsOfTransformation.description:
Returns: A list of lists of positive integers.

ComponentsOfTransformation returns a list of the components of the transformation f. Each component is a subset of [ 1 .. DegreeOfTransformation( f ) ], and the union of the components is [ 1 .. DegreeOfTransformation( f ) ].
ComponentsOfTransformation.title: ComponentsOfTransformation

NrComponentsOfTransformation.name: NrComponentsOfTransformation
NrComponentsOfTransformation.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

NrComponentsOfTransformation returns the number of components of the transformation f on the range [ 1 .. DegreeOfTransformation( f ) ].
NrComponentsOfTransformation.title: NrComponentsOfTransformation

ComponentRepsOfTransformation.name: ComponentRepsOfTransformation
ComponentRepsOfTransformation.description:
Returns: A list of lists of positive integers.

ComponentRepsOfTransformation returns the representatives, in the following sense, of the components of the transformation f. For every i in [ 1 .. DegreeOfTransformation( f ) ] there exists a representative j and a positive integer k such that i ^ (f ^ k) = j. The representatives returned by ComponentRepsOfTransformation are partitioned according to the component they belong to. ComponentRepsOfTransformation returns the least number of representatives.
ComponentRepsOfTransformation.title: ComponentRepsOfTransformation

CyclesOfTransformation.name: CyclesOfTransformation
CyclesOfTransformation.description:
Returns: A list of lists of positive integers.

When the arguments of this function are a transformation f and a list list, it returns a list of the cycles of the components of f containing any element of list.

If the optional second argument is not present, then the range [ 1 .. DegreeOfTransformation( f ) ] is used as the default value for list.
CyclesOfTransformation.title: CyclesOfTransformation

CycleTransformationInt.name: CycleTransformationInt
CycleTransformationInt.description:
Returns: A list of positive integers.

If f is a transformation and n is a positive integer, then CycleTransformationInt returns the cycle of the component of f containing n.
CycleTransformationInt.title: CycleTransformationInt

LeftOne.name: LeftOne
LeftOne.description:
Returns: A transformation.

LeftOne returns an idempotent transformation e such that the kernel (with respect to the degree of f) of e equals the kernel of the transformation f and e * f = f.

RightOne returns an idempotent transformation e such that the image set (with respect to the degree of f) of e equals the image set of f and f * e = f.
LeftOne.title: LeftOne

LeftOne.name: LeftOne
LeftOne.description:
Returns: A partial permutation.

LeftOne returns the identity partial permutation e such that the domain and image of e equal the domain of the partial permutation f and such that e*f=f.

RightOne returns the identity partial permutation e such that the domain and image of e equal the image of f and such that f*e=f.
LeftOne.title: LeftOne

RightOne.name: RightOne
RightOne.description:
Returns: A transformation.

LeftOne returns an idempotent transformation e such that the kernel (with respect to the degree of f) of e equals the kernel of the transformation f and e * f = f.

RightOne returns an idempotent transformation e such that the image set (with respect to the degree of f) of e equals the image set of f and f * e = f.
RightOne.title: LeftOne

RightOne.name: RightOne
RightOne.description:
Returns: A partial permutation.

LeftOne returns the identity partial permutation e such that the domain and image of e equal the domain of the partial permutation f and such that e*f=f.

RightOne returns the identity partial permutation e such that the domain and image of e equal the image of f and such that f*e=f.
RightOne.title: LeftOne

TrimTransformation.name: TrimTransformation
TrimTransformation.description:
Returns: Nothing.

It can happen that the internal representation of a transformation uses more memory than necessary. For example, this can happen when composing transformations where it is possible that the resulting transformation f belongs to IsTrans4Rep and stores its images as 32-bit integers, while none of its moved points exceeds 65536. The purpose of TrimTransformation is to change the internal representation of such an f to remove the trailing fixed points in the internal representation of f.

If the optional second argument n is provided, then the internal representation of f is reduced to the images of the first n positive integers. Please note that it must be the case that i ^ f <= n for all i in the range [ 1 .. n ] otherwise the resulting object will not define a transformation.
TrimTransformation.title: TrimTransformation

IsTransformationSemigroup.name: IsTransformationSemigroup
IsTransformationSemigroup.description:
Returns: true or false.

A transformation semigroup is simply a semigroup consisting of transformations. An object obj is a transformation semigroup in GAP if it satisfies IsSemigroup (51.1-1) and IsTransformationCollection (53.1-2).

A transformation monoid is a monoid consisting of transformations. An object obj is a transformation monoid in GAP if it satisfies IsMonoid (51.2-1) and IsTransformationCollection (53.1-2).
IsTransformationSemigroup.title: IsTransformationSemigroup

IsTransformationMonoid.name: IsTransformationMonoid
IsTransformationMonoid.description:
Returns: true or false.

A transformation semigroup is simply a semigroup consisting of transformations. An object obj is a transformation semigroup in GAP if it satisfies IsSemigroup (51.1-1) and IsTransformationCollection (53.1-2).

A transformation monoid is a monoid consisting of transformations. An object obj is a transformation monoid in GAP if it satisfies IsMonoid (51.2-1) and IsTransformationCollection (53.1-2).
IsTransformationMonoid.title: IsTransformationSemigroup

DegreeOfTransformationSemigroup.name: DegreeOfTransformationSemigroup
DegreeOfTransformationSemigroup.description:
Returns: A non-negative integer.

The degree of a transformation semigroup S is just the maximum of the degrees of the elements of S.
DegreeOfTransformationSemigroup.title: DegreeOfTransformationSemigroup

FullTransformationSemigroup.name: FullTransformationSemigroup
FullTransformationSemigroup.description:
Returns: The full transformation semigroup of degree n.

If n is a positive integer, then FullTransformationSemigroup returns the monoid consisting of all transformations with degree at most n, called the full transformation semigroup.

The full transformation semigroup is regular, has n ^ n elements, and is generated by any set containing transformations that generate the symmetric group on n points and any transformation of rank n - 1.
FullTransformationSemigroup.title: FullTransformationSemigroup

FullTransformationMonoid.name: FullTransformationMonoid
FullTransformationMonoid.description:
Returns: The full transformation semigroup of degree n.

If n is a positive integer, then FullTransformationSemigroup returns the monoid consisting of all transformations with degree at most n, called the full transformation semigroup.

The full transformation semigroup is regular, has n ^ n elements, and is generated by any set containing transformations that generate the symmetric group on n points and any transformation of rank n - 1.
FullTransformationMonoid.title: FullTransformationSemigroup

IsFullTransformationSemigroup.name: IsFullTransformationSemigroup
IsFullTransformationSemigroup.description:
Returns: true or false.

If the transformation semigroup S of degree n contains every transformation of degree at most n, then IsFullTransformationSemigroup returns true and otherwise it returns false.

IsFullTransformationMonoid is a synonym of IsFullTransformationSemigroup. It is common in the literature for the full transformation monoid to be referred to as the full transformation semigroup.
IsFullTransformationSemigroup.title: IsFullTransformationSemigroup

IsFullTransformationMonoid.name: IsFullTransformationMonoid
IsFullTransformationMonoid.description:
Returns: true or false.

If the transformation semigroup S of degree n contains every transformation of degree at most n, then IsFullTransformationSemigroup returns true and otherwise it returns false.

IsFullTransformationMonoid is a synonym of IsFullTransformationSemigroup. It is common in the literature for the full transformation monoid to be referred to as the full transformation semigroup.
IsFullTransformationMonoid.title: IsFullTransformationSemigroup

IsomorphismTransformationSemigroup.name: IsomorphismTransformationSemigroup
IsomorphismTransformationSemigroup.description:
Returns: An isomorphism to a transformation semigroup or monoid.

Returns an isomorphism from the finite semigroup S to a transformation semigroup. For most types of objects in GAP the degree of this transformation semigroup will be equal to the size of S plus 1.

Let S ^ 1 denote the monoid obtained from S by adjoining an identity element. Then S acts faithfully on S ^ 1 by right multiplication, i.e. every element of S describes a transformation on 1, .. , |S| + 1. The isomorphism from S to the transformation semigroup described in this way is called the right regular representation of S. In most cases, IsomorphismTransformationSemigroup will return the right regular representation of S.
IsomorphismTransformationSemigroup.title: IsomorphismTransformationSemigroup

IsomorphismTransformationMonoid.name: IsomorphismTransformationMonoid
IsomorphismTransformationMonoid.description:
Returns: An isomorphism to a transformation semigroup or monoid.

Returns an isomorphism from the finite semigroup S to a transformation semigroup. For most types of objects in GAP the degree of this transformation semigroup will be equal to the size of S plus 1.

Let S ^ 1 denote the monoid obtained from S by adjoining an identity element. Then S acts faithfully on S ^ 1 by right multiplication, i.e. every element of S describes a transformation on 1, .. , |S| + 1. The isomorphism from S to the transformation semigroup described in this way is called the right regular representation of S. In most cases, IsomorphismTransformationSemigroup will return the right regular representation of S.
IsomorphismTransformationMonoid.title: IsomorphismTransformationSemigroup

AntiIsomorphismTransformationSemigroup.name: AntiIsomorphismTransformationSemigroup
AntiIsomorphismTransformationSemigroup.description:
Returns: An anti-isomorphism.

If S is a semigroup, then AntiIsomorphismTransformationSemigroup returns an anti-isomorphism from S to a transformation semigroup. At present, the degree of the resulting transformation semigroup equals the size of S plus 1, and, consequently, this function is of limited use.
AntiIsomorphismTransformationSemigroup.title: AntiIsomorphismTransformationSemigroup

IsPartialPerm.name: IsPartialPerm
IsPartialPerm.description:
Returns: true or false.

Every partial permutation in GAP belongs to the category IsPartialPerm. Basic operations for partial permutations are DomainOfPartialPerm (54.3-4), ImageListOfPartialPerm (54.3-6), ImageSetOfPartialPerm (54.3-7), RankOfPartialPerm (54.3-3), DegreeOfPartialPerm (54.3-1), multiplication of two partial permutations is via *, and exponentiation with the first argument a positive integer i and second argument a partial permutation f where the result is the image i^f of the point i under f. The inverse of a partial permutation f can be obtains using f^-1.
IsPartialPerm.title: IsPartialPerm

IsPartialPermCollection.name: IsPartialPermCollection
IsPartialPermCollection.description: Every collection of partial permutations belongs to the category IsPartialPermCollection. For example, a semigroup of partial permutations belongs in IsPartialPermCollection.
IsPartialPermCollection.title: IsPartialPermCollection

PartialPermFamily.name: PartialPermFamily
PartialPermFamily.description: The family of all partial permutations is PartialPermFamily
PartialPermFamily.title: PartialPermFamily

PartialPerm.name: PartialPerm
PartialPerm.description:
Returns: A partial permutation.

Partial permutations can be created in two ways: by giving the domain and the image, or the dense image list.

The partial permutation defined by a domain dom and image img, where dom is a set of positive integers and img is a duplicate free list of positive integers, maps dom[i] to img[i]. For example, the partial permutation mapping 1 and 5 to 20 and 2 can be created using:
PartialPerm.title: PartialPerm

PartialPerm.name: PartialPerm
PartialPerm.description:
Returns: A partial permutation.

Partial permutations can be created in two ways: by giving the domain and the image, or the dense image list.

The partial permutation defined by a domain dom and image img, where dom is a set of positive integers and img is a duplicate free list of positive integers, maps dom[i] to img[i]. For example, the partial permutation mapping 1 and 5 to 20 and 2 can be created using:
PartialPerm.title: PartialPerm

PartialPermOp.name: PartialPermOp
PartialPermOp.description:
Returns: A partial permutation or fail.

PartialPermOp returns the partial permutation that corresponds to the action of the object obj on the domain or list list via the function func. If the optional third argument func is not specified, then the action OnPoints (41.2-1) is used by default. Note that the returned partial permutation refers to the positions in list even if list itself consists of integers.

This function is the analogue in the context of partial permutations of Permutation (41.9-1) or TransformationOp (53.2-5).
PartialPermOp.title: PartialPermOp

PartialPermOpNC.name: PartialPermOpNC
PartialPermOpNC.description:
Returns: A partial permutation or fail.

PartialPermOp returns the partial permutation that corresponds to the action of the object obj on the domain or list list via the function func. If the optional third argument func is not specified, then the action OnPoints (41.2-1) is used by default. Note that the returned partial permutation refers to the positions in list even if list itself consists of integers.

This function is the analogue in the context of partial permutations of Permutation (41.9-1) or TransformationOp (53.2-5).
PartialPermOpNC.title: PartialPermOp

RestrictedPartialPerm.name: RestrictedPartialPerm
RestrictedPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A partial permutation.

RestrictedPartialPerm returns a new partial permutation that acts on the points in the set of positive integers set in the same way as the partial permutation f, and that is undefined on those points that are not in set.
RestrictedPartialPerm.title: RestrictedPartialPerm

JoinOfPartialPerms.name: JoinOfPartialPerms
JoinOfPartialPerms.description:
Returns: A partial permutation or fail.

The join of partial permutations f and g is just the join, or supremum, of f and g under the natural partial ordering of partial permutations.

JoinOfPartialPerms returns the union of the partial permutations in its argument if this defines a partial permutation, and fail if it is not. The argument arg can be a partial permutation collection or a number of partial permutations.
JoinOfPartialPerms.title: JoinOfPartialPerms

JoinOfIdempotentPartialPermsNC.name: JoinOfIdempotentPartialPermsNC
JoinOfIdempotentPartialPermsNC.description:
Returns: A partial permutation or fail.

The join of partial permutations f and g is just the join, or supremum, of f and g under the natural partial ordering of partial permutations.

JoinOfPartialPerms returns the union of the partial permutations in its argument if this defines a partial permutation, and fail if it is not. The argument arg can be a partial permutation collection or a number of partial permutations.
JoinOfIdempotentPartialPermsNC.title: JoinOfPartialPerms

MeetOfPartialPerms.name: MeetOfPartialPerms
MeetOfPartialPerms.description:
Returns: A partial permutation.

The meet of partial permutations f and g is just the meet, or infimum, of f and g under the natural partial ordering of partial permutations. In other words, the meet is the greatest partial permutation which is a restriction of both f and g.

Note that unlike the join of partial permutations, the meet always exists.
MeetOfPartialPerms.title: MeetOfPartialPerms

EmptyPartialPerm.name: EmptyPartialPerm
EmptyPartialPerm.description:
Returns: The empty partial permutation.

The empty partial permutation is returned by this function when it is called with no arguments. This is just short hand for PartialPerm([]);.
EmptyPartialPerm.title: EmptyPartialPerm

RandomPartialPerm.name: RandomPartialPerm
RandomPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A random partial permutation.

In its first form, RandomPartialPerm returns a randomly chosen partial permutation where points in the domain and image are bounded above by the positive integer n.

In its second form, RandomPartialPerm returns a randomly chosen partial permutation with points in the domain and image contained in the set of positive integers set.
RandomPartialPerm.title: RandomPartialPerm

RandomPartialPerm.name: RandomPartialPerm
RandomPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A random partial permutation.

In its first form, RandomPartialPerm returns a randomly chosen partial permutation where points in the domain and image are bounded above by the positive integer n.

In its second form, RandomPartialPerm returns a randomly chosen partial permutation with points in the domain and image contained in the set of positive integers set.
RandomPartialPerm.title: RandomPartialPerm

RandomPartialPerm.name: RandomPartialPerm
RandomPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A random partial permutation.

In its first form, RandomPartialPerm returns a randomly chosen partial permutation where points in the domain and image are bounded above by the positive integer n.

In its second form, RandomPartialPerm returns a randomly chosen partial permutation with points in the domain and image contained in the set of positive integers set.
RandomPartialPerm.title: RandomPartialPerm

DegreeOfPartialPerm.name: DegreeOfPartialPerm
DegreeOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A non-negative integer.

The degree of a partial permutation f is the largest positive integer where it is defined, i.e. the maximum element in the domain of f.

The degree a collection of partial permutations coll is the largest degree of any partial permutation in coll.
DegreeOfPartialPerm.title: DegreeOfPartialPerm

DegreeOfPartialPermCollection.name: DegreeOfPartialPermCollection
DegreeOfPartialPermCollection.description:
Returns: A non-negative integer.

The degree of a partial permutation f is the largest positive integer where it is defined, i.e. the maximum element in the domain of f.

The degree a collection of partial permutations coll is the largest degree of any partial permutation in coll.
DegreeOfPartialPermCollection.title: DegreeOfPartialPerm

CodegreeOfPartialPerm.name: CodegreeOfPartialPerm
CodegreeOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A non-negative integer.

The codegree of a partial permutation f is the largest positive integer in its image.

The codegree a collection of partial permutations coll is the largest codegree of any partial permutation in coll.
CodegreeOfPartialPerm.title: CodegreeOfPartialPerm

CodegreeOfPartialPermCollection.name: CodegreeOfPartialPermCollection
CodegreeOfPartialPermCollection.description:
Returns: A non-negative integer.

The codegree of a partial permutation f is the largest positive integer in its image.

The codegree a collection of partial permutations coll is the largest codegree of any partial permutation in coll.
CodegreeOfPartialPermCollection.title: CodegreeOfPartialPerm

RankOfPartialPerm.name: RankOfPartialPerm
RankOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A non-negative integer.

The rank of a partial permutation f is the size of its domain, or equivalently the size of its image set or image list.

The rank of a partial permutation collection coll is the size of the union of the domains of the elements of coll, or equivalently, the total number of points on which the elements of coll act. Note that this is value may not the same as the size of the union of the images of the elements in coll.
RankOfPartialPerm.title: RankOfPartialPerm

RankOfPartialPermCollection.name: RankOfPartialPermCollection
RankOfPartialPermCollection.description:
Returns: A non-negative integer.

The rank of a partial permutation f is the size of its domain, or equivalently the size of its image set or image list.

The rank of a partial permutation collection coll is the size of the union of the domains of the elements of coll, or equivalently, the total number of points on which the elements of coll act. Note that this is value may not the same as the size of the union of the images of the elements in coll.
RankOfPartialPermCollection.title: RankOfPartialPerm

DomainOfPartialPerm.name: DomainOfPartialPerm
DomainOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A set of positive integers (maybe empty).

The domain of a partial permutation f is the set of positive integers where f is defined.

The domain of a partial permutation collection coll is the union of the domains of its elements.
DomainOfPartialPerm.title: DomainOfPartialPerm

DomainOfPartialPermCollection.name: DomainOfPartialPermCollection
DomainOfPartialPermCollection.description:
Returns: A set of positive integers (maybe empty).

The domain of a partial permutation f is the set of positive integers where f is defined.

The domain of a partial permutation collection coll is the union of the domains of its elements.
DomainOfPartialPermCollection.title: DomainOfPartialPerm

ImageOfPartialPermCollection.name: ImageOfPartialPermCollection
ImageOfPartialPermCollection.description:
Returns: A set of positive integers (maybe empty).
