The image of a partial permutation collection coll is the union of the images of its elements.
ImageOfPartialPermCollection.title: ImageOfPartialPermCollection

ImageListOfPartialPerm.name: ImageListOfPartialPerm
ImageListOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: The list of images of a partial permutation.

The image list of a partial permutation f is the list of images of the elements of the domain f where ImageListOfPartialPerm(f)[i]=DomainOfPartialPerm(f)[i]^f for any i in the range from 1 to the rank of f.
ImageListOfPartialPerm.title: ImageListOfPartialPerm

ImageSetOfPartialPerm.name: ImageSetOfPartialPerm
ImageSetOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: The image set of a partial permutation.

The image set of a partial permutation f is just the set of points in the image list (i.e. the image list after it has been sorted into increasing order).
ImageSetOfPartialPerm.title: ImageSetOfPartialPerm

FixedPointsOfPartialPerm.name: FixedPointsOfPartialPerm
FixedPointsOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A set of positive integers.

FixedPointsOfPartialPerm returns the set of points i in the domain of the partial permutation f such that i^f=i.

When the argument is a collection of partial permutations coll, FixedPointsOfPartialPerm returns the set of points fixed by every element of the collection of partial permutations coll.
FixedPointsOfPartialPerm.title: FixedPointsOfPartialPerm

FixedPointsOfPartialPerm.name: FixedPointsOfPartialPerm
FixedPointsOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A set of positive integers.

FixedPointsOfPartialPerm returns the set of points i in the domain of the partial permutation f such that i^f=i.

When the argument is a collection of partial permutations coll, FixedPointsOfPartialPerm returns the set of points fixed by every element of the collection of partial permutations coll.
FixedPointsOfPartialPerm.title: FixedPointsOfPartialPerm

NrFixedPoints.name: NrFixedPoints
NrFixedPoints.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

NrFixedPoints returns the number of points i in the domain of the partial permutation f such that i^f=i.

When the argument is a collection of partial permutations coll, NrFixedPoints returns the number of points fixed by every element of the collection of partial permutations coll.
NrFixedPoints.title: NrFixedPoints

NrFixedPoints.name: NrFixedPoints
NrFixedPoints.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

NrFixedPoints returns the number of points i in the domain of the partial permutation f such that i^f=i.

When the argument is a collection of partial permutations coll, NrFixedPoints returns the number of points fixed by every element of the collection of partial permutations coll.
NrFixedPoints.title: NrFixedPoints

IndexPeriodOfPartialPerm.name: IndexPeriodOfPartialPerm
IndexPeriodOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A pair of positive integers.

Returns the least positive integers m, r such that f^(m+r)=f^m, which are known as the index and period of the partial permutation f.
IndexPeriodOfPartialPerm.title: IndexPeriodOfPartialPerm

ComponentsOfPartialPerm.name: ComponentsOfPartialPerm
ComponentsOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A list of lists of positive integer.

ComponentsOfPartialPerm returns a list of the components of the partial permutation f. Each component is a subset of the domain of f, and the union of the components equals the domain.
ComponentsOfPartialPerm.title: ComponentsOfPartialPerm

NrComponentsOfPartialPerm.name: NrComponentsOfPartialPerm
NrComponentsOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A positive integer.

NrComponentsOfPartialPerm returns the number of components of the partial permutation f on its domain.
NrComponentsOfPartialPerm.title: NrComponentsOfPartialPerm

ComponentRepsOfPartialPerm.name: ComponentRepsOfPartialPerm
ComponentRepsOfPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A list of positive integers.

ComponentRepsOfPartialPerm returns the representatives, in the following sense, of the components of the partial permutation f. Every component of f contains a unique element in the domain but not the image of f; this element is called the representative of the component. If i is a representative of a component of f, then for every jnot=i in the component of i, there exists a positive integer k such that i ^ (f ^ k) = j. Unlike transformations, there is exactly one representative for every component of f. ComponentRepsOfPartialPerm returns the least number of representatives.
ComponentRepsOfPartialPerm.title: ComponentRepsOfPartialPerm

AsPartialPerm.name: AsPartialPerm
AsPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A partial permutation.

A permutation f defines a partial permutation when it is restricted to any finite set of positive integers. AsPartialPerm can be used to obtain this partial permutation.

There are several possible arguments for AsPartialPerm:
AsPartialPerm.title: AsPartialPerm

AsPartialPerm.name: AsPartialPerm
AsPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A partial permutation.

A permutation f defines a partial permutation when it is restricted to any finite set of positive integers. AsPartialPerm can be used to obtain this partial permutation.

There are several possible arguments for AsPartialPerm:
AsPartialPerm.title: AsPartialPerm

AsPartialPerm.name: AsPartialPerm
AsPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A partial permutation.

A permutation f defines a partial permutation when it is restricted to any finite set of positive integers. AsPartialPerm can be used to obtain this partial permutation.

There are several possible arguments for AsPartialPerm:
AsPartialPerm.title: AsPartialPerm

AsPartialPerm.name: AsPartialPerm
AsPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A partial permutation or fail.

A transformation f defines a partial permutation when it is restricted to a set of positive integers where it is injective. AsPartialPerm can be used to obtain this partial permutation.

There are several possible arguments for AsPartialPerm:
AsPartialPerm.title: AsPartialPerm

AsPartialPerm.name: AsPartialPerm
AsPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A partial permutation or fail.

A transformation f defines a partial permutation when it is restricted to a set of positive integers where it is injective. AsPartialPerm can be used to obtain this partial permutation.

There are several possible arguments for AsPartialPerm:
AsPartialPerm.title: AsPartialPerm

PermLeftQuoPartialPerm.name: PermLeftQuoPartialPerm
PermLeftQuoPartialPerm.description:
Returns: A permutation.

Returns the permutation on the image set of f induced by f^-1*g when the partial permutations f and g have equal domain and image set.

PermLeftQuoPartialPerm verifies that f and g have equal domains and image sets, and returns an error if they do not. PermLeftQuoPartialPermNC does no checks.
PermLeftQuoPartialPerm.title: PermLeftQuoPartialPerm

PermLeftQuoPartialPermNC.name: PermLeftQuoPartialPermNC
PermLeftQuoPartialPermNC.description:
Returns: A permutation.

Returns the permutation on the image set of f induced by f^-1*g when the partial permutations f and g have equal domain and image set.

PermLeftQuoPartialPerm verifies that f and g have equal domains and image sets, and returns an error if they do not. PermLeftQuoPartialPermNC does no checks.
PermLeftQuoPartialPermNC.title: PermLeftQuoPartialPerm

PreImagePartialPerm.name: PreImagePartialPerm
PreImagePartialPerm.description:
Returns: A positive integer or fail.

PreImagePartialPerm returns the preimage of the positive integer i under the partial permutation f if i belongs to the image of f. If i does not belong to the image of f, then fail is returned.

The same result can be obtained by using i/f as described in Section 54.5.
PreImagePartialPerm.title: PreImagePartialPerm

ComponentPartialPermInt.name: ComponentPartialPermInt
ComponentPartialPermInt.description:
Returns: A set of positive integers.

ComponentPartialPermInt returns the elements of the component of f containing i that can be obtained by repeatedly applying f to i.
ComponentPartialPermInt.title: ComponentPartialPermInt

NaturalLeqPartialPerm.name: NaturalLeqPartialPerm
NaturalLeqPartialPerm.description:
Returns: true or false.

The natural partial order ≤ on an inverse semigroup S is defined by s≤t if there exists an idempotent e in S such that s=et. Hence if f and g are partial permutations, then f≤g if and only if f is a restriction of g; see RestrictedPartialPerm (54.2-3).

NaturalLeqPartialPerm returns true if f is a restriction of g and false if it is not. Note that since this is a partial order and not a total order, it is possible that f and g are incomparable with respect to the natural partial order.
NaturalLeqPartialPerm.title: NaturalLeqPartialPerm

ShortLexLeqPartialPerm.name: ShortLexLeqPartialPerm
ShortLexLeqPartialPerm.description:
Returns: true or false.

ShortLexLeqPartialPerm returns true if the concatenation of the domain and image list of f is short-lex less than the corresponding concatenation for g and false otherwise.

Note that this is not the natural partial order on partial permutation or the same as comparing f and g using \<.
ShortLexLeqPartialPerm.title: ShortLexLeqPartialPerm

TrimPartialPerm.name: TrimPartialPerm
TrimPartialPerm.description:
Returns: Nothing.

It can happen that the internal representation of a partial permutation uses more memory than necessary. For example, by composing a partial permutation with codegree less than 65536 with a partial permutation with codegree greater than 65535. It is possible that the resulting partial permutation f has its codegree and images stored as 32-bit integers, while none of its image points exceeds 65536. The purpose of this function is to change the internal representation of such an f from using 32-bit to using 16-bit integers.

Note that the partial permutation f is changed in-place, and nothing is returned by this function.
TrimPartialPerm.title: TrimPartialPerm

IsPartialPermSemigroup.name: IsPartialPermSemigroup
IsPartialPermSemigroup.description:
Returns: true or false.

A partial perm semigroup is simply a semigroup consisting of partial permutations, which may or may not be an inverse semigroup. An object obj in GAP is a partial perm semigroup if and only if it satisfies IsSemigroup (51.1-1) and IsPartialPermCollection (54.1-2).

A partial perm monoid is a monoid consisting of partial permutations. An object in GAP is a partial perm monoid if it satisfies IsMonoid (51.2-1) and IsPartialPermCollection (54.1-2).
IsPartialPermSemigroup.title: IsPartialPermSemigroup

IsPartialPermMonoid.name: IsPartialPermMonoid
IsPartialPermMonoid.description:
Returns: true or false.

A partial perm semigroup is simply a semigroup consisting of partial permutations, which may or may not be an inverse semigroup. An object obj in GAP is a partial perm semigroup if and only if it satisfies IsSemigroup (51.1-1) and IsPartialPermCollection (54.1-2).

A partial perm monoid is a monoid consisting of partial permutations. An object in GAP is a partial perm monoid if it satisfies IsMonoid (51.2-1) and IsPartialPermCollection (54.1-2).
IsPartialPermMonoid.title: IsPartialPermSemigroup

DegreeOfPartialPermSemigroup.name: DegreeOfPartialPermSemigroup
DegreeOfPartialPermSemigroup.description:
Returns: A non-negative integer.

The degree of a partial permutation semigroup S is the largest degree of any partial permutation in S.

The codegree of a partial permutation semigroup S is the largest positive integer in its image.
DegreeOfPartialPermSemigroup.title: DegreeOfPartialPermSemigroup

CodegreeOfPartialPermSemigroup.name: CodegreeOfPartialPermSemigroup
CodegreeOfPartialPermSemigroup.description:
Returns: A non-negative integer.

The degree of a partial permutation semigroup S is the largest degree of any partial permutation in S.

The codegree of a partial permutation semigroup S is the largest positive integer in its image.
CodegreeOfPartialPermSemigroup.title: DegreeOfPartialPermSemigroup

RankOfPartialPermSemigroup.name: RankOfPartialPermSemigroup
RankOfPartialPermSemigroup.description:
Returns: A non-negative integer.

The degree of a partial permutation semigroup S is the largest degree of any partial permutation in S.

The codegree of a partial permutation semigroup S is the largest positive integer in its image.
RankOfPartialPermSemigroup.title: DegreeOfPartialPermSemigroup

SymmetricInverseSemigroup.name: SymmetricInverseSemigroup
SymmetricInverseSemigroup.description:
Returns: The symmetric inverse semigroup of degree n.

If n is a non-negative integer, then SymmetricInverseSemigroup returns the inverse semigroup consisting of all partial permutations with degree and codegree at most n. Note that n must be non-negative, but in particular, can equal 0.

The symmetric inverse semigroup has ∑_r=0^nnchoose r^2⋅ r! elements and is generated by any set that of partial permutations that generate the symmetric group on n points and any partial permutation of rank n-1.
SymmetricInverseSemigroup.title: SymmetricInverseSemigroup

SymmetricInverseMonoid.name: SymmetricInverseMonoid
SymmetricInverseMonoid.description:
Returns: The symmetric inverse semigroup of degree n.

If n is a non-negative integer, then SymmetricInverseSemigroup returns the inverse semigroup consisting of all partial permutations with degree and codegree at most n. Note that n must be non-negative, but in particular, can equal 0.

The symmetric inverse semigroup has ∑_r=0^nnchoose r^2⋅ r! elements and is generated by any set that of partial permutations that generate the symmetric group on n points and any partial permutation of rank n-1.
SymmetricInverseMonoid.title: SymmetricInverseSemigroup

IsSymmetricInverseSemigroup.name: IsSymmetricInverseSemigroup
IsSymmetricInverseSemigroup.description:
Returns: true or false.

If the partial perm semigroup S of degree and codegree n equals the symmetric inverse semigroup on n points, then IsSymmetricInverseSemigroup return true and otherwise it returns false.

IsSymmetricInverseMonoid is a synonym of IsSymmetricInverseSemigroup. It is common in the literature for the symmetric inverse monoid to be referred to as the symmetric inverse semigroup.
IsSymmetricInverseSemigroup.title: IsSymmetricInverseSemigroup

IsSymmetricInverseMonoid.name: IsSymmetricInverseMonoid
IsSymmetricInverseMonoid.description:
Returns: true or false.

If the partial perm semigroup S of degree and codegree n equals the symmetric inverse semigroup on n points, then IsSymmetricInverseSemigroup return true and otherwise it returns false.

IsSymmetricInverseMonoid is a synonym of IsSymmetricInverseSemigroup. It is common in the literature for the symmetric inverse monoid to be referred to as the symmetric inverse semigroup.
IsSymmetricInverseMonoid.title: IsSymmetricInverseSemigroup

NaturalPartialOrder.name: NaturalPartialOrder
NaturalPartialOrder.description:
Returns: The natural partial order on an inverse semigroup.

The natural partial order ≤ on an inverse semigroup S is defined by s≤t if there exists an idempotent e in S such that s=et. Hence if f and g are partial permutations, then f≤g if and only if f is a restriction of g; see RestrictedPartialPerm (54.2-3).

NaturalPartialOrder returns the natural partial order on the inverse semigroup of partial permutations S as a list of sets of positive integers where entry i in NaturalPartialOrder(S) is the set of positions in Elements(S) of elements which are less than Elements(S)[i]. See also NaturalLeqPartialPerm (54.5-13).
NaturalPartialOrder.title: NaturalPartialOrder

ReverseNaturalPartialOrder.name: ReverseNaturalPartialOrder
ReverseNaturalPartialOrder.description:
Returns: The natural partial order on an inverse semigroup.

The natural partial order ≤ on an inverse semigroup S is defined by s≤t if there exists an idempotent e in S such that s=et. Hence if f and g are partial permutations, then f≤g if and only if f is a restriction of g; see RestrictedPartialPerm (54.2-3).

NaturalPartialOrder returns the natural partial order on the inverse semigroup of partial permutations S as a list of sets of positive integers where entry i in NaturalPartialOrder(S) is the set of positions in Elements(S) of elements which are less than Elements(S)[i]. See also NaturalLeqPartialPerm (54.5-13).
ReverseNaturalPartialOrder.title: NaturalPartialOrder

IsomorphismPartialPermSemigroup.name: IsomorphismPartialPermSemigroup
IsomorphismPartialPermSemigroup.description:
Returns: An isomorphism.

IsomorphismPartialPermSemigroup(S) returns an isomorphism from the inverse semigroup S to an inverse semigroup of partial permutations.

IsomorphismPartialPermMonoid(S) returns an isomorphism from the inverse semigroup S to an inverse monoid of partial permutations, if possible.
IsomorphismPartialPermSemigroup.title: IsomorphismPartialPermSemigroup

IsomorphismPartialPermMonoid.name: IsomorphismPartialPermMonoid
IsomorphismPartialPermMonoid.description:
Returns: An isomorphism.

IsomorphismPartialPermSemigroup(S) returns an isomorphism from the inverse semigroup S to an inverse semigroup of partial permutations.

IsomorphismPartialPermMonoid(S) returns an isomorphism from the inverse semigroup S to an inverse monoid of partial permutations, if possible.
IsomorphismPartialPermMonoid.title: IsomorphismPartialPermSemigroup

IsNearAdditiveMagma.name: IsNearAdditiveMagma
IsNearAdditiveMagma.description: A near-additive magma in GAP is a domain A with an associative but not necessarily commutative addition +: A × A → A.
IsNearAdditiveMagma.title: IsNearAdditiveMagma

IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.name: IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero
IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.description:
A near-additive magma-with-zero in GAP is a near-additive magma A with an operation 0* (or Zero (31.10-3)) that yields the zero element of A.

So a near-additive magma-with-zero A does always contain a unique additively neutral element z, i.e., z + a = a = a + z holds for all a ∈ A (see AdditiveNeutralElement (55.3-5)). This zero element z can be computed with the operation Zero (31.10-3), by applying this function to A or to any element a in A. The zero element can be computed also as 0 * a, for any a in A.

Note that it may happen that a near-additive magma containing a zero does not lie in the category IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero (see 31.6).
IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.title: IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero

IsNearAdditiveGroup.name: IsNearAdditiveGroup
IsNearAdditiveGroup.description:
A near-additive group in GAP is a near-additive magma-with-zero A with an operation -1*: A → A that maps each element a of A to its additive inverse -1*a (or AdditiveInverse( a ), see AdditiveInverse (31.10-9)).

The addition + of A is assumed to be associative, so a near-additive group is not more than a near-additive magma-with-inverses. IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithInverses is just a synonym for IsNearAdditiveGroup, and can be used alternatively in all function names involving the string "NearAdditiveGroup".

Note that not every trivial near-additive magma is a near-additive magma-with-zero, but every trivial near-additive magma-with-zero is a near-additive group.
IsNearAdditiveGroup.title: IsNearAdditiveGroup

IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithInverses.name: IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithInverses
IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithInverses.description:
A near-additive group in GAP is a near-additive magma-with-zero A with an operation -1*: A → A that maps each element a of A to its additive inverse -1*a (or AdditiveInverse( a ), see AdditiveInverse (31.10-9)).

The addition + of A is assumed to be associative, so a near-additive group is not more than a near-additive magma-with-inverses. IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithInverses is just a synonym for IsNearAdditiveGroup, and can be used alternatively in all function names involving the string "NearAdditiveGroup".

Note that not every trivial near-additive magma is a near-additive magma-with-zero, but every trivial near-additive magma-with-zero is a near-additive group.
IsNearAdditiveMagmaWithInverses.title: IsNearAdditiveGroup

IsAdditiveMagma.name: IsAdditiveMagma
IsAdditiveMagma.description: An additive magma in GAP is a domain A with an associative and commutative addition +: A × A → A, see IsNearAdditiveMagma (55.1-1) and IsAdditivelyCommutative (55.3-1).
IsAdditiveMagma.title: IsAdditiveMagma

IsAdditiveMagmaWithZero.name: IsAdditiveMagmaWithZero
IsAdditiveMagmaWithZero.description:
An additive magma-with-zero in GAP is an additive magma A (see IsAdditiveMagma (55.1-4) with an operation 0* (or Zero (31.10-3)) that yields the zero of A.

So an additive magma-with-zero A does always contain a unique additively neutral element z, i.e., z + a = a = a + z holds for all a ∈ A (see AdditiveNeutralElement (55.3-5)). This element z can be computed with the operation Zero (31.10-3) as Zero( A ), and z is also equal to Zero( a ) and to 0*a for each element a in A.

Note that it may happen that an additive magma containing a zero does not lie in the category IsAdditiveMagmaWithZero (see 31.6).
IsAdditiveMagmaWithZero.title: IsAdditiveMagmaWithZero

IsAdditiveGroup.name: IsAdditiveGroup
IsAdditiveGroup.description:
An additive group in GAP is an additive magma-with-zero A with an operation -1*: A → A that maps each element a of A to its additive inverse -1*a (or AdditiveInverse( a ), see AdditiveInverse (31.10-9)).

The addition + of A is assumed to be commutative and associative, so an additive group is not more than an additive magma-with-inverses. IsAdditiveMagmaWithInverses is just a synonym for IsAdditiveGroup, and can be used alternatively in all function names involving the string "AdditiveGroup".

Note that not every trivial additive magma is an additive magma-with-zero, but every trivial additive magma-with-zero is an additive group.
IsAdditiveGroup.title: IsAdditiveGroup

IsAdditiveMagmaWithInverses.name: IsAdditiveMagmaWithInverses
IsAdditiveMagmaWithInverses.description:
An additive group in GAP is an additive magma-with-zero A with an operation -1*: A → A that maps each element a of A to its additive inverse -1*a (or AdditiveInverse( a ), see AdditiveInverse (31.10-9)).

The addition + of A is assumed to be commutative and associative, so an additive group is not more than an additive magma-with-inverses. IsAdditiveMagmaWithInverses is just a synonym for IsAdditiveGroup, and can be used alternatively in all function names involving the string "AdditiveGroup".

Note that not every trivial additive magma is an additive magma-with-zero, but every trivial additive magma-with-zero is an additive group.
IsAdditiveMagmaWithInverses.title: IsAdditiveGroup

NearAdditiveMagma.name: NearAdditiveMagma
NearAdditiveMagma.description: returns the (near-)additive magma A that is generated by the elements in the list gens, that is, the closure of gens under addition +. The family Fam of A can be entered as first argument; this is obligatory if gens is empty (and hence also A is empty).
NearAdditiveMagma.title: NearAdditiveMagma

NearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.name: NearAdditiveMagmaWithZero
NearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.description: returns the (near-)additive magma-with-zero A that is generated by the elements in the list gens, that is, the closure of gens under addition + and Zero (31.10-3). The family Fam of A can be entered as first argument; this is obligatory if gens is empty (and hence A is trivial).
NearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.title: NearAdditiveMagmaWithZero

NearAdditiveGroup.name: NearAdditiveGroup
NearAdditiveGroup.description: returns the (near-)additive group A that is generated by the elements in the list gens, that is, the closure of gens under addition +, Zero (31.10-3), and AdditiveInverse (31.10-9). The family Fam of A can be entered as first argument; this is obligatory if gens is empty (and hence A is trivial).
NearAdditiveGroup.title: NearAdditiveGroup

SubnearAdditiveMagma.name: SubnearAdditiveMagma
SubnearAdditiveMagma.description:
SubnearAdditiveMagma returns the near-additive magma generated by the elements in the list gens, with parent the domain D. SubnearAdditiveMagmaNC does the same, except that it does not check whether the elements of gens lie in D.

SubadditiveMagma and SubadditiveMagmaNC are just synonyms of these functions.
SubnearAdditiveMagma.title: SubnearAdditiveMagma

SubadditiveMagmaWithZero and SubadditiveMagmaWithZeroNC are just synonyms of these functions.
