SubadditiveGroupNC.title: SubnearAdditiveGroup

IsAdditivelyCommutative.name: IsAdditivelyCommutative
IsAdditivelyCommutative.description:
A near-additive magma A in GAP is additively commutative if for all elements a, b ∈ A the equality a + b = b + a holds.

Note that the commutativity of the multiplication * in a multiplicative structure can be tested with IsCommutative (35.4-9).
IsAdditivelyCommutative.title: IsAdditivelyCommutative

GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagma.name: GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagma
GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagma.description: is a list of elements of the near-additive magma A that generates A as a near-additive magma, that is, the closure of this list under addition is A.
GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagma.title: GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagma

GeneratorsOfAdditiveMagma.name: GeneratorsOfAdditiveMagma
GeneratorsOfAdditiveMagma.description: is a list of elements of the near-additive magma A that generates A as a near-additive magma, that is, the closure of this list under addition is A.
GeneratorsOfAdditiveMagma.title: GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagma

GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.name: GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero
GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.description: is a list of elements of the near-additive magma-with-zero A that generates A as a near-additive magma-with-zero, that is, the closure of this list under addition and Zero (31.10-3) is A.
GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.title: GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero

GeneratorsOfAdditiveMagmaWithZero.name: GeneratorsOfAdditiveMagmaWithZero
GeneratorsOfAdditiveMagmaWithZero.description: is a list of elements of the near-additive magma-with-zero A that generates A as a near-additive magma-with-zero, that is, the closure of this list under addition and Zero (31.10-3) is A.
GeneratorsOfAdditiveMagmaWithZero.title: GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveMagmaWithZero

GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveGroup.name: GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveGroup
GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveGroup.description: is a list of elements of the near-additive group A that generates A as a near-additive group, that is, the closure of this list under addition, taking the zero element, and taking additive inverses (see AdditiveInverse (31.10-9)) is A.
GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveGroup.title: GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveGroup

GeneratorsOfAdditiveGroup.name: GeneratorsOfAdditiveGroup
GeneratorsOfAdditiveGroup.description: is a list of elements of the near-additive group A that generates A as a near-additive group, that is, the closure of this list under addition, taking the zero element, and taking additive inverses (see AdditiveInverse (31.10-9)) is A.
GeneratorsOfAdditiveGroup.title: GeneratorsOfNearAdditiveGroup

AdditiveNeutralElement.name: AdditiveNeutralElement
AdditiveNeutralElement.description:
returns the element z in the near-additive magma A with the property that z + a = a = a + z holds for all a ∈ A, if such an element exists. Otherwise fail is returned.

A near-additive magma that is not a near-additive magma-with-zero can have an additive neutral element z; in this case, z cannot be obtained as Zero( A ) or as 0*a for an element a in A, see Zero (31.10-3).
AdditiveNeutralElement.title: AdditiveNeutralElement

TrivialSubnearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.name: TrivialSubnearAdditiveMagmaWithZero
TrivialSubnearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.description: is the additive magma-with-zero that has the zero of the near-additive magma-with-zero A as its only element.
TrivialSubnearAdditiveMagmaWithZero.title: TrivialSubnearAdditiveMagmaWithZero

ClosureNearAdditiveGroup.name: ClosureNearAdditiveGroup
ClosureNearAdditiveGroup.description: returns the closure of the near-additive magma A with the element a or with the near-additive magma B, w.r.t. addition, taking the zero element, and taking additive inverses.
ClosureNearAdditiveGroup.title: ClosureNearAdditiveGroup

ClosureNearAdditiveGroup.name: ClosureNearAdditiveGroup
ClosureNearAdditiveGroup.description: returns the closure of the near-additive magma A with the element a or with the near-additive magma B, w.r.t. addition, taking the zero element, and taking additive inverses.
ClosureNearAdditiveGroup.title: ClosureNearAdditiveGroup

ShowAdditionTable.name: ShowAdditionTable
ShowAdditionTable.description: For a structure R with an addition given by +, respectively a structure M with a multiplication given by *, this command displays the addition (multiplication) table of the structure in a pretty way.
ShowAdditionTable.title: ShowAdditionTable

ShowMultiplicationTable.name: ShowMultiplicationTable
ShowMultiplicationTable.description: For a structure R with an addition given by +, respectively a structure M with a multiplication given by *, this command displays the addition (multiplication) table of the structure in a pretty way.
ShowMultiplicationTable.title: ShowAdditionTable

IsRing.name: IsRing
IsRing.description:
A ring in GAP is an additive group (see IsAdditiveGroup (55.1-6)) that is also a magma (see IsMagma (35.1-1)), such that addition + and multiplication * are distributive, see IsDistributive (56.4-5).

The multiplication need not be associative (see IsAssociative (35.4-7)). For example, a Lie algebra (see 64) is regarded as a ring in GAP.
IsRing.title: IsRing

Ring.name: Ring
Ring.description:
In the first form Ring returns the smallest ring that contains all the elements r, s, ... In the second form Ring returns the smallest ring that contains all the elements in the collection coll. If any element is not an element of a ring or if the elements lie in no common ring an error is raised.

Ring differs from DefaultRing (56.1-3) in that it returns the smallest ring in which the elements lie, while DefaultRing (56.1-3) may return a larger ring if that makes sense.
Ring.title: Ring

Ring.name: Ring
Ring.description:
In the first form Ring returns the smallest ring that contains all the elements r, s, ... In the second form Ring returns the smallest ring that contains all the elements in the collection coll. If any element is not an element of a ring or if the elements lie in no common ring an error is raised.

Ring differs from DefaultRing (56.1-3) in that it returns the smallest ring in which the elements lie, while DefaultRing (56.1-3) may return a larger ring if that makes sense.
Ring.title: Ring

DefaultRing.name: DefaultRing
DefaultRing.description:
In the first form DefaultRing returns a ring that contains all the elements r, s, ... etc. In the second form DefaultRing returns a ring that contains all the elements in the collection coll. If any element is not an element of a ring or if the elements lie in no common ring an error is raised.

The ring returned by DefaultRing need not be the smallest ring in which the elements lie. For example for elements from cyclotomic fields, DefaultRing may return the ring of integers of the smallest cyclotomic field in which the elements lie, which need not be the smallest ring overall, because the elements may in fact lie in a smaller number field which is itself not a cyclotomic field.

(For the exact definition of the default ring of a certain type of elements, look at the corresponding method installation.)
DefaultRing.title: DefaultRing

DefaultRing.name: DefaultRing
DefaultRing.description:
In the first form DefaultRing returns a ring that contains all the elements r, s, ... etc. In the second form DefaultRing returns a ring that contains all the elements in the collection coll. If any element is not an element of a ring or if the elements lie in no common ring an error is raised.

The ring returned by DefaultRing need not be the smallest ring in which the elements lie. For example for elements from cyclotomic fields, DefaultRing may return the ring of integers of the smallest cyclotomic field in which the elements lie, which need not be the smallest ring overall, because the elements may in fact lie in a smaller number field which is itself not a cyclotomic field.

(For the exact definition of the default ring of a certain type of elements, look at the corresponding method installation.)
DefaultRing.title: DefaultRing

DefaultRing.name: DefaultRing
DefaultRing.description: DefaultField and DefaultRing for finite field elements are defined to return the smallest field containing the given elements.
DefaultRing.title: DefaultField

RingByGenerators.name: RingByGenerators
RingByGenerators.description: RingByGenerators returns the ring generated by the elements in the collection C, i. e., the closure of C under addition, multiplication, and taking additive inverses.
RingByGenerators.title: RingByGenerators

DefaultRingByGenerators.name: DefaultRingByGenerators
DefaultRingByGenerators.description: For a collection coll, returns a default ring in which coll is contained.
DefaultRingByGenerators.title: DefaultRingByGenerators

GeneratorsOfRing.name: GeneratorsOfRing
GeneratorsOfRing.description: GeneratorsOfRing returns a list of elements such that the ring R is the closure of these elements under addition, multiplication, and taking additive inverses.
GeneratorsOfRing.title: GeneratorsOfRing

Subring.name: Subring
Subring.description: returns the ring with parent R generated by the elements in gens. When the second form, SubringNC is used, it is not checked whether all elements in gens lie in R.
Subring.title: Subring

SubringNC.name: SubringNC
SubringNC.description: returns the ring with parent R generated by the elements in gens. When the second form, SubringNC is used, it is not checked whether all elements in gens lie in R.
SubringNC.title: Subring

ClosureRing.name: ClosureRing
ClosureRing.description: For a ring R and either an element r of its elements family or a ring S, ClosureRing returns the ring generated by both arguments.
ClosureRing.title: ClosureRing

ClosureRing.name: ClosureRing
ClosureRing.description: For a ring R and either an element r of its elements family or a ring S, ClosureRing returns the ring generated by both arguments.
ClosureRing.title: ClosureRing

Quotient.name: Quotient
Quotient.description:
Quotient returns a (right) quotient of the two ring elements r and s in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)). More specifically, it returns a ring element q such that r = q * s holds, or fail if no such elements exists in the respective ring.

The result may not be unique if the ring contains zero divisors.

(To perform the division in the quotient field of a ring, use the quotient operator /.)
Quotient.title: Quotient

TwoSidedIdeal.name: TwoSidedIdeal
TwoSidedIdeal.description:
Let R be a ring, and gens a list of collection of elements in R. TwoSidedIdeal, LeftIdeal, and RightIdeal return the two-sided, left, or right ideal, respectively, I in R that is generated by gens. The ring R can be accessed as LeftActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) or RightActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) (or both) of I.

If R is a left F-module then also I is a left F-module, in particular the LeftActingDomain (57.1-11) values of R and I are equal.

If the optional argument "basis" is given then gens are assumed to be a list of basis vectors of I viewed as a free F-module. (This is mainly applicable to ideals in algebras.) In this case, it is not checked whether gens really is linearly independent and whether gens is a subset of R.
TwoSidedIdeal.title: TwoSidedIdeal

Ideal.name: Ideal
Ideal.description:
Let R be a ring, and gens a list of collection of elements in R. TwoSidedIdeal, LeftIdeal, and RightIdeal return the two-sided, left, or right ideal, respectively, I in R that is generated by gens. The ring R can be accessed as LeftActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) or RightActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) (or both) of I.

If R is a left F-module then also I is a left F-module, in particular the LeftActingDomain (57.1-11) values of R and I are equal.

If the optional argument "basis" is given then gens are assumed to be a list of basis vectors of I viewed as a free F-module. (This is mainly applicable to ideals in algebras.) In this case, it is not checked whether gens really is linearly independent and whether gens is a subset of R.
Ideal.title: TwoSidedIdeal

LeftIdeal.name: LeftIdeal
LeftIdeal.description:
Let R be a ring, and gens a list of collection of elements in R. TwoSidedIdeal, LeftIdeal, and RightIdeal return the two-sided, left, or right ideal, respectively, I in R that is generated by gens. The ring R can be accessed as LeftActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) or RightActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) (or both) of I.

If R is a left F-module then also I is a left F-module, in particular the LeftActingDomain (57.1-11) values of R and I are equal.

If the optional argument "basis" is given then gens are assumed to be a list of basis vectors of I viewed as a free F-module. (This is mainly applicable to ideals in algebras.) In this case, it is not checked whether gens really is linearly independent and whether gens is a subset of R.
LeftIdeal.title: TwoSidedIdeal

RightIdeal.name: RightIdeal
RightIdeal.description:
Let R be a ring, and gens a list of collection of elements in R. TwoSidedIdeal, LeftIdeal, and RightIdeal return the two-sided, left, or right ideal, respectively, I in R that is generated by gens. The ring R can be accessed as LeftActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) or RightActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) (or both) of I.

If R is a left F-module then also I is a left F-module, in particular the LeftActingDomain (57.1-11) values of R and I are equal.

If the optional argument "basis" is given then gens are assumed to be a list of basis vectors of I viewed as a free F-module. (This is mainly applicable to ideals in algebras.) In this case, it is not checked whether gens really is linearly independent and whether gens is a subset of R.
RightIdeal.title: TwoSidedIdeal

TwoSidedIdealNC.name: TwoSidedIdealNC
TwoSidedIdealNC.description: The effects of TwoSidedIdealNC, LeftIdealNC, and RightIdealNC are the same as TwoSidedIdeal (56.2-1), LeftIdeal (56.2-1), and RightIdeal (56.2-1), respectively, but they do not check whether all entries of gens lie in R.
TwoSidedIdealNC.title: TwoSidedIdealNC

IdealNC.name: IdealNC
IdealNC.description: The effects of TwoSidedIdealNC, LeftIdealNC, and RightIdealNC are the same as TwoSidedIdeal (56.2-1), LeftIdeal (56.2-1), and RightIdeal (56.2-1), respectively, but they do not check whether all entries of gens lie in R.
IdealNC.title: TwoSidedIdealNC

LeftIdealNC.name: LeftIdealNC
LeftIdealNC.description: The effects of TwoSidedIdealNC, LeftIdealNC, and RightIdealNC are the same as TwoSidedIdeal (56.2-1), LeftIdeal (56.2-1), and RightIdeal (56.2-1), respectively, but they do not check whether all entries of gens lie in R.
LeftIdealNC.title: TwoSidedIdealNC

RightIdealNC.name: RightIdealNC
RightIdealNC.description: The effects of TwoSidedIdealNC, LeftIdealNC, and RightIdealNC are the same as TwoSidedIdeal (56.2-1), LeftIdeal (56.2-1), and RightIdeal (56.2-1), respectively, but they do not check whether all entries of gens lie in R.
RightIdealNC.title: TwoSidedIdealNC

IsTwoSidedIdeal.name: IsTwoSidedIdeal
IsTwoSidedIdeal.description: The properties IsTwoSidedIdealInParent etc., are attributes of the ideal, and once known they are stored in the ideal.
IsTwoSidedIdeal.title: IsTwoSidedIdeal

IsLeftIdeal.name: IsLeftIdeal
IsLeftIdeal.description: The properties IsTwoSidedIdealInParent etc., are attributes of the ideal, and once known they are stored in the ideal.
IsLeftIdeal.title: IsTwoSidedIdeal

IsRightIdeal.name: IsRightIdeal
IsRightIdeal.description: The properties IsTwoSidedIdealInParent etc., are attributes of the ideal, and once known they are stored in the ideal.
IsRightIdeal.title: IsTwoSidedIdeal

IsTwoSidedIdealInParent.name: IsTwoSidedIdealInParent
IsTwoSidedIdealInParent.description: The properties IsTwoSidedIdealInParent etc., are attributes of the ideal, and once known they are stored in the ideal.
IsTwoSidedIdealInParent.title: IsTwoSidedIdeal

IsLeftIdealInParent.name: IsLeftIdealInParent
IsLeftIdealInParent.description: The properties IsTwoSidedIdealInParent etc., are attributes of the ideal, and once known they are stored in the ideal.
IsLeftIdealInParent.title: IsTwoSidedIdeal

IsRightIdealInParent.name: IsRightIdealInParent
IsRightIdealInParent.description: The properties IsTwoSidedIdealInParent etc., are attributes of the ideal, and once known they are stored in the ideal.
IsRightIdealInParent.title: IsTwoSidedIdeal

TwoSidedIdealByGenerators.name: TwoSidedIdealByGenerators
TwoSidedIdealByGenerators.description:
TwoSidedIdealByGenerators returns the ring that is generated by the elements of the collection gens under addition, multiplication, and multiplication with elements of the ring R from the left and from the right.

R can be accessed by LeftActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) or RightActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10), gens can be accessed by GeneratorsOfTwoSidedIdeal (56.2-7).
TwoSidedIdealByGenerators.title: TwoSidedIdealByGenerators

IdealByGenerators.name: IdealByGenerators
IdealByGenerators.description:
TwoSidedIdealByGenerators returns the ring that is generated by the elements of the collection gens under addition, multiplication, and multiplication with elements of the ring R from the left and from the right.

R can be accessed by LeftActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) or RightActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10), gens can be accessed by GeneratorsOfTwoSidedIdeal (56.2-7).
IdealByGenerators.title: TwoSidedIdealByGenerators

LeftIdealByGenerators.name: LeftIdealByGenerators
LeftIdealByGenerators.description:
LeftIdealByGenerators returns the ring that is generated by the elements of the collection gens under addition, multiplication, and multiplication with elements of the ring R from the left.

R can be accessed by LeftActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10), gens can be accessed by GeneratorsOfLeftIdeal (56.2-8).
LeftIdealByGenerators.title: LeftIdealByGenerators

RightIdealByGenerators.name: RightIdealByGenerators
RightIdealByGenerators.description:
RightIdealByGenerators returns the ring that is generated by the elements of the collection gens under addition, multiplication, and multiplication with elements of the ring R from the right.

R can be accessed by RightActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10), gens can be accessed by GeneratorsOfRightIdeal (56.2-9).
RightIdealByGenerators.title: RightIdealByGenerators

GeneratorsOfTwoSidedIdeal.name: GeneratorsOfTwoSidedIdeal
GeneratorsOfTwoSidedIdeal.description: is a list of generators for the ideal I, with respect to the action of the rings that are stored as the values of LeftActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) and RightActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10), from the left and from the right, respectively.
GeneratorsOfTwoSidedIdeal.title: GeneratorsOfTwoSidedIdeal

GeneratorsOfIdeal.name: GeneratorsOfIdeal
GeneratorsOfIdeal.description: is a list of generators for the ideal I, with respect to the action of the rings that are stored as the values of LeftActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10) and RightActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10), from the left and from the right, respectively.
GeneratorsOfIdeal.title: GeneratorsOfTwoSidedIdeal

GeneratorsOfLeftIdeal.name: GeneratorsOfLeftIdeal
GeneratorsOfLeftIdeal.description: is a list of generators for the left ideal I, with respect to the action from the left of the ring that is stored as the value of LeftActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10).
GeneratorsOfLeftIdeal.title: GeneratorsOfLeftIdeal

GeneratorsOfRightIdeal.name: GeneratorsOfRightIdeal
GeneratorsOfRightIdeal.description: is a list of generators for the right ideal I, with respect to the action from the right of the ring that is stored as the value of RightActingRingOfIdeal (56.2-10).
GeneratorsOfRightIdeal.title: GeneratorsOfRightIdeal

LeftActingRingOfIdeal.name: LeftActingRingOfIdeal
LeftActingRingOfIdeal.description: returns the left (resp. right) acting ring of an ideal I.
LeftActingRingOfIdeal.title: LeftActingRingOfIdeal

RightActingRingOfIdeal.name: RightActingRingOfIdeal
RightActingRingOfIdeal.description: returns the left (resp. right) acting ring of an ideal I.
RightActingRingOfIdeal.title: LeftActingRingOfIdeal

AsLeftIdeal.name: AsLeftIdeal
AsLeftIdeal.description:
Let S be a subring of the ring R.

If S is a left ideal in R then AsLeftIdeal returns this left ideal, otherwise fail is returned.

If S is a right ideal in R then AsRightIdeal returns this right ideal, otherwise fail is returned.
AsLeftIdeal.title: AsLeftIdeal

AsRightIdeal.name: AsRightIdeal
AsRightIdeal.description:
Let S be a subring of the ring R.

If S is a left ideal in R then AsLeftIdeal returns this left ideal, otherwise fail is returned.

If S is a right ideal in R then AsRightIdeal returns this right ideal, otherwise fail is returned.
AsRightIdeal.title: AsLeftIdeal

AsTwoSidedIdeal.name: AsTwoSidedIdeal
AsTwoSidedIdeal.description:
Let S be a subring of the ring R.

If S is a left ideal in R then AsLeftIdeal returns this left ideal, otherwise fail is returned.

If S is a right ideal in R then AsRightIdeal returns this right ideal, otherwise fail is returned.
AsTwoSidedIdeal.title: AsLeftIdeal

IsRingWithOne.name: IsRingWithOne
IsRingWithOne.description:
A ring-with-one in GAP is a ring (see IsRing (56.1-1)) that is also a magma-with-one (see IsMagmaWithOne (35.1-2)).

Note that the identity and the zero of a ring-with-one need not be distinct. This means that a ring that consists only of its zero element can be regarded as a ring-with-one.

This is especially useful in the case of finitely presented rings, in the sense that each factor of a ring-with-one is again a ring-with-one.
IsRingWithOne.title: IsRingWithOne

RingWithOne.name: RingWithOne
RingWithOne.description: In the first form RingWithOne returns the smallest ring with one that contains all the elements r, s, ... In the second form RingWithOne returns the smallest ring with one that contains all the elements in the collection C. If any element is not an element of a ring or if the elements lie in no common ring an error is raised.
RingWithOne.title: RingWithOne

RingWithOne.name: RingWithOne
RingWithOne.description: In the first form RingWithOne returns the smallest ring with one that contains all the elements r, s, ... In the second form RingWithOne returns the smallest ring with one that contains all the elements in the collection C. If any element is not an element of a ring or if the elements lie in no common ring an error is raised.
RingWithOne.title: RingWithOne

RingWithOneByGenerators.name: RingWithOneByGenerators
RingWithOneByGenerators.description: RingWithOneByGenerators returns the ring-with-one generated by the elements in the collection coll, i. e., the closure of coll under addition, multiplication, taking additive inverses, and taking the identity of an element.
RingWithOneByGenerators.title: RingWithOneByGenerators

GeneratorsOfRingWithOne.name: GeneratorsOfRingWithOne
GeneratorsOfRingWithOne.description:
GeneratorsOfRingWithOne returns a list of elements such that the ring R is the closure of these elements under addition, multiplication, taking additive inverses, and taking the identity element One( R ).

R itself need not be known to be a ring-with-one.
GeneratorsOfRingWithOne.title: GeneratorsOfRingWithOne

SubringWithOne.name: SubringWithOne
SubringWithOne.description: returns the ring with one with parent R generated by the elements in gens. When the second form, SubringWithOneNC is used, it is not checked whether all elements in gens lie in R.
SubringWithOne.title: SubringWithOne

SubringWithOneNC.name: SubringWithOneNC
SubringWithOneNC.description: returns the ring with one with parent R generated by the elements in gens. When the second form, SubringWithOneNC is used, it is not checked whether all elements in gens lie in R.
SubringWithOneNC.title: SubringWithOne

IsIntegralRing.name: IsIntegralRing
IsIntegralRing.description: A ring-with-one R is integral if it is commutative, contains no nontrivial zero divisors, and if its identity is distinct from its zero.
IsIntegralRing.title: IsIntegralRing

IsUniqueFactorizationRing.name: IsUniqueFactorizationRing
IsUniqueFactorizationRing.description:
A ring R is called a unique factorization ring if every nonzero element has a unique factorization into irreducible elements, i.e., a unique representation as product of irreducibles (see IsIrreducibleRingElement (56.5-7)). Unique in this context means unique up to permutations of the factors and up to multiplication of the factors by units (see Units (56.5-2)).

(Note that we cannot install a subset maintained method for this filter since the factorization of an element needs not exist in a subring. As an example, consider the subring 4 ℕ + 1 of the ring 4 ℤ + 1; in the subring, the element 3 ⋅ 3 ⋅ 11 ⋅ 7 has the two factorizations 33 ⋅ 21 = 9 ⋅ 77, but in the large ring there is the unique factorization (-3) ⋅ (-3) ⋅ (-11) ⋅ (-7), and it is easy to see that every element in 4 ℤ + 1 has a unique factorization.)
IsUniqueFactorizationRing.title: IsUniqueFactorizationRing

IsLDistributive.name: IsLDistributive
IsLDistributive.description: is true if the relation a * ( b + c ) = ( a * b ) + ( a * c ) holds for all elements a, b, c in the collection C, and false otherwise.
IsLDistributive.title: IsLDistributive

IsRDistributive.name: IsRDistributive
IsRDistributive.description: is true if the relation ( a + b ) * c = ( a * c ) + ( b * c ) holds for all elements a, b, c in the collection C, and false otherwise.
IsRDistributive.title: IsRDistributive

IsDistributive.name: IsDistributive
IsDistributive.description: is true if the collection C is both left and right distributive (see IsLDistributive (56.4-3), IsRDistributive (56.4-4)), and false otherwise.
IsDistributive.title: IsDistributive

IsAnticommutative.name: IsAnticommutative
IsAnticommutative.description: is true if the relation a * b = - b * a holds for all elements a, b in the ring R, and false otherwise.
IsAnticommutative.title: IsAnticommutative

IsZeroSquaredRing.name: IsZeroSquaredRing
IsZeroSquaredRing.description: is true if a * a is the zero element of the ring R for all a in R, and false otherwise.
IsZeroSquaredRing.title: IsZeroSquaredRing

IsJacobianRing.name: IsJacobianRing
IsJacobianRing.description: is true if the Jacobi identity holds in the ring R, and false otherwise. The Jacobi identity means that x * (y * z) + z * (x * y) + y * (z * x) is the zero element of R, for all elements x, y, z in R.
IsJacobianRing.title: IsJacobianRing

IsUnit.name: IsUnit
IsUnit.description:
IsUnit returns true if r is a unit in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in its default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)). If r is not a unit then false is returned.

An element r is called a unit in a ring R, if r has an inverse in R.

IsUnit may call Quotient (56.1-9).
IsUnit.title: IsUnit

Units.name: Units
Units.description:
Units returns the group of units of the ring R. This may either be returned as a list or as a group.

An element r is called a unit of a ring R if r has an inverse in R. It is easy to see that the set of units forms a multiplicative group.
Units.title: Units

IsAssociated.name: IsAssociated
IsAssociated.description:
IsAssociated returns true if the two ring elements r and s are associated in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)). If the two elements are not associated then false is returned.

Two elements r and s of a ring R are called associated if there is a unit u of R such that r u =s.
IsAssociated.title: IsAssociated

Associates.name: Associates
Associates.description:
Associates returns the set of associates of r in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in its default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)).

Two elements r and s of a ring R are called associated if there is a unit u of R such that r u = s.
Associates.title: Associates

StandardAssociate.name: StandardAssociate
StandardAssociate.description:
StandardAssociate returns the standard associate of the ring element r in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in its default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)).

The standard associate of a ring element r of R is an associated element of r which is, in a ring dependent way, distinguished among the set of associates of r. For example, in the ring of integers the standard associate is the absolute value.
StandardAssociate.title: StandardAssociate

StandardAssociateUnit.name: StandardAssociateUnit
StandardAssociateUnit.description:
StandardAssociateUnit returns a unit in the ring R such that the ring element r times this unit equals the standard associate of r in R.

If R is not given, the default ring of r is used instead. (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)).
StandardAssociateUnit.title: StandardAssociateUnit

IsIrreducibleRingElement.name: IsIrreducibleRingElement
IsIrreducibleRingElement.description:
IsIrreducibleRingElement returns true if the ring element r is irreducible in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in its default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)). If r is not irreducible then false is returned.

An element r of a ring R is called irreducible if r is not a unit in R and if there is no nontrivial factorization of r in R, i.e., if there is no representation of r as product s t such that neither s nor t is a unit (see IsUnit (56.5-1)). Each prime element (see IsPrime (56.5-8)) is irreducible.
IsIrreducibleRingElement.title: IsIrreducibleRingElement

IsPrime.name: IsPrime
IsPrime.description:
IsPrime returns true if the ring element r is a prime in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in its default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)). If r is not a prime then false is returned.

An element r of a ring R is called prime if for each pair s and t such that r divides s t the element r divides either s or t. Note that there are rings where not every irreducible element (see IsIrreducibleRingElement (56.5-7)) is a prime.
IsPrime.title: IsPrime

Factors.name: Factors
Factors.description: Factors returns the factorization of the ring element r in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in its default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)). The factorization is returned as a list of primes (see IsPrime (56.5-8)). Each element in the list is a standard associate (see StandardAssociate (56.5-5)) except the first one, which is multiplied by a unit as necessary to have Product( Factors( R, r ) ) = r. This list is usually also sorted, thus smallest prime factors come first. If r is a unit or zero, Factors( R, r ) = [ r ].
Factors.title: Factors

Factors.name: Factors
Factors.description: Factoring of polynomials over abelian number fields consisting of cyclotomics works in principle but is not very efficient if the degree of the field extension is large.
Factors.title: Factors

Factors.name: Factors
Factors.description:
returns a list of the irreducible factors of the polynomial poly in the polynomial ring R. (That is factors over the CoefficientsRing (66.15-3) value of R.)

For univariate factorizations, it is possible to pass a record opt as a third argument. This record can contain the following components:

is a set of positive integers. The factorization assumes that all irreducible factors have a degree in this set.
Factors.title: Factors

PadicValuation.name: PadicValuation
PadicValuation.description: PadicValuation is the operation to compute the p-adic valuation of a ring element r.
PadicValuation.title: PadicValuation

IsEuclideanRing.name: IsEuclideanRing
IsEuclideanRing.description: A ring R is called a Euclidean ring if it is a non-trivial commutative ring and there exists a function δ, called the Euclidean degree, from R-{0_R} into a well-ordered set (such as the nonnegative integers), such that for every pair r ∈ R and s ∈ R-{0_R} there exists an element q such that either r - q s = 0_R or δ(r - q s) < δ( s ). In GAP the Euclidean degree δ is implicitly built into a ring and cannot be changed. The existence of this division with remainder implies that the Euclidean algorithm can be applied to compute a greatest common divisor of two elements, which in turn implies that R is a unique factorization ring.
IsEuclideanRing.title: IsEuclideanRing

EuclideanDegree.name: EuclideanDegree
EuclideanDegree.description:
EuclideanDegree returns the Euclidean degree of the ring element r in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in its default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)).

The ring R must be a Euclidean ring (see IsEuclideanRing (56.6-1)).
EuclideanDegree.title: EuclideanDegree

EuclideanQuotient.name: EuclideanQuotient
EuclideanQuotient.description:
EuclideanQuotient returns the Euclidean quotient of the ring elements r and m in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)).

The ring R must be a Euclidean ring (see IsEuclideanRing (56.6-1)), otherwise an error is signalled.
EuclideanQuotient.title: EuclideanQuotient

EuclideanRemainder.name: EuclideanRemainder
EuclideanRemainder.description:
EuclideanRemainder returns the Euclidean remainder of the ring element r modulo the ring element m in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)).

The ring R must be a Euclidean ring (see IsEuclideanRing (56.6-1)), otherwise an error is signalled.
EuclideanRemainder.title: EuclideanRemainder

QuotientRemainder.name: QuotientRemainder
QuotientRemainder.description:
QuotientRemainder returns the Euclidean quotient and the Euclidean remainder of the ring elements r and m in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring (see DefaultRing (56.1-3)). The result is a pair of ring elements.

The ring R must be a Euclidean ring (see IsEuclideanRing (56.6-1)), otherwise an error is signalled.
QuotientRemainder.title: QuotientRemainder

Gcd.name: Gcd
Gcd.description:
Gcd returns the greatest common divisor of the ring elements r1, r2, ... resp. of the ring elements in the list list in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring, see DefaultRing (56.1-3).

Gcd returns the standard associate (see StandardAssociate (56.5-5)) of the greatest common divisors.

A divisor of an element r in the ring R is an element d∈ R such that r is a multiple of d. A common divisor of the elements r_1, r_2, ... in the ring R is an element d∈ R which is a divisor of each r_1, r_2, .... A greatest common divisor d in addition has the property that every other common divisor of r_1, r_2, ... is a divisor of d.
Gcd.title: Gcd

Gcd.name: Gcd
Gcd.description:
Gcd returns the greatest common divisor of the ring elements r1, r2, ... resp. of the ring elements in the list list in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring, see DefaultRing (56.1-3).

Gcd returns the standard associate (see StandardAssociate (56.5-5)) of the greatest common divisors.

A divisor of an element r in the ring R is an element d∈ R such that r is a multiple of d. A common divisor of the elements r_1, r_2, ... in the ring R is an element d∈ R which is a divisor of each r_1, r_2, .... A greatest common divisor d in addition has the property that every other common divisor of r_1, r_2, ... is a divisor of d.
Gcd.title: Gcd

GcdOp.name: GcdOp
GcdOp.description: GcdOp is the operation to compute the greatest common divisor of two ring elements r, s in the ring R or in their default ring.
GcdOp.title: GcdOp

GcdRepresentation.name: GcdRepresentation
GcdRepresentation.description:
GcdRepresentation returns a representation of the greatest common divisor of the ring elements r1, r2, ... resp. of the ring elements in the list list in the Euclidean ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring, see DefaultRing (56.1-3).

A representation of the gcd g of the elements r_1, r_2, ... of a ring R is a list of ring elements s_1, s_2, ... of R, such that g = s_1 r_1 + s_2 r_2 + ⋯. Such representations do not exist in all rings, but they do exist in Euclidean rings (see IsEuclideanRing (56.6-1)), which can be shown using the Euclidean algorithm, which in fact can compute those coefficients.

Gcdex (14.3-5) provides similar functionality over the integers.
GcdRepresentation.title: GcdRepresentation

GcdRepresentation.name: GcdRepresentation
GcdRepresentation.description:
GcdRepresentation returns a representation of the greatest common divisor of the ring elements r1, r2, ... resp. of the ring elements in the list list in the Euclidean ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring, see DefaultRing (56.1-3).

A representation of the gcd g of the elements r_1, r_2, ... of a ring R is a list of ring elements s_1, s_2, ... of R, such that g = s_1 r_1 + s_2 r_2 + ⋯. Such representations do not exist in all rings, but they do exist in Euclidean rings (see IsEuclideanRing (56.6-1)), which can be shown using the Euclidean algorithm, which in fact can compute those coefficients.

Gcdex (14.3-5) provides similar functionality over the integers.
GcdRepresentation.title: GcdRepresentation

GcdRepresentationOp.name: GcdRepresentationOp
GcdRepresentationOp.description: GcdRepresentationOp is the operation to compute the representation of the greatest common divisor of two ring elements r, s in the Euclidean ring R or in their default ring, respectively.
GcdRepresentationOp.title: GcdRepresentationOp

ShowGcd.name: ShowGcd
ShowGcd.description: This function takes two elements a and b of an Euclidean ring and returns their greatest common divisor. It will print out the steps performed by the Euclidean algorithm, as well as the rearrangement of these steps to express the gcd as a ring combination of a and b.
ShowGcd.title: ShowGcd

Lcm.name: Lcm
Lcm.description:
Lcm returns the least common multiple of the ring elements r1, r2, ... resp. of the ring elements in the list list in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring, see DefaultRing (56.1-3).

Lcm returns the standard associate (see StandardAssociate (56.5-5)) of the least common multiples.

A least common multiple of the elements r_1, r_2, ... of the ring R is an element m that is a multiple of r_1, r_2, ..., and every other multiple of these elements is a multiple of m.
Lcm.title: Lcm

Lcm.name: Lcm
Lcm.description:
Lcm returns the least common multiple of the ring elements r1, r2, ... resp. of the ring elements in the list list in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring, see DefaultRing (56.1-3).

Lcm returns the standard associate (see StandardAssociate (56.5-5)) of the least common multiples.

A least common multiple of the elements r_1, r_2, ... of the ring R is an element m that is a multiple of r_1, r_2, ..., and every other multiple of these elements is a multiple of m.
Lcm.title: Lcm

LcmOp.name: LcmOp
LcmOp.description:
LcmOp is the operation to compute the least common multiple of two ring elements r, s in the ring R or in their default ring, respectively.

The default methods for this uses the equality lcm( m, n ) = m*n / gcd( m, n ) (see GcdOp (56.7-2)).
LcmOp.title: LcmOp

QuotientMod.name: QuotientMod
QuotientMod.description:
QuotientMod returns a quotient of the ring elements r and s modulo the ring element m in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring, see DefaultRing (56.1-3).

R must be a Euclidean ring (see IsEuclideanRing (56.6-1)) so that EuclideanRemainder (56.6-4) can be applied. If no modular quotient exists, fail is returned.

A quotient q of r and s modulo m is an element of R such that q s = r modulo m, i.e., such that q s - r is divisible by m in R and that q is either zero (if r is divisible by m) or the Euclidean degree of q is strictly smaller than the Euclidean degree of m.
QuotientMod.title: QuotientMod

PowerMod.name: PowerMod
PowerMod.description:
PowerMod returns the e-th power of the ring element r modulo the ring element m in the ring R, if given, and otherwise in their default ring, see DefaultRing (56.1-3). e must be an integer.

R must be a Euclidean ring (see IsEuclideanRing (56.6-1)) so that EuclideanRemainder (56.6-4) can be applied to its elements.

If e is positive the result is r^e modulo m. If e is negative then PowerMod first tries to find the inverse of r modulo m, i.e., i such that i r = 1 modulo m. If the inverse does not exist an error is signalled. If the inverse does exist PowerMod returns PowerMod( R, i, -e, m ).
PowerMod.title: PowerMod

InterpolatedPolynomial.name: InterpolatedPolynomial
InterpolatedPolynomial.description: InterpolatedPolynomial returns, for given lists x, y of elements in a ring R of the same length n the unique polynomial of degree less than n which has value y[i] at x[i], for all i ∈ { 1, ..., n }. Note that the elements in x must be distinct.
InterpolatedPolynomial.title: InterpolatedPolynomial

RingGeneralMappingByImages.name: RingGeneralMappingByImages
RingGeneralMappingByImages.description:
is a general mapping from the ring A to the ring S. This general mapping is defined by mapping the entries in the list gens (elements of R) to the entries in the list imgs (elements of S), and taking the additive and multiplicative closure.

gens need not generate R as a ring, and if the specification does not define an additive and multiplicative mapping then the result will be multivalued. Hence, in general it is not a mapping.
RingGeneralMappingByImages.title: RingGeneralMappingByImages

RingHomomorphismByImages.name: RingHomomorphismByImages
RingHomomorphismByImages.description:
RingHomomorphismByImages returns the ring homomorphism with source R and range S that is defined by mapping the list gens of generators of R to the list imgs of images in S.

If gens does not generate R or if the homomorphism does not exist (i.e., if mapping the generators describes only a multi-valued mapping) then fail is returned.

One can avoid the checks by calling RingHomomorphismByImagesNC (56.8-3), and one can construct multi-valued mappings with RingGeneralMappingByImages (56.8-1).
RingHomomorphismByImages.title: RingHomomorphismByImages

RingHomomorphismByImagesNC.name: RingHomomorphismByImagesNC
RingHomomorphismByImagesNC.description:
RingHomomorphismByImagesNC is the operation that is called by the function RingHomomorphismByImages (56.8-2). Its methods may assume that gens generates R as a ring and that the mapping of gens to imgs defines a ring homomorphism. Results are unpredictable if these conditions do not hold.

For creating a possibly multi-valued mapping from R to S that respects addition and multiplication, RingGeneralMappingByImages (56.8-1) can be used.
RingHomomorphismByImagesNC.title: RingHomomorphismByImagesNC

NaturalHomomorphismByIdeal.name: NaturalHomomorphismByIdeal
NaturalHomomorphismByIdeal.description: is the homomorphism of rings provided by the natural projection map of R onto the quotient ring R/I. This map can be used to take pre-images in the original ring from elements in the quotient.
NaturalHomomorphismByIdeal.title: NaturalHomomorphismByIdeal

NaturalHomomorphismByIdeal.name: NaturalHomomorphismByIdeal
NaturalHomomorphismByIdeal.description: For an algebra A and an ideal I in A, the return value of NaturalHomomorphismByIdeal (56.8-4) is a homomorphism of algebras, in particular the range of this mapping is also an algebra.
NaturalHomomorphismByIdeal.title: NaturalHomomorphismByIdeal

SmallRing.name: SmallRing
SmallRing.description: returns the n-th ring of order s from a library of rings of small order (up to isomorphism).
SmallRing.title: SmallRing

NumberSmallRings.name: NumberSmallRings
NumberSmallRings.description: returns the number of (nonisomorphic) rings of order s stored in the library of small rings.
NumberSmallRings.title: NumberSmallRings

Subrings.name: Subrings
Subrings.description: for a finite ring R this function returns a list of all subrings of R.
Subrings.title: Subrings

Ideals.name: Ideals
Ideals.description: for a finite ring R this function returns a list of all ideals of R.
Ideals.title: Ideals

DirectSum.name: DirectSum
DirectSum.description: These functions construct the direct sum of the rings given as arguments. DirectSum takes an arbitrary positive number of arguments and calls the operation DirectSumOp, which takes exactly two arguments, namely a nonempty list of rings and one of these rings. (This somewhat strange syntax allows the method selection to choose a reasonable method for special cases.)
DirectSum.title: DirectSum

DirectSumOp.name: DirectSumOp
DirectSumOp.description: These functions construct the direct sum of the rings given as arguments. DirectSum takes an arbitrary positive number of arguments and calls the operation DirectSumOp, which takes exactly two arguments, namely a nonempty list of rings and one of these rings. (This somewhat strange syntax allows the method selection to choose a reasonable method for special cases.)
DirectSumOp.title: DirectSum

RingByStructureConstants.name: RingByStructureConstants
RingByStructureConstants.description: returns a ring R whose additive group is described by the list moduli, with multiplication defined by the structure constants table sctable. The optional argument nameinfo can be used to prescribe names for the elements of the canonical generators of R; it can be either a string name (then name1, name2 etc. are chosen) or a list of strings which are then chosen.
RingByStructureConstants.title: RingByStructureConstants

IsLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup.name: IsLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup
IsLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup.description: A domain D lies in IsLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup if it is an additive group that is closed under scalar multiplication from the left, and such that λ * ( x + y ) = λ * x + λ * y for all scalars λ and elements x, y ∈ D (here and below by scalars we mean elements of a domain acting on D from left or right as appropriate).
IsLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup.title: IsLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup

IsLeftModule.name: IsLeftModule
IsLeftModule.description: A domain M lies in IsLeftModule if it lies in IsLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup, and the set of scalars forms a ring, and (λ + μ) * x = λ * x + μ * x for scalars λ, μ and x ∈ M, and scalar multiplication satisfies λ * (μ * x) = (λ * μ) * x for scalars λ, μ and x ∈ M.
IsLeftModule.title: IsLeftModule

GeneratorsOfLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup.name: GeneratorsOfLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup
GeneratorsOfLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup.description: returns a list of elements of D that generates D as a left operator additive group.
GeneratorsOfLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup.title: GeneratorsOfLeftOperatorAdditiveGroup

GeneratorsOfLeftModule.name: GeneratorsOfLeftModule
GeneratorsOfLeftModule.description: returns a list of elements of M that generate M as a left module.
GeneratorsOfLeftModule.title: GeneratorsOfLeftModule

AsLeftModule.name: AsLeftModule
AsLeftModule.description: if the domain D forms an additive group and is closed under left multiplication by the elements of R, then AsLeftModule( R, D ) returns the domain D viewed as a left module.
AsLeftModule.title: AsLeftModule

IsRightOperatorAdditiveGroup.name: IsRightOperatorAdditiveGroup
IsRightOperatorAdditiveGroup.description: A domain D lies in IsRightOperatorAdditiveGroup if it is an additive group that is closed under scalar multiplication from the right, and such that ( x + y ) * λ = x * λ + y * λ for all scalars λ and elements x, y ∈ D.
IsRightOperatorAdditiveGroup.title: IsRightOperatorAdditiveGroup

IsRightModule.name: IsRightModule
IsRightModule.description: A domain M lies in IsRightModule if it lies in IsRightOperatorAdditiveGroup, and the set of scalars forms a ring, and x * (λ + μ) = x * λ + x * μ for scalars λ, μ and x ∈ M, and scalar multiplication satisfies (x * μ) * λ = x * (μ * λ) for scalars λ, μ and x ∈ M.
IsRightModule.title: IsRightModule

GeneratorsOfRightOperatorAdditiveGroup.name: GeneratorsOfRightOperatorAdditiveGroup
GeneratorsOfRightOperatorAdditiveGroup.description: returns a list of elements of D that generates D as a right operator additive group.
GeneratorsOfRightOperatorAdditiveGroup.title: GeneratorsOfRightOperatorAdditiveGroup

GeneratorsOfRightModule.name: GeneratorsOfRightModule
GeneratorsOfRightModule.description: returns a list of elements of M that generate M as a left module.
GeneratorsOfRightModule.title: GeneratorsOfRightModule

LeftModuleByGenerators.name: LeftModuleByGenerators
LeftModuleByGenerators.description: returns the left module over R generated by gens.
LeftModuleByGenerators.title: LeftModuleByGenerators

LeftActingDomain.name: LeftActingDomain
LeftActingDomain.description: Let D be an external left set, that is, D is closed under the action of a domain L by multiplication from the left. Then L can be accessed as value of LeftActingDomain for D.
LeftActingDomain.title: LeftActingDomain

Submodule.name: Submodule
Submodule.description: is the left module generated by the collection gens, with parent module M. If the string "basis" is entered as the third argument then the submodule of M is created for which the list gens is known to be a list of basis vectors; in this case, it is not checked whether gens really is linearly independent and whether all in gens lie in M.
Submodule.title: Submodule

SubmoduleNC.name: SubmoduleNC
SubmoduleNC.description: SubmoduleNC does the same as Submodule (57.2-1), except that it does not check whether all in gens lie in M.
SubmoduleNC.title: SubmoduleNC

ClosureLeftModule.name: ClosureLeftModule
ClosureLeftModule.description: is the left module generated by the left module generators of M and the element m.
ClosureLeftModule.title: ClosureLeftModule

TrivialSubmodule.name: TrivialSubmodule
TrivialSubmodule.description: returns the zero submodule of M.
TrivialSubmodule.title: TrivialSubmodule

IsFreeLeftModule.name: IsFreeLeftModule
IsFreeLeftModule.description:
A left module is free as module if it is isomorphic to a direct sum of copies of its left acting domain.

Free left modules can have bases.

The characteristic (see Characteristic (31.10-1)) of a free left module is defined as the characteristic of its left acting domain (see LeftActingDomain (57.1-11)).
IsFreeLeftModule.title: IsFreeLeftModule

FreeLeftModule.name: FreeLeftModule
FreeLeftModule.description:
FreeLeftModule( R, gens ) is the free left module over the ring R, generated by the vectors in the collection gens.

If there are three arguments, a ring R and a collection gens and an element zero, then FreeLeftModule( R, gens, zero ) is the R-free left module generated by gens, with zero element zero.

If the last argument is the string "basis" then the vectors in gens are known to form a basis of the free module.
FreeLeftModule.title: FreeLeftModule

Dimension.name: Dimension
Dimension.description:
A free left module has dimension n if it is isomorphic to a direct sum of n copies of its left acting domain.

(We do not mark Dimension as invariant under isomorphisms since we want to call UseIsomorphismRelation (31.13-3) also for free left modules over different left acting domains.)
Dimension.title: Dimension

IsFiniteDimensional.name: IsFiniteDimensional
IsFiniteDimensional.description: is true if M is a free left module that is finite dimensional over its left acting domain, and false otherwise.
IsFiniteDimensional.title: IsFiniteDimensional

IsFiniteDimensional.name: IsFiniteDimensional
IsFiniteDimensional.description: returns true (always) for a matrix algebra matalg, since matrix algebras are always finite dimensional.
IsFiniteDimensional.title: IsFiniteDimensional

UseBasis.name: UseBasis
UseBasis.description:
The vectors in the list gens are known to form a basis of the free left module V. UseBasis stores information in V that can be derived form this fact, namely

gens are stored as left module generators if no such generators were bound (this is useful especially if V is an algebra),

the dimension of V is stored.
UseBasis.title: UseBasis

IsRowModule.name: IsRowModule
IsRowModule.description: A row module is a free left module whose elements are row vectors.
IsRowModule.title: IsRowModule

IsMatrixModule.name: IsMatrixModule
IsMatrixModule.description: A matrix module is a free left module whose elements are matrices.
IsMatrixModule.title: IsMatrixModule

IsFullRowModule.name: IsFullRowModule
IsFullRowModule.description:
A full row module is a module R^n, for a ring R and a nonnegative integer n.

More precisely, a full row module is a free left module over a ring R such that the elements are row vectors of the same length n and with entries in R and such that the dimension is equal to n.

Several functions delegate their tasks to full row modules, for example Iterator (30.8-1) and Enumerator (30.3-2).
IsFullRowModule.title: IsFullRowModule

FullRowModule.name: FullRowModule
FullRowModule.description: is the row module R^n, for a ring R and a nonnegative integer n.
FullRowModule.title: FullRowModule

IsFullMatrixModule.name: IsFullMatrixModule
IsFullMatrixModule.description:
A full matrix module is a module R^{[m,n]}, for a ring R and two nonnegative integers m, n.

More precisely, a full matrix module is a free left module over a ring R such that the elements are m by n matrices with entries in R and such that the dimension is equal to m n.
IsFullMatrixModule.title: IsFullMatrixModule

FullMatrixModule.name: FullMatrixModule
FullMatrixModule.description: is the matrix module R^[m,n], for a ring R and nonnegative integers m and n.
FullMatrixModule.title: FullMatrixModule

IsDivisionRing.name: IsDivisionRing
IsDivisionRing.description:
A division ring in GAP is a nontrivial associative algebra D with a multiplicative inverse for each nonzero element. In GAP every division ring is a vector space over a division ring (possibly over itself). Note that being a division ring is thus not a property that a ring can get, because a ring is usually not represented as a vector space.

The field of coefficients is stored as the value of the attribute LeftActingDomain (57.1-11) of D.
IsDivisionRing.title: IsDivisionRing

IsField.name: IsField
IsField.description: A field is a commutative division ring (see IsDivisionRing (58.1-1) and IsCommutative (35.4-9)).
IsField.title: IsField

Field.name: Field
Field.description: Field returns the smallest field K that contains all the elements z, ..., or the smallest field K that contains all elements in the list list. If no subfield F is given, K is constructed as a field over itself, i.e. the left acting domain of K is K. Called with a field F and a list list, Field constructs the field generated by F and the elements in list, as a vector space over F.
Field.title: Field

Field.name: Field
Field.description: Field returns the smallest field K that contains all the elements z, ..., or the smallest field K that contains all elements in the list list. If no subfield F is given, K is constructed as a field over itself, i.e. the left acting domain of K is K. Called with a field F and a list list, Field constructs the field generated by F and the elements in list, as a vector space over F.
Field.title: Field

DefaultFieldByGenerators.name: DefaultFieldByGenerators
DefaultFieldByGenerators.description: returns the default field containing the elements z, .... This field may be bigger than the smallest field containing these elements.
DefaultFieldByGenerators.title: DefaultFieldByGenerators

GeneratorsOfDivisionRing.name: GeneratorsOfDivisionRing
GeneratorsOfDivisionRing.description: generators with respect to addition, multiplication, and taking inverses (the identity cannot be omitted ...)
GeneratorsOfDivisionRing.title: GeneratorsOfDivisionRing

GeneratorsOfField.name: GeneratorsOfField
GeneratorsOfField.description: generators with respect to addition, multiplication, and taking inverses. This attribute is the same as GeneratorsOfDivisionRing (58.1-6).
GeneratorsOfField.title: GeneratorsOfField

DivisionRingByGenerators.name: DivisionRingByGenerators
DivisionRingByGenerators.description:
Called with a field F and a list gens of scalars, DivisionRingByGenerators returns the division ring over F generated by gens. The unary version returns the division ring as vector space over FieldOverItselfByGenerators( gens ).

FieldByGenerators is just a synonym for DivisionRingByGenerators.
DivisionRingByGenerators.title: DivisionRingByGenerators

FieldByGenerators.name: FieldByGenerators
FieldByGenerators.description:
Called with a field F and a list gens of scalars, DivisionRingByGenerators returns the division ring over F generated by gens. The unary version returns the division ring as vector space over FieldOverItselfByGenerators( gens ).

FieldByGenerators is just a synonym for DivisionRingByGenerators.
FieldByGenerators.title: DivisionRingByGenerators

AsDivisionRing.name: AsDivisionRing
AsDivisionRing.description:
If the collection C can be regarded as a division ring then AsDivisionRing( C ) is the division ring that consists of the elements of C, viewed as a vector space over its prime field; otherwise fail is returned.

In the second form, if F is a division ring contained in C then the returned division ring is viewed as a vector space over F.

AsField is just a synonym for AsDivisionRing.
AsDivisionRing.title: AsDivisionRing

AsField.name: AsField
AsField.description:
If the collection C can be regarded as a division ring then AsDivisionRing( C ) is the division ring that consists of the elements of C, viewed as a vector space over its prime field; otherwise fail is returned.

In the second form, if F is a division ring contained in C then the returned division ring is viewed as a vector space over F.

AsField is just a synonym for AsDivisionRing.
AsField.title: AsDivisionRing

Subfield.name: Subfield
Subfield.description: Constructs the subfield of F generated by gens.
Subfield.title: Subfield

SubfieldNC.name: SubfieldNC
SubfieldNC.description: Constructs the subfield of F generated by gens.
SubfieldNC.title: Subfield

FieldOverItselfByGenerators.name: FieldOverItselfByGenerators
FieldOverItselfByGenerators.description: This operation is needed for the call of Field (58.1-3) or FieldByGenerators (58.1-8) without explicitly given subfield, in order to construct a left acting domain for such a field.
FieldOverItselfByGenerators.title: FieldOverItselfByGenerators

PrimitiveElement.name: PrimitiveElement
PrimitiveElement.description: is an element of D that generates D as a division ring together with the left acting domain.
PrimitiveElement.title: PrimitiveElement

PrimeField.name: PrimeField
PrimeField.description: The prime field of a division ring D is the smallest field which is contained in D. For example, the prime field of any field in characteristic zero is isomorphic to the field of rational numbers.
PrimeField.title: PrimeField

IsPrimeField.name: IsPrimeField
IsPrimeField.description: A division ring is a prime field if it is equal to its prime field (see PrimeField (58.2-4)).
IsPrimeField.title: IsPrimeField

DegreeOverPrimeField.name: DegreeOverPrimeField
DegreeOverPrimeField.description: is the degree of the field F over its prime field (see PrimeField (58.2-4)).
DegreeOverPrimeField.title: DegreeOverPrimeField

DefiningPolynomial.name: DefiningPolynomial
DefiningPolynomial.description: is the defining polynomial of the field F as a field extension over the left acting domain of F. A root of the defining polynomial can be computed with RootOfDefiningPolynomial (58.2-8).
DefiningPolynomial.title: DefiningPolynomial

RootOfDefiningPolynomial.name: RootOfDefiningPolynomial
RootOfDefiningPolynomial.description: is a root in the field F of its defining polynomial as a field extension over the left acting domain of F. The defining polynomial can be computed with DefiningPolynomial (58.2-7).
RootOfDefiningPolynomial.title: RootOfDefiningPolynomial

FieldExtension.name: FieldExtension
FieldExtension.description: is the field obtained on adjoining a root of the irreducible polynomial poly to the field F.
FieldExtension.title: FieldExtension

Subfields.name: Subfields
Subfields.description: is the set of all subfields of the field F.
Subfields.title: Subfields

MinimalPolynomial.name: MinimalPolynomial
MinimalPolynomial.description: returns the minimal polynomial of z over the field F. This is a generator of the ideal in F[x] of all polynomials which vanish on z. (This definition is consistent with the general definition of MinimalPolynomial (66.8-1) for rings.)
MinimalPolynomial.title: MinimalPolynomial

MinimalPolynomial.name: MinimalPolynomial
MinimalPolynomial.description:
returns the minimal polynomial of elm over the ring R, expressed in the indeterminate number ind. If ind is not given, it defaults to 1.

The minimal polynomial is the monic polynomial of smallest degree with coefficients in R that has value zero at elm.
MinimalPolynomial.title: MinimalPolynomial

TracePolynomial.name: TracePolynomial
TracePolynomial.description:
returns the polynomial that is the product of (X - c) where c runs over the conjugates of z in the field extension L over K. The polynomial is returned as a univariate polynomial over K in the indeterminate number inum (defaulting to 1).

This polynomial is sometimes also called the characteristic polynomial of z w.r.t. the field extension L > K. Therefore methods are installed for CharacteristicPolynomial (24.13-1) that call TracePolynomial in the case of field extensions.
TracePolynomial.title: TracePolynomial

Conjugates.name: Conjugates
Conjugates.description:
Conjugates returns the list of conjugates of the field element z. If two fields L and K are given then the conjugates are computed w.r.t. the field extension L>K, if only one field L is given then LeftActingDomain( L ) is taken as default for the subfield K, and if no field is given then DefaultField( z ) is taken as default for L.

The result list will contain duplicates if z lies in a proper subfield of L, or of the default field of z, respectively. The result list need not be sorted.
Conjugates.title: Conjugates

NormalBase.name: NormalBase
NormalBase.description:
Let F be a field that is a Galois extension of its subfield LeftActingDomain( F ). Then NormalBase returns a list of elements in F that form a normal basis of F, that is, a vector space basis that is closed under the action of the Galois group (see GaloisGroup (58.3-1)) of F.

If a second argument elm is given, it is used as a hint for the algorithm to find a normal basis with the algorithm described in [Art73].
NormalBase.title: NormalBase

IsFFE.name: IsFFE
IsFFE.description: Objects in the category IsFFE are used to implement elements of finite fields. In this manual, the term finite field element always means an object in IsFFE. All finite field elements of the same characteristic form a family in GAP (see 13.1). Any collection of finite field elements of the same characteristic (see IsCollection (30.1-1)) lies in IsFFECollection, and a collection of such collections (e.g., a matrix of finite field elements) lies in IsFFECollColl.
IsFFE.title: IsFFE

IsFFECollection.name: IsFFECollection
IsFFECollection.description: Objects in the category IsFFE are used to implement elements of finite fields. In this manual, the term finite field element always means an object in IsFFE. All finite field elements of the same characteristic form a family in GAP (see 13.1). Any collection of finite field elements of the same characteristic (see IsCollection (30.1-1)) lies in IsFFECollection, and a collection of such collections (e.g., a matrix of finite field elements) lies in IsFFECollColl.
IsFFECollection.title: IsFFE

IsFFECollColl.name: IsFFECollColl
IsFFECollColl.description: Objects in the category IsFFE are used to implement elements of finite fields. In this manual, the term finite field element always means an object in IsFFE. All finite field elements of the same characteristic form a family in GAP (see 13.1). Any collection of finite field elements of the same characteristic (see IsCollection (30.1-1)) lies in IsFFECollection, and a collection of such collections (e.g., a matrix of finite field elements) lies in IsFFECollColl.
IsFFECollColl.title: IsFFE

IsFFECollCollColl.name: IsFFECollCollColl
IsFFECollCollColl.description: Objects in the category IsFFE are used to implement elements of finite fields. In this manual, the term finite field element always means an object in IsFFE. All finite field elements of the same characteristic form a family in GAP (see 13.1). Any collection of finite field elements of the same characteristic (see IsCollection (30.1-1)) lies in IsFFECollection, and a collection of such collections (e.g., a matrix of finite field elements) lies in IsFFECollColl.
IsFFECollCollColl.title: IsFFE

Z.name: Z
Z.description:
For creating elements of a finite field, the function Z can be used. The call Z(p,d) (alternatively Z(p^d)) returns the designated generator of the multiplicative group of the finite field with p^d elements. p must be a prime integer.

GAP can represent elements of all finite fields GF(p^d) such that either (1) p^d <= 65536 (in which case an extremely efficient internal representation is used); (2) d = 1, (in which case, for large p, the field is represented using the machinery of residue class rings (see section 14.5) or (3) if the Conway polynomial of degree d over the field with p elements is known, or can be computed (see ConwayPolynomial (59.5-1)).

If you attempt to construct an element of GF(p^d) for which d > 1 and the relevant Conway polynomial is not known, and not necessarily easy to find (see IsCheapConwayPolynomial (59.5-2)), then GAP will stop with an error and enter the break loop. If you leave this break loop by entering return; GAP will attempt to compute the Conway polynomial, which may take a very long time.
Z.title: Z

Z.name: Z
Z.description:
For creating elements of a finite field, the function Z can be used. The call Z(p,d) (alternatively Z(p^d)) returns the designated generator of the multiplicative group of the finite field with p^d elements. p must be a prime integer.

GAP can represent elements of all finite fields GF(p^d) such that either (1) p^d <= 65536 (in which case an extremely efficient internal representation is used); (2) d = 1, (in which case, for large p, the field is represented using the machinery of residue class rings (see section 14.5) or (3) if the Conway polynomial of degree d over the field with p elements is known, or can be computed (see ConwayPolynomial (59.5-1)).

If you attempt to construct an element of GF(p^d) for which d > 1 and the relevant Conway polynomial is not known, and not necessarily easy to find (see IsCheapConwayPolynomial (59.5-2)), then GAP will stop with an error and enter the break loop. If you leave this break loop by entering return; GAP will attempt to compute the Conway polynomial, which may take a very long time.
Z.title: Z

IsLexOrderedFFE.name: IsLexOrderedFFE
IsLexOrderedFFE.description:
Elements of finite fields can be compared using the operators = and <. The call a = b returns true if and only if the finite field elements a and b are equal. Furthermore a < b tests whether a is smaller than b. The exact behaviour of this comparison depends on which of two categories the field elements belong to:

Finite field elements are ordered in GAP (by \< (31.11-1)) first by characteristic and then by their degree (i.e. the sizes of the smallest fields containing them). Amongst irreducible elements of a given field, the ordering depends on which of these categories the elements of the field belong to (all irreducible elements of a given field should belong to the same one)

Elements in IsLexOrderedFFE are ordered lexicographically by their coefficients with respect to the canonical basis of the field.
IsLexOrderedFFE.title: IsLexOrderedFFE

IsLogOrderedFFE.name: IsLogOrderedFFE
IsLogOrderedFFE.description:
Elements of finite fields can be compared using the operators = and <. The call a = b returns true if and only if the finite field elements a and b are equal. Furthermore a < b tests whether a is smaller than b. The exact behaviour of this comparison depends on which of two categories the field elements belong to:

Finite field elements are ordered in GAP (by \< (31.11-1)) first by characteristic and then by their degree (i.e. the sizes of the smallest fields containing them). Amongst irreducible elements of a given field, the ordering depends on which of these categories the elements of the field belong to (all irreducible elements of a given field should belong to the same one)

Elements in IsLexOrderedFFE are ordered lexicographically by their coefficients with respect to the canonical basis of the field.
IsLogOrderedFFE.title: IsLexOrderedFFE

DegreeFFE.name: DegreeFFE
DegreeFFE.description: DegreeFFE returns the degree of the smallest finite field F containing the element z, respectively all elements of the row vector vec over a finite field (see 23), or the matrix mat over a finite field (see 24).
DegreeFFE.title: DegreeFFE

DegreeFFE.name: DegreeFFE
DegreeFFE.description: DegreeFFE returns the degree of the smallest finite field F containing the element z, respectively all elements of the row vector vec over a finite field (see 23), or the matrix mat over a finite field (see 24).
DegreeFFE.title: DegreeFFE

DegreeFFE.name: DegreeFFE
DegreeFFE.description: DegreeFFE returns the degree of the smallest finite field F containing the element z, respectively all elements of the row vector vec over a finite field (see 23), or the matrix mat over a finite field (see 24).
DegreeFFE.title: DegreeFFE

LogFFE.name: LogFFE
LogFFE.description:
LogFFE returns the discrete logarithm of the element z in a finite field with respect to the root r. An error is signalled if z is zero. fail is returned if z is not a power of r.

The discrete logarithm of the element z with respect to the root r is the smallest nonnegative integer i such that r^i = z holds.
LogFFE.title: LogFFE

IntFFE.name: IntFFE
IntFFE.description:
IntFFE returns the integer corresponding to the element z, which must lie in a finite prime field. That is, IntFFE returns the smallest nonnegative integer i such that i * One( z ) = z.

The correspondence between elements from a finite prime field of characteristic p (for p < 2^16) and the integers between 0 and p-1 is defined by choosing Z(p) the element corresponding to the smallest primitive root mod p (see PrimitiveRootMod (15.3-4)).

IntFFE is installed as a method for the operation Int (14.2-3) with argument a finite field element.
IntFFE.title: IntFFE

IntFFESymm.name: IntFFESymm
IntFFESymm.description:
For a finite prime field element z, IntFFESymm returns the corresponding integer of smallest absolute value. That is, IntFFESymm returns the integer i of smallest absolute value such that i * One( z ) = z holds.

For a vector vec of FFEs, the operation returns the result of applying IntFFESymm to every entry of the vector.

The correspondence between elements from a finite prime field of characteristic p (for p < 2^16) and the integers between -p/2 and p/2 is defined by choosing Z(p) the element corresponding to the smallest positive primitive root mod p (see PrimitiveRootMod (15.3-4)) and reducing results to the -p/2 .. p/2 range.
IntFFESymm.title: IntFFESymm

IntFFESymm.name: IntFFESymm
IntFFESymm.description:
For a finite prime field element z, IntFFESymm returns the corresponding integer of smallest absolute value. That is, IntFFESymm returns the integer i of smallest absolute value such that i * One( z ) = z holds.

For a vector vec of FFEs, the operation returns the result of applying IntFFESymm to every entry of the vector.

The correspondence between elements from a finite prime field of characteristic p (for p < 2^16) and the integers between -p/2 and p/2 is defined by choosing Z(p) the element corresponding to the smallest positive primitive root mod p (see PrimitiveRootMod (15.3-4)) and reducing results to the -p/2 .. p/2 range.
IntFFESymm.title: IntFFESymm

IntVecFFE.name: IntVecFFE
IntVecFFE.description: is the list of integers corresponding to the vector vecffe of finite field elements in a prime field (see IntFFE (59.2-3)).
IntVecFFE.title: IntVecFFE

AsInternalFFE.name: AsInternalFFE
AsInternalFFE.description: return an internal FFE equal to ffe if one exists, otherwise fail
AsInternalFFE.title: AsInternalFFE

RootFFE.name: RootFFE
RootFFE.description: RootFFE returns a finite field element r from F whose k-th power is z. If no such element exists then fail is returned.
RootFFE.title: RootFFE

GaloisField.name: GaloisField
GaloisField.description:
GaloisField returns a finite field. It takes two arguments. The form GaloisField( p, d ), where p, d are integers, can also be given as GaloisField( p^d ). GF is an abbreviation for GaloisField.

The first argument specifies the subfield S over which the new field is to be taken. It can be a prime integer or a finite field. If it is a prime p, the subfield is the prime field of this characteristic.

The second argument specifies the extension. It can be an integer or an irreducible polynomial over the field S. If it is an integer d, the new field is constructed as the polynomial extension w.r.t. the Conway polynomial (see ConwayPolynomial (59.5-1)) of degree d over S. If it is an irreducible polynomial pol over S, the new field is constructed as polynomial extension of S with this polynomial; in this case, pol is accessible as the value of DefiningPolynomial (58.2-7) for the new field, and a root of pol in the new field is accessible as the value of RootOfDefiningPolynomial (58.2-8).
GaloisField.title: GaloisField

PrimitiveRoot.name: PrimitiveRoot
PrimitiveRoot.description: A primitive root of a finite field is a generator of its multiplicative group. A primitive root is always a primitive element (see PrimitiveElement (58.2-3)), the converse is in general not true.
PrimitiveRoot.title: PrimitiveRoot

FrobeniusAutomorphism.name: FrobeniusAutomorphism
FrobeniusAutomorphism.description:
returns the Frobenius automorphism of the finite field F as a field homomorphism (see 32.12).

The Frobenius automorphism f of a finite field F of characteristic p is the function that takes each element z of F to its p-th power. Each field automorphism of F is a power of f. Thus f is a generator for the Galois group of F relative to the prime field of F, and an appropriate power of f is a generator of the Galois group of F over a subfield (see GaloisGroup (58.3-1)).

The image of an element z under the i-th power of f is computed as the p^i-th power of z. The product of the i-th power and the j-th power of f is the k-th power of f, where k is i j mod Size(F)-1. The zeroth power of f is IdentityMapping( F ).
FrobeniusAutomorphism.title: FrobeniusAutomorphism

ConwayPolynomial.name: ConwayPolynomial
ConwayPolynomial.description:
is the Conway polynomial of the finite field GF(p^n) as polynomial over the prime field in characteristic p.

The Conway polynomial Φ_{n,p} of GF(p^n) is defined by the following properties.

First define an ordering of polynomials of degree n over GF(p), as follows. f = ∑_{i = 0}^n (-1)^i f_i x^i is smaller than g = ∑_{i = 0}^n (-1)^i g_i x^i if and only if there is an index m ≤ n such that f_i = g_i for all i > m, and tilde{f_m} < tilde{g_m}, where tilde{c} denotes the integer value in { 0, 1, ..., p-1 } that is mapped to c ∈ GF(p) under the canonical epimorphism that maps the integers onto GF(p).
ConwayPolynomial.title: ConwayPolynomial

IsCheapConwayPolynomial.name: IsCheapConwayPolynomial
IsCheapConwayPolynomial.description: Returns true if ConwayPolynomial( p, n ) will give a result in reasonable time. This is either the case when this polynomial is pre-computed, or if n is a not too big prime.
IsCheapConwayPolynomial.title: IsCheapConwayPolynomial

RandomPrimitivePolynomial.name: RandomPrimitivePolynomial
RandomPrimitivePolynomial.description:
For a finite field F and a positive integer n this function returns a primitive polynomial of degree n over F, that is a zero of this polynomial has maximal multiplicative order |F|^n-1. If i is given then the polynomial is written in variable number i over F (see Indeterminate (66.1-1)), the default for i is 1.

Alternatively, F can be a prime power q, then F = GF(q) is assumed. And i can be a univariate polynomial over F, then the result is a polynomial in the same variable.

This function can work for much larger fields than those for which Conway polynomials are available, of course GAP must be able to factorize |F|^n-1.
RandomPrimitivePolynomial.title: RandomPrimitivePolynomial

CyclotomicField.name: CyclotomicField
CyclotomicField.description:
The first version creates the n-th cyclotomic field ℚ_n. The second version creates the smallest cyclotomic field containing the elements in the list gens. In both cases the field can be generated as an extension of a designated subfield subfield (cf. 60.3).

CyclotomicField can be abbreviated to CF, this form is used also when GAP prints cyclotomic fields.

Fields constructed with the one argument version of CF are stored in the global list CYCLOTOMIC_FIELDS, so repeated calls of CF just fetch these field objects after they have been created once.
CyclotomicField.title: CyclotomicField

CF.title: CyclotomicField

AbelianNumberField.name: AbelianNumberField
AbelianNumberField.description:
For a positive integer n and a list stab of prime residues modulo n, AbelianNumberField returns the fixed field of the group described by stab (cf. GaloisStabilizer (60.2-5)), in the n-th cyclotomic field. AbelianNumberField is mainly thought for internal use and for printing fields in a standard way; Field (58.1-3) (cf. also 60.2) is probably more suitable if one knows generators of the field in question.

AbelianNumberField can be abbreviated to NF, this form is used also when GAP prints abelian number fields.

Fields constructed with NF are stored in the global list ABELIAN_NUMBER_FIELDS, so repeated calls of NF just fetch these field objects after they have been created once.
AbelianNumberField.title: AbelianNumberField

NF.name: NF
NF.description:
For a positive integer n and a list stab of prime residues modulo n, AbelianNumberField returns the fixed field of the group described by stab (cf. GaloisStabilizer (60.2-5)), in the n-th cyclotomic field. AbelianNumberField is mainly thought for internal use and for printing fields in a standard way; Field (58.1-3) (cf. also 60.2) is probably more suitable if one knows generators of the field in question.

AbelianNumberField can be abbreviated to NF, this form is used also when GAP prints abelian number fields.

Fields constructed with NF are stored in the global list ABELIAN_NUMBER_FIELDS, so repeated calls of NF just fetch these field objects after they have been created once.
NF.title: AbelianNumberField

GaussianRationals.name: GaussianRationals
GaussianRationals.description:
GaussianRationals is the field ℚ_4 = ℚ(sqrt{-1}) of Gaussian rationals, as a set of cyclotomic numbers, see Chapter 18 for basic operations. This field can also be obtained as CF(4) (see CyclotomicField (60.1-1)).

The filter IsGaussianRationals returns true for the GAP object GaussianRationals, and false for all other GAP objects.

(For details about the field of rationals, see Chapter Rationals (17.1-1).)
GaussianRationals.title: GaussianRationals

IsGaussianRationals.name: IsGaussianRationals
IsGaussianRationals.description:
GaussianRationals is the field ℚ_4 = ℚ(sqrt{-1}) of Gaussian rationals, as a set of cyclotomic numbers, see Chapter 18 for basic operations. This field can also be obtained as CF(4) (see CyclotomicField (60.1-1)).

The filter IsGaussianRationals returns true for the GAP object GaussianRationals, and false for all other GAP objects.

(For details about the field of rationals, see Chapter Rationals (17.1-1).)
IsGaussianRationals.title: GaussianRationals

IsNumberField.name: IsNumberField
IsNumberField.description: returns true if the field F is a finite dimensional extension of a prime field in characteristic zero, and false otherwise.
IsNumberField.title: IsNumberField

IsAbelianNumberField.name: IsAbelianNumberField
