Here B is a basis of an algebra module and x is an element of the algebra that acts on this module. This function returns the matrix of the action of x with respect to B. If x acts from the left, then the coefficients of the images of the basis elements of B (under the action of x) are the columns of the output. If x acts from the right, then they are the rows of the output.

If the module is a bi-module, then the third parameter side must be specified. This is the string "left", or "right" depending whether x acts from the left or the right.
MatrixOfAction.title: MatrixOfAction

SubAlgebraModule.name: SubAlgebraModule
SubAlgebraModule.description: is the sub-module of the algebra module M, generated by the vectors in gens. If as an optional argument the string basis is added, then it is assumed that the vectors in gens form a basis of the submodule.
SubAlgebraModule.title: SubAlgebraModule

LeftModuleByHomomorphismToMatAlg.name: LeftModuleByHomomorphismToMatAlg
LeftModuleByHomomorphismToMatAlg.description: Here A is an algebra and hom a homomorphism from A into a matrix algebra. This function returns the left A-module defined by the homomorphism hom.
LeftModuleByHomomorphismToMatAlg.title: LeftModuleByHomomorphismToMatAlg

RightModuleByHomomorphismToMatAlg.name: RightModuleByHomomorphismToMatAlg
RightModuleByHomomorphismToMatAlg.description:
Here A is an algebra and hom a homomorphism from A into a matrix algebra. This function returns the right A-module defined by the homomorphism hom.

First we produce a structure constants algebra with basis elements x, y, z such that x^2 = x, y^2 = y, xz = z, zy = z and all other products are zero.

Now we construct an isomorphic matrix algebra.
RightModuleByHomomorphismToMatAlg.title: RightModuleByHomomorphismToMatAlg

AdjointModule.name: AdjointModule
AdjointModule.description: returns the A-module defined by the left action of A on itself.
AdjointModule.title: AdjointModule

FaithfulModule.name: FaithfulModule
FaithfulModule.description:
returns a faithful finite-dimensional left-module over the algebra A. This is only implemented for associative algebras, and for Lie algebras of characteristic 0. (It may also work for certain Lie algebras of characteristic p > 0.)

A is a 2-dimensional algebra where all products are zero.
FaithfulModule.title: FaithfulModule

ModuleByRestriction.name: ModuleByRestriction
ModuleByRestriction.description: Here V is an algebra module and sub1 is a subalgebra of the acting algebra of V. This function returns the module that is the restriction of V to sub1. So it has the same underlying vector space as V, but the acting algebra is sub. If two subalgebras sub1, sub2 are given then V is assumed to be a bi-module, and sub1 a subalgebra of the algebra acting on the left, and sub2 a subalgebra of the algebra acting on the right.
ModuleByRestriction.title: ModuleByRestriction

NaturalHomomorphismBySubAlgebraModule.name: NaturalHomomorphismBySubAlgebraModule
NaturalHomomorphismBySubAlgebraModule.description:
Here V must be a sub-algebra module of V. This function returns the projection from V onto V/W. It is a linear map, that is also a module homomorphism. As usual images can be formed with Image( f, v ) and pre-images with PreImagesRepresentative( f, u ).

The quotient module can also be formed by entering V/W.
NaturalHomomorphismBySubAlgebraModule.title: NaturalHomomorphismBySubAlgebraModule

DirectSumOfAlgebraModules.name: DirectSumOfAlgebraModules
DirectSumOfAlgebraModules.description:
Here list must be a list of algebra modules. This function returns the direct sum of the elements in the list (as an algebra module). The modules must be defined over the same algebras.

In the second form is short for DirectSumOfAlgebraModules( [ V, W ] )
DirectSumOfAlgebraModules.title: DirectSumOfAlgebraModules

DirectSumOfAlgebraModules.name: DirectSumOfAlgebraModules
DirectSumOfAlgebraModules.description:
Here list must be a list of algebra modules. This function returns the direct sum of the elements in the list (as an algebra module). The modules must be defined over the same algebras.

In the second form is short for DirectSumOfAlgebraModules( [ V, W ] )
DirectSumOfAlgebraModules.title: DirectSumOfAlgebraModules

TranslatorSubalgebra.name: TranslatorSubalgebra
TranslatorSubalgebra.description:
Here M is an algebra module, and U and W are two subspaces of M. Let A be the algebra acting on M. This function returns the subspace of elements of A that map U into W. If W is a sub-algebra-module (i.e., closed under the action of A), then this space is a subalgebra of A.

This function works for left, or right modules over a finite-dimensional algebra. We stress that it is not checked whether U and W are indeed subspaces of M. If this is not the case nothing is guaranteed about the behaviour of the function.
TranslatorSubalgebra.title: TranslatorSubalgebra

LieObject.name: LieObject
LieObject.description: Let obj be a ring element. Then LieObject( obj ) is the corresponding Lie object. If obj lies in the family F, then LieObject( obj ) lies in the family LieFamily( F ) (see LieFamily (64.1-3)).
LieObject.title: LieObject

IsLieObject.name: IsLieObject
IsLieObject.description: An object lies in IsLieObject if and only if it lies in a family constructed by LieFamily (64.1-3).
IsLieObject.title: IsLieObject

IsLieObjectCollection.name: IsLieObjectCollection
IsLieObjectCollection.description: An object lies in IsLieObject if and only if it lies in a family constructed by LieFamily (64.1-3).
IsLieObjectCollection.title: IsLieObject

IsRestrictedLieObject.name: IsRestrictedLieObject
IsRestrictedLieObject.description: An object lies in IsLieObject if and only if it lies in a family constructed by LieFamily (64.1-3).
IsRestrictedLieObject.title: IsLieObject

IsRestrictedLieObjectCollection.name: IsRestrictedLieObjectCollection
IsRestrictedLieObjectCollection.description: An object lies in IsLieObject if and only if it lies in a family constructed by LieFamily (64.1-3).
IsRestrictedLieObjectCollection.title: IsLieObject

LieFamily.name: LieFamily
LieFamily.description:
is a family F in bijection with the family Fam, but with the Lie bracket as infix multiplication. That is, for x, y in Fam, the product of the images in F will be the image of x * y - y * x.

The standard type of objects in a Lie family F is F!.packedType.

The bijection from Fam to F is given by Embedding( Fam, F ) (see Embedding (32.2-11)); this bijection respects addition and additive inverses.
LieFamily.title: LieFamily

UnderlyingFamily.name: UnderlyingFamily
UnderlyingFamily.description: If Fam is a Lie family then UnderlyingFamily( Fam ) is a family F such that Fam = LieFamily( F ).
UnderlyingFamily.title: UnderlyingFamily

UnderlyingRingElement.name: UnderlyingRingElement
UnderlyingRingElement.description: Let obj be a Lie object constructed from a ring element r by calling LieObject( r ). Then UnderlyingRingElement( obj ) returns the ring element r used to construct obj. If r lies in the family F, then obj lies in the family LieFamily( F ) (see LieFamily (64.1-3)).
UnderlyingRingElement.title: UnderlyingRingElement

LieAlgebraByStructureConstants.name: LieAlgebraByStructureConstants
LieAlgebraByStructureConstants.description: LieAlgebraByStructureConstants does the same as AlgebraByStructureConstants (62.4-1), and has the same meaning of arguments, except that the result is assumed to be a Lie algebra. Note that the function does not check whether sct satisfies the Jacobi identity. (So if one creates a Lie algebra this way with a table that does not satisfy the Jacobi identity, errors may occur later on.)
LieAlgebraByStructureConstants.title: LieAlgebraByStructureConstants

RestrictedLieAlgebraByStructureConstants.name: RestrictedLieAlgebraByStructureConstants
RestrictedLieAlgebraByStructureConstants.description:
RestrictedLieAlgebraByStructureConstants does the same as LieAlgebraByStructureConstants (64.2-1), and has the same meaning of all arguments, except that the result is assumed to be a restricted Lie algebra (see 64.8) with the p-map given by the additional argument pmapping. This last argument is a list of the length equal to the dimension of the algebra; its i-th entry specifies the p-th power of the i-th basis vector in the same format [ coeff1, position1, coeff2, position2, ... ] as SetEntrySCTable (62.4-5) uses to specify entries of the structure constants table.

Note that the function does not check whether sct satisfies the Jacobi identity, of whether pmapping specifies a legitimate p-mapping.

The following example creates a commutative restricted Lie algebra of dimension 3, in which the p-th power of the i-th basis element is the i+1-th basis element (except for the 3rd basis element which goes to zero).
RestrictedLieAlgebraByStructureConstants.title: RestrictedLieAlgebraByStructureConstants

LieAlgebra.name: LieAlgebra
LieAlgebra.description:
For an associative algebra L, LieAlgebra( L ) is the Lie algebra isomorphic to L as a vector space but with the Lie bracket as product.

LieAlgebra( F, gens ) is the Lie algebra over the division ring F, generated as Lie algebra by the Lie objects corresponding to the vectors in the list gens.

Note that the algebra returned by LieAlgebra does not contain the vectors in gens. The elements in gens are wrapped up as Lie objects (see 64.1). This allows one to create Lie algebras from ring elements with respect to the Lie bracket as product. But of course the product in the Lie algebra is the usual *.
LieAlgebra.title: LieAlgebra

LieAlgebra.name: LieAlgebra
LieAlgebra.description:
For an associative algebra L, LieAlgebra( L ) is the Lie algebra isomorphic to L as a vector space but with the Lie bracket as product.

LieAlgebra( F, gens ) is the Lie algebra over the division ring F, generated as Lie algebra by the Lie objects corresponding to the vectors in the list gens.

Note that the algebra returned by LieAlgebra does not contain the vectors in gens. The elements in gens are wrapped up as Lie objects (see 64.1). This allows one to create Lie algebras from ring elements with respect to the Lie bracket as product. But of course the product in the Lie algebra is the usual *.
LieAlgebra.title: LieAlgebra

FreeLieAlgebra.name: FreeLieAlgebra
FreeLieAlgebra.description: Returns a free Lie algebra of rank rank over the ring R. FreeLieAlgebra( R, name1, name2,...) returns a free Lie algebra over R with generators named name1, name2, and so on. The elements of a free Lie algebra are written on the Hall-Lyndon basis.
FreeLieAlgebra.title: FreeLieAlgebra

FreeLieAlgebra.name: FreeLieAlgebra
FreeLieAlgebra.description: Returns a free Lie algebra of rank rank over the ring R. FreeLieAlgebra( R, name1, name2,...) returns a free Lie algebra over R with generators named name1, name2, and so on. The elements of a free Lie algebra are written on the Hall-Lyndon basis.
FreeLieAlgebra.title: FreeLieAlgebra

FullMatrixLieAlgebra.name: FullMatrixLieAlgebra
FullMatrixLieAlgebra.description: is the full matrix Lie algebra of n × n matrices over the ring R, for a nonnegative integer n.
FullMatrixLieAlgebra.title: FullMatrixLieAlgebra

MatrixLieAlgebra.name: MatrixLieAlgebra
MatrixLieAlgebra.description: is the full matrix Lie algebra of n × n matrices over the ring R, for a nonnegative integer n.
MatrixLieAlgebra.title: FullMatrixLieAlgebra

MatLieAlgebra.name: MatLieAlgebra
MatLieAlgebra.description: is the full matrix Lie algebra of n × n matrices over the ring R, for a nonnegative integer n.
MatLieAlgebra.title: FullMatrixLieAlgebra

RightDerivations.name: RightDerivations
RightDerivations.description:
These functions all return the matrix Lie algebra of derivations of the algebra A with basis B.

RightDerivations( B ) returns the algebra of derivations represented by their right action on the algebra A. This means that with respect to the basis B of A, the derivation D is described by the matrix [ d_{i,j} ] which means that D maps the i-th basis element b_i to ∑_{j = 1}^n d_{i,j} b_j.

LeftDerivations( B ) returns the Lie algebra of derivations represented by their left action on the algebra A. So the matrices contained in the algebra output by LeftDerivations( B ) are the transposes of the matrices contained in the output of RightDerivations( B ).
RightDerivations.title: RightDerivations

LeftDerivations.name: LeftDerivations
LeftDerivations.description:
These functions all return the matrix Lie algebra of derivations of the algebra A with basis B.

RightDerivations( B ) returns the algebra of derivations represented by their right action on the algebra A. This means that with respect to the basis B of A, the derivation D is described by the matrix [ d_{i,j} ] which means that D maps the i-th basis element b_i to ∑_{j = 1}^n d_{i,j} b_j.

LeftDerivations( B ) returns the Lie algebra of derivations represented by their left action on the algebra A. So the matrices contained in the algebra output by LeftDerivations( B ) are the transposes of the matrices contained in the output of RightDerivations( B ).
LeftDerivations.title: RightDerivations

Derivations.name: Derivations
Derivations.description:
These functions all return the matrix Lie algebra of derivations of the algebra A with basis B.

RightDerivations( B ) returns the algebra of derivations represented by their right action on the algebra A. This means that with respect to the basis B of A, the derivation D is described by the matrix [ d_{i,j} ] which means that D maps the i-th basis element b_i to ∑_{j = 1}^n d_{i,j} b_j.

LeftDerivations( B ) returns the Lie algebra of derivations represented by their left action on the algebra A. So the matrices contained in the algebra output by LeftDerivations( B ) are the transposes of the matrices contained in the output of RightDerivations( B ).
Derivations.title: RightDerivations

SimpleLieAlgebra.name: SimpleLieAlgebra
SimpleLieAlgebra.description:
This function constructs the simple Lie algebra of type given by the string type and rank n over the field F. The string type must be one of "A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G", "H", "K", "S", "W" or "M". For the types A to G, n must be a positive integer. The last five types only exist over fields of characteristic p>0. If the type is H, then n must be a list of positive integers of even length. If the type is K, then n must be a list of positive integers of odd length. For the types S and W, n must be a list of positive integers of any length. If the type is M, then the Melikyan algebra is constructed. In this case n must be a list of two positive integers. This Lie algebra only exists over fields of characteristic 5. This Lie algebra is ℤ × ℤ graded; and the grading can be accessed via the attribute Grading(L) (see Grading (62.9-20)). In some cases the Lie algebra returned by this function is not simple. Examples are the Lie algebras of type A_n over a field of characteristic p>0 where p divides n+1, and the Lie algebras of type K_n where n is a list of length 1.

If type is one of A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and F is a field of characteristic zero, then the basis of the returned Lie algebra is a Chevalley basis.
SimpleLieAlgebra.title: SimpleLieAlgebra

LieCentre.name: LieCentre
LieCentre.description:
The Lie centre of the Lie algebra L is the kernel of the adjoint mapping, that is, the set { a ∈ L : ∀ x ∈ L: a x = 0 }.

In characteristic 2 this may differ from the usual centre (that is the set of all a ∈ L such that a x = x a for all x ∈ L). Therefore, this operation is named LieCentre and not Centre (35.4-5).
LieCentre.title: LieCentre

LieCenter.name: LieCenter
LieCenter.description:
The Lie centre of the Lie algebra L is the kernel of the adjoint mapping, that is, the set { a ∈ L : ∀ x ∈ L: a x = 0 }.

In characteristic 2 this may differ from the usual centre (that is the set of all a ∈ L such that a x = x a for all x ∈ L). Therefore, this operation is named LieCentre and not Centre (35.4-5).
LieCenter.title: LieCentre

LieCentralizer.name: LieCentralizer
LieCentralizer.description: is the annihilator of S in the Lie algebra L, that is, the set { a ∈ L : ∀ s ∈ S: a*s = 0 }. Here S may be a subspace or a subalgebra of L.
LieCentralizer.title: LieCentralizer

LieNormalizer.name: LieNormalizer
LieNormalizer.description: is the normalizer of the subspace U in the Lie algebra L, that is, the set N_L(U) = { x ∈ L : [x,U] ⊂ U }.
LieNormalizer.title: LieNormalizer

LieDerivedSubalgebra.name: LieDerivedSubalgebra
LieDerivedSubalgebra.description: is the (Lie) derived subalgebra of the Lie algebra L.
LieDerivedSubalgebra.title: LieDerivedSubalgebra

LieNilRadical.name: LieNilRadical
LieNilRadical.description: This function calculates the (Lie) nil radical of the Lie algebra L.
LieNilRadical.title: LieNilRadical

LieSolvableRadical.name: LieSolvableRadical
LieSolvableRadical.description: Returns the (Lie) solvable radical of the Lie algebra L.
LieSolvableRadical.title: LieSolvableRadical

CartanSubalgebra.name: CartanSubalgebra
CartanSubalgebra.description: A Cartan subalgebra of a Lie algebra L is defined as a nilpotent subalgebra of L equal to its own Lie normalizer in L.
CartanSubalgebra.title: CartanSubalgebra

LieDerivedSeries.name: LieDerivedSeries
LieDerivedSeries.description: is the (Lie) derived series of the Lie algebra L.
LieDerivedSeries.title: LieDerivedSeries

LieLowerCentralSeries.name: LieLowerCentralSeries
LieLowerCentralSeries.description: is the (Lie) lower central series of the Lie algebra L.
LieLowerCentralSeries.title: LieLowerCentralSeries

LieUpperCentralSeries.name: LieUpperCentralSeries
LieUpperCentralSeries.description: is the (Lie) upper central series of the Lie algebra L.
LieUpperCentralSeries.title: LieUpperCentralSeries

IsLieAbelian.name: IsLieAbelian
IsLieAbelian.description: returns true if L is a Lie algebra such that each product of elements in L is zero, and false otherwise.
IsLieAbelian.title: IsLieAbelian

IsLieNilpotent.name: IsLieNilpotent
IsLieNilpotent.description: A Lie algebra L is defined to be (Lie) nilpotent when its (Lie) lower central series reaches the trivial subalgebra.
IsLieNilpotent.title: IsLieNilpotent

IsLieSolvable.name: IsLieSolvable
IsLieSolvable.description: A Lie algebra L is defined to be (Lie) solvable when its (Lie) derived series reaches the trivial subalgebra.
IsLieSolvable.title: IsLieSolvable

SemiSimpleType.name: SemiSimpleType
SemiSimpleType.description: Let L be a semisimple Lie algebra, i.e., a direct sum of simple Lie algebras. Then SemiSimpleType returns the type of L, i.e., a string containing the types of the simple summands of L.
SemiSimpleType.title: SemiSimpleType

ChevalleyBasis.name: ChevalleyBasis
ChevalleyBasis.description: Here L must be a semisimple Lie algebra with a split Cartan subalgebra. Then ChevalleyBasis(L) returns a list consisting of three sublists. Together these sublists form a Chevalley basis of L. The first list contains the positive root vectors, the second list contains the negative root vectors, and the third list the Cartan elements of the Chevalley basis.
ChevalleyBasis.title: ChevalleyBasis

IsRootSystemFromLieAlgebra.name: IsRootSystemFromLieAlgebra
IsRootSystemFromLieAlgebra.description: Category of root systems that come from (semisimple) Lie algebras. They often have special attributes such as UnderlyingLieAlgebra (64.6-6), PositiveRootVectors (64.6-9), NegativeRootVectors (64.6-10), CanonicalGenerators (64.6-14).
IsRootSystemFromLieAlgebra.title: IsRootSystemFromLieAlgebra

RootSystem.name: RootSystem
RootSystem.description: RootSystem calculates the root system of the semisimple Lie algebra L with a split Cartan subalgebra.
RootSystem.title: RootSystem

UnderlyingLieAlgebra.name: UnderlyingLieAlgebra
UnderlyingLieAlgebra.description: For a root system R coming from a semisimple Lie algebra L, returns the Lie algebra L.
UnderlyingLieAlgebra.title: UnderlyingLieAlgebra

PositiveRoots.name: PositiveRoots
PositiveRoots.description: The list of positive roots of the root system R.
PositiveRoots.title: PositiveRoots

NegativeRoots.name: NegativeRoots
NegativeRoots.description: The list of negative roots of the root system R.
NegativeRoots.title: NegativeRoots

PositiveRootVectors.name: PositiveRootVectors
PositiveRootVectors.description: A list of positive root vectors of the root system R that comes from a Lie algebra L. This is a list in bijection with the list PositiveRoots( L ) (see PositiveRoots (64.6-7)). The root vector is a non-zero element of the root space (in L) of the corresponding root.
PositiveRootVectors.title: PositiveRootVectors

NegativeRootVectors.name: NegativeRootVectors
NegativeRootVectors.description: A list of negative root vectors of the root system R that comes from a Lie algebra L. This is a list in bijection with the list NegativeRoots( L ) (see NegativeRoots (64.6-8)). The root vector is a non-zero element of the root space (in L) of the corresponding root.
NegativeRootVectors.title: NegativeRootVectors

SimpleSystem.name: SimpleSystem
SimpleSystem.description: A list of simple roots of the root system R.
SimpleSystem.title: SimpleSystem

CartanMatrix.name: CartanMatrix
CartanMatrix.description: The Cartan matrix of the root system R, relative to the simple roots in SimpleSystem( R ) (see SimpleSystem (64.6-11)).
CartanMatrix.title: CartanMatrix

BilinearFormMat.name: BilinearFormMat
BilinearFormMat.description: The matrix of the bilinear form of the root system R. If we denote this matrix by B, then we have B(i,j) = (α_i, α_j), where the α_i are the simple roots of R.
BilinearFormMat.title: BilinearFormMat

CanonicalGenerators.name: CanonicalGenerators
CanonicalGenerators.description:
Here R must be a root system coming from a semisimple Lie algebra L. This function returns 3l generators of L, x_1, ..., x_l, y_1, ..., y_l, h_1, ..., h_l, where x_i lies in the root space corresponding to the i-th simple root of the root system of L, y_i lies in the root space corresponding to - the i-th simple root, and the h_i are elements of the Cartan subalgebra. These elements satisfy the relations h_i * h_j = 0, x_i * y_j = δ_ij h_i, h_j * x_i = c_ij x_i, h_j * y_i = -c_ij y_i, where c_ij is the entry of the Cartan matrix on position ij.

Also if a is a root of the root system R (so a is a list of numbers), then we have the relation h_i * x = a[i] x, where x is a root vector corresponding to a.
CanonicalGenerators.title: CanonicalGenerators

IsWeylGroup.name: IsWeylGroup
IsWeylGroup.description: A Weyl group is a group generated by reflections, with the attribute SparseCartanMatrix (64.7-2) set.
IsWeylGroup.title: IsWeylGroup

SparseCartanMatrix.name: SparseCartanMatrix
SparseCartanMatrix.description: This is a sparse form of the Cartan matrix of the corresponding root system. If we denote the Cartan matrix by C, then the sparse Cartan matrix of W is a list (of length equal to the length of the Cartan matrix), where the i-th entry is a list consisting of elements [ j, C[i][j] ], where j is such that C[i][j] is non-zero.
SparseCartanMatrix.title: SparseCartanMatrix

WeylGroup.name: WeylGroup
WeylGroup.description: The Weyl group of the root system R. It is generated by the simple reflections. A simple reflection is represented by a matrix, and the result of letting a simple reflection m act on a weight w is obtained by w*m.
WeylGroup.title: WeylGroup

ApplySimpleReflection.name: ApplySimpleReflection
ApplySimpleReflection.description: Here SC is the sparse Cartan matrix of a Weyl group. This function applies the i-th simple reflection to the weight wt, thus changing wt.
ApplySimpleReflection.title: ApplySimpleReflection

LongestWeylWordPerm.name: LongestWeylWordPerm
LongestWeylWordPerm.description: Let g_0 be the longest element in the Weyl group W, and let { α_1, ..., α_l } be a simple system of the corresponding root system. Then g_0 maps α_i to -α_{σ(i)}, where σ is a permutation of (1, ..., l). This function returns that permutation.
LongestWeylWordPerm.title: LongestWeylWordPerm

ConjugateDominantWeight.name: ConjugateDominantWeight
ConjugateDominantWeight.description:
Here W is a Weyl group and wt a weight (i.e., a list of integers). ConjugateDominantWeight returns the unique dominant weight conjugate to wt under W.

ConjugateDominantWeightWithWord returns a list of two elements. The first of these is the dominant weight conjugate to wt. The second element is a list of indices of simple reflections that have to be applied to wt in order to get the dominant weight conjugate to it.
ConjugateDominantWeight.title: ConjugateDominantWeight

ConjugateDominantWeightWithWord.name: ConjugateDominantWeightWithWord
ConjugateDominantWeightWithWord.description:
Here W is a Weyl group and wt a weight (i.e., a list of integers). ConjugateDominantWeight returns the unique dominant weight conjugate to wt under W.

ConjugateDominantWeightWithWord returns a list of two elements. The first of these is the dominant weight conjugate to wt. The second element is a list of indices of simple reflections that have to be applied to wt in order to get the dominant weight conjugate to it.
ConjugateDominantWeightWithWord.title: ConjugateDominantWeight

WeylOrbitIterator.name: WeylOrbitIterator
WeylOrbitIterator.description: Returns an iterator for the orbit of the weight wt under the action of the Weyl group W.
WeylOrbitIterator.title: WeylOrbitIterator

PthPowerImages.name: PthPowerImages
PthPowerImages.description: Here B is a basis of a restricted Lie algebra. This function returns the list of the images of the basis vectors of B under the p-map.
PthPowerImages.title: PthPowerImages

PthPowerImage.name: PthPowerImage
PthPowerImage.description:
This function computes the image of an element x of a restricted Lie algebra under its p-map.

In the first form, a basis of the Lie algebra is provided; this basis stores the pth powers of its elements. It is the traditional form, provided for backwards compatibility.

In its second form, only the element x is provided. It is the only form for elements of Lie algebras with no predetermined basis, such as those constructed by LieObject (64.1-1).
PthPowerImage.title: PthPowerImage

PthPowerImage.name: PthPowerImage
PthPowerImage.description:
This function computes the image of an element x of a restricted Lie algebra under its p-map.

In the first form, a basis of the Lie algebra is provided; this basis stores the pth powers of its elements. It is the traditional form, provided for backwards compatibility.

In its second form, only the element x is provided. It is the only form for elements of Lie algebras with no predetermined basis, such as those constructed by LieObject (64.1-1).
PthPowerImage.title: PthPowerImage

PthPowerImage.name: PthPowerImage
PthPowerImage.description:
This function computes the image of an element x of a restricted Lie algebra under its p-map.

In the first form, a basis of the Lie algebra is provided; this basis stores the pth powers of its elements. It is the traditional form, provided for backwards compatibility.

In its second form, only the element x is provided. It is the only form for elements of Lie algebras with no predetermined basis, such as those constructed by LieObject (64.1-1).
PthPowerImage.title: PthPowerImage

JenningsLieAlgebra.name: JenningsLieAlgebra
JenningsLieAlgebra.description: Let G be a nontrivial p-group, and let G = G_1 ⊃ G_2 ⊃ ⋯ ⊃ G_m = 1 be its Jennings series (see JenningsSeries (39.17-14)). Then the quotients G_i / G_{i+1} are elementary abelian p-groups, i.e., they can be viewed as vector spaces over GF(p). Now the Jennings-Lie algebra L of G is the direct sum of those vector spaces. The Lie bracket on L is induced by the commutator in G. Furthermore, the map g ↦ g^p in G induces a p-map in L making L into a restricted Lie algebra. In the canonical basis of L this p-map is added as an attribute. A Lie algebra created by JenningsLieAlgebra is naturally graded. The attribute Grading (62.9-20) is set.
JenningsLieAlgebra.title: JenningsLieAlgebra

PCentralLieAlgebra.name: PCentralLieAlgebra
PCentralLieAlgebra.description: Here G is a nontrivial p-group. PCentralLieAlgebra( G ) does the same as JenningsLieAlgebra (64.8-4) except that the p-central series is used instead of the Jennings series (see PCentralSeries (39.17-13)). This function also returns a graded Lie algebra. However, it is not necessarily restricted.
PCentralLieAlgebra.title: PCentralLieAlgebra

NaturalHomomorphismOfLieAlgebraFromNilpo.name: NaturalHomomorphismOfLieAlgebraFromNilpotentGroup
NaturalHomomorphismOfLieAlgebraFromNilpo.description: This is an attribute of Lie algebras created by JenningsLieAlgebra (64.8-4) or PCentralLieAlgebra (64.8-5). Then L is the direct sum of quotients of successive terms of the Jennings, or p-central series of a p-group G. Let Gi be the i-th term in this series, and let f = NaturalHomomorphismOfLieAlgebraFromNilpotentGroup( L ), then for g in Gi, f( g, i ) returns the element of L (lying in the i-th homogeneous component) corresponding to g.
NaturalHomomorphismOfLieAlgebraFromNilpo.title: NaturalHomomorphismOfLieAlgebraFromNilpotentGroup

AdjointMatrix.name: AdjointMatrix
AdjointMatrix.description: is the matrix of the adjoint representation of the element x w.r.t. the basis B. The adjoint map is the left multiplication by x. The i-th column of the resulting matrix represents the image of the i-th basis vector of B under left multiplication by x.
AdjointMatrix.title: AdjointMatrix

AdjointAssociativeAlgebra.name: AdjointAssociativeAlgebra
AdjointAssociativeAlgebra.description: is the associative matrix algebra (with 1) generated by the matrices of the adjoint representation of the subalgebra K on the Lie algebra L.
AdjointAssociativeAlgebra.title: AdjointAssociativeAlgebra

KillingMatrix.name: KillingMatrix
KillingMatrix.description: is the matrix of the Killing form κ with respect to the basis B, i.e., the matrix ( κ( b_i, b_j ) ) where b_1, b_2, ... are the basis vectors of B.
KillingMatrix.title: KillingMatrix

KappaPerp.name: KappaPerp
KappaPerp.description:
is the orthogonal complement of the subspace U of the Lie algebra L with respect to the Killing form κ, that is, the set U^{perp} = { x ∈ L; κ( x, y ) = 0 hbox for all y ∈ L }.

U^{perp} is a subspace of L, and if U is an ideal of L then U^{perp} is a subalgebra of L.
KappaPerp.title: KappaPerp

IsNilpotentElement.name: IsNilpotentElement
IsNilpotentElement.description: x is nilpotent in L if its adjoint matrix is a nilpotent matrix.
IsNilpotentElement.title: IsNilpotentElement

NonNilpotentElement.name: NonNilpotentElement
NonNilpotentElement.description: A non-nilpotent element of a Lie algebra L is an element x such that adx is not nilpotent. If L is not nilpotent, then by Engel's theorem non-nilpotent elements exist in L. In this case this function returns a non-nilpotent element of L, otherwise (if L is nilpotent) fail is returned.
NonNilpotentElement.title: NonNilpotentElement

FindSl2.name: FindSl2
FindSl2.description: This function tries to find a subalgebra S of the Lie algebra L with S isomorphic to sl_2 and such that the nilpotent element x of L is contained in S. If such an algebra exists then it is returned, otherwise fail is returned.
FindSl2.title: FindSl2

UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra.name: UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra
UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra.description:
Returns the universal enveloping algebra of the Lie algebra L. The elements of this algebra are written on a Poincare-Birkhoff-Witt basis.

If a second argument B is given, it must be a basis of L, and an isomorphic copy of the universal enveloping algebra is returned, generated by the images (in the universal enveloping algebra) of the elements of B.
UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra.title: UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra

FpLieAlgebraByCartanMatrix.name: FpLieAlgebraByCartanMatrix
FpLieAlgebraByCartanMatrix.description: Here C must be a Cartan matrix. The function returns the finitely-presented Lie algebra over the field of rational numbers defined by this Cartan matrix. By Serre's theorem, this Lie algebra is a semisimple Lie algebra, and its root system has Cartan matrix C.
FpLieAlgebraByCartanMatrix.title: FpLieAlgebraByCartanMatrix

NilpotentQuotientOfFpLieAlgebra.name: NilpotentQuotientOfFpLieAlgebra
NilpotentQuotientOfFpLieAlgebra.description:
Here FpL is a finitely presented Lie algebra. Let K be the quotient of FpL by the max+1-th term of its lower central series. This function calculates a surjective homomorphism from FpL onto K. When called with the third argument weights, the k-th generator of FpL gets assigned the k-th element of the list weights. In that case a quotient is calculated of FpL by the ideal generated by all elements of weight max+1. If the list weights only consists of 1's then the two calls are equivalent. The default value of weights is a list (of length equal to the number of generators of FpL) consisting of 1's.

If the relators of FpL are homogeneous, then the resulting algebra is naturally graded.
NilpotentQuotientOfFpLieAlgebra.title: NilpotentQuotientOfFpLieAlgebra

IsCochain.name: IsCochain
IsCochain.description: Categories of cochains and of collections of cochains.
IsCochain.title: IsCochain

IsCochainCollection.name: IsCochainCollection
IsCochainCollection.description: Categories of cochains and of collections of cochains.
IsCochainCollection.title: IsCochain

Cochain.name: Cochain
Cochain.description: Constructs a s-cochain given by the data in obj, with respect to the Lie algebra module V. If s is non-zero, then obj must be a list.
Cochain.title: Cochain

CochainSpace.name: CochainSpace
CochainSpace.description: Returns the space of all s-cochains with respect to V.
CochainSpace.title: CochainSpace

ValueCochain.name: ValueCochain
ValueCochain.description: Here c is an s-cochain. This function returns the value of c when applied to the s elements y1 to ys (that lie in the Lie algebra acting on the module corresponding to c). It is also possible to call this function with two arguments: first c and then the list containing y1,...,ys.
ValueCochain.title: ValueCochain

LieCoboundaryOperator.name: LieCoboundaryOperator
LieCoboundaryOperator.description: This is a function that takes an s-cochain c, and returns an s+1-cochain. The coboundary operator is applied.
LieCoboundaryOperator.title: LieCoboundaryOperator

Cocycles.name: Cocycles
Cocycles.description: is the space of all s-cocycles with respect to the Lie algebra module V. That is the kernel of the coboundary operator when restricted to the space of s-cochains.
Cocycles.title: Cocycles

Coboundaries.name: Coboundaries
Coboundaries.description: is the space of all s-coboundaries with respect to the Lie algebra module V. That is the image of the coboundary operator, when applied to the space of s-1-cochains. By definition the space of all 0-coboundaries is zero.
Coboundaries.title: Coboundaries

DominantWeights.name: DominantWeights
DominantWeights.description: Returns a list consisting of two lists. The first of these contains the dominant weights (written on the basis of fundamental weights) of the irreducible highest-weight module, with highest weight maxw, over the Lie algebra with the root system R. The i-th element of the second list is the level of the i-th dominant weight. (Where the level is defined as follows. For a weight μ we write μ = λ - ∑_i k_i α_i, where the α_i are the simple roots, and λ the highest weight. Then the level of μ is ∑_i k_i.)
DominantWeights.title: DominantWeights

DominantCharacter.name: DominantCharacter
DominantCharacter.description:
For a highest weight maxw and a semisimple Lie algebra L, this returns the dominant weights of the highest-weight module over L, with highest weight maxw. The output is a list of two lists, the first list contains the dominant weights; the second list contains their multiplicities.

The first argument can also be a root system, in which case the dominant character of the highest-weight module over the corresponding semisimple Lie algebra is returned.
DominantCharacter.title: DominantCharacter

DominantCharacter.name: DominantCharacter
DominantCharacter.description:
For a highest weight maxw and a semisimple Lie algebra L, this returns the dominant weights of the highest-weight module over L, with highest weight maxw. The output is a list of two lists, the first list contains the dominant weights; the second list contains their multiplicities.

The first argument can also be a root system, in which case the dominant character of the highest-weight module over the corresponding semisimple Lie algebra is returned.
DominantCharacter.title: DominantCharacter

DecomposeTensorProduct.name: DecomposeTensorProduct
DecomposeTensorProduct.description: Here L is a semisimple Lie algebra and w1, w2 are dominant weights. Let V_i be the irreducible highest-weight module over L with highest weight w_i for i = 1, 2. Let W = V_1 ⊗ V_2. Then in general W is a reducible L-module. Now this function returns a list of two lists. The first of these is the sorted list of highest weights of the irreducible modules occurring in the decomposition of W as a direct sum of irreducible modules. The second list contains the multiplicities of these weights (i.e., the number of copies of the irreducible module with the corresponding highest weight that occur in W). The algorithm uses Klimyk's formula (see [Kli68] or [Kli66] for the original Russian version).
DecomposeTensorProduct.title: DecomposeTensorProduct

DimensionOfHighestWeightModule.name: DimensionOfHighestWeightModule
DimensionOfHighestWeightModule.description: Here L is a semisimple Lie algebra, and w a dominant weight. This function returns the dimension of the highest-weight module over L with highest weight w. The algorithm uses Weyl's dimension formula.
DimensionOfHighestWeightModule.title: DimensionOfHighestWeightModule

IsUEALatticeElement.name: IsUEALatticeElement
IsUEALatticeElement.description: is the category of elements of an admissible lattice in the universal enveloping algebra of a semisimple Lie algebra L.
IsUEALatticeElement.title: IsUEALatticeElement

IsUEALatticeElementCollection.name: IsUEALatticeElementCollection
IsUEALatticeElementCollection.description: is the category of elements of an admissible lattice in the universal enveloping algebra of a semisimple Lie algebra L.
IsUEALatticeElementCollection.title: IsUEALatticeElement

IsUEALatticeElementFamily.name: IsUEALatticeElementFamily
IsUEALatticeElementFamily.description: is the category of elements of an admissible lattice in the universal enveloping algebra of a semisimple Lie algebra L.
IsUEALatticeElementFamily.title: IsUEALatticeElement

LatticeGeneratorsInUEA.name: LatticeGeneratorsInUEA
LatticeGeneratorsInUEA.description:
Here L must be a semisimple Lie algebra of characteristic 0. This function returns a list of generators of an admissible lattice in the universal enveloping algebra of L, relative to the Chevalley basis contained in ChevalleyBasis( L ) (see ChevalleyBasis (64.6-2)). First are listed the negative root vectors (denoted by y_1, ..., y_s), then the positive root vectors (denoted by x_1, ..., x_s). At the end of the list there are the Cartan elements. They are printed as ( hi/1 ), which means

In general the printed form ( hi/ k ) means

Also y_i^(m) is printed as yi^(m), which means that entering yi^m at the GAP prompt results in the output m!*yi^(m).
LatticeGeneratorsInUEA.title: LatticeGeneratorsInUEA

ObjByExtRep.name: ObjByExtRep
ObjByExtRep.description:
An UEALattice element is represented by a list of the form [ m1, c1, m2, c2, ... ], where the c1, c2 etc. are coefficients, and the m1, m2 etc. are monomials. A monomial is a list of the form [ ind1, e1, ind2, e2, ... ] where ind1, ind2 are indices, and e1, e2 etc. are exponents. Let N be the number of positive roots of the underlying Lie algebra L. The indices lie between 1 and dim(L). If an index lies between 1 and N, then it represents a negative root vector (corresponding to the root NegativeRoots( R )[ind], where R is the root system of L; see NegativeRoots (64.6-8)). This leads to a factor yind1^(e1) in the printed form of the monomial (which equals z^e1/e1!, where z is a basis element of L). If an index lies between N+1 and 2N, then it represents a positive root vector. Finally, if ind lies between 2N+1 and 2N+rank, then it represents an element of the Cartan subalgebra. This is printed as ( h_1/ e_1 ), meaning h_1 choose e_1, where h_1, ..., h_rank are the canonical Cartan generators.

The zero element is represented by the empty list, the identity element by the list [ [], 1 ].
ObjByExtRep.title: ObjByExtRep

ObjByExtRep.name: ObjByExtRep
ObjByExtRep.description:
ExtRepOfObj returns the external representation of its argument, and ObjByExtRep returns an object in the family fam that has external representation data.

Of course, ObjByExtRep( FamilyObj( obj ), ExtRepOfObj( obj ) ) must be equal to obj w.r.t. the operation \= (31.11-1). But it is not required that equal objects have equal external representations.

Note that if one defines a new representation of objects for which an external representation does already exist then one must install a method to compute this external representation for the objects in the new representation.
ObjByExtRep.title: ExtRepOfObj

IsWeightRepElement.name: IsWeightRepElement
IsWeightRepElement.description:
Is a category of vectors, that is used to construct elements of highest-weight modules (by HighestWeightModule (64.14-5)).

WeightRepElements are represented by a list of the form [ v1, c1, v2, c2, ....], where the vi are basis vectors, and the ci are coefficients. Furthermore a basis vector v is a weight vector. It is represented by a list of the form [ k, mon, wt ], where k is an integer (the basis vectors are numbered from 1 to dim V, where V is the highest weight module), mon is an UEALatticeElement (which means that the result of applying mon to a highest weight vector is v; see IsUEALatticeElement (64.14-1)) and wt is the weight of v. A WeightRepElement is printed as mon*v0, where v0 denotes a fixed highest weight vector.

If v is a WeightRepElement, then ExtRepOfObj( v ) returns the corresponding list, and if list is such a list and fam a WeightRepElementFamily, then ObjByExtRep( list, fam ) returns the corresponding WeightRepElement.
IsWeightRepElement.title: IsWeightRepElement

IsWeightRepElementCollection.name: IsWeightRepElementCollection
IsWeightRepElementCollection.description:
Is a category of vectors, that is used to construct elements of highest-weight modules (by HighestWeightModule (64.14-5)).

WeightRepElements are represented by a list of the form [ v1, c1, v2, c2, ....], where the vi are basis vectors, and the ci are coefficients. Furthermore a basis vector v is a weight vector. It is represented by a list of the form [ k, mon, wt ], where k is an integer (the basis vectors are numbered from 1 to dim V, where V is the highest weight module), mon is an UEALatticeElement (which means that the result of applying mon to a highest weight vector is v; see IsUEALatticeElement (64.14-1)) and wt is the weight of v. A WeightRepElement is printed as mon*v0, where v0 denotes a fixed highest weight vector.

If v is a WeightRepElement, then ExtRepOfObj( v ) returns the corresponding list, and if list is such a list and fam a WeightRepElementFamily, then ObjByExtRep( list, fam ) returns the corresponding WeightRepElement.
IsWeightRepElementCollection.title: IsWeightRepElement

IsWeightRepElementFamily.name: IsWeightRepElementFamily
IsWeightRepElementFamily.description:
Is a category of vectors, that is used to construct elements of highest-weight modules (by HighestWeightModule (64.14-5)).

WeightRepElements are represented by a list of the form [ v1, c1, v2, c2, ....], where the vi are basis vectors, and the ci are coefficients. Furthermore a basis vector v is a weight vector. It is represented by a list of the form [ k, mon, wt ], where k is an integer (the basis vectors are numbered from 1 to dim V, where V is the highest weight module), mon is an UEALatticeElement (which means that the result of applying mon to a highest weight vector is v; see IsUEALatticeElement (64.14-1)) and wt is the weight of v. A WeightRepElement is printed as mon*v0, where v0 denotes a fixed highest weight vector.

If v is a WeightRepElement, then ExtRepOfObj( v ) returns the corresponding list, and if list is such a list and fam a WeightRepElementFamily, then ObjByExtRep( list, fam ) returns the corresponding WeightRepElement.
IsWeightRepElementFamily.title: IsWeightRepElement

HighestWeightModule.name: HighestWeightModule
HighestWeightModule.description:
returns the highest weight module with highest weight wt of the semisimple Lie algebra L of characteristic 0.

Note that the elements of such a module lie in the category IsLeftAlgebraModuleElement (62.11-9) (and in particular they do not lie in the category IsWeightRepElement (64.14-4)). However, if v is an element of such a module, then ExtRepOfObj( v ) is a WeightRepElement.

Note that for the following examples of this chapter we increase the line length limit from its default value 80 to 81 in order to make some long output expressions fit into the lines.
HighestWeightModule.title: HighestWeightModule

TensorProductOfAlgebraModules.name: TensorProductOfAlgebraModules
TensorProductOfAlgebraModules.description: Here the elements of list must be algebra modules. The tensor product is returned as an algebra module. The two-argument version works in the same way and returns the tensor product of its arguments.
TensorProductOfAlgebraModules.title: TensorProductOfAlgebraModules

TensorProductOfAlgebraModules.name: TensorProductOfAlgebraModules
TensorProductOfAlgebraModules.description: Here the elements of list must be algebra modules. The tensor product is returned as an algebra module. The two-argument version works in the same way and returns the tensor product of its arguments.
TensorProductOfAlgebraModules.title: TensorProductOfAlgebraModules

ExteriorPowerOfAlgebraModule.name: ExteriorPowerOfAlgebraModule
ExteriorPowerOfAlgebraModule.description: Here V must be an algebra module, defined over a Lie algebra. This function returns the k-th exterior power of V as an algebra module.
ExteriorPowerOfAlgebraModule.title: ExteriorPowerOfAlgebraModule

SymmetricPowerOfAlgebraModule.name: SymmetricPowerOfAlgebraModule
SymmetricPowerOfAlgebraModule.description: Here V must be an algebra module. This function returns the k-th symmetric power of V (as an algebra module).
SymmetricPowerOfAlgebraModule.title: SymmetricPowerOfAlgebraModule

FreeMagmaRing.name: FreeMagmaRing
FreeMagmaRing.description: is a free magma ring over the ring R, free on the magma M.
FreeMagmaRing.title: FreeMagmaRing

GroupRing.name: GroupRing
GroupRing.description: is the group ring of the group G, over the ring R.
GroupRing.title: GroupRing

IsFreeMagmaRing.name: IsFreeMagmaRing
IsFreeMagmaRing.description:
A domain lies in the category IsFreeMagmaRing if it has been constructed as a free magma ring. In particular, if D lies in this category then the operations LeftActingDomain (57.1-11) and UnderlyingMagma (65.1-6) are applicable to D, and yield the ring R and the magma M such that D is the magma ring RM.

So being a magma ring in GAP includes the knowledge of the ring and the magma. Note that a magma ring RM may abstractly be generated as a magma ring by a magma different from the underlying magma M. For example, the group ring of the dihedral group of order 8 over the field with 3 elements is also spanned by a quaternion group of order 8 over the same field.
IsFreeMagmaRing.title: IsFreeMagmaRing

IsFreeMagmaRingWithOne.name: IsFreeMagmaRingWithOne
IsFreeMagmaRingWithOne.description: IsFreeMagmaRingWithOne is just a synonym for the meet of IsFreeMagmaRing (65.1-3) and IsMagmaWithOne (35.1-2).
IsFreeMagmaRingWithOne.title: IsFreeMagmaRingWithOne

IsGroupRing.name: IsGroupRing
IsGroupRing.description: A group ring is a magma ring where the underlying magma is a group.
IsGroupRing.title: IsGroupRing

UnderlyingMagma.name: UnderlyingMagma
UnderlyingMagma.description: stores the underlying magma of a free magma ring.
UnderlyingMagma.title: UnderlyingMagma

AugmentationIdeal.name: AugmentationIdeal
AugmentationIdeal.description: is the augmentation ideal of the group ring RG, i.e., the kernel of the trivial representation of RG.
AugmentationIdeal.title: AugmentationIdeal

IsMagmaRingObjDefaultRep.name: IsMagmaRingObjDefaultRep
IsMagmaRingObjDefaultRep.description:
The default representation of a magma ring element is a list of length 2, at first position the zero coefficient, at second position a list with the coefficients at the even positions, and the magma elements at the odd positions, with the ordering as defined for the magma elements.

It is assumed that arithmetic operations on magma rings produce only normalized elements.
IsMagmaRingObjDefaultRep.title: IsMagmaRingObjDefaultRep

IsElementOfFreeMagmaRing.name: IsElementOfFreeMagmaRing
IsElementOfFreeMagmaRing.description: The category of elements of a free magma ring (See IsFreeMagmaRing (65.1-3)).
IsElementOfFreeMagmaRing.title: IsElementOfFreeMagmaRing

IsElementOfFreeMagmaRingCollection.name: IsElementOfFreeMagmaRingCollection
IsElementOfFreeMagmaRingCollection.description: The category of elements of a free magma ring (See IsFreeMagmaRing (65.1-3)).
IsElementOfFreeMagmaRingCollection.title: IsElementOfFreeMagmaRing

IsElementOfFreeMagmaRingFamily.name: IsElementOfFreeMagmaRingFamily
IsElementOfFreeMagmaRingFamily.description: Elements of families in this category have trivial normalisation, i.e., efficient methods for \= and \<.
IsElementOfFreeMagmaRingFamily.title: IsElementOfFreeMagmaRingFamily

CoefficientsAndMagmaElements.name: CoefficientsAndMagmaElements
CoefficientsAndMagmaElements.description: is a list that contains at the odd positions the magma elements, and at the even positions their coefficients in the element elm.
CoefficientsAndMagmaElements.title: CoefficientsAndMagmaElements

ZeroCoefficient.name: ZeroCoefficient
ZeroCoefficient.description: For an element elm of a magma ring (modulo relations) RM, ZeroCoefficient returns the zero element of the coefficient ring R.
ZeroCoefficient.title: ZeroCoefficient

ElementOfMagmaRing.name: ElementOfMagmaRing
ElementOfMagmaRing.description: ElementOfMagmaRing returns the element ∑_{i = 1}^n c_i m_i', where coeffs = [ c_1, c_2, ..., c_n ] is a list of coefficients, mgmelms = [ m_1, m_2, ..., m_n ] is a list of magma elements, and m_i' is the image of m_i under an embedding of a magma containing m_i into a magma ring whose elements lie in the family Fam. zerocoeff must be the zero of the coefficient ring containing the c_i.
ElementOfMagmaRing.title: ElementOfMagmaRing

IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations.name: IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations
IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations.description: This category is used, e. g., for elements of free Lie algebras.
IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations.title: IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations

IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelationsColle.name: IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelationsCollection
IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelationsColle.description: This category is used, e. g., for elements of free Lie algebras.
IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelationsColle.title: IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations

IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelationsFamil.name: IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelationsFamily
IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelationsFamil.description: The family category for the category IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations (65.4-1).
IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelationsFamil.title: IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelationsFamily

NormalizedElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelati.name: NormalizedElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations
NormalizedElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelati.description:
Let F be a family of magma ring elements modulo relations, and descr the description of an element in a magma ring modulo relations. NormalizedElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations returns a description of the same element, but normalized w.r.t. the relations. So two elements are equal if and only if the result of NormalizedElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations is equal for their internal data, that is, CoefficientsAndMagmaElements (65.2-4) will return the same for the corresponding two elements.

NormalizedElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations is allowed to return descr itself, it need not make a copy. This is the case for example in the case of free magma rings.
NormalizedElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelati.title: NormalizedElementOfMagmaRingModuloRelations

IsMagmaRingModuloRelations.name: IsMagmaRingModuloRelations
IsMagmaRingModuloRelations.description:
A GAP object lies in the category IsMagmaRingModuloRelations if it has been constructed as a magma ring modulo relations. Each element of such a ring has a unique normal form, so CoefficientsAndMagmaElements (65.2-4) is well-defined for it.

This category is not inherited to factor structures, which are in general best described as finitely presented algebras, see Chapter 63.
IsMagmaRingModuloRelations.title: IsMagmaRingModuloRelations

IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloSpanOfZeroFami.name: IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloSpanOfZeroFamily
IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloSpanOfZeroFami.description: We need this for the normalization method, which takes a family as first argument.
IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloSpanOfZeroFami.title: IsElementOfMagmaRingModuloSpanOfZeroFamily

IsMagmaRingModuloSpanOfZero.name: IsMagmaRingModuloSpanOfZero
IsMagmaRingModuloSpanOfZero.description: The category of magma rings modulo the span of a zero element.
IsMagmaRingModuloSpanOfZero.title: IsMagmaRingModuloSpanOfZero

MagmaRingModuloSpanOfZero.name: MagmaRingModuloSpanOfZero
MagmaRingModuloSpanOfZero.description:
Let R be a ring, M a magma, and z an element of M with the property that z * m = z holds for all m ∈ M. The element z could be called a zero element of M, but note that in general z cannot be obtained as Zero( m ) for each m ∈ M, so this situation does not match the definition of Zero (31.10-3).

MagmaRingModuloSpanOfZero returns the magma ring RM modulo the relation given by the identification of z with zero. This is an example of a magma ring modulo relations, see 65.4.
MagmaRingModuloSpanOfZero.title: MagmaRingModuloSpanOfZero

Indeterminate.name: Indeterminate
Indeterminate.description:
returns the indeterminate number nr over the ring R. If nr is not given it defaults to 1. If the number is not specified a list avoid of indeterminates may be given. The function will return an indeterminate that is guaranteed to be different from all the indeterminates in the list avoid. The third usage returns an indeterminate called name (also avoiding the indeterminates in avoid if given).

X is simply a synonym for Indeterminate.
Indeterminate.title: Indeterminate

Indeterminate.name: Indeterminate
Indeterminate.description:
returns the indeterminate number nr over the ring R. If nr is not given it defaults to 1. If the number is not specified a list avoid of indeterminates may be given. The function will return an indeterminate that is guaranteed to be different from all the indeterminates in the list avoid. The third usage returns an indeterminate called name (also avoiding the indeterminates in avoid if given).

X is simply a synonym for Indeterminate.
Indeterminate.title: Indeterminate

Indeterminate.name: Indeterminate
Indeterminate.description:
returns the indeterminate number nr over the ring R. If nr is not given it defaults to 1. If the number is not specified a list avoid of indeterminates may be given. The function will return an indeterminate that is guaranteed to be different from all the indeterminates in the list avoid. The third usage returns an indeterminate called name (also avoiding the indeterminates in avoid if given).

X is simply a synonym for Indeterminate.
Indeterminate.title: Indeterminate

X.name: X
X.description:
returns the indeterminate number nr over the ring R. If nr is not given it defaults to 1. If the number is not specified a list avoid of indeterminates may be given. The function will return an indeterminate that is guaranteed to be different from all the indeterminates in the list avoid. The third usage returns an indeterminate called name (also avoiding the indeterminates in avoid if given).

X is simply a synonym for Indeterminate.
X.title: Indeterminate

The function will return an indeterminate that is guaranteed to be different from all the indeterminates in the list avoid. The third usage returns an indeterminate called name (also avoiding the indeterminates in avoid if given).

X is simply a synonym for Indeterminate.
X.title: Indeterminate

X.name: X
X.description:
returns the indeterminate number nr over the ring R. If nr is not given it defaults to 1. If the number is not specified a list avoid of indeterminates may be given. The function will return an indeterminate that is guaranteed to be different from all the indeterminates in the list avoid. The third usage returns an indeterminate called name (also avoiding the indeterminates in avoid if given).

X is simply a synonym for Indeterminate.
X.title: Indeterminate

IndeterminateNumberOfUnivariateRationalF.name: IndeterminateNumberOfUnivariateRationalFunction
IndeterminateNumberOfUnivariateRationalF.description:
returns the number of the indeterminate in which the univariate rational function rfun is expressed. (This also provides a way to obtain the number of a given indeterminate.)

A constant rational function might not possess an indeterminate number. In this case IndeterminateNumberOfUnivariateRationalFunction will default to a value of 1. Therefore two univariate polynomials may be considered to be in the same univariate polynomial ring if their indeterminates have the same number or one if of them is constant. (see also CIUnivPols (66.1-5) and IsLaurentPolynomialDefaultRep (66.21-7)).
IndeterminateNumberOfUnivariateRationalF.title: IndeterminateNumberOfUnivariateRationalFunction

IndeterminateOfUnivariateRationalFunctio.name: IndeterminateOfUnivariateRationalFunction
IndeterminateOfUnivariateRationalFunctio.description: returns the indeterminate in which the univariate rational function rfun is expressed. (cf. IndeterminateNumberOfUnivariateRationalFunction (66.1-2).)
IndeterminateOfUnivariateRationalFunctio.title: IndeterminateOfUnivariateRationalFunction

IndeterminateName.name: IndeterminateName
IndeterminateName.description:
SetIndeterminateName assigns the name name to indeterminate nr in the rational functions family fam. It issues an error if the indeterminate was already named.

IndeterminateName returns the name of the nr-th indeterminate (and returns fail if no name has been assigned).

HasIndeterminateName tests whether indeterminate nr has already been assigned a name.
IndeterminateName.title: IndeterminateName

HasIndeterminateName.name: HasIndeterminateName
HasIndeterminateName.description:
SetIndeterminateName assigns the name name to indeterminate nr in the rational functions family fam. It issues an error if the indeterminate was already named.

IndeterminateName returns the name of the nr-th indeterminate (and returns fail if no name has been assigned).

HasIndeterminateName tests whether indeterminate nr has already been assigned a name.
HasIndeterminateName.title: IndeterminateName

SetIndeterminateName.name: SetIndeterminateName
SetIndeterminateName.description:
SetIndeterminateName assigns the name name to indeterminate nr in the rational functions family fam. It issues an error if the indeterminate was already named.

IndeterminateName returns the name of the nr-th indeterminate (and returns fail if no name has been assigned).

HasIndeterminateName tests whether indeterminate nr has already been assigned a name.
SetIndeterminateName.title: IndeterminateName

CIUnivPols.name: CIUnivPols
CIUnivPols.description: This function (whose name stands for common indeterminate of univariate polynomials) takes two univariate polynomials as arguments. If both polynomials are given in the same indeterminate number indnum (in this case they are compatible as univariate polynomials) it returns indnum. In all other cases it returns fail. CIUnivPols also accepts if either polynomial is constant but formally expressed in another indeterminate, in this situation the indeterminate of the other polynomial is selected.
CIUnivPols.title: CIUnivPols

IsPolynomialFunction.name: IsPolynomialFunction
IsPolynomialFunction.description:
A rational function is an element of the quotient field of a polynomial ring over an UFD. It is represented as a quotient of two polynomials, its numerator (see NumeratorOfRationalFunction (66.4-2)) and its denominator (see DenominatorOfRationalFunction (66.4-3))

A polynomial function is an element of a polynomial ring (not necessarily an UFD), or a rational function.

GAP considers IsRationalFunction as a subcategory of IsPolynomialFunction.
IsPolynomialFunction.title: IsPolynomialFunction

IsRationalFunction.name: IsRationalFunction
IsRationalFunction.description:
A rational function is an element of the quotient field of a polynomial ring over an UFD. It is represented as a quotient of two polynomials, its numerator (see NumeratorOfRationalFunction (66.4-2)) and its denominator (see DenominatorOfRationalFunction (66.4-3))

A polynomial function is an element of a polynomial ring (not necessarily an UFD), or a rational function.

GAP considers IsRationalFunction as a subcategory of IsPolynomialFunction.
IsRationalFunction.title: IsPolynomialFunction

NumeratorOfRationalFunction.name: NumeratorOfRationalFunction
NumeratorOfRationalFunction.description:
returns the numerator of the rational function ratfun.

As no proper multivariate gcd has been implemented yet, numerators and denominators are not guaranteed to be reduced!
NumeratorOfRationalFunction.title: NumeratorOfRationalFunction

DenominatorOfRationalFunction.name: DenominatorOfRationalFunction
DenominatorOfRationalFunction.description:
returns the denominator of the rational function ratfun.

As no proper multivariate gcd has been implemented yet, numerators and denominators are not guaranteed to be reduced!
DenominatorOfRationalFunction.title: DenominatorOfRationalFunction

IsPolynomial.name: IsPolynomial
IsPolynomial.description:
A polynomial is a rational function whose denominator is one. (If the coefficients family forms a field this is equivalent to the denominator being constant.)

If the base family is not a field, it may be impossible to represent the quotient of a polynomial by a ring element as a polynomial again, but it will have to be represented as a rational function.
IsPolynomial.title: IsPolynomial

AsPolynomial.name: AsPolynomial
AsPolynomial.description: If poly is a rational function that is a polynomial this attribute returns an equal rational function p such that p is equal to its numerator and the denominator of p is one.
AsPolynomial.title: AsPolynomial

IsUnivariateRationalFunction.name: IsUnivariateRationalFunction
IsUnivariateRationalFunction.description: A rational function is univariate if its numerator and its denominator are both polynomials in the same one indeterminate. The attribute IndeterminateNumberOfUnivariateRationalFunction (66.1-2) can be used to obtain the number of this common indeterminate.
IsUnivariateRationalFunction.title: IsUnivariateRationalFunction

CoefficientsOfUnivariateRationalFunction.name: CoefficientsOfUnivariateRationalFunction
CoefficientsOfUnivariateRationalFunction.description: if rfun is a univariate rational function, this attribute returns a list [ ncof, dcof, val ] where ncof and dcof are coefficient lists of univariate polynomials n and d and a valuation val such that rfun = x^val ⋅ n / d where x is the variable with the number given by IndeterminateNumberOfUnivariateRationalFunction (66.1-2). Numerator and denominator are guaranteed to be cancelled.
CoefficientsOfUnivariateRationalFunction.title: CoefficientsOfUnivariateRationalFunction

IsUnivariatePolynomial.name: IsUnivariatePolynomial
IsUnivariatePolynomial.description: A univariate polynomial is a polynomial in only one indeterminate.
IsUnivariatePolynomial.title: IsUnivariatePolynomial

CoefficientsOfUnivariatePolynomial.name: CoefficientsOfUnivariatePolynomial
CoefficientsOfUnivariatePolynomial.description: CoefficientsOfUnivariatePolynomial returns the coefficient list of the polynomial pol, sorted in ascending order. (It returns the empty list if pol is 0.)
CoefficientsOfUnivariatePolynomial.title: CoefficientsOfUnivariatePolynomial

IsLaurentPolynomial.name: IsLaurentPolynomial
IsLaurentPolynomial.description:
A Laurent polynomial is a univariate rational function whose denominator is a monomial. Therefore every univariate polynomial is a Laurent polynomial.

The attribute CoefficientsOfLaurentPolynomial (66.13-2) gives a compact representation as Laurent polynomial.
IsLaurentPolynomial.title: IsLaurentPolynomial

IsConstantRationalFunction.name: IsConstantRationalFunction
IsConstantRationalFunction.description: A constant rational function is a function whose numerator and denominator are polynomials of degree 0.
IsConstantRationalFunction.title: IsConstantRationalFunction

IsPrimitivePolynomial.name: IsPrimitivePolynomial
IsPrimitivePolynomial.title: IsPrimitivePolynomial

SplittingField.name: SplittingField
SplittingField.description: returns the smallest field which contains the coefficients of f and the roots of f.
SplittingField.title: SplittingField

UnivariatePolynomial.name: UnivariatePolynomial
UnivariatePolynomial.description: constructs an univariate polynomial over the ring ring in the indeterminate ind with the coefficients given by cofs.
UnivariatePolynomial.title: UnivariatePolynomial

UnivariatePolynomialByCoefficients.name: UnivariatePolynomialByCoefficients
UnivariatePolynomialByCoefficients.description: constructs an univariate polynomial over the coefficients family fam and in the indeterminate ind with the coefficients given by cofs. This function should be used in algorithms to create polynomials as it avoids overhead associated with UnivariatePolynomial (66.5-1).
UnivariatePolynomialByCoefficients.title: UnivariatePolynomialByCoefficients

DegreeOfLaurentPolynomial.name: DegreeOfLaurentPolynomial
DegreeOfLaurentPolynomial.description: The degree of a univariate (Laurent) polynomial pol is the largest exponent n of a monomial x^n of pol. The degree of a zero polynomial is defined to be -infinity.
DegreeOfLaurentPolynomial.title: DegreeOfLaurentPolynomial

RootsOfPolynomial.name: RootsOfPolynomial
RootsOfPolynomial.description: For a univariate polynomial p, this function returns all roots of p over the ring R. If the ring is not specified, it defaults to the ring specified by the coefficients of p via DefaultRing (56.1-3)).
RootsOfPolynomial.title: RootsOfPolynomial

RootsOfUPol.name: RootsOfUPol
RootsOfUPol.description: This function returns a list of all roots of the univariate polynomial upol in its default domain. If the optional argument field is a field then the roots in this field are computed. If field is the string "split" then the splitting field of the polynomial is taken.
RootsOfUPol.title: RootsOfUPol

QuotRemLaurpols.name: QuotRemLaurpols
QuotRemLaurpols.description:
This internal function for euclidean division of polynomials takes two polynomials left and right and computes their quotient. No test is performed whether the arguments indeed are polynomials. Depending on the integer variable mode, which may take values in a range from 1 to 4, it returns respectively:

the quotient (there might be some remainder),

the remainder,
QuotRemLaurpols.title: QuotRemLaurpols

UnivariatenessTestRationalFunction.name: UnivariatenessTestRationalFunction
UnivariatenessTestRationalFunction.description:
takes a rational function f and tests whether it is univariate rational function (or even a Laurent polynomial). It returns a list [isunivariate, indet, islaurent, cofs].

If f is a univariate rational function then isunivariate is true and indet is the number of the appropriate indeterminate.

Furthermore, if f is a Laurent polynomial, then islaurent is also true. In this case the fourth entry, cofs, is the value of the attribute CoefficientsOfLaurentPolynomial (66.13-2) for f.
UnivariatenessTestRationalFunction.title: UnivariatenessTestRationalFunction

InfoPoly.name: InfoPoly
InfoPoly.description:
is the info class for univariate polynomials.

We remark that some functions for multivariate polynomials (which will be defined in the following sections) permit a different syntax for univariate polynomials which drops the requirement to specify the indeterminate. Examples are Value (66.7-1), Discriminant (66.6-6), Derivative (66.6-5), LeadingCoefficient (66.6-3) and LeadingMonomial (66.6-4):
InfoPoly.title: InfoPoly

DegreeIndeterminate.name: DegreeIndeterminate
DegreeIndeterminate.description: returns the degree of the polynomial pol in the indeterminate (or indeterminate number) ind.
DegreeIndeterminate.title: DegreeIndeterminate

PolynomialCoefficientsOfPolynomial.name: PolynomialCoefficientsOfPolynomial
PolynomialCoefficientsOfPolynomial.description: PolynomialCoefficientsOfPolynomial returns the coefficient list (whose entries are polynomials not involving the indeterminate ind) describing the polynomial pol viewed as a polynomial in ind. Instead of the indeterminate, ind can also be an indeterminate number.
PolynomialCoefficientsOfPolynomial.title: PolynomialCoefficientsOfPolynomial

LeadingCoefficient.name: LeadingCoefficient
LeadingCoefficient.description: returns the leading coefficient (that is the coefficient of the leading monomial, see LeadingMonomial (66.6-4)) of the polynomial pol.
LeadingCoefficient.title: LeadingCoefficient

LeadingMonomial.name: LeadingMonomial
LeadingMonomial.description: returns the leading monomial (with respect to the ordering given by MonomialExtGrlexLess (66.17-14)) of the polynomial pol as a list containing indeterminate numbers and exponents.
LeadingMonomial.title: LeadingMonomial

Derivative.name: Derivative
Derivative.description: If ratfun is a univariate rational function then Derivative returns the derivative of ufun by its indeterminate. For a rational function ratfun, the derivative by the indeterminate ind is returned, regarding ratfun as univariate in ind. Instead of the desired indeterminate, also the number of this indeterminate can be given as ind.
Derivative.title: Derivative

Discriminant.name: Discriminant
Discriminant.description: If pol is a univariate polynomial then Discriminant returns the discriminant of pol by its indeterminate. The two-argument form returns the discriminant of a polynomial pol by the indeterminate number ind, regarding pol as univariate in this indeterminate. Instead of the indeterminate number, the indeterminate itself can also be given as ind.
Discriminant.title: Discriminant

Resultant.name: Resultant
Resultant.description: computes the resultant of the polynomials pol1 and pol2 with respect to the indeterminate ind, or indeterminate number ind. The resultant considers pol1 and pol2 as univariate in ind and returns an element of the corresponding base ring (which might be a polynomial ring).
Resultant.title: Resultant

Value.name: Value
Value.description:
The first variant takes a rational function ratfun and specializes the indeterminates given in indets to the values given in vals, replacing the i-th entry in indets by the i-th entry in vals. If this specialization results in a constant polynomial, an element of the coefficient ring is returned. If the specialization would specialize the denominator of ratfun to zero, an error is raised.

A variation is the evaluation at elements of another ring R, for which a multiplication with elements of the coefficient ring of ratfun are defined. In this situation the identity element of R may be given by a further argument one which will be used for x^0 for any specialized indeterminate x.

The second version takes an univariate rational function and specializes the value of its indeterminate to val. Again, an optional argument one may be given. Value( upol, val ) can also be expressed as upol( val ).
Value.title: Value

Value.name: Value
Value.description:
The first variant takes a rational function ratfun and specializes the indeterminates given in indets to the values given in vals, replacing the i-th entry in indets by the i-th entry in vals. If this specialization results in a constant polynomial, an element of the coefficient ring is returned. If the specialization would specialize the denominator of ratfun to zero, an error is raised.

A variation is the evaluation at elements of another ring R, for which a multiplication with elements of the coefficient ring of ratfun are defined. In this situation the identity element of R may be given by a further argument one which will be used for x^0 for any specialized indeterminate x.

The second version takes an univariate rational function and specializes the value of its indeterminate to val. Again, an optional argument one may be given. Value( upol, val ) can also be expressed as upol( val ).
Value.title: Value

CyclotomicPolynomial.name: CyclotomicPolynomial
CyclotomicPolynomial.description: is the n-th cyclotomic polynomial over the ring F.
CyclotomicPolynomial.title: CyclotomicPolynomial

FactorsSquarefree.name: FactorsSquarefree
FactorsSquarefree.description:
returns a factorization of the squarefree, monic, univariate polynomial upol in the polynomial ring pring; opt must be a (possibly empty) record of options. upol must not have zero as a root. This function is used by the factoring algorithms.

The current method for multivariate factorization reduces to univariate factorization by use of a reduction homomorphism of the form f(x_1,x_2,x_3) ↦ f(x,x^p,x^{p^2}). It can be very time intensive for larger degrees.
FactorsSquarefree.title: FactorsSquarefree

PrimitivePolynomial.name: PrimitivePolynomial
PrimitivePolynomial.description: takes a polynomial f with rational coefficients and computes a new polynomial with integral coefficients, obtained by multiplying with the Lcm of the denominators of the coefficients and casting out the content (the Gcd of the coefficients). The operation returns a list [newpol,coeff] with rational coeff such that coeff*newpol=f.
PrimitivePolynomial.title: PrimitivePolynomial

PolynomialModP.name: PolynomialModP
PolynomialModP.description: for a rational polynomial pol this function returns a polynomial over the field with p elements, obtained by reducing the coefficients modulo p.
PolynomialModP.title: PolynomialModP

GaloisType.name: GaloisType
GaloisType.description:
Let f be an irreducible polynomial with rational coefficients. This function returns the type of Gal(f) (considered as a transitive permutation group of the roots of f). It returns a number i if Gal(f) is permutation isomorphic to TransitiveGroup(n,i) where n is the degree of f.

Identification is performed by factoring appropriate Galois resolvents as proposed in [SM85]. This function is provided for rational polynomials of degree up to 15. However, in some cases the required calculations become unfeasibly large.

For a few polynomials of degree 14, a complete discrimination is not yet possible, as it would require computations, that are not feasible with current factoring methods.
GaloisType.title: GaloisType

ProbabilityShapes.name: ProbabilityShapes
ProbabilityShapes.description:
Let f be an irreducible polynomial with rational coefficients. This function returns a list of the most likely type(s) of Gal(f) (see GaloisType (66.11-3)), based on factorization modulo a set of primes. It is very fast, but the result is only probabilistic.

This function requires the transitive groups library to be installed (see transgrp: Transitive Permutation Groups).
ProbabilityShapes.title: ProbabilityShapes

BombieriNorm.name: BombieriNorm
BombieriNorm.description: computes weighted Norm [pol]_2 of pol which is a good measure for factor coefficients (see [BTW93]).
BombieriNorm.title: BombieriNorm

MinimizedBombieriNorm.name: MinimizedBombieriNorm
MinimizedBombieriNorm.description: This function applies linear Tschirnhaus transformations (x ↦ x + i) to the polynomial f, trying to get the Bombieri norm of f small. It returns a list [new_polynomial, i_of_transformation].
MinimizedBombieriNorm.title: MinimizedBombieriNorm

HenselBound.name: HenselBound
HenselBound.description: returns the Hensel bound of the polynomial pol. If the computation takes place over an algebraic extension, then the minimal polynomial minpol and denominator den must be given.
HenselBound.title: HenselBound

OneFactorBound.name: OneFactorBound
OneFactorBound.description: returns the coefficient bound for a single factor of the rational polynomial pol.
OneFactorBound.title: OneFactorBound

LaurentPolynomialByCoefficients.name: LaurentPolynomialByCoefficients
LaurentPolynomialByCoefficients.description: constructs a Laurent polynomial over the coefficients family fam and in the indeterminate ind (defaulting to 1) with the coefficients given by cofs and valuation val.
LaurentPolynomialByCoefficients.title: LaurentPolynomialByCoefficients

CoefficientsOfLaurentPolynomial.name: CoefficientsOfLaurentPolynomial
CoefficientsOfLaurentPolynomial.description: For a Laurent polynomial laurent, this function returns a pair [cof, val], consisting of the coefficient list (in ascending order) cof and the valuation val of laurent.
CoefficientsOfLaurentPolynomial.title: CoefficientsOfLaurentPolynomial

UnivariateRationalFunctionByCoefficients.name: UnivariateRationalFunctionByCoefficients
UnivariateRationalFunctionByCoefficients.description: constructs a univariate rational function over the coefficients family fam and in the indeterminate ind (defaulting to 1) with numerator and denominator coefficients given by ncof and dcof and valuation val.
UnivariateRationalFunctionByCoefficients.title: UnivariateRationalFunctionByCoefficients

TaylorSeriesRationalFunction.name: TaylorSeriesRationalFunction
TaylorSeriesRationalFunction.description: Computes the taylor series up to degree deg of ratfun at at.
TaylorSeriesRationalFunction.title: TaylorSeriesRationalFunction

PolynomialRing.name: PolynomialRing
PolynomialRing.description:
creates a polynomial ring over the ring R. If a positive integer rank is given, this creates the polynomial ring in rank indeterminates. These indeterminates will have the internal index numbers 1 to rank. The second usage takes a list names of strings and returns a polynomial ring in indeterminates labelled by names. These indeterminates have new internal index numbers as if they had been created by calls to Indeterminate (66.1-1). (If the argument avoid is given it contains indeterminates that should be avoided, in this case internal index numbers are incremented to skip these variables.) In the third version, a list of indeterminates indets is given. This creates the polynomial ring in the indeterminates indets. Finally, the fourth version specifies indeterminates by their index numbers.

To get the indeterminates of a polynomial ring use IndeterminatesOfPolynomialRing (66.15-2). (Indeterminates created independently with Indeterminate (66.1-1) will usually differ, though they might be given the same name and display identically, see Section 66.1.)
PolynomialRing.title: PolynomialRing

PolynomialRing.name: PolynomialRing
PolynomialRing.description:

These indeterminates have new internal index numbers as if they had been created by calls to Indeterminate (66.1-1). PolynomialRing.title: PolynomialRing
