PolynomialRing.title: PolynomialRing

IndeterminatesOfPolynomialRing.name: IndeterminatesOfPolynomialRing
IndeterminatesOfPolynomialRing.description: returns a list of the indeterminates of the polynomial ring pring, respectively the function field ffield.
IndeterminatesOfPolynomialRing.title: IndeterminatesOfPolynomialRing

IndeterminatesOfFunctionField.name: IndeterminatesOfFunctionField
IndeterminatesOfFunctionField.description: returns a list of the indeterminates of the polynomial ring pring, respectively the function field ffield.
IndeterminatesOfFunctionField.title: IndeterminatesOfPolynomialRing

CoefficientsRing.name: CoefficientsRing
CoefficientsRing.description: returns the ring of coefficients of the polynomial ring pring, that is the ring over which pring was defined.
CoefficientsRing.title: CoefficientsRing

IsPolynomialRing.name: IsPolynomialRing
IsPolynomialRing.description: is the category of polynomial rings
IsPolynomialRing.title: IsPolynomialRing

IsFiniteFieldPolynomialRing.name: IsFiniteFieldPolynomialRing
IsFiniteFieldPolynomialRing.description: is the category of polynomial rings over a finite field (see Chapter 59).
IsFiniteFieldPolynomialRing.title: IsFiniteFieldPolynomialRing

IsAbelianNumberFieldPolynomialRing.name: IsAbelianNumberFieldPolynomialRing
IsAbelianNumberFieldPolynomialRing.description: is the category of polynomial rings over a field of cyclotomics (see the chapters 18 and 60).
IsAbelianNumberFieldPolynomialRing.title: IsAbelianNumberFieldPolynomialRing

IsRationalsPolynomialRing.name: IsRationalsPolynomialRing
IsRationalsPolynomialRing.description: is the category of polynomial rings over the rationals (see Chapter 17).
IsRationalsPolynomialRing.title: IsRationalsPolynomialRing

FunctionField.name: FunctionField
FunctionField.description:
creates a function field over the integral ring R. If a positive integer rank is given, this creates the function field in rank indeterminates. These indeterminates will have the internal index numbers 1 to rank. The second usage takes a list names of strings and returns a function field in indeterminates labelled by names. These indeterminates have new internal index numbers as if they had been created by calls to Indeterminate (66.1-1). (If the argument avoid is given it contains indeterminates that should be avoided, in this case internal index numbers are incremented to skip these variables.) In the third version, a list of indeterminates indets is given. This creates the function field in the indeterminates indets. Finally, the fourth version specifies indeterminates by their index number.

To get the indeterminates of a function field use IndeterminatesOfFunctionField (66.15-2). (Indeterminates created independently with Indeterminate (66.1-1) will usually differ, though they might be given the same name and display identically, see Section 66.1.)
FunctionField.title: FunctionField

FunctionField.name: FunctionField
FunctionField.description:

These indeterminates will have the internal index numbers 1 to rank. The second usage takes a list names of strings and returns a function field in indeterminates labelled by names. FunctionField.title: FunctionField

IsFunctionField.name: IsFunctionField
IsFunctionField.description: is the category of function fields
IsFunctionField.title: IsFunctionField

UnivariatePolynomialRing.name: UnivariatePolynomialRing
UnivariatePolynomialRing.description:
returns a univariate polynomial ring in the indeterminate nr over the base ring R. If nr is not given it defaults to 1.

If the number is not specified a list avoid of indeterminates may be given. Then the function will return a ring in an indeterminate that is guaranteed to be different from all the indeterminates in avoid.

Also a string name can be prescribed as the name of the indeterminate chosen (also avoiding the indeterminates in the list avoid if given).
UnivariatePolynomialRing.title: UnivariatePolynomialRing

UnivariatePolynomialRing.name: UnivariatePolynomialRing
UnivariatePolynomialRing.description:
returns a univariate polynomial ring in the indeterminate nr over the base ring R. If nr is not given it defaults to 1.

If the number is not specified a list avoid of indeterminates may be given. Then the function will return a ring in an indeterminate that is guaranteed to be different from all the indeterminates in avoid.

Also a string name can be prescribed as the name of the indeterminate chosen (also avoiding the indeterminates in the list avoid if given).
UnivariatePolynomialRing.title: UnivariatePolynomialRing

IsUnivariatePolynomialRing.name: IsUnivariatePolynomialRing
IsUnivariatePolynomialRing.description: is the category of polynomial rings with one indeterminate.
IsUnivariatePolynomialRing.title: IsUnivariatePolynomialRing

IsMonomialOrdering.name: IsMonomialOrdering
IsMonomialOrdering.description: A monomial ordering is an object representing a monomial ordering. Its attributes MonomialComparisonFunction (66.17-5) and MonomialExtrepComparisonFun (66.17-6) are actual comparison functions.
IsMonomialOrdering.title: IsMonomialOrdering

LeadingMonomialOfPolynomial.name: LeadingMonomialOfPolynomial
LeadingMonomialOfPolynomial.description: returns the leading monomial (with respect to the ordering ord) of the polynomial pol.
LeadingMonomialOfPolynomial.title: LeadingMonomialOfPolynomial

LeadingTermOfPolynomial.name: LeadingTermOfPolynomial
LeadingTermOfPolynomial.description: returns the leading term (with respect to the ordering ord) of the polynomial pol, i.e. the product of leading coefficient and leading monomial.
LeadingTermOfPolynomial.title: LeadingTermOfPolynomial

LeadingCoefficientOfPolynomial.name: LeadingCoefficientOfPolynomial
LeadingCoefficientOfPolynomial.description: returns the leading coefficient (that is the coefficient of the leading monomial, see LeadingMonomialOfPolynomial (66.17-2)) of the polynomial pol.
LeadingCoefficientOfPolynomial.title: LeadingCoefficientOfPolynomial

MonomialComparisonFunction.name: MonomialComparisonFunction
MonomialComparisonFunction.description: If O is an object representing a monomial ordering, this attribute returns a function that can be used to compare or sort monomials (and polynomials which will be compared by their monomials in decreasing order) in this order.
MonomialComparisonFunction.title: MonomialComparisonFunction

MonomialExtrepComparisonFun.name: MonomialExtrepComparisonFun
MonomialExtrepComparisonFun.description: If O is an object representing a monomial ordering, this attribute returns a function that can be used to compare or sort monomials in their external representation (as lists). This comparison variant is used inside algorithms that manipulate the external representation.
MonomialExtrepComparisonFun.title: MonomialExtrepComparisonFun

MonomialLexOrdering.name: MonomialLexOrdering
MonomialLexOrdering.description:
This function creates a lexicographic ordering for monomials. Monomials are compared first by the exponents of the largest variable, then the exponents of the second largest variable and so on.

The variables are ordered according to their (internal) index, i.e., x_1 is larger than x_2 and so on. If vari is given, and is a list of variables or variable indices, instead this arrangement of variables (in descending order; i.e. the first variable is larger than the second) is used as the underlying order of variables.
MonomialLexOrdering.title: MonomialLexOrdering

MonomialGrlexOrdering.name: MonomialGrlexOrdering
MonomialGrlexOrdering.description:
This function creates a degree/lexicographic ordering. In this ordering monomials are compared first by their total degree, then lexicographically (see MonomialLexOrdering (66.17-7)).

The variables are ordered according to their (internal) index, i.e., x_1 is larger than x_2 and so on. If vari is given, and is a list of variables or variable indices, instead this arrangement of variables (in descending order; i.e. the first variable is larger than the second) is used as the underlying order of variables.
MonomialGrlexOrdering.title: MonomialGrlexOrdering

MonomialGrevlexOrdering.name: MonomialGrevlexOrdering
MonomialGrevlexOrdering.description:
This function creates a grevlex ordering. In this ordering monomials are compared first by total degree and then backwards lexicographically. (This is different than grlex ordering with variables reversed.)

The variables are ordered according to their (internal) index, i.e., x_1 is larger than x_2 and so on. If vari is given, and is a list of variables or variable indices, instead this arrangement of variables (in descending order; i.e. the first variable is larger than the second) is used as the underlying order of variables.
MonomialGrevlexOrdering.title: MonomialGrevlexOrdering

EliminationOrdering.name: EliminationOrdering
EliminationOrdering.description:
This function creates an elimination ordering for eliminating the variables in elim. Two monomials are compared first by the exponent vectors for the variables listed in elim (a lexicographic comparison with respect to the ordering indicated in elim). If these submonomial are equal, the submonomials given by the other variables are compared by a graded lexicographic ordering (with respect to the variable order given in rest, if called with two parameters).

Both elim and rest may be a list of variables or a list of variable indices.
EliminationOrdering.title: EliminationOrdering

PolynomialReduction.name: PolynomialReduction
PolynomialReduction.description:
reduces the polynomial poly by the ideal generated by the polynomials in gens, using the order order of monomials. Unless gens is a Gröbner basis the result is not guaranteed to be unique.

The operation returns a list of length two, the first entry is the remainder after the reduction. The second entry is a list of quotients corresponding to gens.

Note that the strategy used by PolynomialReduction differs from the standard textbook reduction algorithm, which is provided by PolynomialDivisionAlgorithm (66.17-13).
PolynomialReduction.title: PolynomialReduction

PolynomialReducedRemainder.name: PolynomialReducedRemainder
PolynomialReducedRemainder.description: this operation does the same way as PolynomialReduction (66.17-11) but does not keep track of the actual quotients and returns only the remainder (it is therefore slightly faster).
PolynomialReducedRemainder.title: PolynomialReducedRemainder

PolynomialDivisionAlgorithm.name: PolynomialDivisionAlgorithm
PolynomialDivisionAlgorithm.description:
This function implements the division algorithm for multivariate polynomials as given in [CLO97, Theorem 3 in Chapter 2]. (It might be slower than PolynomialReduction (66.17-11) but the remainders are guaranteed to agree with the textbook.)

The operation returns a list of length two, the first entry is the remainder after the reduction. The second entry is a list of quotients corresponding to gens.
PolynomialDivisionAlgorithm.title: PolynomialDivisionAlgorithm

MonomialExtGrlexLess.name: MonomialExtGrlexLess
MonomialExtGrlexLess.description:
implements comparison of monomial in their external representation by a grlex order with x_1>x_2 (This is exactly the same as the ordering by MonomialGrlexOrdering (66.17-8), see 66.17). The function takes two monomials a and b in expanded form and returns whether the first is smaller than the second. (This ordering is also used by GAP internally for representing polynomials as a linear combination of monomials.)

See section 66.21 for details on the expanded form of monomials.
MonomialExtGrlexLess.title: MonomialExtGrlexLess

GroebnerBasis.name: GroebnerBasis
GroebnerBasis.description:
Let O be a monomial ordering and L be a list of polynomials that generate an ideal I. This operation returns a Groebner basis of I with respect to the ordering O.

GroebnerBasisNC works like GroebnerBasis with the only distinction that the first argument has to be a list of polynomials and that no test is performed to check whether the ordering is defined for all occurring variables.

Note that GAP at the moment only includes a naïve implementation of Buchberger's algorithm (which is mainly intended as a teaching tool). It might not be sufficient for serious problems.
GroebnerBasis.title: GroebnerBasis

GroebnerBasis.name: GroebnerBasis
GroebnerBasis.description:
Let O be a monomial ordering and L be a list of polynomials that generate an ideal I. This operation returns a Groebner basis of I with respect to the ordering O.

GroebnerBasisNC works like GroebnerBasis with the only distinction that the first argument has to be a list of polynomials and that no test is performed to check whether the ordering is defined for all occurring variables.

Note that GAP at the moment only includes a naïve implementation of Buchberger's algorithm (which is mainly intended as a teaching tool). It might not be sufficient for serious problems.
GroebnerBasis.title: GroebnerBasis

GroebnerBasisNC.name: GroebnerBasisNC
GroebnerBasisNC.description:
Let O be a monomial ordering and L be a list of polynomials that generate an ideal I. This operation returns a Groebner basis of I with respect to the ordering O.

GroebnerBasisNC works like GroebnerBasis with the only distinction that the first argument has to be a list of polynomials and that no test is performed to check whether the ordering is defined for all occurring variables.

Note that GAP at the moment only includes a naïve implementation of Buchberger's algorithm (which is mainly intended as a teaching tool). It might not be sufficient for serious problems.
GroebnerBasisNC.title: GroebnerBasis

ReducedGroebnerBasis.name: ReducedGroebnerBasis
ReducedGroebnerBasis.description:
a Groebner basis B (see GroebnerBasis (66.18-1)) is reduced if no monomial in a polynomial in B is divisible by the leading monomial of another polynomial in B. This operation computes a Groebner basis with respect to the monomial ordering O and then reduces it.

For performance reasons it can be advantageous to define monomial orderings once and then to reuse them:
ReducedGroebnerBasis.title: ReducedGroebnerBasis

ReducedGroebnerBasis.name: ReducedGroebnerBasis
ReducedGroebnerBasis.description:
a Groebner basis B (see GroebnerBasis (66.18-1)) is reduced if no monomial in a polynomial in B is divisible by the leading monomial of another polynomial in B. This operation computes a Groebner basis with respect to the monomial ordering O and then reduces it.

For performance reasons it can be advantageous to define monomial orderings once and then to reuse them:
ReducedGroebnerBasis.title: ReducedGroebnerBasis

StoredGroebnerBasis.name: StoredGroebnerBasis
StoredGroebnerBasis.description: For an ideal I in a polynomial ring, this attribute holds a list [ B, O ] where B is a Groebner basis for the monomial ordering O. this can be used to test membership or canonical coset representatives.
StoredGroebnerBasis.title: StoredGroebnerBasis

InfoGroebner.name: InfoGroebner
InfoGroebner.description: This info class gives information about Groebner basis calculations.
InfoGroebner.title: InfoGroebner

RationalFunctionsFamily.name: RationalFunctionsFamily
RationalFunctionsFamily.description: creates a family containing rational functions with coefficients in fam. All elements of the RationalFunctionsFamily are rational functions (see IsRationalFunction (66.4-1)).
RationalFunctionsFamily.title: RationalFunctionsFamily

IsPolynomialFunctionsFamily.name: IsPolynomialFunctionsFamily
IsPolynomialFunctionsFamily.description: IsPolynomialFunctionsFamily is the category of a family of polynomials. For families over an UFD, the category becomes IsRationalFunctionsFamily (as rational functions and quotients are only provided for families over an UFD.)
IsPolynomialFunctionsFamily.title: IsPolynomialFunctionsFamily

IsRationalFunctionsFamily.name: IsRationalFunctionsFamily
IsRationalFunctionsFamily.description: IsPolynomialFunctionsFamily is the category of a family of polynomials. For families over an UFD, the category becomes IsRationalFunctionsFamily (as rational functions and quotients are only provided for families over an UFD.)
IsRationalFunctionsFamily.title: IsPolynomialFunctionsFamily

CoefficientsFamily.name: CoefficientsFamily
CoefficientsFamily.description:
If rffam has been created as RationalFunctionsFamily(cfam) this attribute holds the coefficients family cfam.

GAP does not embed the base ring in the polynomial ring. While multiplication and addition of base ring elements to rational functions return the expected results, polynomials and rational functions are not equal.
CoefficientsFamily.title: CoefficientsFamily

IsRationalFunctionDefaultRep.name: IsRationalFunctionDefaultRep
IsRationalFunctionDefaultRep.description: is the default representation of rational functions. A rational function in this representation is defined by the attributes ExtRepNumeratorRatFun (66.21-2) and ExtRepDenominatorRatFun (66.21-3), the values of which are external representations of polynomials.
IsRationalFunctionDefaultRep.title: IsRationalFunctionDefaultRep

ExtRepNumeratorRatFun.name: ExtRepNumeratorRatFun
ExtRepNumeratorRatFun.description: returns the external representation of the numerator polynomial of the rational function ratfun. Numerator and denominator are not guaranteed to be cancelled against each other.
ExtRepNumeratorRatFun.title: ExtRepNumeratorRatFun

ExtRepDenominatorRatFun.name: ExtRepDenominatorRatFun
ExtRepDenominatorRatFun.description: returns the external representation of the denominator polynomial of the rational function ratfun. Numerator and denominator are not guaranteed to be cancelled against each other.
ExtRepDenominatorRatFun.title: ExtRepDenominatorRatFun

ZeroCoefficientRatFun.name: ZeroCoefficientRatFun
ZeroCoefficientRatFun.description: returns the zero of the coefficient ring. This might be needed to represent the zero polynomial for which the external representation of the numerator is the empty list.
ZeroCoefficientRatFun.title: ZeroCoefficientRatFun

IsPolynomialDefaultRep.name: IsPolynomialDefaultRep
IsPolynomialDefaultRep.description: is the default representation of polynomials. A polynomial in this representation is defined by the components and ExtRepNumeratorRatFun (66.21-2) where ExtRepNumeratorRatFun (66.21-2) is the external representation of the polynomial.
IsPolynomialDefaultRep.title: IsPolynomialDefaultRep

ExtRepPolynomialRatFun.name: ExtRepPolynomialRatFun
ExtRepPolynomialRatFun.description: returns the external representation of a polynomial. The difference to ExtRepNumeratorRatFun (66.21-2) is that rational functions might know to be a polynomial but can still have a non-vanishing denominator. In this case ExtRepPolynomialRatFun has to call a quotient routine.
ExtRepPolynomialRatFun.title: ExtRepPolynomialRatFun

IsLaurentPolynomialDefaultRep.name: IsLaurentPolynomialDefaultRep
IsLaurentPolynomialDefaultRep.description: This representation is used for Laurent polynomials and univariate polynomials. It represents a Laurent polynomial via the attributes CoefficientsOfLaurentPolynomial (66.13-2) and IndeterminateNumberOfLaurentPolynomial (66.13-3).
IsLaurentPolynomialDefaultRep.title: IsLaurentPolynomialDefaultRep

RationalFunctionByExtRep.name: RationalFunctionByExtRep
RationalFunctionByExtRep.description:
constructs a rational function (in the representation IsRationalFunctionDefaultRep (66.21-1)) in the rational function family rfam, the rational function itself is given by the external representations num and den for numerator and denominator. No cancellation takes place.

The variant RationalFunctionByExtRepNC does not perform any test of the arguments and thus potentially can create illegal objects. It only should be used if speed is required and the arguments are known to be in correct form.
RationalFunctionByExtRep.title: RationalFunctionByExtRep

RationalFunctionByExtRepNC.name: RationalFunctionByExtRepNC
RationalFunctionByExtRepNC.description:
constructs a rational function (in the representation IsRationalFunctionDefaultRep (66.21-1)) in the rational function family rfam, the rational function itself is given by the external representations num and den for numerator and denominator. No cancellation takes place.

The variant RationalFunctionByExtRepNC does not perform any test of the arguments and thus potentially can create illegal objects. It only should be used if speed is required and the arguments are known to be in correct form.
RationalFunctionByExtRepNC.title: RationalFunctionByExtRep

PolynomialByExtRep.name: PolynomialByExtRep
PolynomialByExtRep.description:
constructs a polynomial (in the representation IsPolynomialDefaultRep (66.21-5)) in the rational function family rfam, the polynomial itself is given by the external representation extrep.

The variant PolynomialByExtRepNC does not perform any test of the arguments and thus potentially can create invalid objects. It only should be used if speed is required and the arguments are known to be in correct form.
PolynomialByExtRep.title: PolynomialByExtRep

PolynomialByExtRepNC.name: PolynomialByExtRepNC
PolynomialByExtRepNC.description:
constructs a polynomial (in the representation IsPolynomialDefaultRep (66.21-5)) in the rational function family rfam, the polynomial itself is given by the external representation extrep.

The variant PolynomialByExtRepNC does not perform any test of the arguments and thus potentially can create invalid objects. It only should be used if speed is required and the arguments are known to be in correct form.
PolynomialByExtRepNC.title: PolynomialByExtRep

LaurentPolynomialByExtRep.name: LaurentPolynomialByExtRep
LaurentPolynomialByExtRep.description: creates a Laurent polynomial in the family fam with [cofs,val] as value of CoefficientsOfLaurentPolynomial (66.13-2). No coefficient shifting is performed. This is the lowest level function to create a Laurent polynomial but will rely on the coefficients being shifted properly and will not perform any tests. Unless this is guaranteed for the parameters, LaurentPolynomialByCoefficients (66.13-1) should be used.
LaurentPolynomialByExtRep.title: LaurentPolynomialByExtRep

LaurentPolynomialByExtRepNC.name: LaurentPolynomialByExtRepNC
LaurentPolynomialByExtRepNC.description: creates a Laurent polynomial in the family fam with [cofs,val] as value of CoefficientsOfLaurentPolynomial (66.13-2). No coefficient shifting is performed. This is the lowest level function to create a Laurent polynomial but will rely on the coefficients being shifted properly and will not perform any tests. Unless this is guaranteed for the parameters, LaurentPolynomialByCoefficients (66.13-1) should be used.
LaurentPolynomialByExtRepNC.title: LaurentPolynomialByExtRep

ZippedSum.name: ZippedSum
ZippedSum.description:
computes the sum of two external representations of polynomials z1 and z2. czero is the appropriate coefficient zero and funcs a list [ monomial_less, coefficient_sum ] containing a monomial comparison and a coefficient addition function. This list can be found in the component fam!.zippedSum of the rational functions family.

Note that coefficient_sum must be a proper summation function, not a function computing differences.
ZippedSum.title: ZippedSum

ZippedProduct.name: ZippedProduct
ZippedProduct.description: computes the product of two external representations of polynomials z1 and z2. czero is the appropriate coefficient zero and funcs a list [ monomial_prod, monomial_less, coefficient_sum, coefficient_prod] containing functions to multiply and compare monomials, to add and to multiply coefficients. This list can be found in the component fam!.zippedProduct of the rational functions family.
ZippedProduct.title: ZippedProduct

QuotientPolynomialsExtRep.name: QuotientPolynomialsExtRep
QuotientPolynomialsExtRep.description: Let a and b the external representations of two polynomials in the rational functions family fam. This function computes the external representation of the quotient of both polynomials, it returns fail if the polynomial described by b does not divide the polynomial described by a.
QuotientPolynomialsExtRep.title: QuotientPolynomialsExtRep

RationalFunctionByExtRepWithCancellation.name: RationalFunctionByExtRepWithCancellation
RationalFunctionByExtRepWithCancellation.description: constructs a rational function as RationalFunctionByExtRep (66.22-1) does but tries to cancel out common factors of numerator and denominator, calling TryGcdCancelExtRepPolynomials (66.24-2).
RationalFunctionByExtRepWithCancellation.title: RationalFunctionByExtRepWithCancellation

TryGcdCancelExtRepPolynomials.name: TryGcdCancelExtRepPolynomials
TryGcdCancelExtRepPolynomials.description: Let a and b be the external representations of two polynomials. This function tries to cancel common factors between the corresponding polynomials and returns a list [ a', b' ] of external representations of cancelled polynomials. As there is no proper multivariate GCD cancellation is not guaranteed to be optimal.
TryGcdCancelExtRepPolynomials.title: TryGcdCancelExtRepPolynomials

HeuristicCancelPolynomialsExtRep.name: HeuristicCancelPolynomialsExtRep
HeuristicCancelPolynomialsExtRep.description: is called by TryGcdCancelExtRepPolynomials (66.24-2) to perform the actual work. It will return either fail or a new list of external representations of cancelled polynomials. The cancellation performed is not necessarily optimal.
HeuristicCancelPolynomialsExtRep.title: HeuristicCancelPolynomialsExtRep

AlgebraicExtension.name: AlgebraicExtension
AlgebraicExtension.description:
constructs an extension L of the field K by one root of the irreducible polynomial f, using Kronecker's construction. L is a field whose LeftActingDomain (57.1-11) value is K. The polynomial f is the DefiningPolynomial (58.2-7) value of L and the attribute RootOfDefiningPolynomial (58.2-8) of L holds a root of f in L. By default this root is printed as a, this string can be overwritten with the optional argument nam.

The first version of the command checks that the polynomial f is an irreducible polynomial over K. This check is skipped with the NC variant.
AlgebraicExtension.title: AlgebraicExtension

AlgebraicExtensionNC.name: AlgebraicExtensionNC
AlgebraicExtensionNC.description:
constructs an extension L of the field K by one root of the irreducible polynomial f, using Kronecker's construction. L is a field whose LeftActingDomain (57.1-11) value is K. The polynomial f is the DefiningPolynomial (58.2-7) value of L and the attribute RootOfDefiningPolynomial (58.2-8) of L holds a root of f in L. By default this root is printed as a, this string can be overwritten with the optional argument nam.

The first version of the command checks that the polynomial f is an irreducible polynomial over K. This check is skipped with the NC variant.
AlgebraicExtensionNC.title: AlgebraicExtension

IsAlgebraicExtension.name: IsAlgebraicExtension
IsAlgebraicExtension.description: is the category of algebraic extensions of fields.
IsAlgebraicExtension.title: IsAlgebraicExtension

IsAlgebraicElement.name: IsAlgebraicElement
IsAlgebraicElement.description: is the category for elements of an algebraic extension.
IsAlgebraicElement.title: IsAlgebraicElement

IdealDecompositionsOfPolynomial.name: IdealDecompositionsOfPolynomial
IdealDecompositionsOfPolynomial.description:
Let f be a univariate, rational, irreducible, polynomial. A pair g,h of polynomials of degree strictly smaller than that of f, such that f(x)|g(h(x)) is called an ideal decomposition. In the context of field extensions, if α is a root of f in a suitable extension and Q the field of rational numbers. Such decompositions correspond to (proper) subfields Q < Q(β) < Q(α), where g is the minimal polynomial of β. This function determines such decompositions up to equality of the subfields Q(β), thus determining subfields of a given algebraic extension. It returns a list of pairs [g,h] (and an empty list if no such decomposition exists). If the option onlyone is given it returns at most one such decomposition (and performs faster).

In this example the given polynomial is regular with Galois group Q_8, as expected we get four proper subfields.
IdealDecompositionsOfPolynomial.title: IdealDecompositionsOfPolynomial

PurePadicNumberFamily.name: PurePadicNumberFamily
PurePadicNumberFamily.description: returns the family of pure p-adic numbers over the prime p with precision digits. That is to say, the approximate value will differ from the correct value by a multiple of p^digits.
PurePadicNumberFamily.title: PurePadicNumberFamily

PadicNumber.name: PadicNumber
PadicNumber.description:
returns the element of the p-adic number family fam that approximates the rational number rat.

p-adic numbers allow the usual operations for fields.

See PadicNumber (68.2-2) for other methods for PadicNumber.
PadicNumber.title: PadicNumber

PadicNumber.name: PadicNumber
PadicNumber.description:
(see also PadicNumber (68.1-2)).

PadicNumber creates a p-adic number in the p-adic numbers family fam. The first form returns the p-adic number corresponding to the rational rat.

The second form takes a pure p-adic numbers family purefam and a list list of length two, and returns the number p^list[1] * list[2]. It must be guaranteed that no entry of list[2] is divisible by the prime p. (Otherwise precision will get lost.)
PadicNumber.title: PadicNumber

PadicNumber.name: PadicNumber
PadicNumber.description:
(see also PadicNumber (68.1-2)).

PadicNumber creates a p-adic number in the p-adic numbers family fam. The first form returns the p-adic number corresponding to the rational rat.

The second form takes a pure p-adic numbers family purefam and a list list of length two, and returns the number p^list[1] * list[2]. It must be guaranteed that no entry of list[2] is divisible by the prime p. (Otherwise precision will get lost.)
PadicNumber.title: PadicNumber

PadicNumber.name: PadicNumber
PadicNumber.description:
(see also PadicNumber (68.1-2)).

PadicNumber creates a p-adic number in the p-adic numbers family fam. The first form returns the p-adic number corresponding to the rational rat.

The second form takes a pure p-adic numbers family purefam and a list list of length two, and returns the number p^list[1] * list[2]. It must be guaranteed that no entry of list[2] is divisible by the prime p. (Otherwise precision will get lost.)
PadicNumber.title: PadicNumber

Valuation.name: Valuation
Valuation.description: The valuation is the p-part of the p-adic number. See also PValuation (15.7-1).
Valuation.title: Valuation

ShiftedPadicNumber.name: ShiftedPadicNumber
ShiftedPadicNumber.description: ShiftedPadicNumber takes a p-adic number padic and an integer shift and returns the p-adic number c, that is padic * p^shift.
ShiftedPadicNumber.title: ShiftedPadicNumber

IsPurePadicNumber.name: IsPurePadicNumber
IsPurePadicNumber.description: The category of pure p-adic numbers.
IsPurePadicNumber.title: IsPurePadicNumber

IsPurePadicNumberFamily.name: IsPurePadicNumberFamily
IsPurePadicNumberFamily.description: The family of pure p-adic numbers.
IsPurePadicNumberFamily.title: IsPurePadicNumberFamily

PadicExtensionNumberFamily.name: PadicExtensionNumberFamily
PadicExtensionNumberFamily.description:
An extended p-adic field L is given by two polynomials h and g with coefficient lists unram (for the unramified part) and ram (for the ramified part). Then L is isomorphic to Q_p[x,y]/(h(x),g(y)).

This function takes the prime number p and the two coefficient lists unram and ram for the two polynomials. The polynomial given by the coefficients in unram must be a cyclotomic polynomial and the polynomial given by ram must be either an Eisenstein polynomial or 1+x. This is not checked by GAP.

Every number in L is represented as a coefficient list w. r. t. the basis { 1, x, x^2, ..., y, xy, x^2 y, ... } of L. The integer precision is the number of digits that all the coefficients have.
PadicExtensionNumberFamily.title: PadicExtensionNumberFamily

IsPadicExtensionNumber.name: IsPadicExtensionNumber
IsPadicExtensionNumber.description: The category of elements of the extended p-adic field.
IsPadicExtensionNumber.title: IsPadicExtensionNumber

IsPadicExtensionNumberFamily.name: IsPadicExtensionNumberFamily
IsPadicExtensionNumberFamily.description: Family of elements of the extended p-adic field.
IsPadicExtensionNumberFamily.title: IsPadicExtensionNumberFamily

GModuleByMats.name: GModuleByMats
GModuleByMats.description:
creates a MeatAxe module over field from a list of invertible matrices gens which reflect a group's action. If the list of generators is empty, the dimension must be given as second argument.

MeatAxe routines are on a level with Gaussian elimination. Therefore they do not deal with GAP modules but essentially with lists of matrices. For the MeatAxe, a module is a record with components

A list of matrices which represent a group operation on a finite dimensional row vector space.
GModuleByMats.title: GModuleByMats

GModuleByMats.name: GModuleByMats
GModuleByMats.description:
creates a MeatAxe module over field from a list of invertible matrices gens which reflect a group's action. If the list of generators is empty, the dimension must be given as second argument.

MeatAxe routines are on a level with Gaussian elimination. Therefore they do not deal with GAP modules but essentially with lists of matrices. For the MeatAxe, a module is a record with components

A list of matrices which represent a group operation on a finite dimensional row vector space.
GModuleByMats.title: GModuleByMats

NaturalGModule.name: NaturalGModule
NaturalGModule.description: creates a MeatAxe module over field from the generators of the matrix group group. If field is not provided then the value returned by DefaultFieldOfMatrixGroup (44.2-2) is used instead.
NaturalGModule.title: NaturalGModule

PermutationGModule.name: PermutationGModule
PermutationGModule.description: Called with a permutation group G and a field F (F may be infinite), PermutationGModule returns the natural permutation module M over F for the group of permutation matrices that acts on the canonical basis of M in the same way as G acts on the points up to its largest moved point (see LargestMovedPoint (42.3-2)).
PermutationGModule.title: PermutationGModule

TrivialGModule.name: TrivialGModule
TrivialGModule.description: Called with a group G and a field F (F may be infinite), TrivialGModule returns the trivial module over F.
TrivialGModule.title: TrivialGModule

TensorProductGModule.name: TensorProductGModule
TensorProductGModule.description: TensorProductGModule calculates the tensor product of the modules m1 and m2. They are assumed to be modules over the same algebra so, in particular, they should have the same number of generators.
TensorProductGModule.title: TensorProductGModule

WedgeGModule.name: WedgeGModule
WedgeGModule.description: WedgeGModule calculates the wedge product of a G-module. That is the action on antisymmetric tensors.
WedgeGModule.title: WedgeGModule

MTX.name: MTX
MTX.description: All MeatAxe routines are accessed via the global variable MTX, which is a record whose components hold the various functions. It is possible to have several implementations of a MeatAxe available. Each MeatAxe represents its routines in an own global variable and assigning MTX to this variable selects the corresponding MeatAxe.
MTX.title: MTX

MTX.Generators.name: MTX.Generators
MTX.Generators.description: returns a list of matrix generators of module.
MTX.Generators.title: MTX.Generators

MTX.Dimension.name: MTX.Dimension
MTX.Dimension.description: returns the dimension in which the matrices act.
MTX.Dimension.title: MTX.Dimension

MTX.Field.name: MTX.Field
MTX.Field.description: returns the field over which module is defined.
MTX.Field.title: MTX.Field

MTX.IsIrreducible.name: MTX.IsIrreducible
MTX.IsIrreducible.description: tests whether the module module is irreducible (i.e. contains no proper submodules.)
MTX.IsIrreducible.title: MTX.IsIrreducible

MTX.IsAbsolutelyIrreducible.name: MTX.IsAbsolutelyIrreducible
MTX.IsAbsolutelyIrreducible.description: A module is absolutely irreducible if it remains irreducible over the algebraic closure of the field. (Formally: If the tensor product L ⊗_K M is irreducible where M is the module defined over K and L is the algebraic closure of K.)
MTX.IsAbsolutelyIrreducible.title: MTX.IsAbsolutelyIrreducible

MTX.DegreeSplittingField.name: MTX.DegreeSplittingField
MTX.DegreeSplittingField.description: returns the degree of the splitting field as extension of the prime field.
MTX.DegreeSplittingField.title: MTX.DegreeSplittingField

MTX.Indecomposition.name: MTX.Indecomposition
MTX.Indecomposition.description: returns a decomposition of module as a direct sum of indecomposable modules. It returns a list, each entry is a list of form [B,ind] where B is a list of basis vectors for the indecomposable component and ind the induced module action on this component. (Such a decomposition is not unique.)
MTX.Indecomposition.title: MTX.Indecomposition

MTX.HomogeneousComponents.name: MTX.HomogeneousComponents
MTX.HomogeneousComponents.description:
computes the homogeneous components of module given as sums of indecomposable components. The function returns a list, each entry of which is a record corresponding to one isomorphism type of indecomposable components. The record has the following components.

the index numbers of the indecomposable components, as given by MTX.Indecomposition (69.6-2), that are in the homogeneous component,

one of the indecomposable components,
MTX.HomogeneousComponents.title: MTX.HomogeneousComponents

MTX.SubmoduleGModule.name: MTX.SubmoduleGModule
MTX.SubmoduleGModule.description: subspace should be a subspace of (or a vector in) the underlying vector space of module i.e. the full row space of the same dimension and over the same field as module. A normalized basis of the submodule of module generated by subspace is returned.
MTX.SubmoduleGModule.title: MTX.SubmoduleGModule

MTX.SubGModule.name: MTX.SubGModule
MTX.SubGModule.description: subspace should be a subspace of (or a vector in) the underlying vector space of module i.e. the full row space of the same dimension and over the same field as module. A normalized basis of the submodule of module generated by subspace is returned.
MTX.SubGModule.title: MTX.SubmoduleGModule

MTX.ProperSubmoduleBasis.name: MTX.ProperSubmoduleBasis
MTX.ProperSubmoduleBasis.description: returns the basis of a proper submodule of module and fail if no proper submodule exists.
MTX.ProperSubmoduleBasis.title: MTX.ProperSubmoduleBasis

MTX.BasesSubmodules.name: MTX.BasesSubmodules
MTX.BasesSubmodules.description: returns a list containing a basis for every submodule.
MTX.BasesSubmodules.title: MTX.BasesSubmodules

MTX.BasesMinimalSubmodules.name: MTX.BasesMinimalSubmodules
MTX.BasesMinimalSubmodules.description: returns a list of bases of all minimal submodules.
MTX.BasesMinimalSubmodules.title: MTX.BasesMinimalSubmodules

MTX.BasesMaximalSubmodules.name: MTX.BasesMaximalSubmodules
MTX.BasesMaximalSubmodules.description: returns a list of bases of all maximal submodules.
MTX.BasesMaximalSubmodules.title: MTX.BasesMaximalSubmodules

MTX.BasisRadical.name: MTX.BasisRadical
MTX.BasisRadical.description: returns a basis of the radical of module.
MTX.BasisRadical.title: MTX.BasisRadical

MTX.BasisSocle.name: MTX.BasisSocle
MTX.BasisSocle.description: returns a basis of the socle of module.
MTX.BasisSocle.title: MTX.BasisSocle

MTX.BasesMinimalSupermodules.name: MTX.BasesMinimalSupermodules
MTX.BasesMinimalSupermodules.description: returns a list of bases of all minimal supermodules of the submodule given by the basis sub.
MTX.BasesMinimalSupermodules.title: MTX.BasesMinimalSupermodules

MTX.BasesCompositionSeries.name: MTX.BasesCompositionSeries
MTX.BasesCompositionSeries.description: returns a list of bases of submodules in a composition series in ascending order.
MTX.BasesCompositionSeries.title: MTX.BasesCompositionSeries

MTX.CompositionFactors.name: MTX.CompositionFactors
MTX.CompositionFactors.description: returns a list of composition factors of module in ascending order.
MTX.CompositionFactors.title: MTX.CompositionFactors

MTX.CollectedFactors.name: MTX.CollectedFactors
MTX.CollectedFactors.description: returns a list giving all irreducible composition factors with their frequencies.
MTX.CollectedFactors.title: MTX.CollectedFactors

MTX.NormedBasisAndBaseChange.name: MTX.NormedBasisAndBaseChange
MTX.NormedBasisAndBaseChange.description: returns a list [bas, change ] where bas is a normed basis (i.e. in echelon form with pivots normed to 1) for sub and change is the base change from bas to sub (the basis vectors of bas expressed in coefficients for sub).
MTX.NormedBasisAndBaseChange.title: MTX.NormedBasisAndBaseChange

MTX.InducedActionSubmodule.name: MTX.InducedActionSubmodule
MTX.InducedActionSubmodule.description: creates a new module corresponding to the action of module on the non-trivial submodule sub. In the NB version the basis sub must be normed. (That is it must be in echelon form with pivots normed to 1, see MTX.NormedBasisAndBaseChange (69.8-1).)
MTX.InducedActionSubmodule.title: MTX.InducedActionSubmodule

MTX.InducedActionSubmoduleNB.name: MTX.InducedActionSubmoduleNB
MTX.InducedActionSubmoduleNB.description: creates a new module corresponding to the action of module on the non-trivial submodule sub. In the NB version the basis sub must be normed. (That is it must be in echelon form with pivots normed to 1, see MTX.NormedBasisAndBaseChange (69.8-1).)
MTX.InducedActionSubmoduleNB.title: MTX.InducedActionSubmodule

MTX.InducedActionFactorModule.name: MTX.InducedActionFactorModule
MTX.InducedActionFactorModule.description:
creates a new module corresponding to the action of module on the factor of the proper submodule sub. If compl is given, it has to be a basis of a (vector space-)complement of sub. The action then will correspond to compl.

The basis sub has to be given in normed form. (That is it must be in echelon form with pivots normed to 1, see MTX.NormedBasisAndBaseChange (69.8-1))
MTX.InducedActionFactorModule.title: MTX.InducedActionFactorModule

MTX.InducedActionSubMatrix.name: MTX.InducedActionSubMatrix
MTX.InducedActionSubMatrix.description: work the same way as the above functions for modules, but take as input only a single matrix.
MTX.InducedActionSubMatrix.title: MTX.InducedActionSubMatrix

MTX.InducedActionSubMatrixNB.name: MTX.InducedActionSubMatrixNB
MTX.InducedActionSubMatrixNB.description: work the same way as the above functions for modules, but take as input only a single matrix.
MTX.InducedActionSubMatrixNB.title: MTX.InducedActionSubMatrix

MTX.InducedActionFactorMatrix.name: MTX.InducedActionFactorMatrix
MTX.InducedActionFactorMatrix.description: work the same way as the above functions for modules, but take as input only a single matrix.
MTX.InducedActionFactorMatrix.title: MTX.InducedActionSubMatrix

MTX.InducedAction.name: MTX.InducedAction
MTX.InducedAction.description:
Computes induced actions on submodules or factor modules and also returns the corresponding bases. The action taken is binary encoded in type: 1 stands for subspace action, 2 for factor action, and 4 for action of the full module on a subspace adapted basis. The routine returns the computed results in a list in sequence (sub,quot,both,basis) where basis is a basis for the whole space, extending sub. (Actions which are not computed are omitted, so the returned list may be shorter.) If no type is given, it is assumed to be 7. The basis given in sub must be normed!

All these routines return fail if sub is not a proper subspace.
MTX.InducedAction.title: MTX.InducedAction

MTX.BasisModuleHomomorphisms.name: MTX.BasisModuleHomomorphisms
MTX.BasisModuleHomomorphisms.description: returns a basis of all module homomorphisms from module1 to module2. Homomorphisms are by matrices, whose rows give the images of the standard basis vectors of module1 in the standard basis of module2.
MTX.BasisModuleHomomorphisms.title: MTX.BasisModuleHomomorphisms

MTX.BasisModuleEndomorphisms.name: MTX.BasisModuleEndomorphisms
MTX.BasisModuleEndomorphisms.description: returns a basis of all module homomorphisms from module to module.
MTX.BasisModuleEndomorphisms.title: MTX.BasisModuleEndomorphisms

MTX.IsomorphismModules.name: MTX.IsomorphismModules
MTX.IsomorphismModules.description: If module1 and module2 are isomorphic modules, this function returns an isomorphism from module1 to module2 in form of a matrix. It returns fail if the modules are not isomorphic.
MTX.IsomorphismModules.title: MTX.IsomorphismModules

MTX.ModuleAutomorphisms.name: MTX.ModuleAutomorphisms
MTX.ModuleAutomorphisms.description: returns the module automorphisms of module (the set of all isomorphisms from module to itself) as a matrix group.
MTX.ModuleAutomorphisms.title: MTX.ModuleAutomorphisms

MTX.IsEquivalent.name: MTX.IsEquivalent
MTX.IsEquivalent.description: tests two irreducible modules for equivalence.
MTX.IsEquivalent.title: MTX.IsEquivalent

MTX.IsomorphismIrred.name: MTX.IsomorphismIrred
MTX.IsomorphismIrred.description: returns an isomorphism from module1 to module2 (if one exists), and fail otherwise. It requires that one of the modules is known to be irreducible. It implicitly assumes that the same group is acting, otherwise the results are unpredictable. The isomorphism is given by a matrix M, whose rows give the images of the standard basis vectors of module1 in the standard basis of module2. That is, conjugation of the generators of module2 with M yields the generators of module1.
MTX.IsomorphismIrred.title: MTX.IsomorphismIrred

MTX.Homomorphism.name: MTX.Homomorphism
MTX.Homomorphism.description: mat should be a dim1 × dim2 matrix defining a homomorphism from module1 to module2. This function verifies that mat really does define a module homomorphism, and then returns the corresponding homomorphism between the underlying row spaces of the modules. This can be used for computing kernels, images and pre-images.
MTX.Homomorphism.title: MTX.Homomorphism

MTX.Homomorphisms.name: MTX.Homomorphisms
MTX.Homomorphisms.description: returns a basis of the space of all homomorphisms from the irreducible module module1 to module2.
MTX.Homomorphisms.title: MTX.Homomorphisms

MTX.Distinguish.name: MTX.Distinguish
MTX.Distinguish.description: Let cf be the output of MTX.CollectedFactors (69.7-11). This routine tries to find a group algebra element that has nullity zero on all composition factors except number nr.
MTX.Distinguish.title: MTX.Distinguish

MTX.InvariantBilinearForm.name: MTX.InvariantBilinearForm
MTX.InvariantBilinearForm.description: returns an invariant bilinear form, which may be symmetric or anti-symmetric, of module, or fail if no such form exists.
MTX.InvariantBilinearForm.title: MTX.InvariantBilinearForm

MTX.InvariantSesquilinearForm.name: MTX.InvariantSesquilinearForm
MTX.InvariantSesquilinearForm.description: returns an invariant hermitian (= self-adjoint) sesquilinear form of module, which must be defined over a finite field whose order is a square, or fail if no such form exists.
MTX.InvariantSesquilinearForm.title: MTX.InvariantSesquilinearForm

MTX.InvariantQuadraticForm.name: MTX.InvariantQuadraticForm
MTX.InvariantQuadraticForm.description:
returns either the matrix of an invariant quadratic form of the absolutely irreducible module module, or fail.

If the characteristic of module is odd then fail is returned if there is no nonzero invariant bilinear form, otherwise a matrix of the bilinear form divided by 2 is returned; note that this matrix may be antisymmetric and thus describe the zero quadratic form. If the characteristic of module is 2 then fail is returned if module does not admit a nonzero quadratic form, otherwise a lower triangular matrix describing the form is returned.

An error is signalled if module is not absolutely irreducible.
MTX.InvariantQuadraticForm.title: MTX.InvariantQuadraticForm

MTX.BasisInOrbit.name: MTX.BasisInOrbit
MTX.BasisInOrbit.description: returns a basis of the underlying vector space of module which is contained in an orbit of the action of the generators of module on that space. This is used by MTX.InvariantQuadraticForm (69.11-3) in characteristic 2.
MTX.BasisInOrbit.title: MTX.BasisInOrbit

MTX.OrthogonalSign.name: MTX.OrthogonalSign
MTX.OrthogonalSign.description:
Let module be an absolutely irreducible G-module. If module does not fix a nondegenerate quadratic form see MTX.InvariantQuadraticForm (69.11-3) then fail is returned. Otherwise the sign ϵ ∈ { -1, 0, 1 } is returned such that G embeds into the general orthogonal group GO^ϵ(d, q) w.r.t. the invariant quadratic form, see GeneralOrthogonalGroup (50.2-6). That is, 0 is returned if module has odd dimension, and 1 or -1 is returned if the orthogonal group has plus or minus type, respectively.

An error is signalled if module is not absolutely irreducible.

The SMTX implementation uses an algorithm due to Jon Thackray.
MTX.OrthogonalSign.title: MTX.OrthogonalSign

SMTX.RandomIrreducibleSubGModule.name: SMTX.RandomIrreducibleSubGModule
SMTX.RandomIrreducibleSubGModule.description: returns the module action on a random irreducible submodule.
SMTX.RandomIrreducibleSubGModule.title: SMTX.RandomIrreducibleSubGModule

SMTX.GoodElementGModule.name: SMTX.GoodElementGModule
SMTX.GoodElementGModule.description: finds an element with minimal possible nullspace dimension if module is known to be irreducible.
SMTX.GoodElementGModule.title: SMTX.GoodElementGModule

SMTX.SortHomGModule.name: SMTX.SortHomGModule
SMTX.SortHomGModule.description: Function to sort the output of Homomorphisms.
SMTX.SortHomGModule.title: SMTX.SortHomGModule

SMTX.MinimalSubGModules.name: SMTX.MinimalSubGModules
SMTX.MinimalSubGModules.description: returns (at most max) bases of submodules of module2 which are isomorphic to the irreducible module module1.
SMTX.MinimalSubGModules.title: SMTX.MinimalSubGModules

SMTX.Setter.name: SMTX.Setter
SMTX.Setter.description: returns a setter function for the component smashMeataxe.(string).
SMTX.Setter.title: SMTX.Setter

SMTX.Getter.name: SMTX.Getter
SMTX.Getter.description: returns a getter function for the component smashMeataxe.(string).
SMTX.Getter.title: SMTX.Getter

SMTX.IrreducibilityTest.name: SMTX.IrreducibilityTest
SMTX.IrreducibilityTest.description: Tests for irreducibility and sets a subbasis if reducible. It neither sets an irreducibility flag, nor tests it. Thus the routine also can simply be called to obtain a random submodule.
SMTX.IrreducibilityTest.title: SMTX.IrreducibilityTest

SMTX.AbsoluteIrreducibilityTest.name: SMTX.AbsoluteIrreducibilityTest
SMTX.AbsoluteIrreducibilityTest.description: Tests for absolute irreducibility and sets splitting field degree. It neither sets an absolute irreducibility flag, nor tests it.
SMTX.AbsoluteIrreducibilityTest.title: SMTX.AbsoluteIrreducibilityTest

SMTX.MinimalSubGModule.name: SMTX.MinimalSubGModule
SMTX.MinimalSubGModule.description: returns the basis of a minimal submodule of module containing the indicated composition factor. It assumes Distinguish has been called already.
SMTX.MinimalSubGModule.title: SMTX.MinimalSubGModule

SMTX.MatrixSum.name: SMTX.MatrixSum
SMTX.MatrixSum.description: creates the direct sum of two matrix lists.
SMTX.MatrixSum.title: SMTX.MatrixSum

SMTX.CompleteBasis.name: SMTX.CompleteBasis
SMTX.CompleteBasis.description: extends the partial basis pbasis to a basis of the full space by action of module. It returns whether it succeeded.
SMTX.CompleteBasis.title: SMTX.CompleteBasis

SMTX.AlgEl.name: SMTX.AlgEl
SMTX.AlgEl.description: list [newgens,coefflist] giving an algebra element used for chopping.
SMTX.AlgEl.title: SMTX.AlgEl

SMTX.AlgElNullspaceVec.name: SMTX.AlgElNullspaceVec
SMTX.AlgElNullspaceVec.description: nullspace of the matrix evaluated under this factor.
SMTX.AlgElNullspaceVec.title: SMTX.AlgElNullspaceVec

SMTX.CentMat.name: SMTX.CentMat
SMTX.CentMat.description: matrix centralising all generators which is computed as a byproduct of SMTX.AbsoluteIrreducibilityTest (69.12-8).
SMTX.CentMat.title: SMTX.CentMat

TableOfMarks.name: TableOfMarks
TableOfMarks.description:
In the first form, G must be a finite group, and TableOfMarks constructs the table of marks of G. This computation requires the knowledge of the complete subgroup lattice of G (see LatticeSubgroups (39.20-1)). If the lattice is not yet stored then it will be constructed. This may take a while if G is large. The result has the IsTableOfMarksWithGens (70.10-3) value true.

In the second form, string must be a string, and TableOfMarks gets the table of marks with name string from the GAP library (see 70.13). If no table of marks with this name is contained in the library then fail is returned.

In the third form, matrix must be a matrix or a list of rows describing a lower triangular matrix where the part above the diagonal is omitted. For such an argument matrix, TableOfMarks returns a table of marks object (see 70.2) for which matrix is the matrix of marks. Note that not every matrix (containing only nonnegative integers and having lower triangular shape) describes a table of marks of a group. Necessary conditions are checked with IsInternallyConsistent (70.9-1) (see 70.9), and fail is returned if matrix is proved not to describe a matrix of marks; but if TableOfMarks returns a table of marks object created from a matrix then it may still happen that this object does not describe the table of marks of a group.
TableOfMarks.title: TableOfMarks

TableOfMarks.name: TableOfMarks
TableOfMarks.description:
In the first form, G must be a finite group, and TableOfMarks constructs the table of marks of G. This computation requires the knowledge of the complete subgroup lattice of G (see LatticeSubgroups (39.20-1)). If the lattice is not yet stored then it will be constructed. This may take a while if G is large. The result has the IsTableOfMarksWithGens (70.10-3) value true.

In the second form, string must be a string, and TableOfMarks gets the table of marks with name string from the GAP library (see 70.13). If no table of marks with this name is contained in the library then fail is returned.

In the third form, matrix must be a matrix or a list of rows describing a lower triangular matrix where the part above the diagonal is omitted. For such an argument matrix, TableOfMarks returns a table of marks object (see 70.2) for which matrix is the matrix of marks. Note that not every matrix (containing only nonnegative integers and having lower triangular shape) describes a table of marks of a group. Necessary conditions are checked with IsInternallyConsistent (70.9-1) (see 70.9), and fail is returned if matrix is proved not to describe a matrix of marks; but if TableOfMarks returns a table of marks object created from a matrix then it may still happen that this object does not describe the table of marks of a group.
TableOfMarks.title: TableOfMarks

TableOfMarks.name: TableOfMarks
TableOfMarks.description:
In the first form, G must be a finite group, and TableOfMarks constructs the table of marks of G. This computation requires the knowledge of the complete subgroup lattice of G (see LatticeSubgroups (39.20-1)). If the lattice is not yet stored then it will be constructed. This may take a while if G is large. The result has the IsTableOfMarksWithGens (70.10-3) value true.

In the second form, string must be a string, and TableOfMarks gets the table of marks with name string from the GAP library (see 70.13). If no table of marks with this name is contained in the library then fail is returned.

In the third form, matrix must be a matrix or a list of rows describing a lower triangular matrix where the part above the diagonal is omitted. For such an argument matrix, TableOfMarks returns a table of marks object (see 70.2) for which matrix is the matrix of marks. Note that not every matrix (containing only nonnegative integers and having lower triangular shape) describes a table of marks of a group. Necessary conditions are checked with IsInternallyConsistent (70.9-1) (see 70.9), and fail is returned if matrix is proved not to describe a matrix of marks; but if TableOfMarks returns a table of marks object created from a matrix then it may still happen that this object does not describe the table of marks of a group.
TableOfMarks.title: TableOfMarks

TableOfMarksByLattice.name: TableOfMarksByLattice
TableOfMarksByLattice.description: TableOfMarksByLattice computes the table of marks of the group G from the lattice of subgroups of G. This lattice is computed via LatticeSubgroups (39.20-1) if it is not yet stored in G. The function TableOfMarksByLattice is installed as a method for TableOfMarks (70.3-1) for solvable groups and groups with stored subgroup lattice, and is available as a global variable only in order to provide explicit access to this method.
TableOfMarksByLattice.title: TableOfMarksByLattice

LatticeSubgroupsByTom.name: LatticeSubgroupsByTom
LatticeSubgroupsByTom.description: LatticeSubgroupsByTom computes the lattice of subgroups of G from the table of marks of G, using RepresentativeTom (70.10-4).
LatticeSubgroupsByTom.title: LatticeSubgroupsByTom

SortedTom.name: SortedTom
SortedTom.description:
SortedTom returns a table of marks where the rows and columns of the table of marks tom are reordered according to the permutation perm.

Note that in each table of marks in GAP, the matrix of marks is assumed to have lower triangular shape (see 70.2). If the permutation perm does not have this property then the functions for tables of marks might return wrong results when applied to the output of SortedTom.

The returned table of marks has only those attribute values stored that are known for tom and listed in TableOfMarksComponents (70.6-4).
SortedTom.title: SortedTom

PermutationTom.name: PermutationTom
PermutationTom.description:
For the table of marks tom of the group G stored as UnderlyingGroup (70.7-7) value of tom, PermutationTom is a permutation π such that the i-th conjugacy class of subgroups of G belongs to the i^π-th column and row of marks in tom.

This attribute value is bound only if tom was obtained from another table of marks by permuting with SortedTom (70.5-1), and there is no default method to compute its value.

The attribute is necessary because the original and the sorted table of marks have the same identifier and the same group, and information computed from the group may depend on the ordering of marks, for example the fusion from the ordinary character table of G into tom.
PermutationTom.title: PermutationTom

InfoTom.name: InfoTom
InfoTom.description: is the info class for computations concerning tables of marks.
InfoTom.title: InfoTom

IsTableOfMarks.name: IsTableOfMarks
IsTableOfMarks.description: Each table of marks belongs to this category.
IsTableOfMarks.title: IsTableOfMarks

TableOfMarksFamily.name: TableOfMarksFamily
TableOfMarksFamily.description: Each table of marks belongs to this family.
TableOfMarksFamily.title: TableOfMarksFamily

TableOfMarksComponents.name: TableOfMarksComponents
TableOfMarksComponents.description: The list TableOfMarksComponents is used when a table of marks object is created from a record via ConvertToTableOfMarks (70.6-5). TableOfMarksComponents contains at position 2i-1 a name of an attribute and at position 2i the corresponding attribute getter function.
TableOfMarksComponents.title: TableOfMarksComponents

ConvertToTableOfMarks.name: ConvertToTableOfMarks
ConvertToTableOfMarks.description: ConvertToTableOfMarks converts a record with components from TableOfMarksComponents (70.6-4) into a table of marks object with the corresponding attributes.
ConvertToTableOfMarks.title: ConvertToTableOfMarks

MarksTom.name: MarksTom
MarksTom.description:
The matrix of marks (see 70.1) of the table of marks tom is stored in a compressed form where zeros are omitted, using the attributes MarksTom and SubsTom. If M is the square matrix of marks of tom (see MatTom (70.7-10)) then the SubsTom value of tom is a list that contains at position i the list of all positions of nonzero entries of the i-th row of M, and the MarksTom value of tom is a list that contains at position i the list of the corresponding marks.

MarksTom and SubsTom are defining attributes of tables of marks (see 70.2). There is no default method for computing the SubsTom value, and the default MarksTom method needs the values of NrSubsTom (70.7-2) and OrdersTom (70.7-2).
MarksTom.title: MarksTom

SubsTom.name: SubsTom
SubsTom.description:
The matrix of marks (see 70.1) of the table of marks tom is stored in a compressed form where zeros are omitted, using the attributes MarksTom and SubsTom. If M is the square matrix of marks of tom (see MatTom (70.7-10)) then the SubsTom value of tom is a list that contains at position i the list of all positions of nonzero entries of the i-th row of M, and the MarksTom value of tom is a list that contains at position i the list of the corresponding marks.

MarksTom and SubsTom are defining attributes of tables of marks (see 70.2). There is no default method for computing the SubsTom value, and the default MarksTom method needs the values of NrSubsTom (70.7-2) and OrdersTom (70.7-2).
SubsTom.title: MarksTom

NrSubsTom.name: NrSubsTom
NrSubsTom.description:
Instead of storing the marks (see MarksTom (70.7-1)) of the table of marks tom one can use a matrix which contains at position (i,j) the number of subgroups of conjugacy class j that are contained in one member of the conjugacy class i. These values are stored in the NrSubsTom value in the same way as the marks in the MarksTom (70.7-1) value.

OrdersTom returns a list that contains at position i the order of a representative of the i-th conjugacy class of subgroups of tom.

One can compute the NrSubsTom and OrdersTom values from the MarksTom (70.7-1) value of tom and vice versa.
NrSubsTom.title: NrSubsTom

OrdersTom.name: OrdersTom
OrdersTom.description:
Instead of storing the marks (see MarksTom (70.7-1)) of the table of marks tom one can use a matrix which contains at position (i,j) the number of subgroups of conjugacy class j that are contained in one member of the conjugacy class i. These values are stored in the NrSubsTom value in the same way as the marks in the MarksTom (70.7-1) value.

OrdersTom returns a list that contains at position i the order of a representative of the i-th conjugacy class of subgroups of tom.

One can compute the NrSubsTom and OrdersTom values from the MarksTom (70.7-1) value of tom and vice versa.
OrdersTom.title: NrSubsTom

LengthsTom.name: LengthsTom
LengthsTom.description: For a table of marks tom, LengthsTom returns a list of the lengths of the conjugacy classes of subgroups.
LengthsTom.title: LengthsTom

ClassTypesTom.name: ClassTypesTom
ClassTypesTom.description:
ClassTypesTom distinguishes isomorphism types of the classes of subgroups of the table of marks tom as far as this is possible from the SubsTom (70.7-1) and MarksTom (70.7-1) values of tom.

Two subgroups are clearly not isomorphic if they have different orders. Moreover, isomorphic subgroups must contain the same number of subgroups of each type.

Each type is represented by a positive integer. ClassTypesTom returns the list which contains for each class of subgroups its corresponding type.
ClassTypesTom.title: ClassTypesTom

ClassNamesTom.name: ClassNamesTom
ClassNamesTom.description:
ClassNamesTom constructs generic names for the conjugacy classes of subgroups of the table of marks tom. In general, the generic name of a class of non-cyclic subgroups consists of three parts and has the form "(o)_{t}l", where o indicates the order of the subgroup, t is a number that distinguishes different types of subgroups of the same order, and l is a letter that distinguishes classes of subgroups of the same type and order. The type of a subgroup is determined by the numbers of its subgroups of other types (see ClassTypesTom (70.7-4)). This is slightly weaker than isomorphism.

The letter is omitted if there is only one class of subgroups of that order and type, and the type is omitted if there is only one class of that order. Moreover, the braces {} around the type are omitted if the type number has only one digit.

For classes of cyclic subgroups, the parentheses round the order and the type are omitted. Hence the most general form of their generic names is "o,l". Again, the letter is omitted if there is only one class of cyclic subgroups of that order.
ClassNamesTom.title: ClassNamesTom

FusionsTom.name: FusionsTom
FusionsTom.description:
For a table of marks tom, FusionsTom is a list of fusions into other tables of marks. Each fusion is a list of length two, the first entry being the Identifier (70.7-9)) value of the image table, the second entry being the list of images of the class positions of tom in the image table.

This attribute is mainly used for tables of marks in the GAP library (see 70.13).
FusionsTom.title: FusionsTom

UnderlyingGroup.name: UnderlyingGroup
UnderlyingGroup.description: UnderlyingGroup is used to access an underlying group that is stored on the table of marks tom. There is no default method to compute an underlying group if it is not stored.
UnderlyingGroup.title: UnderlyingGroup

UnderlyingGroup.name: UnderlyingGroup
UnderlyingGroup.description:
For an ordinary character table ordtbl of a finite group, the group can be stored as value of UnderlyingGroup.

Brauer tables do not store the underlying group, they access it via the ordinary table (see OrdinaryCharacterTable (71.8-4)).
UnderlyingGroup.title: UnderlyingGroup

IdempotentsTom.name: IdempotentsTom
IdempotentsTom.description:
IdempotentsTom encodes the idempotents of the integral Burnside ring described by the table of marks tom. The return value is a list l of positive integers such that each row vector describing a primitive idempotent has value 1 at all positions with the same entry in l, and 0 at all other positions.

According to A. Dress [Dre69] (see also [Pfe97]), these idempotents correspond to the classes of perfect subgroups, and each such idempotent is the characteristic function of all those subgroups that arise by cyclic extension from the corresponding perfect subgroup (see CyclicExtensionsTom (70.9-7)).

IdempotentsTomInfo returns a record with components fixpointvectors and primidems, both bound to lists. The i-th entry of the fixpointvectors list is the 0-1-vector describing the i-th primitive idempotent, and the i-th entry of primidems is the decomposition of this idempotent in the rows of tom.
IdempotentsTom.title: IdempotentsTom

IdempotentsTomInfo.name: IdempotentsTomInfo
IdempotentsTomInfo.description:
IdempotentsTom encodes the idempotents of the integral Burnside ring described by the table of marks tom. The return value is a list l of positive integers such that each row vector describing a primitive idempotent has value 1 at all positions with the same entry in l, and 0 at all other positions.

According to A. Dress [Dre69] (see also [Pfe97]), these idempotents correspond to the classes of perfect subgroups, and each such idempotent is the characteristic function of all those subgroups that arise by cyclic extension from the corresponding perfect subgroup (see CyclicExtensionsTom (70.9-7)).

IdempotentsTomInfo returns a record with components fixpointvectors and primidems, both bound to lists. The i-th entry of the fixpointvectors list is the 0-1-vector describing the i-th primitive idempotent, and the i-th entry of primidems is the decomposition of this idempotent in the rows of tom.
IdempotentsTomInfo.title: IdempotentsTom

Identifier.name: Identifier
Identifier.description:
The identifier of a table of marks tom is a string. It is used for printing the table of marks (see 70.4) and in fusions between tables of marks (see FusionsTom (70.7-6)).

If tom is a table of marks from the GAP library of tables of marks (see 70.13) then it has an identifier, and if tom was constructed from a group with Name (12.8-2) then this name is chosen as Identifier value. There is no default method to compute an identifier in all other cases.
Identifier.title: Identifier

Identifier.name: Identifier
Identifier.description:
is a string that identifies the character table tbl in the current GAP session. It is used mainly for class fusions into tbl that are stored on other character tables. For character tables without group, the identifier is also used to print the table; this is the case for library tables, but also for tables that are constructed as direct products, factors etc. involving tables that may or may not store their groups.

The default method for ordinary tables constructs strings of the form "CTn", where n is a positive integer. LARGEST_IDENTIFIER_NUMBER is a list containing the largest integer n used in the current GAP session.

The default method for Brauer tables returns the concatenation of the identifier of the ordinary table, the string "mod", and the (string of the) underlying characteristic.
Identifier.title: Identifier

MatTom.name: MatTom
MatTom.description: MatTom returns the square matrix of marks (see 70.1) of the table of marks tom which is stored in a compressed form using the attributes MarksTom (70.7-1) and SubsTom (70.7-1) This may need substantially more space than the values of MarksTom (70.7-1) and SubsTom (70.7-1).
MatTom.title: MatTom

MoebiusTom.name: MoebiusTom
MoebiusTom.description:
MoebiusTom computes the Möbius values both of the subgroup lattice of the group G with table of marks tom and of the poset of conjugacy classes of subgroups of G. It returns a record where the component mu contains the Möbius values of the subgroup lattice, and the component nu contains the Möbius values of the poset.

Moreover, according to an observation of Isaacs et al. (see [HIÖ89], [Pah93]), the values on the subgroup lattice often can be derived from those of the poset of conjugacy classes. These expected values are returned in the component ex, and the list of numbers of those subgroups where the expected value does not coincide with the actual value are returned in the component hyp. For the computation of these values, the position of the derived subgroup of G is needed (see DerivedSubgroupTom (70.9-2)). If it is not uniquely determined then the result does not have the components ex and hyp.
MoebiusTom.title: MoebiusTom

WeightsTom.name: WeightsTom
WeightsTom.description:
WeightsTom extracts the weights from the table of marks tom, i.e., the diagonal entries of the matrix of marks (see MarksTom (70.7-1)), indicating the index of a subgroup in its normalizer.

This information may be used to obtain the numbers of conjugate supergroups from the marks.
WeightsTom.title: WeightsTom

DerivedSubgroupTom.name: DerivedSubgroupTom
DerivedSubgroupTom.description:
For a table of marks tom and a positive integer sub, DerivedSubgroupTom returns either a positive integer i or a list l of positive integers. In the former case, the result means that the derived subgroups of the subgroups in the sub-th class of tom lie in the i-th class. In the latter case, the class of the derived subgroups could not be uniquely determined, and the position of the class of derived subgroups is an entry of l.

Values computed with DerivedSubgroupTom are stored using the attribute DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible (70.9-3).

DerivedSubgroupsTom is just the list of DerivedSubgroupTom values for all values of sub.
DerivedSubgroupTom.title: DerivedSubgroupTom

DerivedSubgroupsTom.name: DerivedSubgroupsTom
DerivedSubgroupsTom.description:
For a table of marks tom and a positive integer sub, DerivedSubgroupTom returns either a positive integer i or a list l of positive integers. In the former case, the result means that the derived subgroups of the subgroups in the sub-th class of tom lie in the i-th class. In the latter case, the class of the derived subgroups could not be uniquely determined, and the position of the class of derived subgroups is an entry of l.

Values computed with DerivedSubgroupTom are stored using the attribute DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible (70.9-3).

DerivedSubgroupsTom is just the list of DerivedSubgroupTom values for all values of sub.
DerivedSubgroupsTom.title: DerivedSubgroupTom

DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible.name: DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible
DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible.description:
Let tom be a table of marks. The value of the attribute DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible is a list in which the value at position i –if bound– is a positive integer or a list; the meaning of the entry is the same as in DerivedSubgroupTom (70.9-2).

If the value of the attribute DerivedSubgroupsTomUnique is known for tom then it is a list of positive integers, the value at position i being the position of the class of derived subgroups of the i-th class of subgroups in tom.

The derived subgroups are in general not uniquely determined by the table of marks if no UnderlyingGroup (70.7-7) value is stored, so there is no default method for DerivedSubgroupsTomUnique. But in some cases the derived subgroups are explicitly set when the table of marks is constructed. In this case, DerivedSubgroupTom (70.9-2) does not set values in the DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible list.
DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible.title: DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible

DerivedSubgroupsTomUnique.name: DerivedSubgroupsTomUnique
DerivedSubgroupsTomUnique.description:
Let tom be a table of marks. The value of the attribute DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible is a list in which the value at position i –if bound– is a positive integer or a list; the meaning of the entry is the same as in DerivedSubgroupTom (70.9-2).

If the value of the attribute DerivedSubgroupsTomUnique is known for tom then it is a list of positive integers, the value at position i being the position of the class of derived subgroups of the i-th class of subgroups in tom.

The derived subgroups are in general not uniquely determined by the table of marks if no UnderlyingGroup (70.7-7) value is stored, so there is no default method for DerivedSubgroupsTomUnique. But in some cases the derived subgroups are explicitly set when the table of marks is constructed. In this case, DerivedSubgroupTom (70.9-2) does not set values in the DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible list.
DerivedSubgroupsTomUnique.title: DerivedSubgroupsTomPossible

NormalizerTom.name: NormalizerTom
NormalizerTom.description:
Let tom be the table of marks of a group G. NormalizerTom tries to find the conjugacy class of the normalizer N in G of a subgroup U in the sub-th class of tom. The return value is either the list of class numbers of those subgroups that have the right size and contain the subgroup and all subgroups that clearly contain it as a normal subgroup, or the class number of the normalizer if it is uniquely determined by these conditions. If tom knows the subgroup lattice of G (see IsTableOfMarksWithGens (70.10-3)) then all normalizers are uniquely determined. NormalizerTom should never return an empty list.

NormalizersTom returns the list of positions of the classes of normalizers of subgroups in tom. In addition to the criteria for a single class of subgroup used by NormalizerTom, the approximations of normalizers for several classes are used and thus NormalizersTom may return better approximations than NormalizerTom.

The example shows that a subgroup with class number 4 in A_5 (which is a Kleinian four group) is normalized by a subgroup in class 8. This class contains the subgroups of A_5 which are isomorphic to A_4.
NormalizerTom.title: NormalizerTom

NormalizersTom.name: NormalizersTom
NormalizersTom.description:
Let tom be the table of marks of a group G. NormalizerTom tries to find the conjugacy class of the normalizer N in G of a subgroup U in the sub-th class of tom. The return value is either the list of class numbers of those subgroups that have the right size and contain the subgroup and all subgroups that clearly contain it as a normal subgroup, or the class number of the normalizer if it is uniquely determined by these conditions. If tom knows the subgroup lattice of G (see IsTableOfMarksWithGens (70.10-3)) then all normalizers are uniquely determined. NormalizerTom should never return an empty list.

NormalizersTom returns the list of positions of the classes of normalizers of subgroups in tom. In addition to the criteria for a single class of subgroup used by NormalizerTom, the approximations of normalizers for several classes are used and thus NormalizersTom may return better approximations than NormalizerTom.

The example shows that a subgroup with class number 4 in A_5 (which is a Kleinian four group) is normalized by a subgroup in class 8. This class contains the subgroups of A_5 which are isomorphic to A_4.
NormalizersTom.title: NormalizerTom

ContainedTom.name: ContainedTom
ContainedTom.description: ContainedTom returns the number of subgroups in class sub1 of the table of marks tom that are contained in one fixed member of the class sub2.
ContainedTom.title: ContainedTom

ContainingTom.name: ContainingTom
ContainingTom.description: ContainingTom returns the number of subgroups in class sub2 of the table of marks tom that contain one fixed member of the class sub1.
ContainingTom.title: ContainingTom

CyclicExtensionsTom.name: CyclicExtensionsTom
CyclicExtensionsTom.description:
According to A. Dress [Dre69], two columns of the table of marks tom are equal modulo the prime p if and only if the corresponding subgroups are connected by a chain of normal extensions of order p.

Called with tom and p, CyclicExtensionsTom returns the classes of this equivalence relation.

In the second form, list must be a list of primes, and the return value is the list of classes of the relation obtained by considering chains of normal extensions of prime order where all primes are in list. The default value for list is the set of prime divisors of the order of the group of tom.
CyclicExtensionsTom.title: CyclicExtensionsTom

CyclicExtensionsTom.name: CyclicExtensionsTom
CyclicExtensionsTom.description:
According to A. Dress [Dre69], two columns of the table of marks tom are equal modulo the prime p if and only if the corresponding subgroups are connected by a chain of normal extensions of order p.

Called with tom and p, CyclicExtensionsTom returns the classes of this equivalence relation.

In the second form, list must be a list of primes, and the return value is the list of classes of the relation obtained by considering chains of normal extensions of prime order where all primes are in list. The default value for list is the set of prime divisors of the order of the group of tom.
CyclicExtensionsTom.title: CyclicExtensionsTom

DecomposedFixedPointVector.name: DecomposedFixedPointVector
DecomposedFixedPointVector.description:
Let tom be the table of marks of a group G and let fix be a vector of fixed point numbers w.r.t. an action of G, i.e., a vector which contains for each class of subgroups the number of fixed points under the given action. DecomposedFixedPointVector returns the decomposition of fix into rows of the table of marks. This decomposition corresponds to a decomposition of the action into transitive constituents. Trailing zeros in fix may be omitted.

The vector fix may be any vector of integers. The resulting decomposition, however, will not be integral, in general.
DecomposedFixedPointVector.title: DecomposedFixedPointVector

EulerianFunctionByTom.name: EulerianFunctionByTom
EulerianFunctionByTom.description:
